**Key Quote:** "The topics leading to Pariksit Maharaj meeting Shukadeva Goswami and consequently Shukadeva Goswami spoke the Bhagavatam to Pariksit."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the context of the lecture, focusing on Pariksit Maharaj's encounter with Shukadeva Goswami and the subsequent narration of the Srimad Bhagavatam.

In this session, we will be discussing from the fourth verse of the sixteenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This chapter relates how Pariksit received the age of Kali, the beginning of the age of Kali. The fourth verse is as follows. The meaning of this verse is, once when Maharaj Pariksit was on his way to conquer the world, he saw the master of Kali Yuga, who is lower than a Shudra, disguised as a king and hurting the legs of a cow and bull. The king at once caught hold of him to deal sufficient punishment. So, the description continues by Suta Goswami to the sage Naimisharanya. The topics leading to Pariksit Maharaj meeting Shukadeva Goswami and consequently Shukadeva Goswami spoke the Bhagavatam to Pariksit.

### Kali Yuga, King's Duty, Cow Protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/StbTedGw_0I?t=252
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The purpose of a king's going out to conquer the world is not for self-aggrandizement."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the disguised nature of Kali Yuga's representative and Pariksit's reaction, then transitions to explaining the true purpose of a king's world tour, emphasizing service and not self-aggrandizement.

And he is disguising himself as a king, Nripalingadhara. He is dressed as a Kshatriya, externally, as a king. But his activities are low class. What was he doing? He was hurting the legs of a cow and bull. And Pariksit could not tolerate this. Immediately, Pariksit caught hold of this Kalipurusha and decided to give him sufficient punishment to stop him completely from hurting a cow and bull. So, Srila Prabhupada explains, in the purport, the purpose of a king's going out to conquer the world is not for self-aggrandizement. What is the meaning of self-aggrandizement? He doesn't want to accumulate power, wealth. And it is not for self-aggrandizement. He is not interested in people glorifying him. It is explained here. Maharaj Pariksit went out to conquer the world after his ascendance to the throne. But this was not for the purpose of aggression on other states. It was not for conquering some other places. Because it is said here, he was the emperor of the world and all small states were already under his regime.

### King's Duty, Godly State, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/StbTedGw_0I?t=375
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "His purpose in going out was to see how things were going on in terms of the godly state."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on the king's duty to ensure a godly state, acting as a representative of Krishna and executing His will, emphasizing the purpose of creation and the king's role in fulfilling it.

So what is the purpose for his going out on a world tour? His purpose in going out was to see how things were going on in terms of the godly state. This is the real purpose of the king touring his kingdom. To see how things are going on in terms of the godly state. Srila Prabhupada further says, the king being the representative of the Lord, Supreme Lord Krishna, has to execute the will of the Lord Krishna. This is the Lord's duty. Ultimately, who is the supreme controller of all controllers? It is Krishna. And Krishna has got a certain plan. How this world should be run. There is a purpose for this creation. So whether the purpose of the creation is being met, that is what should be the concern of the king as the representative of Krishna.

### Krishna Consciousness, Dharma, King's Duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/StbTedGw_0I?t=464
**Verse References:** BG 5.29, Isha Upanishad
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The king knows very well that this entire world belongs to Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment reinforces the concept of Krishna as the supreme proprietor and controller, highlighting Pariksit's understanding of this truth and his commitment to upholding dharma as prescribed by Krishna, emphasizing the king's duty to enforce these principles.

There is no question of self-aggrandizement. Means, the king knows very well that this entire world belongs to Krishna. Isha vasya midam sarvam. Or as the Bhagavad Gita says, Karvaloka Maheshwaram. Krishna is the supreme controller, supreme lord of all planets, all universes. And in the Isha Upanishad, the supreme lord is the supreme proprietor of every place. So, Pariksit is not under illusion that, I am the enjoyer, I am the controller, I am the master, I am powerful. He is not under such an illusion. So he wanted to see that things are going on exactly according to the codes of dharma as given by Krishna. Whether people are following the code of dharma. The king has to enforce that. That's the duty of the king. Thus, as soon as Maharaj Pariksit saw that a lower class man in the dress of a king was hurting the legs of a common bull, he at once arrested and punished him. So this is the duty of the king. This is the characteristic of a God conscious king who is ruling exactly according to the plan of the supreme lord.

### Cow Protection, Brahmanical Culture, Spiritual Progress

**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of cow protection and the protection of brahmanas as essential components of brahmanical culture, which ultimately leads to spiritual progress and the opportunity to return to Godhead.

So Srila Prabhupada says, the king cannot tolerate insults to the most important animal, the cow. Nor can he tolerate disrespect for the most important man, the brahmana. So, Krishna as the supreme lord is offered prayer. Namo brahmanya devaya go brahmana hitaya jaya. Krishna wants to see that these two personalities or these two factors are always taken care. Go brahmana. The cows are protected and the brahmanas are protected. This is brahmanical culture which is ultimately meant for the spiritual progress of everyone in the whole world so that they utilize the human form of life for preparing themselves to go back to Godhead. Human civilization means to advance the cause of brahmanical culture. And to maintain it, cow protection is essential. Brahmanical culture is centered around the understanding that every living being is the soul not the body and Krishna is the supreme soul and we are all part and parcel of Krishna. We belong to Krishna and we are meant to return to Krishna in his personal abode in the spiritual kingdom of God. So that is the brahmanical culture. And this brahmanical culture is maintained by cow protection. What is the connection between brahmanical culture and cow protection?

### Cow Protection, Kali Yuga, Mismanagement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/StbTedGw_0I?t=881
**Verse References:** BG 18.44
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The age of Kali means mismanagement and quarrel."
**Summary:** This segment describes the characteristics of Kali Yuga, emphasizing mismanagement and quarrel stemming from unqualified individuals in positions of power, particularly their disregard for cow protection and preference for sinful activities.

Specially in the Bhagavad Gita, it is said, go raksha. No other animal is mentioned as deserving special protection. Only the cow deserves special protection. Of course, the scriptures say, don't harm any other creature. Ahimsa. But, the cow is to be given special protection in addition to the injunction, Vedic injunction, don't harm any other creature. Don't harm others. There is also the injunction that the cow has to be given extra care of special protection. Because it is the most important animal in human society. So, the characteristic of this age of Kali, the present age we are living in, is being described here by Srila Prabhupada. The age of Kali means mismanagement and quarrel. And the root cause of all mismanagement and quarrel is that worthless men with the lower modes, who are situated in lower modes, who have no higher ambition in life, come to the helm of this state management. And, such people, they don't know the importance of cow protection. Instead of taking the milk of the cow, they want to drink the blood of the cow. So, this is because they are situated in lower modes, and they are very sinful, and they are very, very, they are unqualified. So, therefore, Pariksit Maharaj wanted to stop this. And, therefore, we will see what steps he took. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

So, the sages of Naimisharanya, they are very eager to hear topics connected with Krishna. In the beginning itself, they ask six questions related to Krishna. So, these questions pertaining to Krishna are completely satisfying to the soul, to the real self. Therefore, such questions are very, very important, and answers to such questions can solve all problems, can bring about peace and prosperity in the entire world. It can purify the heart of the conditioned souls. It brings in all auspiciousness. It brings in enlightenment. So, therefore, it's so important to discuss these topics. Now, in the Purpose of the Lord Krishna, there is a very important topic. What Srila Prabhupada says, Savanaka and the rishis were astonished to hear that the pious Maharaj Pariksit simply punished the culprit and did not kill him. See, the sages of Naimisharanya, they were expecting Pariksit as the Mahabhagavata, as the ideal king, ideal emperor, ideal ruler, that he will never... that he will never tolerate even a little harm done to a cow. ### Kali Yuga, cow protection, leadership, sin
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UQB_jav9ggQ?t=283
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Simply kill the fellow on the spot. No mercy."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Srila Prabhupada's perspective on the incident, emphasizing the importance of immediate and severe punishment for those who cheat and insult cows, contrasting it with the degraded state of Kali Yuga where unqualified individuals are elected as administrators and mass cow slaughter occurs.

In general, a conditioned soul is inclined to cheat, but this kind of cheating, dressing like a king while actually behaving as the lowest class person, is real, real big cheating. Big cheating. His actions suggest that he is a very low class fellow, but externally he appears to be dressed like a king. So, this cheating has to be at once stopped. And how can it be stopped? Simply kill the fellow on the spot. No mercy. No relief for him. No pardon. No excuse. Not at all. So, Srila Prabhupada says, the rishis in those days, headed by Saunaka, however, could not even imagine that in the advanced days of the age of Kali, the lowest of the shudras will be elected as administrators. Then there was one person, Parikshit's time. But now there are so many of, so many people like that Kali Purusha. And they are the lowest among men in the society, but they are elected as administrators, playing the role of the king. And such people will open organized slaughterhouses for killing cows. Mass killing. They could not even imagine that Kali Yuga, as time progresses, will become so degraded. Then one person was just beating a cow and a bull. Just beating, mind you. That itself, the rishi's reaction was, he should have immediately killed. Then what is the punishment for somebody opens a slaughterhouse and mass killing of cows. Helplessly, these innocent, pure animals are being killed by the thousands of, tens of thousands of, lakhs, millions. Especially in America.

### cow protection, sin, Krishna consciousness, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UQB_jav9ggQ?t=454
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everything in this material world in relationship with the three gunas is actually impure. But when dovetailed with Krishna everything becomes purified."
**Summary:** The speaker continues on the topic of cow killing, citing scriptures that describe the severe punishment for cow killers. He then returns to the main point: that the sages are interested in any topic connected to Krishna, because Krishna is the purifying agent.

So, because the scriptures say, a cow killer is punished by Yamaraj to be sent to hell for punishment for as many years as there are hair on the body of the cow. You can imagine how severe the punishment is if he escapes punishment before he dies. After death, punishment is abating those who escape punishment before they die. Suitable punishment. So, this cow killing is the greatest sin. Greatest sin. There can't be any greater sin than cow killing. So, further Srila Prabhupada says, anyway, although hearing about a Shudraka who was a cheat and an insulter of a cow was not very interesting to the great rishis, they nevertheless wanted to hear about it to see if the event had any connection with Lord Krishna. See, they are interested in hearing all topics connected with Krishna. It doesn't matter what the topic is. It should be in relationship with Krishna. That's all they want to hear. So, Prabhupada says the characteristic of Bhagavatam is there are so many topics about sociology, politics, economics, cultural affairs, but all of them are in relation with Krishna. Therefore, all of them are worth hearing because Krishna is the purifying agent in all these matters. Everything in this material world in relationship with the three gunas is actually impure. But when dovetailed with Krishna everything becomes purified. That's the principle of the Bhagavatam.

### devotion, Krishna consciousness, transcendental knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UQB_jav9ggQ?t=595
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The devotees of the Lord are accustomed to licking up the honey available from the lotus feet of the Lord. What is the use of topics which simply waste one's valuable life?"
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the sixth verse, explaining that devotees are only interested in topics related to Krishna, as all other topics are a waste of time. He then quotes Srila Prabhupada, explaining that topics of Krishna and his devotees are equally good.

The sixth verse. athavasya padam bhoja makaranda liham satam kim manyair asad alaapair ayusho yad asad vyayaha. The meaning of the sixth verse is: The devotees of the Lord are accustomed to licking up the honey available from the lotus feet of the Lord. What is the use of topics which simply waste one's valuable life? Characteristic of a devotee of Krishna is the devotee only wants to hear topics connected with Krishna. All other topics are waste of time. So Srila Prabhupada says in the purport Lord Krishna and his devotees are both on the transcendental plane above the three gunas. Therefore the topics of Lord Krishna and of his pure devotees are equally good. Prabhupada gives an example The battle of Kurukshetra is full of politics and diplomacy. But because the topics are related with Lord Krishna the Bhagavad Gita is therefore adored all over the world. There is no need to eradicate politics, economics, sociology etc. which are mundane to the mundane-ers.

### devotion, Krishna consciousness, transcendental knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UQB_jav9ggQ?t=675
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For a pure devotee he actually sees everything in relationship with Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that while some philosophers reject worldly topics, pure devotees see everything in relation to Krishna and dovetail everything with the Lord's service. He emphasizes that even seemingly mundane topics like the Pandavas' activities or Pariksit Maharaj's dealings are important because of their connection to Krishna.

Some class of philosophers they consider topics like economics, politics, sociology connected with this world, the world we live in as material subject matter and they don't want to hear at all. But for a pure devotee he actually sees everything in relationship with Krishna. And therefore he is able to relate everything with Krishna. So that relationship of everything with Krishna is possible when everything is dovetailed with the Lord's service, with pure devotional service. So Prabhupada is explaining We have heard and talked about the activities of the Pandavas and we are now dealing with topics of Maharaj Paricharan. But because all these topics are related to the Supreme Lord Krishna they are all transcendental and pure devotees have great interest in hearing them. You see. How? Even the fighting on the battlefield of Kurukshetra or Paricharan Maharaj dealing with a low class person who was hurting a cow these are important for us because they are in relationship with Krishna. Because the Pandavas are pure devotees of Krishna Paricharan Maharaj is a pure devotee of Krishna therefore those topics are of interest to us as devotees.

### time management, self-realization, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UQB_jav9ggQ?t=794
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is our duty to see that not a moment of our life is wasted in topics which are not related with Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of not wasting time on topics unrelated to Krishna, given the limited and uncertain duration of life. He advises those serious about self-realization to focus on Krishna-related topics.

We have already discussed this matter in connection with the prayers of Bhishma and Deva. In the ninth chapter of the first canto this is discussed in detail. Our duration of life is not very long and there is no certainty of when we shall be ordered to leave everything for the next stage. Of course we don't die a spirit soul, but we have to quit this body at a certain time when ordered to do so. There is a certain life span we can live in this body and we exactly do not know how many years we are going to live. It is our duty to see that not a moment of our life is wasted in topics which are not related with Krishna. Somebody who is serious about self-realization or going back to Godhead should not waste a valuable time in this human form of life in topics which are disconnected from Krishna.

---
### devotion, Krishna consciousness, spiritual abode
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UQB_jav9ggQ?t=860
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To take shelter of the lotus feet of the Lord means to be engaged always in a relationship with topics of devotional service to Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of 'licking up the honey' from the Lord's lotus feet, describing Krishna's eternal abode, Goloka Vrindavana, and the nectar-like relish devotees experience when taking shelter of Krishna's lotus feet, which means engaging in devotional service to Krishna.

Now, Srila Prabhupada explains what is this licking up the honey available at the lotus feet of the Lord. Prabhupada says the spiritual planet Goloka Vrindavana, the eternal abode of Krishna, is shaped like the wall of a lotus flower. Even when the Lord descends to any of the mundane planets, any of the planets in this material world, He does so by manifesting His own abode as it is. Krishna's abode comes down with Him. When He comes His feet remain always on the same big wall of the lotus flower. And His feet are also as beautiful as the lotus flower. Therefore it is said Lord Krishna has lotus feet. You would have heard this many times. Krishna's feet are described as lotus feet because His feet are always resting on the lotus flower. So there is a certain honey available in the lotus flower. And that honey is what the devotees are accustomed to tasting when they take shelter of Krishna's lotus feet. That's why devotees are attached to Krishna's lotus feet. And that honey is as relishable as nectar. Therefore constantly the devotees want to be sheltered by Krishna's lotus feet. And that's why it is said devotees are accustomed to licking up the honey available from the lotus feet of the Lord. So to take shelter of the lotus feet of the Lord means to be engaged always in a relationship with topics of devotional service to Krishna. That is the meaning of taking shelter of the lotus feet of the Lord. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Key Quote:** "All of us desire freedom from death, especially those who are devotees, and freedom from death means getting eternal life."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the seventh verse of the sixteenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, discussing the desire for freedom from death and the path to eternal life through devotion.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devi Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 7th and 8th verses of the 16th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. 7th verse: Shudra Ayusham Dranam Anga Martyanaam Rutaam Ichchatam Ihopahuto Bhagavan Mrutyu Shamitra Karmani. The meaning of this verse is, O Suta Goswami, there are those amongst men who desire freedom from death and get eternal life. They escape the slaughtering process by calling the controller of death, Yamaraja. A very important verse. All of us desire freedom from death, especially those who are devotees. And freedom from death means getting eternal life. In Krishna's abode, eternal life full of bliss and knowledge. Now what is the way to get this freedom from death and get eternal life? It is to escape the slaughtering process by calling the controller of death, Yamaraja. So what is the meaning of this?

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Modern scientists, they do not know the real self, the spirit. They think only in terms of the body, but this body is always a dead body."
**Summary:** This segment explains Srila Prabhupada's purport, contrasting the futile attempts of modern science to avoid death through physical means with the spiritual understanding of the soul's eternality.

Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, the living entity as he develops from lower animal life to a higher human being and gradually to higher intelligence becomes anxious to get free from death. It is to get free from the clutches of death. We know nobody wants to die. We all know that, but then death is forced upon us. So in the human form of life, we have developed intelligence to understand how can we become deathless. That is the prerogative of the human being because of developed intelligence. Modern scientists try to avoid death by physiochemical advancement of knowledge. Modern scientists, they do not know the real self, the spirit. They think only in terms of the body, but this body is always a dead body. It appears living because of the presence of the spirit soul. Otherwise this body is always a dead body because it is made of a matter. It doesn't have life by itself. It doesn't have life. So any attempt to avoid death by analyzing the chemicals, by manipulating the chemicals or the physical body, this is never going to work. So Srila Prabhupada says, even though they try to avoid death, the controller of death, Yamaraj, is so cruel that he does not spare even the very life of the scientist himself. The scientist who is trying to do research, how to avoid death by some chemical manipulation, even that scientist himself has to die. So nobody is spared by Yamaraj. The scientist who put forward the theory of stopping death by advancement of scientific knowledge becomes himself a victim of death when he is called by Yamaraj.

---
### death, devotion, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E-7pQuc0ZJE?t=276
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The only hope of suspending the cruel slaughtering process of Yamaraj is to call him to hear and chant the holy name of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the only way to avoid death is to invite Yamaraj to hear the chanting of the holy name of the Lord, referencing the sacrifice performed at Naimisharanya.

Yamaraj, exactly when the span of life is over, he sends the Yamadutas to take the soul out of the body, to drag the soul. Now we will be forced to leave the body, not that we have a choice, whether you want to leave the body or you want to continue to live in the body. No. What to speak of stopping death? No one can enhance the short period of life even by a fraction of a moment. So what is the way out then? The only hope of suspending the cruel slaughtering process of Yamaraj is to call him to hear and chant the holy name of the Lord. This is the process. Invite Yamaraj for Sankirtana involving the chanting and hearing of the Hare Krishna mantra, the holy name of the Supreme Lord. Yamaraj is a great devotee of the Lord and he likes to be invited to kirtanas and sacrifices by pure devotees who are constantly engaged in the devotional service of the Supreme Lord. Thus the great sages headed by Saunaka and others, invited Yamaraj to attend the sacrifice performed at Naimisharanya. This was good for those who did not want to die. It is described in the beginning of the Bhagavatam, the first canto beginning, that the sages of Naimisharanya undertook to perform a thousand year sacrifice. And what was this thousand year sacrifice? It was all about chanting and hearing the glory of the Lord. The stories of the Supreme Lord.

---
### death, devotion, Bhagavatam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E-7pQuc0ZJE?t=386
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.8
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As long as Yamarāja, who causes everyone's death, is present here, no one shall meet with death... Living beings who are under his grip should take advantage by hearing the deathless nectar in the form of this narration of the transcendental pastimes of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment explains that engaging in devotional activities, especially hearing the transcendental pastimes of the Lord in the Bhagavatam, allows one to transcend material existence and avoid death, ultimately leading to Krishna's abode.

So they began inquiry about Krishna and they wanted to hear the glorification of Krishna. So that is the way to stop death, to avoid death. Those who are engaged in devotional activities transcend the conditions of material life, birth, disease, old age, death, three-fold miseries, everything they are able to transcend. The eighth verse, na kaścin mriyate tāvad yāvad āsta īhāntakaḥ etad arthaṁ hi bhagavān āhūtaḥ paramarṣivihi aho nṛloke pīyeta hari-līlāmṛtaṁ va caḥ. The meaning of this eighth verse is as follows. As long as Yamarāja, who causes everyone's death, is present here, no one shall meet with death. The great sages have invited the controller of death, Yamarāja, who is the representative of the Lord. Living beings who are under his grip should take advantage by hearing the deathless nectar in the form of this narration of the transcendental pastimes of the Lord. So the method is how to escape death? To be engaged in hearing the transcendental pastimes of the Lord as described in this Bhagavatam. Then Yamarāja will not touch us. Then what happens? Yamarāja will not touch us. Yamarāja will not drag us out of this body for another term of material existence in another body. No. Then what happens? Actually, when the time comes for quitting the body, Kṛṣṇa will take us to His personal abode for eternal life, full of bliss and knowledge.

---
### death, Bhagavatam, spiritual body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E-7pQuc0ZJE?t=521
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The surest remedy for avoiding death is to accustom oneself to hearing the nectarean pastimes of the Lord as they are systematically narrated in the text of Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that hearing Srimad Bhagavatam daily is the surest way to avoid death, uncover the spiritual body, and ultimately return to Godhead.

Not that we are going to continue in this body beyond the lifespan. No. That is not the program. That's not the spiritual program. Don't mistake. Mistakenly think that I'm going to live in this body forever. This body is perishable. antavanta ime deha nityasyokta śarīrinaha. The soul inside the body is eternal. This body is perishable. So in the Purport Śrīla Prabhupāda says, every human being dislikes meeting death, but he does not know how to get rid of death. The surest remedy for avoiding death is to accustom oneself to hearing the nectarean pastimes of the Lord as they are systematically narrated in the text of Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam. So we have begun everyday Bhāgavatam, little, little, little. You keep hearing. In this way you can conquer death. That's what is being said here. It is advised herein, therefore, that any human being who desires freedom from death should take to this course of life as recommended by the rishis headed by Saunaka. Every day hear Bhāgavatam. Therefore we have entitled this series as Daily Evening Bhāgavatam Discourse. Of course, in every ISKCON center, there is a program of daily morning Bhāgavatam class. That's also available. But those who cannot attend that daily morning Bhāgavatam class, either in the temple or online, we have this Daily Evening Bhāgavatam class. So at least evening you can attend. So this is the way to conquer death. Now how does it work? Simply if I hear Bhāgavatam, how do I become deathless? What does this hearing Bhāgavatam do? We have already discussed this in the first canto, second chapter. śṛṇvatāṁ śukathāḥ kṛṣṇahaḥ puṇyaḥ śravaṇaḥ kīrtanaḥ hṛdyantastohi abhadrāṇi vidhunoti surucatām.

---
### Krishna, purification, incarnation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E-7pQuc0ZJE?t=660
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is seated in our heart. When he sees that we are eager to hear Kṛṣṇa kathā in the Bhāgavatam, then he cleanses our desire for material enjoyment."
**Summary:** This segment explains how hearing Bhagavatam cleanses the heart of material desires through Krishna's mercy, emphasizing the importance of the Bhagavatam, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and the Hare Krishna mantra as incarnations in Kali Yuga.

Krishna is seated in our heart. When he sees that we are eager to hear Kṛṣṇa kathā in the Bhāgavatam, then he cleanses our desire for material enjoyment. It is Kṛṣṇa's blessing, Kṛṣṇa's mercy for those who hear Bhāgavatam, because Bhāgavatam is another incarnation of Kṛṣṇa in this Kali Yuga. Kali Yuga three incarnations are very important. The first incarnation which is important in Kali Yuga is the incarnation of Chaitanya Mahāprabhu. He teaches us simply chant Hare Kṛṣṇa and hear Bhāgavatam and take prasādam and thereby prepare yourself to go back to Godhead. Then the second incarnation of the Supreme Lord in Kali Yuga is this Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam. It contains narrations about Kṛṣṇa and His pure devotees. Simply hearing this every day will purify you and make you eligible to go back to Godhead at the end of this life. And the third incarnation is the incarnation of the Hare Kṛṣṇa mantra, the holy name of the Supreme Lord in the form of the Hare Kṛṣṇa mantra. So these three incarnations are very, very important. So here, specific recommendation is hear the Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam every day. At your convenience you can hear.

---
### purification, devotional service, spiritual body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E-7pQuc0ZJE?t=773
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "After life in this body is completed, straight go to Kṛṣṇa's abode in your spiritual body."
**Summary:** This segment reiterates the importance of daily hearing of Bhagavatam, chanting, and accepting Prasadam to purify the heart, realize one's spiritual identity, engage in devotional service, and ultimately return to Krishna's abode.

If you can't hear live, then later on you can always hear, but hear every day little, little, little, little, little. This is important. And then you become purified. Your heart becomes cleansed of all misgivings. And then you understand that you are not the body. You realize that. And then when you realize you are not the body, you understand what is the purpose of this body. The purpose of this body is simply to engage in devotional service to Kṛṣṇa and thereby become free from all desires for extending our material existence into another material body after quitting this body. Don't extend. After life in this body is completed, straight go to Kṛṣṇa's abode in your spiritual body. So to uncover the spiritual body before death comes, it is possible by this practice of hearing Bhāgavatam every day. It is possible by daily chanting and hearing Hare Kṛṣṇa mantra. It is possible by daily accepting Prasādam. In this way you can prepare yourself. And you'll be guaranteed if you do this every day little, little, little, little, little, you're guaranteed to go back to Godhead at the end of this life. Thank you very much. Hare Kṛṣṇa.

### human life, intelligence, laziness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nrf5hawpRxc?t=48
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "There are the intelligent people who engage in hearing Bhagavatam and escape the slaughtering process of Yamaraj; they escape death."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the ninth verse of the sixteenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, contrasting intelligent people who engage in spiritual practices with lazy and less intelligent individuals who waste their human life.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 9th verse of the 16th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Mandasya Mandasya Prajnasya Vayo Mandayushas Chavai Nidrayahriyate Enaktam Divacha Vyartakarmabhihi. The meaning of this verse is lazy human beings with paltry intelligence and a short duration of life pass the night sleeping and the day performing activities that are for naught. So here the description is being given by Shounaka Rishi. There are the intelligent people who engage in hearing Bhagavatam and escape the slaughtering process of Yamaraj; they escape death. They pass on from this body to Krishna's abode for eternal life. And then there are lazy, less intelligent, foolish human beings also who miss out the rare opportunity in the human form of life to put an end to this suffering of death and disease and old age. They are described here; first of all, their characteristic is such unfortunate people are lazy.

---
### laziness, intelligence, time
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nrf5hawpRxc?t=126
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Less intelligent means they misuse their intelligence for material sense enjoyment; they don't utilize their intelligence for spiritual activities for spiritual knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the characteristics of lazy and less intelligent people, emphasizing their misuse of intelligence for material enjoyment and their wasting of the short lifespan in Kali Yuga on sleeping and useless activities.

They are lazy, especially they are lazy when it comes to exerting oneself to hear Bhagavatam, lazy when it comes to chanting Hare Krishna, lazy when it comes to avoiding sinful activities. They're lazy, and another characteristic is paltry inclinations, paltry intelligence, very, very little brain substance. They're less intelligent. Now, less intelligent, does it mean that some people have born less intelligent, so what can they do? No, this is not what is meant by less intelligent. Less intelligent means they misuse their intelligence for material sense enjoyment. They don't utilize their intelligence for spiritual activities for spiritual knowledge. They don't use their intelligence properly. That's the meaning here. Please don't misunderstand this. And the third characteristic is that life in Kali Yuga is characterized by short duration, and even that short duration of life, they are wasting, completely losing out all the time available in this human body. In what way? At night, they are simply engaged in sleeping, and in the daytime, they are engaged in useless activities which are not at all helpful for their conquering death, that are not meant for conquering death, that are not meant for gaining eternal life. They are simply a waste of time. So Prabhupada describes about this time.

---
### human life, suffering, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nrf5hawpRxc?t=289
**Verse References:** BG 8.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The human form is a special gift of material nature; what is that gift? It is a chance to achieve the highest boon of life, namely to get out of the entanglement of repeated birth and death."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Prabhupada's purport, explaining that less intelligent people don't understand the value of human life as a chance to escape the cycle of birth and death, which is full of suffering.

So Prabhupada describes about this in the purport. The less intelligent do not know the real value of the human form of life. The human form is a special gift of material nature in the course of her enforcing stringent laws of miseries upon the living being. Every type of body, 84 lakh species are there, different types of bodies, birds and bees and insects and worms and reptiles and aquatics and plants and germs, so many varieties, different types of bodies. And in each and every body, there is suffering, there is misery. But still, human form of body also there is suffering, there is misery, but still this human form is a special gift of material nature. What is that gift? It is a chance to achieve the highest boon of life, namely to get out of the entanglement of repeated birth and death. This is explained in the Bhagavad Gita also: jaatas yahi dhruva mrtyur dhruvam janmam ritasya cha. One has taken birth in this material world in a material body, sure to die. That means has to quit the body. Now, after taking birth, one has to live for certain number of years and quit the body. That's called death. Now, after quitting the body, if one is not prepared to go back to Godhead, one has to again take birth. Dhruvam janmam ritasya cha, birth is certain, another body, another term of suffering and misery. So this is called entanglement in the repetition of birth and death. So to get out of this is possible in the human form of life, and how do you take benefit of this human form of life to get out of the cycle of birth and death of life.

---

### sense enjoyment, materialism, economic development
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nrf5hawpRxc?t=544
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such people they become more interested in so-called Economic development work very hard throughout life simply for sense enjoyment of the temporary body."
**Summary:** The speaker criticizes those who prioritize economic development and sense enjoyment over spiritual pursuits, pointing out the temporary nature of the material body and the futility of seeking lasting happiness through sensory experiences.

So such people, they become more interested in so-called Economic development, work very hard throughout life simply for sense enjoyment of the temporary body. This body anyway is going to perish. It is simply meant for suffering, suffering something, but though they don't like suffering, they think I can overcome this suffering by replacing suffering with some material pleasure. And they have devised so many ways of trying to get some material pleasure by sense enjoyment. So we have senses, the eyes, the ears, the nose, the tongue, and the skin, and we also have the genitals, we have the belly, we have the voice, we have hands, we have legs. All this, all these senses for the materialistic persons, for the less intelligent persons, for the foolish persons, for the lazy persons, for the unfortunate persons, simply they want to use these for enjoying, enjoying, enjoying, enjoying material happiness, material material. And what they don't realize is by the laws of nature, every creature, including the lower animals, birds, worms, insects, the aquatics, the plants, every living being is allowed by nature to enjoy certain type of sense pleasure. Don't think human beings are the only one who are enjoying life and materially. Even the hogs and dogs are enjoying this, particularly pointed out by bhagavata.

---
### sense enjoyment, materialism, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nrf5hawpRxc?t=675
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Sense enjoyment is not the ultimate goal of human life. It is available anyway in all forms of creature."
**Summary:** Using the example of a hog relishing stool, the speaker illustrates that sense enjoyment is available to all creatures, not just humans, and that it is not the ultimate goal of human life.

The stool eating hog, wit varaha, so stool eating hog, it eats rubbish, but it relishes that, it becomes fat, it takes lot pleasure in that. And we as human beings will look at the hog and say what a an abominable creature, dirty, filthy, how can it enjoy eating some filly stool, rubbish, rejected stuff? But the hog doesn't think like we think, so it is enjoying in its own way. Every creature is enjoying it's own way, but that doesn't mean they're only enjoying all the time, know, suffering by miseries inflicted upon them by material nature. So therefore, Prabhupada says, one should definitely try to understand that sense enjoyment is not the ultimate goal of human life. It is available anyway in all forms of creature. Herein it is said that during the daytime one works for nothing. When it is said one works for nothing means there is only one goal ultimately of all materialistic persons. They work simply for sense enjoyment, that's all. But that sense enjoyment is not at all giving us happiness or real pleasure.

---
### sense enjoyment, spiritual happiness, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nrf5hawpRxc?t=777
**Verse References:** BG 5.22
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "There is spiritual happiness which is not momentary, which is not by sense contact, which is the natural pleasure of the soul, which is always the normal condition of spiritual existence without any endeavor to get some pleasure."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the fleeting nature of material pleasure derived from sense contact with the eternal and natural happiness of the soul in spiritual existence, advocating for Krishna consciousness as the path to this lasting joy.

It doesn't give us happiness or pleasure because it is a sensation by sense contact which is momentary, which is just for the short moment that there is contact. Just like you place one piece of sweet on your tongue, that contact, supposing you like that sweet, that sweet taste. Moment the contact, there is some sensation of sweet taste, ah, so nice. But can you keep on telling, oh very nice, sweet taste, very nice, sweet taste, very nice, sweet taste, very nice, sweet taste, very nice. Can you keep on telling like that? No. That sweet taste, the sensation will last only for a moment. And then after that you have to place one more piece of sweet for one more sensation by contact. samsparishvaja bhoga. This is described in Bhagavad Gita. This material pleasure is nothing but the sense contact producing a sensation for a moment which we think is oh so nice, so enjoyable, so pleasure. But this is waste of time. There is spiritual happiness which is not momentary, which is not by sense contact, which is the natural pleasure of the soul, which is always the normal condition of spiritual existence without any endeavor to get some pleasure. So that is what awaits us in the human form of life if we take to Krishna consciousness. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Kali Yuga, Dharma, Cow Protection

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of the verse, describing Pariksit Maharaja's observation of Kali Purusha, dressed as a king, beating a cow and a bull.

The meaning of this verse is Suta Goswami said, after reaching that place, Maharaja Pariksit observes that Maharaja Pariksit observes that a lower caste Shudra dressed like a king was beating a cow and a bull with a club as if they had no owner. So the previous chapter ended with the description that while Mother Earth and the personality of religious principles Dharma, Dharini and Dharma were engaged in conversation the saintly king Pariksit reached that place and now the description continues what did Pariksit observe when he reached that place he saw that this Kali Purusha that this Kali Purusha he is dressed like a king but actually he doesn't have the qualification of a king he is not a qualified Kshatriya according to the Vedic understanding he is actually unqualified person but he is dressed like a king means he is trying to occupy the post of the ruler or the or the King without qualification and it was also very clear that the Kshatriyas are meant for protecting the citizens and the domestic animals but here we see this person he is dressed like a king and then he is dressed like a king dressed like a king but he is beating a cow and a bull as if they had no owner.

### Kali Yuga, Varnasrama Dharma, Shudra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-gwH2yw48GE?t=241
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The principal sign of the age of Kali is that lower caste's shudras, men without personal culture and men without spiritual initiation, would be dressed like administrators or Kings."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's purport, explaining the significance of the verse for Kali Yuga, focusing on the characteristics of shudras and their role as unqualified administrators.

So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport what does this mean for us who are living in Kali Yuga it has got a lot of significance this description here the principal sign Srila Prabhupada says in the purport the principal sign of the age of Kali is that lower caste's shudras, what does it mean lower caste shudras men withoutersonical culture and men without spiritual initiation would be dressed like administrators or Kings and the principal business of such non-Ksatria rulers would be to kill the investors. Innocent animals, especially the cows and bulls, who shall be unprotected by their masters, the bona fide vices, the mercantile community. Sushila Prabhupada clearly explains a often misunderstood terminology. In the Vedic scriptures, in the description of the varana and ashrama system of dharma, it is described that the society should be divided according to occupational duties and according to spiritual development into four varanas and four ashramas, four social orders and four spiritual orders. Now, this varana classification has become completely topsy-turvy in this Kali Yuga because of the misunderstanding that one belongs to a varana according to birth in a certain family, which is not. What is explained in the scriptures, clearly in the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says, the quality based on qualifications, based on the qualities possessed by a person, that person should be classified according to the qualities and according to the occupational duties. The one who performs the same occupation according to the work he performs, according to the natural inclination to work in a certain way, a person should actually be classified as a brahmana, kshatriya, vaishya or shudra. So very clearly who are to be classified as shudras, those who do not have brahmanical culture and they do not have spiritual initiation. Sushila Prabhupada explains. That means they are more or less godless people and they are bereft of the brahmanical culture. They are uncultured. According to Vedic description, they are uncultured. So, uncultured, godless people are to be classified as Shudras. Not according to birth. Regardless of which type of family they take birth in, it doesn't matter. If they are uncultured, if they are godless, then they are to be classified as Shudras. Such people will be considered as Shudras. They will be dressed like administrators or kings. This is the sign of Kalyuga.

---
### Kali Yuga, Cow Protection, Animal Slaughter
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-gwH2yw48GE?t=497
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They will simply indulge in killing innocent animals, especially the cow and the bull."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to describe the characteristics of unqualified administrators in Kali Yuga, emphasizing their tendency to kill innocent animals, especially cows and bulls, due to the lack of protection from qualified Vaishyas.

And these administrators who are unqualified, but somehow they have managed to occupy the post of the administrator. They will simply indulge in killing innocent animals. Especially the cow and the bull. And these cows and bulls will be killed by such unqualified administrators. Because such cows and bulls which are meant to be protected by the Vaishya class, the mercantile community in the Vedic culture. These animals will be unprotected. They will be unprotected. So, therefore these administrators can freely kill without anybody questioning them. Without the animals, innocent animals who give lot of service to the human society, not getting any protection. They are helpless. They are innocent. They are helpless. So, this is the situation. Even in this age of Kali, that we are finding it right now in our present times.

---
### Vaishyas, Cow Protection, Agriculture, Trade
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-gwH2yw48GE?t=580
**Verse References:** BG 18.44
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Vaishyas are meant to deal in agriculture, cow protection and trade."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Bhagavad Gita to explain the duties of Vaishyas, highlighting their responsibility for cow protection, agriculture, and trade, and laments the degraded state of Vaishyas in Kali Yuga who send cows to slaughterhouses.

Exact description given in the Srimad Bhagavatam. So, Srila Prabhupada further says, in the Bhagavad Gita, in the 18th chapter, in the 44th verse, it is said, that the Vaishyas are meant to deal in agriculture, cow protection and trade. Krishi, Goraksha, Vaanijjam, Vaishya, Karma, Swabhavajam. By their acquired nature. There are certain class of people in the civilized human society who are meant to deal in three things. Krishi or agriculture. Goraksha, cow protection. Goraksha. And Vaanijja, trade, commerce. So, the cow protection was specifically entrusted to the Vaishyas in the Vedic culture. Now, there are no qualified Vaishyas. So, therefore, the cows are not getting the protection that they deserve. And in this age of Kali, the degraded Vaishyas, the mercantile men, are engaged in supplying cows to slaughterhouses. As long as the cow gives milk, they keep the cow and take the milk and do business with milk and milk products. And when the cow stops giving milk, send it to the slaughterhouse. This is their business. Not that the cow has given so much service and it should be given protection. No such consideration at all. Merciless, heartless, cruel people. Very, very mean-minded. Right? Yes. And it is said in the scriptures that the Kshatriyas, the qualified Kshatriyas, are meant to protect the citizens of the state. Whereas the Vaishyas are meant to protect the cows and bulls and utilize them to produce grains and milk. So, both the humans and the cows and bulls require protection.

---
### Cow Protection, Vedic Injunctions, Karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-gwH2yw48GE?t=742
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So such animals should never be killed. Should never be killed. So that is the Vedic injunction. And it is the greatest sin to kill the cow."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of protecting cows and bulls, highlighting their usefulness and the sin of killing them, warning of the karmic consequences for those who are merciless and cruel.

Because human form of life is very, very important for it affords the opportunity for self-realization. And cows and bulls are especially meant for producing grains and milk. And the cow gives a lot of service. Every part of the cow is very, very useful. Even the cow, the urine and stool of the cow is very useful for human society. Goarka. Cow urine is called Goarka. It has got a lot of medicinal properties. It can be used in so many ways. So many useful ways for human beings. And the cow dung is first-class medicine. It is first-class manure. It is the best fuel for dried cow dung cakes. And it helps in so many ways. So many ways the cow dung can be used. It has got antiseptic properties. When cow dung cake, dried cake is burnt, it drives away mosquitoes. That smoke drives away mosquitoes. So like that, there are innumerable benefits of cow dung and cow urine. Such an animal is very, very, very useful. After the cow dies a natural death, the horn of the cow, the horns of the cow are very useful. The skin of the cow is very useful. Very useful also. So cow is so valuable. The cow tail, the tail of the cow is also very useful. So like this, the cow is such a useful animal. And most importantly, the cow gives us milk, which is the most precious food substance for human beings, especially for human beings. So such animals should never be killed. Should never be killed. So that is the Vedic injunction. And it is the greatest sin to kill the cow. Gohatya is the greatest sin. And people have to suffer in very horrible ways by the laws of nature imposed on such foolish people, ignorant people, merciless people, cruel people. By nature, by God, through the material nature, this punishment will be enforced. People have to suffer. So I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Kali Yuga, Dharma, Shudra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZKuo8LIZWMY?t=54
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.2
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The next symptom of the age of Kali is that principles of religion which are all spotlessly white, like the white lotus flower, will be attacked by the uncultured Shudra population of the age."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam describing the bull (Dharma) being attacked in Kali Yuga, explaining that the bull represents religious principles and the attacker represents the uncultured Shudra population.

In this session we will be discussing from the second verse of the seventeenth chapter of the first canto of Sriman Bhagavatam. Vrisham Vrindala Dhavalam Mehantam Iva Bhyatam Vepamanam Padaikenam Siddhantam Shudra Taditam. The meaning of this verse is, the bull was as white as a white lotus flower. He was terrified of the Shudra who was beating him and he was so afraid that he was standing on one leg trembling and urinating. So further descriptions of the age of Kali is being given here by Suta Goswami, Srila Suta Goswami. The bull is actually the personality of religious principles, Dharma, in the shape, in the formality, in the form of a bull. The personality of religious principles has appeared in order to give us a message. Now Srila Prabhupada explains what is the significance of this bull being described as as white as a white lotus flower. The purpose Srila Prabhupada says, the next symptom of the age of Kali is that principles of religion which are all spotlessly white, like the white lotus flower, will be attacked by the uncultured Shudra population of the age. In the scriptures it is said, Kalau Shudra Sambhavaha, in Kali Yuga majority of the population will be of the Shudra category. Shudra category means uncultured, uncultured. They don't follow the cultural principles.

---
### Garbhadhana, Culture, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZKuo8LIZWMY?t=174
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without such a purificatory process, the children born will be uncultured, will have criminal mentality."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the lack of cultural principles in Kali Yuga, specifically mentioning the Garbha Dhana ritual for conceiving children and how its absence leads to an uncultured population.

They don't have a system for purification of the contamination due to this age. Just like to beget a child according to scriptural directions or injunctions, one should follow what is called as a Garbha Dhana. Garbha Dhana is a ritual where the parents before they combine to conceive a child, they undergo cultural purification. They prepare their consciousness to attract a good soul to take a shelter of good parents. Without such a purificatory process, the children born will be uncultured, will have criminal mentality. So this is very very important that the parents should prepare themselves to beget good class of population, to beget good children. Now because in Kalyuga these principles are not followed, so the majority of the population is of the Shudra quality, means uncultured, contaminated by bad qualities like that. So I think this is very important that the parents should prepare themselves to beget good class of population, to beget good children. Now because in Kalyuga these principles are not followed, so the majority of the population is of the Shudra quality, means uncultured, contaminated by bad qualities like that.

---
### Religious Principles, Kali Yuga, Vedic Wisdom
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZKuo8LIZWMY?t=291
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Religious principles are not a question of belief. That is what people should understand."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to describe the degradation of religious principles in Kali Yuga, emphasizing the defiance of these principles by the uncultured population and comparing it to disobeying state laws in a civilized society.

So the principles of religion are compared to a white lotus flower, means they are spotless, they are perfect. But in Kalyuga, the Shudra class of people who are uncultured, they will not follow the religious principles as it is. So Prabhupada says how they actually spoil the religious principles. Prabhupada says in the parpa, they may be descendants of Brahmana or Kshatriya forefathers, but in this age of Kali, for want of sufficient education and culture of Vedic wisdom, such a Shudra like population will defy the principles of religion. They will not follow the principles of religion. They will go against it, or they won't care for it. Or they will say, we don't believe in such and such religious principles. Religious principles are not a question of belief. That is what people should understand. Just like when we live in a civilized state, the state laws are to be followed by all civilized people, all cultured people. Good citizens, means they are law-abiding. It is not that I don't believe in some rules and regulations which are given by the state, or I don't like to follow, or I don't think I should care about them, or I just don't bother about them. I do whatever I like to do. This sort of attitude is killing the whole vitality of the human population in this age of Kali. So these are, these kind of people are actually upstarts. They actually are less intelligent, though they pose themselves as very, very intelligent. They may claim like that, that they are very intelligent, because they put forward all kinds of arguments against following religious principles. But they are actually uneducated, uncultured, they are less intelligent, they are foolish, and they suffer the consequences of not taking the Vedic wisdom. They don't have sufficient education in the proper way. They don't have proper system of Vedic knowledge. Neither they have the culture of the Vedic wisdom. They don't have good habits, good practices, good traditions. They don't have any of those. So they will defy the principles of religion. And persons who are religiously endowed will be terrified by such things. They also harass others who want to follow the religious principles.

That means real religious principles nobody will follow, most people will not follow, but they will declare that they have their own religious beliefs they are their own religious system that they want to follow because state also gives freedom, freedom, freedom means you can do whatever you like in the name of religion so why one religious system says you should not kill innocent animals another system will say oh as as far as eating is eating habits are concerned that's the person's choice some people like to eat vegetables some people like to eat meat so they will kill animals for eating the flesh of the animal they say our religious system does not prohibit your religious system may prohibit but we don't think it is sinful to kill innocent animals helpless animals even though there is plenty of fruits and vegetables and grains without harming any animal you can eat but no they don't want they don't want to restrict themselves though they also eat fruits vegetables if they want to eat vegetables they don't want to restrict themselves though they also eat fruits vegetables if they the like this is our choice we eat what we like to eat so this is the nature of the people in Kali Yuga.

### Secularism, Religious Indifference, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZKuo8LIZWMY?t=649
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In Kali Yuga, in the name of secularism there will be no religious principles which are proper principles which will be followed by people."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how the proliferation of different religious systems in Kali Yuga leads to a state of secularism and indifference towards real religious principles, with people acting according to their whims and fancies.

And these different so-called register systems will be so many curbs without any foundation, without any proper understanding, without any proper basis, irrational, unreasonable sort of arguments or basis. Whimsical they will have their own so-called religious system. The state will be declared to be secular or without any particular principle of religion. See the religious principles are not to be based on some people's whims and fancies. That can never be the proper system within a state. But in Kali Yuga, in the name of secularism there will be no religious principles which are proper principles which will be followed by people. As a result there will be total indifference to the real principles of religion. Nobody cares. Like the real religious principle is to tell the truth but now they don't care whether they are telling the truth or not. They don't care. lies. As it suits them, if they want to tell truth, they want to be truthful, they are truthful. If they don't want to be truthful, they are not truthful. That's all. They don't care. So like this, the indifference to the actual religious principles.

### Sadhu, Shastra, Guru, Godlessness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZKuo8LIZWMY?t=772
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "People don't care for religious principles. Even if there is a deterioration or decline, they just don't bother."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the Vedic standards of Sadhu, Shastra, and Guru, and how they are disregarded in Kali Yuga, leading to a decline in religious principles and a lack of understanding of the connection between God consciousness and societal peace.

The citizens will be free to act as they like without respect for sadhu, shastra and guru. There are three standards according to our Vedic tradition. Sadhu means the saintly persons who are completely well established in following the religious principles and teaching them to the others because they are solidly well established in understanding, getting trained, getting educated, having knowledge. Having certain good practices and having a sober mind, such saintly people, sadhu, no regard for them, no respect for them in this Kali Yuga. Shastra is the authentic bona fide scriptures, especially the Vedic literature. People don't care for the Vedic literature, what is explained in the Vedas. The truth, the actual facts of life, of this material world, of material existence, that they don't care. And guru, the bona fide spiritual master, though there are many gurus, but the bona fide spiritual masters are ignored, neglected, they are not given respect. So, the bulls standing on one leg indicates that the principles of religion are gradually diminishing and diminishing. Even the fragmental existence of religious principles would be embraced by so many obstacles, as if in the trembling condition of falling down at any time. People don't care for religious principles. Even if there is a deterioration or decline, they just don't bother. They say, don't bring in religion or God when we want to, when we want to point out that the real cause of all problems is this godlessness or irreligiosity. Then they say, don't bring in that. Because they have no understanding as to how God consciousness is directly linked up with the peace and prosperity of this human society. That they do not know. So, I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Verse References:** SB 1.17.3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam describing the distressed condition of the cow, representing Mother Earth, and its exploitation in Kali Yuga.

In this session, we are discussing from the third verse of the 17th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Gaamcha Dharma Dughaam Deenam Prasham Shudra Padahatam Vivatsam Ashruvadanam Ksham Maam Yavasam Ichchateem. The meaning of this verse is, although the cow is beneficial because one can draw religious principles from her, she was now rendered poor and calfless. Her legs were being beaten by a shudra. There were tears in her eyes, and she was distressed and weak. She was hankering after some grass in the field. Earlier the condition of the bull was described. Now the condition of the cow is being described. Cow is representing mother earth. And how people in Kali Yuga exploit the resources of material.

The next part is a lot more interesting. Nature of the earth and because of that there are so many anomalies in this age so the cow particularly as an animal in human society it is very very important to protect the cow because one can draw religious principles from her. How is that? Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport the next symptom of the age of Kali is the distressed condition of the cow. Milking the cow means drawing the principles of religion in a liquid form. What is the meaning of this? Prabhupada explains further the great rishis and munis would live only on milk. Milk is the only food which is complete, which provides complete nutrition for a human being. There is no other natural food item which gives complete nutrition for a human being except the cow's milk and of course the mother's milk. So the saintly persons would generally live only on milk and particularly in the Bhagavatam this is particularly described Srila Shukadeva Goswami would go to a householder while he was milking a cow and he would simply take a little quantity of that milk for his subsistence and Srila Prabhupada particularly says here even 50 years ago Prabhupada was writing this purport sometime in the 60s that means in the beginning of the 20th century Prabhupada says no one would deprive a sadhu of a quart or two of milk this quart is a volume measure quart means about a liter so no one would deprive a sadhu of 1 liter of milk and every householder would give milk like water they would freely supply that milk to the householder and then the milk would be given to the milk because every householder would have cows and bulls and they would just milk the cow and give it protection so there was plenty of milk and milk products in every house. So Srila Prabhupada says for a Sanatanist a follower of Vedic principles it is the duty of every householder to have cows and bulls as household paraphernalia not only for drinking milk but also for deriving religious principles. The milk is complete nutrition it is very very nutritious food but beyond just that from the cow you can derive religious principles.

### cow protection, Kali Yuga, sinful acts
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TtT5oSfhFCQ?t=521
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "These greatly sinful acts are responsible for all the troubles in the present society."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the mistreatment of cows and calves in Kali Yuga, highlighting the separation of calves from their mothers, the artificial milking process, and the eventual slaughter of cows, emphasizing the sinful nature of these acts and their consequences for society.

Now, further Srila Prabhupada says, the cow's calf, the young one of the cow, not only is beautiful to look at but also gives satisfaction to the cow and the cow delivers as much milk as possible when the calf is properly protected and the cow is able to have the calf with her. But in Kali Yuga, the calves are separated from the cows as early as possible for purposes which cannot be mentioned in these pages of the Srimad Bhagavatam. They don't want to allow especially the male calves to remain and to survive because a cow may give birth to a male calf or a female calf. But they want only the female calf because they can milk it. Whereas the male calf, they don't know how to utilize the bull, the male calf. When it grows, it can actually produce grains. It can do so many things, so many things. But because they do not know how to engage the bulls, they provide milk to the cow. They give them milk but they don't want to do that. They allow the cow to get the milk of the cow and give them protection and utilize them in a proper way. Therefore, they don't allow these calves to survive, to remain, especially male calves. The cow stands with tears in her eyes and the shuttle milkman draws milk from the cow artificially, The cow will give milk only after the calf has taken its share. The cow will be satisfied to give plenty of milk. But now the calf is not allowed to drink the cow's milk at all. And the calf is separated from the cow very, very early. And how do they make the cow give milk? They force the cow to give milk. They will use a pump, artificial pump to suck out the milk from the cow. The cow is unwilling to give when the calf is not there. And when there is no milk, the cow is sent to be slaughtered. The cow will give milk for some number of years. After it becomes old, it stops giving milk and immediately send it to the slaughterhouse for being slaughtered. The cow is then butchered for cow flesh. These greatly sinful acts are responsible for all the troubles in the present society.

---
### cow protection, laws of nature, peace and prosperity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TtT5oSfhFCQ?t=703
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you want to be happy as a human being, you should ensure that cows and bulls are protected and they are happy in all respects."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the importance of cow and bull protection for societal peace and prosperity, highlighting the consequences of violating the laws of nature and the need for Brahmanical culture.

Very, very sinful. To kill the cow, to kill the male calf, doesn't even have a chance of surviving, living its normal lifespan. And people do not understand that this is very, very sinful. It means the laws of nature will work regardless of whether people have belief or people are educated or uneducated. And people have to suffer in so many ways. People do not know what they are doing in the name of economic development. They are just concerned with somehow making money. But they do not realize that while it is not entirely wrong to strive for economic development or earning money, but the way it is done is if it is not in accordance with the laws of nature, the laws of God, then people have to suffer so much. The consequences of violating God's laws. The influence of Kali will keep the people in this age in the darkness of ignorance. Despite all endeavors for peace and prosperity, they must try to see the cows and the bulls happy in all respects. If you want to be happy as a human being, you should ensure that cows and bulls are protected and they are happy in all respects. Otherwise, if the cows and bulls are not happy, they are not given proper protection, human beings have to suffer and there will be no peace and prosperity in human society. Where cows and bulls are particularly not properly protected. Foolish people do not know how one can earn happiness by making the cows and bulls happy. But it is a fact by laws of nature. The laws of nature people do not know. They are doing some research to find out. Some research to find out how things are working in nature. But they do not have the authoritative knowledge from the perfect source, from God to the Vedas. Let us take it from the authority of Srimad Bhagavatam and adopt the principles for the total happiness of human society. So Bhagavatam teaches us that we should have this Brahmanical culture. Where by Brahmanas are protected, Brahmanas direct the society and the cows are protected and the cows are giving us wonderful milk and they are happy in every respect. So that there is peace and prosperity in human society. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudhirayet. In this session we are discussing from two verses, the fourth and fifth verses of the 17 Chakras, the first chapter of the first canto of Sriman Bhagavatam. The fourth verse is as follows: Paprachya Ratham Aarudhaha Kartasvara Paricchadam Megha Gambhir Raya Vacha Samaropita Karmukaha. The meaning of this verse is, the fourth verse. Maharaj Pariksit well equipped with arrows and bow and seated on a gold embossed chariot, spoke to him, the shudra, with a deep voice sounding like thunder.
