### Pariksit Maharaj, Kshatriya, Authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ttO4vPDaMUM?t=89
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pariksit is a qualified Kshatriya. He has got the courage to face such upstarts. He is well equipped to deal sufficient punishment to the miscreants and he has got his authority to enforce law and order."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Maharaj Pariksit's actions, highlighting his role as a qualified Kshatriya with the authority and courage to challenge the negative influences of Kali Yuga and enforce law and order.

Pariksit Maharaj came to that spot, he noticed that Kali Purusha was actually beating an innocent carbon bull. And he immediately, with a deep voice sounding like thunder, he questioned this low class, unqualified person and Pariksit's words will be described in the next two verses. So in the Parpochila, Prabhupada says an administrative head or king like Maharaj Pariksit with full majestic authority well equipped with weapons to chastise miscreants can challenge the agents of the age of Kali. Pariksit is a qualified Kshatriya. He has got the courage to face such upstarts. He is well equipped to deal sufficient punishment to the miscreants and he has got his authority to enforce law and order. So he is competent to challenge the agents of the age of Kali.

---
### Kali Yuga, Leadership, Degradation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ttO4vPDaMUM?t=176
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unfortunately we don't have qualified Kshatriyas like Pariksit Maharaj in our present time."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the ideal leadership of Maharaj Pariksit with the lack of qualified Kshatriyas in the present Kali Yuga, emphasizing the increase in sinful activities and the ineffectiveness of modern, unqualified leaders.

And as opposed to this in the absence of such strong executive heads there is always disruption of tranquility. Unfortunately we don't have qualified Kshatriyas like Pariksit Maharaj in our present time. If there is no qualified Kshatriya then it is not possible to counteract the influence, evil influence of this degraded Kali Yuga. What is that evil influence of Kali Yuga? People are exposed to too much sinful activities. The atmosphere is surcharged with tendency to commit sins more and more and more and more without any restriction. So this sinful age, the sinful age Kali Yuga. So the absence of strong executive heads means people become more and more sinful. They are already sinful but they become more sinful without caring for any adverse effect by their sinful activities.. they don't realise that. The elected show bottle executive head. Show bottle means.. he appears like an executive but he doesn't have qualification. He doesn't have strength, he doesn't have courage, he doesn't have knowledge, he doesn't have training, he doesn't have skills.. he simply doesn't have training, he doesn't have skill.

---
### Leadership, Degradation, Qualifications
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ttO4vPDaMUM?t=289
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The dress or style of royal order does not count. It is one's actions which are counted. It is very important. The actions are important."
**Summary:** The speaker criticizes the current system of electing leaders, arguing that unqualified individuals are chosen by a degraded public, and that true leadership is defined by actions backed by knowledge and culture, not mere appearances.

Such a person is called show-bottling skater. He is a representative of a degraded public because by votes a person becomes a ruler or a legislator or a governor by votes. And who is voting this person. He gets but he is supposed to get majority votes from people who are degraded themselves. They do not know what should be the qualification of a proper Chhatriya who can rule properly they do not know. Neither the the representative is actually qualified. and Prabhupada says such show bottle executive heads cannot be equal to the strong king like Maharaj Pariksit. You can never expect the situation now to be like what it was when Pariksit was ruling or Yudhishthira Maharaj was ruling. The dress or style of royal order does not count. It is one's actions which are counted. It is very important. The actions are important.

### Actions, Knowledge, Culture, Pariksit Maharaj
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ttO4vPDaMUM?t=379
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those activities should be backed up by proper knowledge, by good culture. Then only those actions will be good for the welfare of the entire human society."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of actions being rooted in proper knowledge and culture for the welfare of society, and then returns to the narrative of Pariksit Maharaj questioning Kali Purusha.

According to the proper understanding a person will perform different activities. So those activities should be backed up by proper knowledge, by good culture. Then only those actions will be good for the welfare of the entire human society. The meaning of this verse is, O who are you? You appear to be strong and yet you dare kill within my protection those who are helpless. By your grace, you have saved my life. By your grace, you pose yourself to be a godly man, that means a king, Naradeva. But by your deeds, you are opposing the principles of the twice-born Kshatriyas. So Pariksit Maharaj is questioning this Kali Purusha, this Kali Purusha, this personality of this age of Kali. That who are you? Because how dare you actually try to kill such helpless, innocent animals like the cow and the bull. Even though you appear like a godly person, a Kshatriya is supposed to be qualified and a god-conscious person. But you are not a godly person. You are a Kshatriya. Your dress is externally appealing like a Kshatriya, but your actions are totally opposed to those activities which are performed by a qualified Kshatriya.

---
### Twice-born, Spiritual Initiation, Kshatriya, Protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ttO4vPDaMUM?t=498
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is always second birth for those who are Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya, and Vedi culture. What is second birth? Second birth is a cultural rejuvenation, reviving the spiritual culture."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the concept of 'twice-born' (dvija) for Brahmanas, Kshatriyas, and Vaishyas, emphasizing the importance of spiritual initiation and cultural rejuvenation, and highlighting the Kshatriya's duty to protect the helpless.

So in the Parpotsha Prabhupada says, the Brahmanas, Kshatriyas and Vaishyas are called twice-born, vija. Vija means they have two kinds of birth. For the two-born Kshatriyas, they are called the two-born Kshatriyas. These classes of men, that is, Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya, there is one birth by parental conjugation. Parents combine to conceive a child. That is the first birth. And there is another birth of cultural rejuvenation by spiritual initiation from the bona fide Acharya or spiritual master. So there is always second birth for those who are Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya, and Vedi culture. What is second birth? Second birth is a cultural rejuvenation, reviving the spiritual culture. And how does it happen? It happens by spiritual initiation obtained from the bona fide Acharya, by spiritual initiation. So a Kshatriya is also twice born like a Brahmana. It is not only the Brahmanas who are having this second birth. It is also the Kshatriyas and the Vaishyas. The Kshatriya's duty is to give protection to the helpless humans. The Kshatriya king is considered to be representative of God to give protection to the helpless and chastise the miscreants. It is called Naradeva. Because, Deva because is the representative of God in human society. He is the representative of God in human society.

---
### Kshatriya, Administration, Kali Yuga, Incarnation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ttO4vPDaMUM?t=610
**Verse References:** BG 4.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whenever there are anomalies in the routine work by administrators there is an incarnation of the Lord to re-establish the principles of the Godly Kingdom."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the role of a Kshatriya king as a representative of God, contrasting it with the lack of qualified Kshatriyas in Kali Yuga, and invoking Krishna's promise to incarnate whenever there is a decline in religious principles.

He himself is a human being but he is meant to give protection to the human society by following the rules and regulations of the authoritative scriptures to administer properly to the human beings. Whereas in Kali Yuga there is no qualified Kshatriya. Whenever there are anomalies in the routine work by administrators there is an incarnation of the Lord to re-establish the principles of the Godly Kingdom. Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, I incarnate whenever there is neglect of religious principles and there is rise of irreligion. I incarnate. And when Krishna incarnates, whenever, wherever he incarnates, paritranaya sadhuna, he actually protects, saves the saintly persons, the law-abiding citizens or those following strict religious principles. And Vinashayaji Dushkrutami, he destroys these miscreants, these upstarts. In the age of Kali, the poor helpless animals, especially the cows, which are meant to receive all sorts of protection from the administrative age, are killed without restriction. Nobody is there to voice the concern for the cows. They are helpless. They are innocent. And they are being killed without any restriction. There is no one to protect them, no one to raise their voice against the cows being killed. Thus the administrative heads under whose noses such things happen are representatives of God in names only. The king is supposed to be a representative of God. But such kings or administrators who are not God-conscious, who have not undergone spiritual initiation, they are actually representatives of God in name only.

---
### Maladministration, Kali Yuga, Unhappiness, Leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ttO4vPDaMUM?t=776
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating the theme of maladministration in Kali Yuga, leading to widespread unhappiness, and emphasizing the need for qualified leaders to ensure peace and prosperity.

That means they are namesake administrators. They are not real administrators. Such powerful administrators are rulers of the poor citizens by dress or office. Simply they dress as if they are kings or administrators. But they don't have the proper knowledge, the proper authority to actually properly enforce the laws and the rules for ruling citizens. Factually they are worthless lower class men without cultural assets of the twice born. They don't have the culture to properly rule over the citizens. No one can expect justice or equality of treatment from once born spiritually uncultured lower class men. We can't expect when by votes some unqualified person comes and tries to carry out the administration. We cannot expect any real benefit. Therefore in the age of Kali everyone is unhappy due to the maladministration of the state. People in general are very unhappy in Kali. Therefore the people's government by the people who are not twice born must be a government of Kali in which everyone is unhappy. You see, people should be unhappy if they don't have a proper leader, proper trained Kshatriya to rule them. People must be unhappy. And there will be no peace and prosperity in such a state. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.5.16 | 20.09.2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### spiritual knowledge, material attachment, transcendental realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NKuv6zPZ1bU?t=79
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, those who are not so well situated due to material attachment should be shown the ways of transcendental realization by your goodness through descriptions of the transcendental activities of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam 1.5.16, explaining that only those detached from material happiness can understand spiritual knowledge, and those attached should be shown the path through descriptions of the Lord's activities.

In this session, we will be discussing the 16th verse of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This chapter concerns instructions by Narad Muni to Vyasa Deva about Srimad Bhagavatam. The verse is: Vichakshanoya Asyarhati Veditum Vibhor Anantaparasya Nivritita Sukham Pravartamanasya Gunair Anatmanah Tato Bhavan Darsaya Chestitam Vibho. The meaning of this verse is: The Supreme Lord is unlimited. Only a very expert personality retired from the activities of material happiness deserves to understand this knowledge of spiritual values. Therefore, those who are not so well situated due to material attachment should be shown the ways of transcendental realization by your goodness through descriptions of the transcendental activities of the Supreme Lord.

---
### detachment, spiritual knowledge, Supreme Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NKuv6zPZ1bU?t=157
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Knowledge about the Supreme Lord Krishna is completely spiritual; it has nothing to do with any material knowledge or material happiness or material things."
**Summary:** This segment explains that transcendentalists are compassionate to fallen souls, distinguishing between materially attached and detached individuals, and emphasizing the unlimited nature of the Supreme Lord, Krishna, and the purely spiritual nature of knowledge about Him.

What's the meaning of this verse? The transcendentalists are very compassionate upon the fallen souls of this world. In this world, there are two kinds of people: category people who are materially attached, another category people who are materially detached. Now, the Supreme Lord is unlimited; He is not a limited person like any of us. So that is where the difficulty lies in understanding properly who is God as a person. So Narada Muni is telling, even though God or Krishna appears like one of us, He is not at all limited like us. He is an unlimited personality. Therefore, Narada Muni says only a very expert person who is limited is the one who is not limited, and who is not limited is the one who is limited, retired from the activities of material happiness, deserves to understand this knowledge of spiritual values. Knowledge about the Supreme Lord Krishna is completely spiritual; it has nothing to do with any material knowledge or material happiness or material things. It has nothing to do with material. So there are people who are materially attached.

---
### understanding God, transcendental activities, compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NKuv6zPZ1bU?t=269
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who are having material attachment, they should be shown the way of transcendentalism, transcendental realization by Vyasa Deva, and what is the method? He is telling through descriptions of the transcendental activities of the Supreme Lord exclusively."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the difficulty materially attached people have in understanding God, emphasizing that understanding God solves all problems, and that transcendentalists like Narada Muni and Vyasa Deva aim to help people understand through descriptions of Krishna's transcendental activities.

And they can't easily understand who is Krishna, what is God, because as soon as one understands scientifically, factually, what is God, all his problems are solved. So therefore, how to understand God? So the transcendentalists like Narada Muni, Vyasa Deva, they are all thinking how to actually make people understand. So Narada Muni is telling here, those who are having material attachment, they should be shown the way of transcendentalism, transcendental realization by Vyasa Deva, and what is the method? He is telling through descriptions of the transcendental activities of the Supreme Lord exclusively. Describe Krishna's transcendental activities. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport...

### theological science, transcendental nature of God, material attachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NKuv6zPZ1bU?t=347
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Materially attached people cannot understand the nature of God; they have to become detached somehow or the other."
**Summary:** This segment explains that theological science, dealing with the transcendental nature of God, is difficult to understand for those attached to material activities, as most people are, and that detachment is necessary to understand God.

Theological science is a difficult subject matter, especially when it deals with the transcendental nature of God. Theological science means the science dealing with the subject matter of God. So the transcendental nature of God, God is the supreme person, is the original person, but He is a transcendental personality. So the transcendental nature of God is the original person, but He is a transcendental personality. What does he mean? Transcendental personality means He is not having a material body like we have. He is not under the clutches of the material nature. He is the master, He is the Lord, He is the controller, He is above, and is eternally so. Therefore, this subject matter of God as this transcendental person is not to be understood by persons who are too much attached to material activities, which is the nature of most common people. Majority of the people in this world, they are attached to material activities, that's their life. So materially attached people cannot understand the nature of God; they have to become detached somehow or the other.

---
### detachment, spiritual knowledge, Krishna science
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NKuv6zPZ1bU?t=441
**Verse References:** BG 7.3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Expert people who are retired from materialistic activities by culture of spiritual knowledge, those who cultivate spiritual knowledge, they understand the worthlessness of all these materialistic activities."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the difficulty and slowness of traditional detachment methods, questioning if there's a quicker way, and highlighting that cultivating spiritual knowledge helps one understand the worthlessness of materialistic activities, leading to detachment and the ability to study Krishna science, referencing Bhagavad Gita's statement about the rarity of those seeking perfection.

Now, if they have to become detached to understand who is God, then what is the way? Any of the traditional ways are very slow, very difficult, very tedious. So is there a quick way of becoming materially detached? What is the way? So here it is said that first of all, understand the point. Prabhupada is telling, understand this point, that expert people who are retired from materialistic activities by culture of spiritual knowledge, those who cultivate spiritual knowledge, they understand the worthlessness of all these materialistic activities. They are useless. They really don't give us any happiness. They have no eternal benefit. They are no benefit for the soul. They are simply some illusory activities which give some illusory happiness, feeling of happiness, no real happiness. So give those activities, retire from those activities, then they may be admitted to study this great science of the personality of God, Krishna science. Now, Bhagavad Gita clearly states: Manushyanam sahasreshu kashchit yatati siddhaye yatatam api siddhanam kashchinmam vetti tattvatah.

---
### liberation, theological science, transcendental activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NKuv6zPZ1bU?t=551
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Describe the science of God by directly relating His transcendental activities; they are very attractive, they are wonderful, they work within the depths of the heart of conditioned souls."
**Summary:** This segment explains the meaning of the Bhagavad Gita verse, highlighting the rarity of those seeking liberation and understanding God, and emphasizing Narada's advice to Vyasadeva to describe God's attractive transcendental activities to benefit people.

What is the meaning of this verse? Although many hundreds and thousands of people, hardly there may be one or two people who are not in the spiritual world, one rare person who actually is interested in the perfection of life, human life. What is siddhi? What is the meaning of liberation from material bondage? Most people don't even know we are bound up in this material world; they don't know. So one who knows and also takes interest in how to get out of this material bondage, to put an end to all our material problems, such a person is a rare person. And among many thousands of transcendently realized persons, very, very, very, very few people can understand the theological science dealing with God as person. So this being the case, Vyasadeva is advised by Narada, describe the science of God by directly relating His transcendental activities; they are very attractive, they are wonderful, they work within the depths of the heart of conditioned souls. Even those who have no understanding, no interest, they are materially attached, even such people, some or the other, if they hear this subject matter, describing exclusively the transcendental activities of Krishna, then that sound vibration enters their ears, penetrates the heart, and cleanses the heart of desire for material enjoyment.

---
### purification, Krishna consciousness, transcendental activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NKuv6zPZ1bU?t=670
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.17
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Simply give oral reception, give your ears somehow to descriptions of the transcendental activities of Krishna, then it is going to bring about a change of heart; it is going to cleanse the heart."
**Summary:** This segment explains that simply hearing descriptions of Krishna's transcendental activities can purify the heart and lead to a change of heart, emphasizing Vyasadeva's expertise and detachment, and his son Shukadeva Goswami's receptiveness, making Srimad Bhagavatam the topmost theological science that can act as medicine for common people, awakening their interest in Krishna consciousness.

This is already described in the second chapter, apadrani vidunoti. Simply give oral reception, give your ears somehow to descriptions of the transcendental activities of Krishna, then it is going to bring about a change of heart; it is going to cleanse the heart. Automatically you become purified of all material contamination. And it is also told here Vyasadeva is the right person because he is expert in this science, and he is also detached completely from material enjoyment. So he is the right person to describe this subject matter of the transcendental activities of Krishna. And Shukadeva Goswami, his son, is the right person to receive this knowledge. Therefore, Srimad Bhagavatam is the topmost theological science, and it can act on the common people as a medicine in the beginning. Medicine means it may not be very palatable in the beginning, but the moment that medicine works, then people awaken their real interest in Krishna consciousness, in hearing about Krishna, Krishna's transcendental activities.

---
### literary incarnation, association with Krishna, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NKuv6zPZ1bU?t=777
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This Srimad Bhagavatam is the literary incarnation of Krishna... if the common people they simply hear the Bhagavatam, they are able to associate directly with Krishna and they become gradually purified from all kinds of material contamination."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that the transcendental activities of the Lord and the Lord Himself are non-different, as both are absolute, and that Srimad Bhagavatam is the literary incarnation of Krishna in Kali Yuga, allowing common people to associate directly with Krishna and become purified by hearing it.

And the another important point Prabhupada says is this transcendental activities of the Lord and the Lord Himself, there is no difference because both are absolute. So this literature, this Srimad Bhagavatam, is the literary incarnation of Krishna. Krishna is incarnated in the form of Bhagavatam in this Kali Yuga. Krishnae Swadhaamopagate Dharma Gyanadi Visaha, in the third chapter it was described that when Krishna went back after performing his pastimes, then this Bhagavatam has arisen as the brilliant sun which can dispel the dense darkness of Kali. So Bhagavatam is a very special literature among all the Vedic literatures. Bhagavatam is very special. So if the common people they simply hear the Bhagavatam, they are able to associate directly with Krishna and they become gradually purified from all kinds of material contamination.

### transcendental descriptions, benefit, perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NKuv6zPZ1bU?t=852
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Kindly describe these activities, and that way people can be actually benefited; they can get out of this material existence and suffering and be promoted to the highest perfection very easily."
**Summary:** This segment encourages expert devotees to find intelligent ways to inject transcendental descriptions of Krishna's activities into the ears of common people, highlighting Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's expert dexterity in this, and emphasizing that describing these activities benefits people, helping them escape material existence and achieve perfection easily.

And those who are expert devotees, they should find ways and means to inject this transcendental descriptions of Krishna's activities into the ears of the common people. And there are so many intelligent ways in which this can be broadcast, not just one way, not just the standard way that there is a discourse and people sit in the discourse and try to hear when a learned person is speaking on the subject matter, not just that. There are so many ways, so many ways. And Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and His followers exhibited expert dexterity in doing this. And Narada Muni is telling that to Vyasadeva, kindly describe these activities, and that way people can be actually benefited; they can get out of this material existence and suffering and be promoted to the highest perfection very easily. Hare Krishna. Thank you very much.

### devotional service, surrender, material duties
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XzSeADH0qdY?t=69
**Verse References:** SB 1.5.17
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is a very, very strong statement by Narada Muni and we have to carefully understand this in order to really become serious about devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 17th verse of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, emphasizing the importance of understanding Narada Muni's statement about devotional service and its implications.

In this session we will be discussing from the 17th verse of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Shreemad Bhagavatam. Tyaktva Swadharmam Charanam Bujam Hare, Bhajanna Pakvata Pate Tato Yadi, Yatra Kvava Bhadram Abhudha Mushyakim, Kovartha Aapto Abhajatam Swadharmataha. Translation of this verse by Srila Prabhupada: One who has forsaken his material occupations to engage in the devotional service of the Lord may sometimes fall down while in an immature stage. Yet there is no danger of his being unsuccessful. On the other hand, a non-devotee, though fully engaged in occupational duties, does not gain anything. This is a very, very strong statement by Narada Muni and we have to carefully understand this in order to really become serious about devotional service. Srila Prabhupada said, Narada Muni said, Narada Muni said, Narada Muni said.

---
### material duties, spiritual duties, karma, obligations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XzSeADH0qdY?t=145
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, materially we are obligated and we have to discharge duties in order to liquidate our debt."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the two types of duties: material and spiritual. Material duties are related to the body and mind and create obligations, while spiritual duties aim for liberation. Failure to fulfill material duties leads to accruing karma and repeated births and deaths.

Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport. Before we go to the purport, just a point about this verse itself, that everybody as a human being has got two kinds of duties, material duties and spiritual duties. Material duties are connected with the body and the mind, the material body and the material mind, and they are based on the obligations that we have because of taking birth in a certain family, because of the facilities that we have to take birth there, are provided by the Devatas for our material existence in this body that we take. Because of the knowledge that we receive, Deva, Rishi, Bhuta, Aapta, Niranam, Pitranam, as soon as one takes birth as a human being there are six different personalities to whom we are obligated. And if we do not perform our duties in relationship with our mind and body because we are taking so many benefits for our existence, so we become debtors. So we become debtors. We accrue some karma. And that karma forces us to again and again take birth. Repetition of birth and death. So, materially we are obligated and we have to discharge duties in order to liquidate our debt.

---
### devotional service, surrender, liberation, spiritual duties
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XzSeADH0qdY?t=283
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, spiritual duties are meant for the liberation of the soul from material bondage."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts material obligations with the freedom offered by devotional service. Surrendering to Krishna frees one from all material obligations, and spiritual duties are essential for liberation from suffering and material bondage.

But, the Srimad Bhagavatam declares that if anybody takes to devotional service, pure devotional service by completely surrendering to Krishna, such a person is completely freed from all material obligations simply because the person has surrendered to the lotus feet of Krishna and is engaged in pure devotional service. So, spiritual duties are meant for the liberation of the soul from material bondage. Nobody wants miseries. Nobody wants to suffer in this material world. But then they are forced to suffer. Miseries are forced upon a person. Now, what is the way out of these miseries? What is the way out of these fundamental problems of material existence? That is, doing our spiritual duties. Spiritual duties, ultimately the understanding is, as spirit soul, each one of us, irrespective of our karma, irrespective of the type of family we are born, irrespective of the type of past activities we might have done, good or bad, whatever, we have actually, we are receiving all maintenance from the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is our eternal well-wisher, with whom we have an eternal relationship. Under all circumstances, in all times, in all situations. So, spiritual duties are definitely very, very important.

---
### devotional service, material duties, spiritual progress, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XzSeADH0qdY?t=414
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is no loss for such a person who in an immature stage, after having taken to pure devotional service, gives up pure devotional service and goes back to material way of life. There is no loss at all."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the common practice of balancing material and spiritual duties, but highlights Narada Muni's revolutionary idea that surrendering to Krishna eliminates material obligations. Even if someone falls from devotional service due to immaturity, there is no loss, as the spiritual progress made is eternally attached to the soul.

So, generally, people who are aware of these material duties and spiritual duties, they try to discharge both of them. Any civilized person will have some sense of responsibility for discharging material duties, and also will be doing some kind of worship of the Supreme, some kind of spiritual duties one will be discharging. But here, Narad Muni is telling something very, very revolutionary, that if somebody is giving up the material duties, in order to engage in pure devotional service, as instructed in the Bhagavatam, that when you engage in pure devotional service by surrendering to Krishna, then you have no obligation, no material obligations at all. Then such a person, if such a person performs pure devotional service, and becomes completely successful in getting ultimate liberation, then very good. The person is liberated, freed from all material contamination, purified, no more material obligations, and has attained the success in pure devotional service of getting ultimate liberation, going back home, back to Godhead. But what if they fall down after taking to pure devotional service? That happens when somebody is still in an immature stage. bhajan apapvo atha patetato yadi. Then they fall down means they give up devotional, pure devotional service, and they take to material life once again, which they had given up in order to do pure devotional service. Then what about this person's situation? Neither has he gained anything materially by engaging in pure devotional service, nor has that person been successful in doing his pure devotional service. So he is a loser both ways. So Narada Muni is telling, there is no loss for such a person who in an immature stage, after having taken to pure devotional service, gives up pure devotional service and goes back to material way of life. There is no loss at all. There is no loss. Why?

---
### spiritual progress, karma, materialism, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XzSeADH0qdY?t=597
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whereas, Narada Muni is telling, even if a person is a non-devotee and fully engages in occupational duties, whatever occupational duties the person does, all such occupational duties do not have any spiritual benefit for a non-devotee engaged in occupational duties."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the credit for devotional service is eternally attached to the soul, ensuring future progress. In contrast, occupational duties performed by non-devotees offer no spiritual benefit and only temporary material gains.

Two reasons. One is that whatever pure devotional service somebody performs, the credit, for having done that pure devotional service, is eternally attached to the soul. It is not connected to the body. The spiritual advancement that a person makes by rendering pure devotional service is attached to the soul. It is permanent. It is forever that credit is available. So such a person resumes pure devotional service any time in the future, will resume from the point where he or she has stopped pure devotional service. Supposing they make 2% advancement and then they give up pure devotional service. So next time they take up pure devotional service, whenever that is in the future, then they'll begin from 3%. It is never lost. Whereas, Narada Muni is telling, even if a person is a non-devotee and fully engages in occupational duties, whatever occupational duties the person does, all such occupational duties do not have any spiritual benefit for a non-devotee engaged in occupational duties. According to the Shastras we are talking, there is no spiritual benefit at all. And what about the material benefit one gets by doing some occupational duty? That is all temporary. It is all temporary. Just like with this body, whatever we may gain while working with this body, all that is, just as long as this body is there, my wealth, my possessions, my relationships based on the body, my material knowledge, material power, material position, everything is finished the moment this body is, life on this body is over. When I quit the body, I cannot take my money with me, I cannot take my degrees with me, I cannot take my power with me, my position with me, my influence with me, nothing I can take. So what is the gain? Oh, in this life, because he worked very hard, he could enjoy some nice power, nice position. Oh, that enjoyment is illusory happiness. Illusory happiness. It's not real happiness. It is not real satisfaction. It has nothing to do with soul happiness, soul satisfaction.

---
### devotional service, examples, karma, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XzSeADH0qdY?t=781
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is no loss if one falls down. But, if one does occupational duties without doing pure devotional service, there is no gain at all."
**Summary:** The speaker illustrates the principle of retained spiritual progress through the stories of Bharat Maharaj and Chitraketu, who fell from their devotional practices but were later delivered due to their past service. He reiterates that devotional service is the only path to true gain.

Deep within, there is no satisfaction. Even if one is getting some material pleasure or material enjoyment or material happiness, deep within there is nothing that is worthwhile that one can experience. So, here it is said that there are instances in history of somebody who performed pure devotional service and fell down. There is an instance of one Bharat Maharaj. He performed, he gave up his kingdom and went to a holy place and he was practicing pure devotional service. And then, towards the end of his life, he became attached to a deer and he died thinking of the deer. So, he took birth, next birth, as a deer. But, because he had done pure devotional service, the Lord was kind enough to give him memory of what he did in his previous life. In the body of a deer, he was able to remember. And in that body of a deer, he was associating with saintly persons and hearing their discourses. He was not interested in eating or sleeping or nothing, even in the body of a deer. Similarly, there is an instance of Chitraketu who actually committed some offense at the feet of Shiva and he fell down. But, next life, he was delivered. He realized his mistake and he was delivered. Next life, even though he took birth as a demon, in the body of a demon, not as a demon, in the body of a demon, he was delivered, completely delivered. He got ultimate liberation. He was given memory by the Supreme Lord. So, there is no loss if one falls down. But, if one does occupational duties without doing pure devotional service, there is no gain at all. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

### intelligence, sense enjoyment, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DhIhxyKFPeg?t=78
**Verse References:** SB 1.5.18
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Persons who are actually intelligent and philosophically inclined should endeavor only for that purposeful end which is not obtainable even by wandering from the topmost planet, Brahmaloka, down to the lowest planet, Patala."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 18th verse of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, emphasizing that intelligent individuals should strive for what is unattainable through material means, even throughout the universe.

In this session we will be discussing from the 18th verse of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The translation of this verse as given by Srila Prabhupada: Persons who are actually intelligent and philosophically inclined should endeavor only for that purposeful end which is not obtainable even by wandering from the topmost planet, Brahmaloka, down to the lowest planet, Patala. As far as happiness derived from sense enjoyment is concerned, it can be obtained automatically in course of time.

---
### devotional service, intelligence, material world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DhIhxyKFPeg?t=133
**Verse References:** SB 1.5.18
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is pure devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the previous verse, contrasting the futility of seeking material happiness with the value of pure devotional service, which is unattainable through material means.

Just as in course of time we obtain miseries even though we do not desire them. So, the previous verse was, we were discussing that there is no loss in pure devotional service even if one does not complete it in one lifetime, to achieve the 100% success of perfection. But, another point that is being made now is that those who are intelligent human beings, intelligent human beings, who are philosophically inclined, they question, 'Who am I? What is this world?' Such fundamental questions they are able to raise. Such people, Narada Muni is telling, should endeavor only for one purposeful end. What is that? That which is not obtainable in any place, in any place within the whole universe, from the topmost planet, Brahmaloka, up to the bottom most, Patala. By wandering anywhere, you cannot get that which you cannot get. What is that which is not available from the topmost planet, up to the bottom most, Patala? Brahmaloka to Patala. It is pure devotional service.

---
### karma, material happiness, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DhIhxyKFPeg?t=240
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It can only give them some material happiness."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how material happiness and facilities are obtained through past karma and vary across different planets and circumstances, but ultimately lead to mixed happiness and miseries.

By dint of one's past karma, whatever happiness one can get, whatever material facilities one can get in different planets, in different circumstances, in different types of good work, now that is available differently in different places. Even people who do different kinds of activities, they enjoy different kinds of facilities. So just like even in this life we can see in our own situation, when we see other people in our own neighborhood, there are some people who are born with not so much of facilities or benefits. Somebody is born in a very poor family, or somebody is born in a very rich family. Somebody is born with good material intelligence. Somebody is born with not so good material intelligence, not so much of material intelligence. So we see variations. But in any case, whatever benefits they may have by birth, or they may acquire knowledge in this life by working very hard, or they may acquire wealth, or they may acquire a nice position, power, influence, whatever they may get, or whatever they already have, it can only give them some material happiness. It is miseries and mixed material happiness.

---
### suffering, material world, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DhIhxyKFPeg?t=364
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Don't endeavor for material happiness, or material facilities, or material enjoyment."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the inevitability of miseries in the material world, even in higher planets like Brahmaloka, and advises against striving for material happiness, which is available even to animals.

These two things are available for everyone throughout the universe. Miseries you cannot avoid. Even in Brahmaloka, the four fundamental problems, janma-mṛtyu-jarāvyādhi-dukkha-doṣānudarśanam, birth, death, disease, old age, associated with birth in the material world, anywhere, any planet, with however much good karma, one cannot avoid these four fundamental problems. And, the three-fold miseries are always there everywhere. Material existence means the three gunas are acting, and the three-fold miseries are always there. Adi-bhautika, adhyatmika, adidaivika klesha. Misery is due to one's own body and mind, misery is due to other living beings, and misery is due to supernatural forces of nature. So, the Bhagavata is telling, this verse is telling, don't endeavor for material happiness, or material facilities, or material enjoyment.

---
### devotional service, eternal life, material enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DhIhxyKFPeg?t=451
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is not available by any amount of material activities, by any good karma, by birth in any planet within this whole universe, it's not available."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the temporary and limited nature of material enjoyment, available even to animals and demigods, with the eternal benefits of pure devotional service.

Which is available even for the dogs and hogs. Of course, according to their standard, according to the body and mentality they have, they have their own enjoyment. And, many gods are enjoying on a different scale, or different level. In their own heavenly planets, they have their own long span of life, they have beautiful body, they have material power, they have influence, and they have facilities. But, two features of existence in this material world is miseries and mixed happiness. It's available everywhere, for every living being. But, this pure devotional service, by which one can get eternal life, one can get eternal bliss, one can get eternal spiritual knowledge, that is not available by any amount of material activities, by any good karma, by birth in any planet within this whole universe, it's not available.

---
### destiny, material happiness, endeavor
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DhIhxyKFPeg?t=538
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You cannot increase material happiness, you cannot reduce material distress."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that material happiness and distress are destined based on one's body and cannot be significantly altered through material endeavors.

And, one more point, Narad Muni is telling here, as far as happiness derived from sense enjoyment is concerned, it can be obtained automatically in course of time, even without your separate endeavor. Just as miseries come even without your endeavoring for them. So, there is something called destiny. Destiny. Deha Yogena Dehina. Another place in the Bhagavatam it is said, Sukham Aindriyakam Daitya Deha Yogena Dehina. Deha Yoga. The moment we get a certain type of material body, along with that body, there is a certain amount of material happiness and material distress, which is destined in this body, in this life. It is destined. You cannot reduce, by material endeavor, you cannot reduce the material miseries or material distress that you are destined to suffer. And, by any material endeavor, you cannot increase the material happiness, the material happiness that is due to you. That is destined. You cannot increase material happiness, you cannot reduce material distress. Then, why are you endeavoring, this is foolishness, to think that I can increase my material happiness by gaining more wealth, by having more possessions, by having nice family relationships, by getting some material power, by transferring myself to heavenly planets in the next life, either in this life or next life, you cannot increase more than what is destined. You cannot.

---
### devotional service, anxiety, eternal life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DhIhxyKFPeg?t=658
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nobody is free from anxiety in the material existence."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the temporary nature of material gains with the eternal happiness, life, and knowledge offered by pure devotional service, emphasizing freedom from anxiety in the spiritual realm.

So, and even if somebody thinks that in this life I will work hard and gain good karma, by which in my next life, in my next life I can enjoy, that is also temporary. It is temporary. It will be finished with passage of time. Whereas, pure devotional service gives us eternal happiness, eternal life, eternal knowledge. So, please think over what is the value of pure devotional service. Freedom from anxiety, complete freedom from anxiety. In fact, the word, the term that is used for the spiritual planets is called Vaikuntha Loka. Vaikuntha means Vigatakuntha, without any anxiety. Life, in the eternal life, in the spiritual planets of the Supreme Lord, personal abode of the Supreme Lord, is life without any anxiety, zero anxiety. Whereas, material world is characterized by anxiety. Even Lord Brahma, when Hiranyakashipu was terrorizing the whole universe, Brahma was in so much anxiety what to do for cutting down the terrorist activities of Hiranyakashipu, and giving some protection and relief for the people who are thus suffering under his torture. So, Brahma also is in anxiety. Brahma also is in anxiety. So, nobody is free from anxiety in the material existence. Nobody, none of the Devatas, none of the Yogis, Karmis, Jnanis, Tapasvis, nobody, only pure devotees are completely free from anxiety.

---
### devotional service, Krishna consciousness, eternal bliss
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DhIhxyKFPeg?t=789
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna wants every one of His parts and parcels in this material world to engage in pure devotional service and return to Him in His personal abode for leading an eternal life full of bliss and knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by urging listeners to endeavor for advancement in Krishna Consciousness and pure devotional service, highlighting Krishna's desire for all beings to return to His abode for eternal bliss and knowledge.

And those engaged in pure devotional service, they understand by executing pure devotional service, we can become free from anxiety. So, therefore, one should endeavor for advancement in Krishna Consciousness, in pure devotional service. Only for that one should endeavor. And such endeavor is worthwhile because simply because of some past karma, you are not going to get any spiritual happiness. No. Spiritual happiness is entirely dependent on our executing pure devotional service. And the benefit of pure devotional service is eternal benefit. Eternal benefit. The advancement made is never lost, even after we quit the body. It is eternally to our credit, whatever advancement we make in pure devotional service, it's never lost. So, therefore, one should endeavor only for advancement in Krishna Consciousness, in pure devotional service. And one more point of pure devotional service is, you always have Krishna's help. You always have Krishna's encouragement. Krishna wants every one of His parts and parcels in this material world to engage in pure devotional service and return to Him in His personal abode for leading an eternal life full of bliss and knowledge, in association with the Supreme Lord whose anandamaya abhyasat, whose existence itself is fully blissful, total bliss. He is the source of all transcendental bliss. So, I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.5.21 | 26-09-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### instruction, pastimes of Krishna, compilation of Bhagavatam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sEYKHopPCdA?t=42
**Verse References:** SB 1.5.21
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Please therefore describe the transcendental pastimes of the supreme personality of Godhead Shri Krishna more vividly."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on Narada Muni instructing Vyasadeva to vividly describe the transcendental pastimes of Krishna in the Srimad Bhagavatam.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devim Saraswathim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing the 21st verse of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Tvam Atmana Atmanam Avehi Amoghadrik Parasya Pumsah Paramatmanah Kalam Ajam Prajatam Jagatashivayatat Mahanubhavabhyudayo Adhiganyatam. The meaning of this verse is, your goodness has perfect vision. You yourself can know the super soul personality of Godhead because you are present as the plenary portion of the Lord. Although you are birthless, you have appeared on this earth for the well-being of all people. Please therefore describe the transcendental pastimes of the supreme personality of Godhead Shri Krishna more vividly.

---
### Vyasadeva, Bhagavatam, Krishna's pastimes
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sEYKHopPCdA?t=133
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the earlier work of Vyasa Deva, the different Puranas and the Mahabharata, he has described the pastimes of the Supreme Lord, but not as detailed as it will be described by him in the Bhagavatam under the instruction of Narada."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Narada Muni is instructing Vyasadeva on compiling the Bhagavatam, which should vividly describe Krishna's pastimes in detail, unlike Vyasadeva's previous works.

This is yet another verse spoken by Shri Narada to Vyasadeva. He is instructing Vyasadeva on compiling Bhagavatam. And Bhagavatam is specifically describing, supposed to describe very detailed way, the various transcendental pastimes of the supreme personality of Godhead Krishna. Krishna means Krishna and his various transcendental forms of expansion, various incarnations. So all of them are included. So here Vyasadeva is essentially being told that Vyasa is actually the incarnation of the Supreme Lord for the sake of compiling the Vedic literature. So he has perfect vision. He is himself the plenary portion. Plenary portion means the portion of the Supreme Lord who has got the power of the Supreme Lord. So he himself knows the Supersoul personality of Godhead by his perfect vision. And even though he is birthless, Ajam Prajatam, Ajam means he is birthless, Prajatam means he has taken birth for the well-being of all people. Shivaya for the well-being of all people. Therefore, Narada Muni is telling, describe the transcendental pastimes of the Supreme Personality of Godhead Sri Krishna more vividly. In the earlier work of Vyasa Deva, the different Puranas and the Mahabharata, he has described the pastimes of the Supreme Lord, but not as detailed as it will be described by him in the Bhagavatam under the instruction of Narada.

---
### Srimad Bhagavatam, Krishna's pastimes, Transcendental nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sEYKHopPCdA?t=269
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All the pastimes are very nicely, vividly, detailed way described in the Bhagavatam 10th canto."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the 10th canto of Srimad Bhagavatam as fully dedicated to describing Krishna's pastimes in great detail, and explains how the other cantos establish Krishna's identity and his relationship with the world and its inhabitants.

Just like the 10th canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam is fully dedicated for describing the pastimes of Krishna very, very, very detailed way. It is the biggest canto in the entire Srimad Bhagavatam. And it is also describing the pastimes of Krishna's birth, then the childhood pastimes in Vrindavan. Then the pastimes of Krishna as youth in the Mathura Dham, then his pastimes as the king of Dwaraka in Dwaraka. All the pastimes are very nicely, vividly, detailed way described in the Bhagavatam 10th canto. And the other cantos are establishing the pastimes of Krishna. Who is this personality of Godhead Krishna? What is his transcendental nature? What is his relationship with this material world, with the spiritual world, with the living beings, with the liberated souls, with the jnanis, tapasvis, karmis, yogis, devotees, everybody? All that is very scientifically described in connection with his pastimes. This is the special thing. Everything is connected with Krishna's pastimes.

---
### Purpose of Incarnations, Fallen Souls, Vedic Literature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sEYKHopPCdA?t=360
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Eternally we are meant simply for rendering pure devotional service, loving devotional service to Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Vyasadeva descended to deliver fallen souls, who have forgotten their relationship with Krishna, and that Vedic literatures are compiled to help them take advantage of human life and be freed from material bondage.

So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, Vyasa Deva has descended by his costless mercy to deliver the fallen souls in this material world. That's the purpose of all incarnations. Every incarnation is descending, coming from the spiritual world, from the spiritual world to the material world, for the sake of delivering the fallen souls, those who are lost in this material world. Now the people in this world are mostly forgetful of who they are, who is Krishna, what is their relationship. So they are detached from the transcendental loving service of Krishna, which is the natural condition, healthy condition, the spiritual state of our existence. Eternally we are meant simply for rendering pure devotional service, loving devotional service to Krishna. The Vedic literatures are compiled for the benefit of such fallen souls. And the duty of the fallen souls is to take care of them, especially in the human form of life. Take advantage of such literature to be freed from the bondage of material existence.

---
### Spiritual Master, Vyasadeva, Shelter of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sEYKHopPCdA?t=459
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He is teaching by his own example that one must accept a spiritual master even though he is God himself."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that although Vyasadeva is an incarnation of the Lord, he accepts Narada Muni as his spiritual master to teach by example the importance of accepting a spiritual master, even for incarnations of God.

Now Narada Muni is formally accepted as the spiritual master of Vyasa Deva. But Vyasa Deva being the incarnation of the Lord is not dependent at all on anyone. He is completely enlightened always. And he is the spiritual master of everyone else. But as doing the work of an Acharya, he is teaching by his own example that one must accept a spiritual master even though he is God himself. For example Lord Krishna, Lord Rama, Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, all are incarnations of the Supreme Lord. But they accepted formally a spiritual master. They were already enlightened in all transcendental knowledge. They are the source of all knowledge. But still to direct people they actually accept a spiritual master, teach by their example. Similarly Vyasa Deva is directed by Narada Rishi as his spiritual master to describe the transcendental pastimes of Krishna in order to direct people to take shelter of the lotus feet of Krishna. That is the only shelter we actually have.

---
### Krishna's Lotus Feet, Material Existence, Vamanadeva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sEYKHopPCdA?t=557
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's lotus feet are the most suitable boat for crossing over this ocean of material existence."
**Summary:** This segment describes Krishna's lotus feet as the most suitable boat for crossing the ocean of material existence, referencing the story of Vamanadeva covering the planetary systems in two steps.

We don't have any other shelter. In the Bhagavatam it is described in a later portion, Samashrita ye pada pallava plavam. Krishna's lotus feet are the most suitable boat for crossing over this ocean of material existence. Plavam means boat. Pada pallava means it is like lotus feet. Krishna's lotus feet is like a boat. The most suitable boat for crossing over the ocean of material existence. Mahapadampunya Yesho Murari. And Krishna is famous as Murari, the killer of the Mura demon, whose pain as the deliverer of all the fallen souls, all the fallen souls in this material world. Whenever he incarnates he delivers them. His fame as the deliverer is spotless. Punya Yesho. It's pure fame, spotless fame. Mahapadam. Now he became famous as keeping that giant step, when he appeared as Vaman Dev, Trivikrama. In one step he covered the earth and all the lower planets. And he stretched his other foot to pierce the covering of the top portion of the universe beyond Brahma Loka. So he covered all the upper planets with the second step. And there was no place left for covering the third step, for keeping the third step and covering some land. There was no land left. All the middle, lower, upper, all three planetary systems were covered in two steps.

---
### Krishna's Lotus Feet, Material Existence, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sEYKHopPCdA?t=694
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The whole material existence becomes shrunk to the quantity of water contained in the footprint of a calf."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that taking shelter of Krishna's lotus feet shrinks the vast material existence to the size of a calf's footprint, making it easy to cross over and reach the supreme abode.

So Mahapadampunya Yesho Murari. Bhavambudhir Vatsapadam. What happens when you take shelter of Krishna's lotus feet? How do you cross over this material existence? So in this verse it is described. The whole material existence becomes shrunk to the quantity of water contained in the footprint of a calf. Vatsapadam. Vatsa means calf. So a huge ocean shrinks into water contained in the footprint of a calf. Bhavambudhir. Bhava means this material existence is compared to a huge ocean that compresses or condenses or shrinks into a very very small quantity of water, so that you can easily cross over, just as easily as one can put his foot across a small puddle of water. Parampadam. And you cross over material existence to reach the supreme abode of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. That is the result of taking shelter of Krishna's lotus feet. This is the glorification of the incarnation of Krishna and all the other incarnations. Taking shelter of the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord, Personality of Godhead is the easiest and the most effective way of crossing over this ocean of material existence. Otherwise the other methods are very very very difficult, very slow, and very very tedious. This is very very easy, the method of taking shelter of the lotus feet of Krishna.

---
### Surrender, Krishna, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sEYKHopPCdA?t=825
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, surrender to me alone, giving up all other processes of religion, simply surrender."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the importance of taking shelter of Krishna's lotus feet, referencing Krishna's instruction in the Bhagavad Gita to surrender to Him alone and be freed from sinful reactions.

So everywhere it is described in the Bhagavatam. This very literature is directing us, take shelter of the lotus feet of Krishna. Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, surrender to me alone, giving up all other processes of religion, simply surrender. And don't worry about the consequences of such surrender. Don't worry about it. Mahashu Chaha, because Krishna says, I will take charge of all your sinful reactions. Even if there are any sinful reactions, don't worry. I will nullify them. Aham Tvam Sarva Pape Bhya Moksha Isha. So Krishna specially gives the assurance. So this transcendental literature, Vedic literature, are directing the fallen souls how they can come to the point of taking shelter of the lotus feet of Krishna. That's the direction that is ultimately given. And that's the perfection. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna. Thank you very much.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.5.22 | 27-09-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### purpose of knowledge, transcendental description of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wuIr7oYVfxA?t=46
**Verse References:** SB 1.5.22
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Learned circles have positively concluded that the infallible purpose of the advancement of knowledge culminates in the transcendental descriptions of the Lord who is defined in choice poetry."
**Summary:** This segment introduces verse 22 of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that the ultimate purpose of all advancement of knowledge is to describe the Lord.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udhirayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 22nd verse of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam. Idam Hi Pumsas Tapasas Shrutasya Va Swishtasya Suktasya Chabuddhi Dattayo Ho Avicchuto Arthak Kavibhir Nirupito Yaruttama Shloka Gunanu Varnanam. The meaning of this verse is, learned circles have positively concluded that the infallible purpose of the advancement of knowledge, namely, austerities, study of the Vedas, sacrifice, chanting of hymns and charity, culminates in the transcendental descriptions of the Lord who is defined in choice poetry.

---
### glorifying Krishna, purpose of scriptures
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wuIr7oYVfxA?t=117
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All the scriptures, ultimately it is a supreme personality of Godhead Lord Hari who is glorified."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the verse, stating that all methods of advancement of knowledge ultimately lead to the transcendental description of the Lord, and that all pious activities are meant for glorifying Krishna.

So this verse, spoken again by Narada Muni to Vyasadeva, describes ultimately what is the purpose of advancement of knowledge through various methods. The methods are listed here. There are many methods of advancement of transcendental knowledge. One can perform austerities, one can study the Vedas, one can perform various sacrifices, or somebody may chant the Vedic hymns, or one may give in charity. Yajna, dana, tapa, swadhyaya, and then chanting the hymns... So all these, ultimately, ends in what? According to the Vedic Scriptures, it ends in transcendental descriptions of the Lord! Everything is meant for transcendental description of the Lord! Every pious activity is ultimately meant for glorifying Krishna. Hari Sarvatra Giyate, this is what Vilamadvacharya says. All the scriptures, ultimately it is a supreme personality of Godhead Lord Hari who is glorified. So that's why Narada is telling Vyasa Deva you must describe in detail the transcendental activities of the supreme personality of Godhead Krishna.

---
### self-realization, Vishnu Maya, sense enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wuIr7oYVfxA?t=222
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who are enamored by this material energy, they do not understand that perfection of self-realization depends on Vishnu, the personality of Godhead, that they do not understand, they do not know."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Srila Prabhupada's explanation that human intellect should be used to understand the transcendental activities of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, leading to self-realization and devotional service to Vishnu, contrasting this with being enamored by Vishnu Maya and sense enjoyment.

So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport here, human intellect is developed for advancement of learning in art, science, philosophy, physics, chemistry, psychology, economics, politics, etc. So many subjects human beings have, they have developed that. Now it is expected that you will be able to understand the transcendental activities of the Supreme Personality of Godhead that by culture of such knowledge, the human society can attain perfection of life. That is the purpose. Now this perfection of life itself, what is the perfection of life? It is self-realization. Now that self-realization, therefore the Shruti or the original Vedas directs those who are actually advanced in learning, they should aspire for service of Lord Vishnu, devotional service to Vishnu. Now there are persons who are enamored by the external beauty of Vishnu Maya. The external enamelling. The external energy of the Supreme Lord Vishnu. He has many energies of which this material energy is very prominent in this material world. So those who are enamored by this material energy, they do not understand that perfection of self-realization depends on Vishnu, the personality of Godhead, that they do not understand, they do not know.

---
### sense gratification, transient nature, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wuIr7oYVfxA?t=337
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only when advancement of knowledge is applied in devotion and service of the Supreme Lord, the whole process becomes absolute."
**Summary:** This segment describes Vishnu maya as sense enjoyment, which is transient and miserable, and how those trapped by it use knowledge for sense gratification, emphasizing that true advancement of knowledge lies in devotion and service to the Supreme Lord.

Now, Vishnu maya means sense enjoyment. The effect of maya, the influence of maya is such that one is inclined simply to try to enjoy sense gratification. Now, what is the nature of the sense gratification? Everywhere in the scriptures it is described. It is transient, means it is flickering. Just for a very short time it is there. That enjoyment is there for a very short time. And also, it is actually miserable. Now, those who are in the trap of this Vishnu maya, they utilize advancement of knowledge for sense enjoyment. And that is very, very prominent in the material world. Kali Yuga. Everywhere you see people engaging in sense gratification in various ways, even those who are cultivating knowledge, even those who are cultivating spiritual knowledge, they desire a subtle type of sense gratification. Now, already Narad Muni has explained that all this paraphernalia, all this knowledge of the cosmic universe is an emanation of the Supreme Lord through his different energies. He has created this world, he maintains and he destroys. And the Lord has set in motion the actions and reactions of this material world by his inconceivable energy. So, in effect, ultimately, everything is non-different from this Lord because it is a manifestation of his own energy. At the same time, the Lord is always different from his energies. Therefore, only when advancement of knowledge is applied in devotion and service of the Supreme Lord, the whole process becomes absolute.

---
### devotional service, glorifying the Lord, examples
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wuIr7oYVfxA?t=479
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All the sages and devotees of the Lord have therefore recommended that whatever be the subject matter of advancement of knowledge, they should simply be applied in service of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that all branches of knowledge should be applied in the service of the Lord, giving examples of Valmiki Muni and Vyasa Deva who became immortal by glorifying the Lord through their writings.

Because the personality of Godhead is non-different from his name, his form, his qualities, his activities, his paraphernalia, is non-different. So, all the sages and devotees of the Lord have therefore recommended that whatever be the subject matter of advancement of knowledge, whether it be art or science or philosophy or physics or chemistry or psychology, they should simply be applied in service of the Lord. How can they be applied in the service of the Lord? So, literature, poetry may be used in glorifying the Lord. The fiction writers and poets generally are writing some subject matter of self-gratification. But if they turn towards the service of the Lord, they can beautifully describe the transcendental pastimes of the Lord. For example, Valviki Muni, he was a great poet. But he has composed Ramayana, the transcendental pastimes of the personality of Godhead, Lord Rama. And it is such a wonderful composition. But it is completely transcendental because it is glorifying the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Rama. Vyasa Deva is a great writer. But he has simply engaged all his talent in composing nice poetry, simply in glorifying the Supreme Lord in his various incarnations. So, they have become immortal, Valviki and Vyasa, simply by glorifying the Supreme Lord through poetry.

---
### science and philosophy, establishing the glory of the Lord, goal of knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wuIr7oYVfxA?t=622
**Verse References:** BG 7.19
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The goal of all knowledge is to know the Supreme Person, Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment explains that science and philosophy should also be used in the service of the Lord, to establish His glory, and that the ultimate goal of all knowledge is to know and realize Krishna.

Sanskrit poetry. Similarly, Shila Prabhupada says, science and philosophy also should be used in the service of the Lord. Simply, some people are busy presenting some dry speculative theories for self-gratification. That is useless. That cannot satisfy them, that cannot satisfy any of their readers. Ultimately, people become frustrated. So, Prabhupada is telling that philosophy and science should be engaged to establish the glory of the Supreme Lord. Because those who are advanced in intellect, they want to understand what is the ultimate reality through the medium of science or through the medium of philosophy. Therefore, a great scientist should endeavor to prove the existence of the Lord on a scientific basis. Similarly, philosophical speculation should be used to establish the supreme absolute truth as sentient, conscious person and all-powerful, absolutely powerful. All branches of knowledge should always be engaged in the service of the Lord. Bhagavad Gita also confirms the same fact. Apply all knowledge in the service of the Lord. Bhagavanam janmanamante jnanavan mam prapadyate vasudeva sarvamiti vedaisya sarvair ahameva vedhyam Neyam, the object of knowledge, is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The goal of all knowledge is to know the Supreme Person, Krishna. Like this, knowledge ultimately should be aimed at knowing Krishna, at realizing Krishna. Knowledge should be aimed at engaging in devotional service to Krishna.

---
### nescience, harikirtana, glorifying Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wuIr7oYVfxA?t=777
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Advanced knowledge is simply meant for establishing the glories of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** This segment concludes that knowledge not engaged in the service of the Lord is nescience, and that all activities, including scientific knowledge, should be used for harikirtana, glorifying Krishna, regardless of one's field.

And Prabhupada further says, knowledge not engaged in the service of the Lord is nescience. Nescience means darkness of ignorance. Therefore, the real import of this verse in the Bhagavatam is that advanced knowledge is simply meant for establishing the glories of the Supreme Lord. Now further, Prabhupada says, all such activities, including employing scientific knowledge in service of the Lord, is factual harikirtana or glorification of the Lord. This can be done by anybody in their own field, whether somebody is a scientist or an artist or a philosopher or a teacher, anybody can utilize their talent, their learning, their advanced intelligence simply for glorifying the Supreme Lord. Even the creation, entire creation is nothing but the creation of the Supreme Lord. So therefore, the ultimate goal is Krishna only and nothing else or no one else. Therefore, this verse is stressing, everything should be utilized for glorification of Krishna, especially knowledge. So I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami
