Om Agyana Timiran Dhasya Gyanan Janashalakaya Chaksharon Meenitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnu Padaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Srivasadi Gauravakta Vrinda. Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanaraya Narottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasaam Tatho Jaya Mudireyate. In this session we will be discussing from the 24th verse of the 5th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Now this is the section where Narad Muni describes his previous life to Vyasa Deva.

---
### humility, detachment, character
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WHCF0CGA7dg?t=74
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although they were impartial by nature, those followers of the Vedanta blessed me with their causeless mercy."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse, focusing on Narada Muni's description of his childhood, emphasizing his self-control, lack of interest in sports, and minimal speech, highlighting his unusual qualities as a young boy.

Now this is the section where Narad Muni describes his previous life to Vyasa Deva. In the previous session we have seen how Narad Muni begins the description by telling about his previous birth, that he was born as a son of a maidservant but then he happened to get the association of pure devotees and in their association he engaged in their service and a big transformation took place that is being described. So this 24th verse is Temai Apeta Akhila Chaapale Arbake Dante Adratha Kriyada. The meaning of this verse is, although they were impartial by nature, those followers of the Vedanta blessed me with their causeless mercy. As far as I was concerned, I was self-controlled and had no attachment for sports, even though I was a boy. In addition, I was not naughty and I did not speak more than required.

---
### impartiality, causeless mercy, self-control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WHCF0CGA7dg?t=160
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They are completely impartial, but still, when they see somebody is inclined to actually take up devotional service, then they immediately, out of their causeless mercy, they actually bless such a soul with transcendental enlightenment."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the qualities of the sages who showed causeless mercy to Narada Muni, emphasizing their impartiality and the boy's exceptional self-control and detachment from childish activities.

So, Narada Muni is describing, he was a small boy. He was not very old when the sages were camping due to Chaturmasya in the place where Narada Muni's mother was engaged in such menial service. Their nature is they are impartial. They are impartial by nature. They have no special liking for somebody or hatred towards anybody else. No. They are completely impartial. But still, when they see somebody is inclined to actually take up devotional service, then they immediately, out of their causeless mercy, they actually bless such a soul with transcendental enlightenment. So, Narada Muni says, those sages, they blessed him, with their causeless mercy. Now, what was this boy's behavior? He was self-controlled and he had no attachment for sports, even though he was a boy. Normally, boys will always be playing. Young boys, small boys. Till they come of certain maturity, they generally be engaged in playing, playing, playing, playing, playing. But, Narada Muni, this boy was an unusual boy. He was not interested in sports, childish sports. And, he was controlled, self-controlled. He was not naughty and he did not speak more than required.

---
### Vedic knowledge, relationship with God, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WHCF0CGA7dg?t=285
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu describes that in the Vedas, there are three subjects: To establish the relation of the living entities with the personality of Godhead, to perform the relative duties in devotional service, and thus achieve the ultimate goal of going back home, back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker references Srila Prabhupada and Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, explaining that the Vedas aim to establish the relationship between living entities and God, emphasizing devotional service and the ultimate goal of returning to Godhead, defining Sambandha, Abhideya, and Prayojana.

So, Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, in the Bhagavad Gita, the Lord says, all the Vedas are searching after me. All the Vedic literature are directing one towards Krishna. Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu describes that in the Vedas, there are three subjects. What are the three subjects? To establish the relation of the living entities with the personality of Godhead, to perform the relative duties in devotional service, and thus achieve the ultimate goal of going back home, back to Godhead. It's called Sambandha Abhideya Prayojana. Sambandha is, each one of us have got a relationship with the Supreme Lord. Every living being, without exception, because, we are part and parcel of the Supreme Lord. Mamey Vamsho Jeeva Loke Jeeva Bhuta Sanatana. And, in that relationship, there are certain activities. Relationship means activities. So, such activities performed in the relationship with the Supreme Lord are called Abhideya. And, the result is Prayojana. It results in developing our love for the Supreme Lord, or going back home, back to Godhead, where we belong.

---
### Vedanta, impersonalism, Absolute Truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WHCF0CGA7dg?t=378
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Impersonalists actually have no proper understanding of Vedanta philosophy because they start with an assumption, even though unjustified, that ultimately, the Absolute Truth is impersonal."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that Vedantavadis are pure devotees, distinguishing them from impersonalists who have an incomplete understanding of Vedanta, as they fail to recognize the Paramatma and Bhagavan features of the Absolute Truth.

The Vedantavadis, or the followers of Vedanta, as described in this verse by Narad Muni, indicates pure devotees of the Supreme Lord. Because, sometimes Vedantavadis are mistaken to be those who are impersonalists. Impersonalists actually have no proper understanding of Vedanta philosophy, because, they start with an assumption, even though unjustified, even though unjustified, that, ultimately, the Absolute Truth is impersonal. But, actually, the Absolute Truth has got three features. Impersonal feature is just one of the preliminary understanding of the Absolute Truth. There are two other features, the Paramatma feature and the Bhagavan feature. So, this Paramatma feature and the Bhagavan feature are completely mis-doubted by the impersonalists. Therefore, their understanding of Vedanta, is not complete. It is imperfect. It is incomplete. And, it is misleading also. That, ultimately, the Absolute Truth is impersonal. No. The Absolute Truth is the personality of Godhead. Therefore, Vedantavadis refers to the pure devotees of the Supreme Lord, who have full understanding of all the three features of the Absolute Truth.

---
### compassion, spiritual awakening, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WHCF0CGA7dg?t=467
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Such Bhaktivedantas, they want to get this ignorant mass of people to get out of ignorance, to re-establish their lost relationship with the Supreme Personality of God, Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Bhaktivedantas as impartial distributors of transcendental knowledge, driven by compassion to awaken people from ignorance and help them re-establish their relationship with Krishna, leading them towards self-realization.

Such Vedantavadis are also called as Bhaktivedantas. They are impartial in distributing the transcendental knowledge of devotional service. To them, nobody is enemy, nobody is friend. Nobody is educated, nobody is uneducated. They don't make distinctions based on all these type of characteristics. Nobody is favorable, nobody is unfavorable. So, they see, their vision is, people in general are engaged in wasting time in self-gratification, because, they do not know the goal of life. By birth, it is described, every conditioned soul is born into ignorance. In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna explains this. Everyone is born into ignorance. So, they have to be awakened by such Bhaktivedantas. So, such Bhaktivedantas, they want to get this ignorant mass of people to get out of ignorance, to re-establish their lost relationship with the Supreme Personality of God, Krishna. Now, by the endeavor of such Bhaktivedantas, even the most forgetful souls, most ignorant person, is awakened to a sense of spiritual life. And, the people, in general,

---
### discipline, causeless mercy, spiritual progress
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WHCF0CGA7dg?t=577
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But, we should always remember the effect of the blessing of such Vedantavadis is very, very powerful. Even if somebody is not so self-controlled, still, they can become very quickly purified by such powerful association."
**Summary:** The speaker returns to the example of Narada Muni's previous life, highlighting his inherent discipline and the compassionate initiation he received from the Vedantavadis, emphasizing the transformative power of their blessings and association.

begin to make progress on the path of self-realization. Now, this boy, Narada in his previous life, when he was the son of a maid servant, this boy, actually, before being initiated even, he was very self-controlled. He was detached from childish sporting. And, he was more or less very disciplined. But, the Vedantavadis, the Bhaktivedantas, they were compassionate upon the boy and they did not consider this boy is too small or he is still of an age where he is meant to engage in childish sports. They did not consider that. In spite of his being a small boy, they did initiate him and they blessed him to actually begin to render devotional service. So, this is their causeless mercy upon this boy. But, this boy also happened to be a disciplined, self-controlled boy who is not naughty, who would not speak more than required like that. So, in his case, we can understand his advancement was very, very, very fast. Very fast. But, we should always remember the effect of the blessing of such Vedantavadis is very, very powerful. Even if somebody is not so self-controlled, still, they can become very quickly purified by such powerful association.

### Brahmacharya, Vedic system, self-control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WHCF0CGA7dg?t=698
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without being self-controlled, nobody can make proper progress in self-realization. That's impossible."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the Vedic system of sending young boys to Gurukuls for Brahmacharya training, emphasizing the importance of self-control and spiritual acumen instilled from a young age, even before birth through Garbhadana Samskar, for achieving self-realization.

So, therefore, Srila Prabhupada says in the Vedic system, small boys who are about five years of age, between five and eight years, they are sent to the Gurukul or the Guru's ashram and they are systematically trained in Brahmacharya, celibacy, because then their mind can be focused on learning the essential knowledge that is required for self-realization. This training is also compulsory to create good citizens of the state. Without such basic training at an early age, it is difficult to prepare somebody for a good future life as a responsible citizen of the society. Now, the present day situation is such that people have become irresponsible. They are simply engaged in sense enjoyment. Neither they have such Brahmacharya training nor are they serious about the real goal of self-realization. But, the Vedic system is the spiritual acumen is injected into the child even before the birth of the child by what is called as Garbhadana Samskar. The parents actually conceive a child with spiritual consciousness. So, the child is injected with the spiritual acumen at the time of conceiving the child in the mother's womb. So, this is extraordinary chance given to a soul, a soul who is taking birth as a human being to prepare for self-realization even before the birth itself. So, this is the way to beget children in complete perfection. So, Srila Prabhupada says without being self-controlled, nobody can make proper progress in self-realization. That's impossible. It's not merely a matter of reading some books or hearing some discourses or studying some literature. No. It is much more than that. It is the systematic training specially in self-control by Brahmacharya. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.5.27 | 03-10-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna Katha, Purification, Transcendental Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fs-xIBfUWw4?t=68
**Verse References:** SB 1.5.27
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such Krishna Katha purifies the heart and in the purified state one can understand what I am and what is the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that hearing Krishna Katha purifies the heart, leading to the realization of one's true self and the nature of the Lord, both being transcendental.

In this session, we will be discussing the 27th verse of the 5th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, O great sage, this is Narada Muni addressing Vedavyasa, as soon as I got a taste for the personality of Godhead, my attention to hear of the Lord was unflinching, and as my taste developed, I could realize that it was only in my ignorance that I had accepted gross and subtle coverings for both the Lord and I are transcendental. So this is a very important realization that has come about by hearing about the glories of the Lord and developing a taste for such Krishna Katha. Such Krishna Katha purifies the heart and in the purified state one can understand what I am and what is the Lord. Neither I am a product of matter, nor is the Lord anything to do with matter in terms of his spiritual form, spiritual existence, spiritual attributes. So this becomes very, very clear as the heart becomes clean. This is the whole point.

---
### Ignorance, Krishna Consciousness, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fs-xIBfUWw4?t=194
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, as soon as the Krishna sun rises in the heart, immediately the darkness of material ignorance is dissipated, completely dissipated."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of darkness and light, this segment explains how ignorance is like darkness, obscuring our understanding of spirit, while Krishna is like the sun, dispelling ignorance and revealing spiritual knowledge when He rises in the heart.

In the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, ignorance in material existence is compared to darkness. Just like, very easy to understand, when we are in darkness, we cannot see anything around us, and therefore we cannot act properly. Similarly, our existence in this material world or material existence is compared to darkness because in ignorance of who we are, we are acting on the material platform. And in the material platform, we are only aware of matter. We are not aware of spirit. We are totally ignorant about spirit on the material platform. So therefore, here it is said, in all Vedic literatures, the personality of Godhead is compared to the sun. Krishna Surya Sama. Maya Hoi Andhakar. Krishna is compared to the sun to help us understand what is the position of Krishna. And Maya is like darkness. Wherever there is light, there cannot be darkness. As soon as there is light, just like in the morning when the sun rises, immediately the darkness is dispelled. Similarly, as soon as the Krishna sun rises in the heart, immediately the darkness of material ignorance is dissipated, completely dissipated.

---
### Hearing, Association, Absolute Truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fs-xIBfUWw4?t=323
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Hearing of the Lord's pastimes is itself transcendental association with the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of hearing Krishna's pastimes from a bona fide source, explaining that such hearing is direct association with Krishna on the transcendental platform, leading to spiritual elevation because Krishna is absolute and identical to His name, form, qualities, pastimes, and devotees.

Now, how does the Krishna sun rise in the heart? So, Srila Prabhupada says, hearing of the Lord's pastimes is itself transcendental association with the Lord. When you hear attentively Krishna's pastimes, and you have to hear from a proper source. We have been discussing this many, many times. We have already discussed earlier. From a bona fide source, from an authorized source, from a proper source. Not from a professional reciter, not from a non-devotee, not from a speculator, but from a pure devotee or a representative of a pure devotee who repeats the words of the pure devotee. You hear the Lord's pastimes. That is same as spiritual association with Krishna directly. It's direct association with Krishna on the transcendental platform. It is not material at all. Even though right now we are situated in the material body, we are in the material world, but such hearing immediately elevates the hearer to the spiritual world, to the spiritual platform. Why? Why does it happen? How does it happen like that? It is because there is no difference between the Lord and His transcendental pastimes. Identical. In a previous session I had described Lord is absolute. Absolute means Krishna, His name, His form, His qualities, His pastimes, His paraphernalia, His pure devotees are all identical. There is no relativity.

---
### Relativity, Absolute, Change of Heart
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fs-xIBfUWw4?t=456
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Whereas Krishna, when you hear about Krishna, because Krishna is fully present in his descriptions, you directly associate with Krishna."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of a phone call, this segment contrasts relative experience with the absolute nature of Krishna, explaining that while a phone call only conveys partial presence, Krishna is fully present in His descriptions, leading to direct association and a change of heart.

There is no relativity. To give another example to help you understand difference between relative and absolute. Now when you receive a phone call from a person who is far distant. Physically the person is situated, present in a very distant place. Let's say you are in Bangalore and the person is in Delhi. So when you receive the phone call, then you speak with the person. But then on speaking you have to recollect about the person's appearance, about the person's qualities, about the person's background, everything. Based on a past experience, you are having some idea. The voice itself of the person is very soothing to hear, particularly if you have a relationship. But still the person is not present in the voice. The person is not present in the voice. All the other attributes of the person are not experienced. For example, you cannot, supposing you are talking to your own child, you cannot pat the back of the child. You cannot embrace the child just when you hear the voice. Whereas Krishna, when you hear about Krishna, because Krishna is fully present in his descriptions, you directly associate with Krishna. Krishna is fully present. So you are coming face to face with Krishna, though you don't realize immediately. But the fact is Krishna is there. Krishna is present, fully present in the descriptions. Therefore the change of heart takes place. Change of heart takes place.

---
### Ignorance, Spiritual Identity, Material Existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fs-xIBfUWw4?t=619
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is only because of spiritual ignorance, ignorance of the spiritual fact, that every person thinks in terms of I am the body, I am a product of material nature."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on how ignorance leads to identifying with the material body and material existence, contrasting this with the true spiritual nature of the soul and the transcendental nature of the Lord, who appears to take birth but is eternally the supreme father.

So, Prabhupada says further, to become associated with the supreme light is to dissipate all ignorance. All ignorance is gone. Material existence means we don't know who is Krishna. I don't know who I am in terms of our spiritual nature. We don't know spiritual nature. So, Prabhupada says, particularly here, by ignorance only, the conditioned soul, conditioned soul means one who is habituated to this material world, material existence, three gunas, and material body, and material surroundings, material possessions, material relationships, everything material. We are not matter. No person is the material body. So, it is only because of spiritual ignorance, ignorance of the spiritual fact, that every person thinks in terms of I am the body, I am a product of material nature. I came into existence when I took birth in this world. This is wrong understanding. No, I exist eternally because I am spirit soul. I am not this body. My body was fabricated by Maya in the mother's womb. And this body was prepared and wrongly I associate myself with the body. Think I am the body. Even the Supreme Lord, he may take birth as son of Vasudeva and Devaki, but he is not the product of material nature. Neither he is actually the son of Vasudeva. He is actually the supreme father of everyone in this world. He is the supreme father. So, where is question of he taking birth? He appears to take birth. He actually does not take birth. So, the fact is, the personality of Godhead and the living beings are transcendental and have nothing to do with materiality, nothing to do with material nature.

---
### Krishna Consciousness, Service, Spiritual Energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fs-xIBfUWw4?t=789
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Both this gross body and the subtle mind, they have got two coverings...Both of them can be engaged in the service of the Lord while we are in this material world."
**Summary:** This segment explains that when ignorance is removed, one realizes that only Krishna and His spiritual energy exist. It then discusses how to engage both the gross body and the subtle mind in the service of the Lord while still in the material world, through physical service and devotional activities like hearing, thinking, and chanting.

So, when ignorance is removed, it is perfectly realized that there is nothing existing other than Krishna. Whole existence, only Krishna is there. Krishna is there means Krishna and his energies, especially which energy? His spiritual energy. His spiritual energy. Krishna is spiritual personality and his spiritual energy is important. Now, while we are in this body, after having learnt about Krishna's spiritual nature and our own spiritual nature, what do we do with this body? So, that Prabhupada explains, both this gross body and the subtle mind, they have got two coverings. The soul, I, you, any person, spirit soul, is covered by a subtle body. That is the inner body. And then over that there is a gross body covering. So, two coverings. Both of them can be engaged in the service of the Lord while we are in this material world, because both are belonging to the material energy of the Supreme Lord. So, gross body should be engaged in acts of rendering physical service to the Lord, like bringing water for cleansing the temple, paying obeisances by bowing down, etc. And, the subtle mind should be engaged in hearing attentively about the transcendental pastimes of the Lord, thinking about the Lord, chanting His name. So, all such activities are completely spiritual.

---

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So, simply by hearing from a pure devotee, all this transformation inside can take place very quickly."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by stating that transformation can occur quickly through hearing from a pure devotee, as illustrated by Narada Muni's life, and emphasizes the importance of associating with pure devotees.

So, simply by hearing from a pure devotee, all this transformation inside can take place very quickly. Otherwise, gradually the change happens through the regular methods. Gradual change happens. Otherwise, quickly the change can take place. And that is being illustrated in the life of Narada Muni when he was a small boy. And he was fortunate to get the association of pure devotees, bhaktivedantas. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### remedial measure, miseries, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TKb4TnbXTC0?t=72
**Verse References:** SB 1.5.32
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is decided by the learned that the best remedial measure for removing all troubles and miseries is to dedicate one's activities to the service of the Supreme Lord Personality of God."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam, stating that dedicating activities to the Supreme Lord is the best way to remove miseries.

In this session, we will be discussing the 32nd verse of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, O Brahmana Vyasa Deva, it is decided by the learned that the best remedial measure for removing all troubles and miseries is to dedicate one's activities to the service of the Supreme Lord Personality of God. So here is the conclusion of the instruction by the Bhaktivedantas to Narada.

---
### material happiness, suffering, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TKb4TnbXTC0?t=118
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Happiness experienced in material existence, which is called material happiness, is described in the scriptures as simply another form of misery only."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that material happiness is ultimately another form of misery, arising from the contact of the senses with sense objects, and is interpreted as happiness or distress according to one's conditioning.

Narada is addressing Vyasa Deva, telling about his previous life, and he is telling it is decided by the learned this is a remedial measure for removing all troubles and miseries. This is because, from the scriptures we understand, and by our personal experience also, we have knowledge about the miseries of this material existence. Now, people may not disagree that there are miseries, but they will say there are also good things. There is happiness and there is distress, both are there. But please understand that happiness experienced in material existence, which is called material happiness, is described in the scriptures as simply another form of misery only. This is a very, very shocking revelation, but it's a fact. What is the nature of this happiness which we experience in this material world? It is called samsparsha-bhoga. Samsparsha means contact, contact of the senses with the sense object. For the eyes it is form, for the ears it is sound, for the nose it is smell or fragrance, for the tongue it is taste, and for the sense of touch it is the touch sensation itself, for the perception of the sense of touch. So, this contact produces a kind of feeling in the mind, and that is interpreted as happiness or distress according to one's conditioning. Anyway, it's a very detailed subject, which I don't want to go into details now. But please understand, the essence of material existence is miseries.

---
### remedial measure, devotional service, practical application
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TKb4TnbXTC0?t=279
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Dedicate all your activities to Krishna, and thereby you become free from all miseries."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of dedicating one's activities to Krishna as a practical solution to miseries, even for those who are busy with worldly duties, and suggests learning from devotees how to do so.

And generally, for most people, when there is absence of misery, or they get relief from some immediate misery, then they consider that as happiness. Real happiness is something else, different from what we expect. So, therefore, the best remedial measure for removing all troubles and miseries, that is being told here. And this is a very, very important point for everyone to note. It is a very practical utility for everyone in this world. What is it? To dedicate one's activities to the service of the Supreme Lord Personality of God. Dedicate all your activities to Krishna, and thereby you become free from all miseries. How does it work? Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport that Narada Muni, from his personal experience, he is telling that simply sit and hear the transcendental activities of the Lord, and thereby you become from the right source, from the bona fide source, and thereby you become completely free from all miseries. Now, what if somebody is not able to do that? For that, Narada Muni is giving another solution. People may say, I am very busy working for maintaining my family, for my own maintenance. So, I don't have time to sit and hear. Even if I am able to hear, I can hear a little. I can't hear all the time. So, for such people whose time is mainly engaged in some work for the sake of maintenance, bodily maintenance, or for some responsibility, or duty, for such people, Narada Muni is telling that you have to learn from devotees how to dedicate your activities in the service of Krishna.

---
### divine sanction, supreme control, examples
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TKb4TnbXTC0?t=421
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Without the sanction of the Supreme Lord, nobody can become free from miseries."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that freedom from miseries requires the sanction of the Supreme Lord, illustrating this with examples of medical treatment and crossing a river, emphasizing the Lord's ultimate control.

How to do that? So, Prabhupada explains in the purport that generally people in this world are struggling very hard to get rid of miseries. Nobody wants miseries. Nobody wants suffering. But they do not know that without the sanction of the Supreme Lord, nobody can become free from miseries. So, three examples are given specifically to illustrate this point. That to cure a patient by medical treatment is useless if it is not sanctioned by the Lord. So, we know that doctors do help by giving some treatment to a patient who has got an infection or a disease. But doctor also is helpless if the patient does not survive the treatment. If the patient doesn't survive the treatment, then the doctor is helpless. Or the particular remedy doesn't work, then the doctor is helpless. So, the point is that sanction of the Supreme Lord is very much necessary for the treatment to be effective. Similarly, another example that is given, to cross the river or the ocean by a suitable boat is no remedial measure if the remedy is not sanctioned by the Lord. So, therefore, the ultimate sanctioning officer is the Supreme Lord.

---
### dedication, service, examples
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TKb4TnbXTC0?t=562
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We must therefore dedicate our activities to the Supreme Lord for ultimate success."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how to dedicate activities to the Supreme Lord, emphasizing His omnipresence, omniscience, and omnipotence, and providing examples for scholars, scientists, philosophers, poets, administrators, statesmen, and warriors.

And we must therefore dedicate our activities to the Supreme Lord for ultimate success. Now, how to do that? First of all, we should understand the Lord is present everywhere and He is all-knowing and He is all-powerful. Omnipotent, omniscient and omnipresent. Present everywhere, He is all-knowing and He is all-powerful. So, therefore, we should learn how to dedicate whatever activities we may do to the Supreme Lord. Whatever one may be, how should one dedicate whatever activity one is doing? So, Prabhupada gives specific examples here. If one is a learned scholar or a learned scientist or a philosopher or poet, he should employ his learning to establish the supremacy of the Lord. This is the purpose of all knowledge, all learning, especially by those who are scholars or scientists or philosophers or poets. They should try to study how the energy of the Lord is working in every sphere of life. The Lord is there, Krishna is there and His energies are there. So, in every sphere of life, the Lord's energies are working. So, try to study that. That is what is presented in the Bhagavad Gita. Especially in the Bhagavad Gita, how the Lord's energies are working in every sphere of life. Srila Prabhupada further says, do not decry Him. Do not try to become like Him. Do not try to take His position simply by some little knowledge. No. So, one should actually understand the glories of the Lord in terms of how His energies are working in every sphere of life. And establish the supremacy of the Lord through one's writings, through one's knowledge, through one's descriptions, through enlightening others who are ignorant, etc. Next, Prabhupada says, if one is an administrator or a statesman or a warrior or a politician, that is a Kshatriya, then one should try to establish the Lord's supremacy in statesmanship. Fight for the cause of the Lord as Sri Arjuna did. A warrior should fight for the cause of the Lord. So, regarding Arjuna, it is particularly mentioned here that in the beginning, Arjuna declined to fight. But when he was convinced by the Lord that the fighting was necessary, then Arjuna changed his decision and fought for the cause of the Lord. Karishye Vachanam Tava I'll simply follow your instructions, your directions. So, similarly,

### dedication, chanting, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TKb4TnbXTC0?t=778
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In this way, one can actually engage in the service of the Lord and that way become free from all miseries."
**Summary:** The speaker continues providing examples of how to dedicate one's activities to the Lord, including businessmen, industrialists, agriculturists, and even those engaged in menial work, emphasizing the importance of chanting Hare Krishna to think of Krishna and become free from miseries.

If one is a businessman or an industrialist, an agriculturist, then one should spend his hard-earned money for the cause of the Lord. How can one do this? Think always that the money which is accumulated is the wealth of the Lord. Because wealth is considered Lakshmi or goddess of fortune. And Lord of Lakshmi is Narayana, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Husband of Lakshmi. So, try to engage the wealth in the service of Lord Narayana and be happy. That is the way to realize the Lord in every sphere of life. Either one works like a Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya or even a Shudra. Shudra is one who does some menial work for getting some maintenance. So, even whatever menial activities one may do, one should try to do it as a service to the Lord. One should think that I am engaged in this particular activity. Of course, the activity should not be a sinful activity. It should be a pious activity. So, engaged in any pious activity, one should think, Krishna is engaging me in this activity. So, think of Krishna. Now, how to think of Krishna? By chanting Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare. Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. This can be done by everyone. This should be done by everyone. That is the scriptural direction. Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Namaiva Kevalam. In this way, one can actually engage in the service of the Lord and that way become free from all miseries. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.5.35 | 11-10-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### karma, spiritual knowledge, fruitive activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zlk-k2C5OJI?t=71
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Most people are generally inclined to work or do some activity."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam and explains the common misconception that performing fruitive work according to scriptures leads to transcendental knowledge and spiritual realization.

In this session, we will be discussing the 35th verse of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is whatever work is done here in this life for the satisfaction of the mission of the Lord is called Bhakti Yoga or Transcendental Loving Service to the Lord, and what is called knowledge becomes a concomitant factor. This verse is explained by Srila Prabhupada very nicely in the purport. I will directly go to the purport. From the purport, we can understand what is being said in this verse by Narada. The general and popular notion is that by discharging fruitive work in terms of the directions of the scriptures, one becomes perfectly able to acquire transcendental knowledge for spiritual realization. Most people are generally inclined to work or do some activity.

---
### spiritual realization, knowledge, bhakti yoga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zlk-k2C5OJI?t=153
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "People think that if we do the particular activities mentioned in the scriptures for getting some material benefit, we will develop this spiritual understanding by which we can develop the spiritual knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the common belief that performing pious activities for material benefits leads to spiritual knowledge and realization, and how people often equate Bhakti Yoga with Karma.

That is the nature of the soul, to be always active. So people think that according to the directions of the Shastra, they should perform certain types of pious activities or those activities recommended in the scriptures for some material benefit, and that will lead to getting transcendental knowledge, spiritual knowledge for spiritual realization. Ultimately, the Shastra say we have to realize we are spirit, so we have to realize the spiritual truth, and for that, spiritual knowledge is required. Where and how do you get that knowledge? Of course, you can get knowledge by studying the scriptures, but to realize that knowledge, merely studying, will it help? No, that is not enough. So therefore, people think that if we do the particular activities mentioned in the scriptures for getting some material benefit, not necessarily in terms of just some gross benefits in this life, but something for the future, could be for the future, could be for some next life like that, so they think that we will develop this spiritual understanding by which we can develop the spiritual knowledge, and the spiritual knowledge which we can actually become realized. Therefore, they consider such people, they consider Bhakti Yoga as another form of Karma only because Bhakti Yoga involves activities, and Karma means doing pious activities recommended in the scriptures, so both are the same.

---
### bhakti yoga, karma, jnana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zlk-k2C5OJI?t=294
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhakti Yoga is above both Karma and Jnana."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that Bhakti Yoga is superior to both Karma and Jnana, a confidential truth revealed in the Srimad Bhagavatam, and that Karma and Jnana are dependent on Bhakti Yoga.

And Shastra gives an explanation how they are... And therefore, they give a higher position to Jnana or spiritual knowledge, but the real fact is that Bhakti Yoga is above both Karma and Jnana. This is not known to many people, and this confidential truth is revealed in the Srimad Bhagavatam. So Bhakti Yoga is independent of Jnana, Bhakti Yoga is independent of Karma. On the other hand, if you really study carefully Karma, Jnana, and Bhakti, then both Karma and Jnana are different. They are dependent on Bhakti Yoga. How are they dependent?

---
### karma, bhakti, supreme lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zlk-k2C5OJI?t=375
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Karma becomes Karma Yoga only when Karma is performed as Yajna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the success of Karma depends on pleasing the Supreme Lord through Bhakti, and spiritual knowledge turns into realization only when employed in pleasing the Supreme Lord, referencing the Bhagavad Gita.

Because whatever activities we may perform, unless those, even pious activities, unless the results of such activities are sanctioned by the Supreme Lord, one will not get the results. So one has to perform such pious activities which are recommended in the scriptures in order to please the Supreme Lord, either directly or indirectly. So the success in performing Karma is dependent on pleasing the Supreme Lord, and pleasing the Supreme Lord means Bhakti or Bhakti Yoga. And as regards knowledge, if somebody is cultivating spiritual knowledge, that spiritual knowledge will turn into realization only when such knowledge is employed in pleasing the Supreme Lord. In the Bhagavad Gita, very clearly it is said that Karma becomes Karma Yoga only when Karma is performed as Yajna. If you want freedom from karma bandhana, then Karma should be done as Yajna. Yajna means to do some work for pleasing the Supreme Lord, which is nothing but Bhakti. So Karma independent of Bhakti is not going to give the real result. Jnana independent of Bhakti will not give the real result of realization, spiritual realization.

---
### spiritual realization, knowledge, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zlk-k2C5OJI?t=514
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who have really come to the stage of mature knowledge, they surrender to Krishna. Only then they become realized."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that only through surrender to Krishna, after attaining mature knowledge, can one achieve spiritual realization, and that theoretical knowledge remains unrealized without Bhakti, referencing the Bhagavad Gita.

That is also explained in the Bhagavad Gita. Those who are jnanavan, those who have really come to the stage of mature knowledge, they surrender to Krishna. Only then they become realized. Otherwise, any amount of knowledge, which is simply some theoretical information, remains as theory. Remains as theory. It doesn't become realized knowledge. It doesn't lead to enlightenment. So enlightenment is possible only by Bhakti, and real results of Karma, sorry, results of Karma is possible only by dedicating those activities in service of the Supreme Lord. That's why Narad Muni is telling here that this Kriya Yoga, as he says here, Kriya Yoga is specifically the principle to satisfy the Supreme Lord by one's activities. Whatever activities one performs, even as duties or as one's own work in terms of one's position in the varnashrama system, whatever work one does, even spiritual activities, what may be called spiritual activities, activities of self-realization, all such activities have to be done to satisfy the Supreme Lord. Because in the fifth chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, Lord Krishna says, Three principles he explains which are very very important to understand.

---
### supreme lord, satisfaction, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zlk-k2C5OJI?t=674
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When you please Krishna, everyone is satisfied."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the three principles from the Bhagavad Gita: Krishna is the supreme enjoyer, proprietor, and well-wisher, emphasizing that pleasing Krishna satisfies everyone, and transcendental knowledge is automatically achieved through devotion.

For ultimate peace, What are the three principles? That Krishna is the supreme enjoyer of all sacrifices and penances. Ultimately, every sacrifice culminates in pleasing the Supreme Lord. Bhoktaram means he is the real enjoyer, ultimate enjoyer of all, yajna tapasam, yajna and tapasya. And he is the supreme proprietor of all planets, all devatas, the entire creation, the entire existence. He is the Lord, he is the master, he is the proprietor. And the third point is he is the topmost well-wisher of every living being, which means that if he is pleased, everyone is satisfied. You can easily understand it is very difficult to separately, individually satisfy every person. We are obligated to so many people, but if we simply satisfy Krishna, that is like watering the root of a tree. Then you are supplying water to the root of the tree is the best way to nourish all the parts of the tree, or supplying food to the stomach. All the parts of the body are nourished. There is an arrangement. So similarly, when you please Krishna, everyone is satisfied. All the parts and parcels, because everyone is a part and parcel of Krishna. So therefore, the recommendation here is that simply do all activities for pleasing the Supreme Lord. Transcendental knowledge or spiritual knowledge or enlightenment is automatically achieved without separate endeavour.

---
### devotional service, knowledge, enlightenment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zlk-k2C5OJI?t=821
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna enlightens the devotee from within."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by stating that Krishna bestows knowledge upon those engaged in loving devotional service, dispelling ignorance and enlightening the devotee from within, emphasizing the power of Bhakti.

Without separate endeavour. This is explained in the Bhagavad Gita also. Krishna says, I give the knowledge to those who are engaged in my loving, devotional service. The principle is to please Krishna, out of love, for pleasing Krishna. And how does it work? Because Krishna is seated in everyone's heart, so he destroys with the shining lamp of knowledge the darkness born of ignorance. All darkness is dispelled by Krishna. So therefore, that's how it works. Devotional service works without separate endeavour, and Krishna enlightens the devotee from within. Of this, there need be no doubt at all. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna Consciousness, Devotional Service, Remembrance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oKbQ-2nrDPw?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 1.5.36
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "While performing duties according to the order of Sri Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, one constantly remembers Him, His names, and His qualities."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces verse 36 from the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, emphasizing the importance of remembering Krishna while performing one's duties.

Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shreemate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gaurabhani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Srivasadi Gaurabhakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudhirayet. This session we will be discussing from the 36th verse of the 5th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Kurvana Yatra Karmaani Bhagavachikshaya Asakrit Grnanthi Gunanamani Krishnasya Anusmaranticha. The meaning of this verse is: While performing duties according to the order of Sri Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, one constantly remembers Him, His names, and His qualities.

---
### Devotional Service, Krishna Consciousness, Vedic Rites
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oKbQ-2nrDPw?t=108
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "An expert devotee of the Lord can mold his life in such a way that while performing all kinds of duties, either for this or the next life, he can constantly remember the Lord's name, fame, qualities, etc."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's purport, explaining how devotees can mold their lives to constantly remember Krishna through His names, qualities, and pastimes, even while performing worldly duties, and how even Vedic rites involve Vishnu.

So, Srila Prabhupada has written a very elaborate purport for this verse. This is spoken by Narada Muni to Vyasa Deva. The principle of always remembering Krishna through remembering his names, his qualities, his pastimes, his forms, etc. This is what is being described here, and that also while performing duties accordingly, according to the instructions of Krishna. So how does it actually work? Srila Prabhupada elaborates this in the purport. He says, an expert devotee of the Lord can mold his life in such a way that while performing all kinds of duties, either for this or the next life, he can constantly remember the Lord's name, fame, qualities, etc. So it is a question of molding one's life in a particular way that is enabling one to remember the Lord's name, qualities, pastimes, etc. The order of the Lord is distinctly there in the Bhagavad Gita. What is that order? One should work only for Lord Krishna in all spheres of life. In every sphere of life the Lord should be situated as the proprietor. According to Vedic rites, even in the worship of some demigods like Indra, Brahma, Saraswatthi, Ganesha, the system is that in all circumstances the representation of Vishnu must be there as Yajneshwara or the controlling power of such sacrifices. So, sacrifice to demigods also involves the representation of Vishnu as Yajneshwara. He is the supreme lord of all sacrifices.

---
### Krishna Consciousness, Devotion, Proprietorship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oKbQ-2nrDPw?t=245
**Verse References:** BG 5.29
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the Bhagavad Gita, Lord Krishna has declared himself to be the supreme enjoyer of everything, the supreme proprietor of every planet and the supreme friend of all beings."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that even when worshipping demigods, Vishnu's presence is compulsory, as He is the supreme lord. He emphasizes that all activities should be offered to Krishna, the supreme enjoyer, proprietor, and friend, as stated in Bhagavad Gita 5.29.

It is recommended that a particular demigod be worshipped for a particular purpose, as far as Karmakanda section of the Vedas is concerned. But still, the presence of Vishnu is compulsory in order to make the function proper, because the fact is, Vishnu is the supreme lord of all the demigods. Apart from such demigods, there are other demigods as well. Apart from such Vedic duties, even in our ordinary dealings, for example, in our business or profession or in our household affairs, we must consider that the result of all activities must be given over to the supreme enjoyer Lord Krishna. In the Bhagavad Gita, Lord Krishna has declared himself to be the supreme enjoyer of everything, the supreme proprietor of every planet and the supreme friend of all beings. This is a famous verse in the 5th chapter, 29th verse: bhoktaram yajnatapasam sarva-loka-maheshwaram suradam sarva-bhutanam jnatvamam shanti-mrcchati. So therefore, no one else but Lord Shri Krishna can claim to be the proprietor of everything within his creation. So therefore, no one else but Lord Shri Krishna can claim to be the proprietor of everything within his creation. A pure devotee remembers this constantly and in doing so, he repeats the transcendental name, fame and qualities of the Lord, which means that he is constantly in touch with Lord Krishna. Now the Lord is identical with his name, fame, etc.

---
### Chanting, Devotional Service, Preaching
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oKbQ-2nrDPw?t=383
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "No one is more dear to him than one who is always engaged in preaching work, of the Lord's name and fame all over the world."
**Summary:** The speaker simplifies the concept by stating that chanting Hare Krishna while working keeps one in touch with the Lord. He then discusses how a significant portion of one's income should be used to carry out Krishna's orders, including preaching His message.

So to be associated with his name, fame, etc. constantly means actually to associate with the Lord himself. So in simple words, while doing any work, if I keep chanting Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, then I am constantly in touch with the Lord himself. I am constantly performing all kinds of duties which are according to scriptural direction, not concocted duties. Then Srila Prabhupada talks about results of the activity. What about that? The major portion of our monetary income, not less than 50%, must be spent to carry out the order of Lord Krishna. Not only should we give the profit of our earning to this cause, but we must also arrange to preach this cult of devotion to others, because that is also one of the orders of the Lord. In the Bhagavad Gita, the Lord definitely says, no one is more dear to him than one who is always engaged in preaching work, of the Lord's name and fame all over the world. The scientific discoveries of the material world can also be equally engaged in carrying out his order.

---
### Preaching, Devotees, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oKbQ-2nrDPw?t=471
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The attempt must go on to convert unwilling men to become his devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the broad scope of Narada Muni's instructions to Vyasa Deva, stating that the Lord wants the message of the Bhagavad Gita preached among devotees. He then addresses the challenge of preaching in Kali Yuga, where most people are non-devotees, and emphasizes the need to convert even unwilling individuals.

The scope of what Narada Muni is telling Vyasa Deva here, is very very broad application of the instruction here. So the Lord wants the message of the Bhagavad Gita to be preached amongst his devotees. It may not be so done amongst those who have no credit of austerities, charity, education etc. So how to apply this? The Lord says, you preach my message amongst the devotees, but not amongst those who are without any austerity, those who are without education, spiritual knowledge, not among those people, among non-devotees. So, Srila Prabhupada says, when it's a fact that in Kali Yuga majority of the people are non-devotees, what should we do? The attempt must go on to convert unwilling men to become his devotees. This is a secret that Prabhupada is revealing here. And this is exactly what Prabhupada did. He went to America on the order of his Guru. And he preached my message.

---
### Preaching, Krishna Consciousness, Lord Chaitanya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oKbQ-2nrDPw?t=570
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Krishna consciousness is eternally established in the heart of every living being."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Prabhupada's preaching to non-devotees, including hippies and materialists, without discrimination. He explains that Krishna consciousness is inherent in everyone's heart, covered by material coverings, and that Lord Chaitanya taught a simple method of preaching through singing, dancing, and refreshment.

He preached amongst non-devotees. Non-devotees means those who are not conversant with devotional service, those who are not from the background of Vedic culture or Sanatana Dharma, culture of Sanatana Dharma, such people, hippies, common people who are materialists, all kinds of people. He did not discriminate. Prabhupada did not discriminate. He preached among all sections of people. And the attempt is how to make them devotees. Why? Why is this suggested by Srila Prabhupada here? Because the Chaitanya Charitamrita says, Krishna Bhakti Nitya Siddha Sadhya Kabunaya. Krishna consciousness is eternally established in the heart of every living being. So it is not some artificial imposition on a non-devotee. No. In that sense, if you see, because every living being is part and parcel of Krishna, every living being has got dormant Krishna consciousness within the heart. It is just covered by the material coverings. It has to be uncovered. So Srila Prabhupada further says, Lord Chaitanya has taught a very simple method in this connection. How is this done? To convert unwilling people to become devotees. He has taught the lesson for preaching the transcendental message through singing, dancing and refreshment. As such, 50% of our income may be spent for this purpose. This is what exactly Prabhupada did. In the beginning, he introduced the non-devotees to Kirtan and singing and dancing and refreshment, Prasadam.

---
### Kirtan, Prasadam, Transformation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oKbQ-2nrDPw?t=693
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "He becomes present when anybody sings His holy name, irrespective of who the person is."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights Prabhupada's initial focus on Kirtan and Prasadam, emphasizing their transformative power. He asserts that if wealthy individuals dedicate 50% of their income to the Lord's service, they can transform the world. He explains that everyone enjoys singing, dancing, and refreshment, and that the Lord becomes present when His holy name is sung.

Kirtan and Prasadam, two things he introduced in the beginning. And in this fallen age of quarrel and dissension, if only the leading and wealthy persons of society agree to spend 50% of their income in the service of the Lord, as it is taught by Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, there is absolute certainty of converting this hell of pandemonium to the transcendental abode of the Lord. And Prabhupada showed it actually, how it works. He practically demonstrated it in many parts of the world. So, further he says, no one will disagree to partake in a function where good singing, dancing and refreshment are administered. Kirtan and Prasadam. Generally, nobody will disagree. Everyone will attend such a function and everyone is sure to feel individually the transcendental presence of the Lord. See, this is the Lord's mercy. He becomes present when anybody sings His holy name, irrespective of who the person is. The Lord becomes present with all His potencies. He is incarnated that way in this Kali Yuga. This alone will help the attendant associate with the Lord and thereby purify himself in spiritual realization.

---
### Purification, Devotee Guidance, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oKbQ-2nrDPw?t=805
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The administering of this whole program of Kirtan and Prasadam among the non-devotees should be done under the guidance of a pure devotee, sent person, pure devotee of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that singing the holy name cleanses the heart, regardless of the dirt covering it, and that Prasadam's potency is as powerful as the Lord. He emphasizes that these activities must be conducted under the guidance of a pure devotee, free from mundane desires, to be effective in converting even unwilling people to Krishna consciousness.

The dirty things in the heart are all cleansed, cleansed away. No matter how much the dirt that is covering the holy name, the transcendental potency of Prasadam will work in a very, very, very powerful way, as powerful as the Lord and cleanse away the heart immediately, instantaneously, cleanse away the dirt. Now, the only condition for successfully executing such spiritual activities is that they must be conducted under the guidance of a pure devotee who is completely free from all mundane desires, fruitive activities and dry speculations about the nature of the Lord. The administering of this whole program of Kirtan and Prasadam among the non-devotees should be done under the guidance of a pure devotee, sent person, pure devotee of the Lord. Then it is effective, because a pure devotee, when he sings or when he directs somebody else to sing, in that direction or in the singing, there is full potency, sent person potency. And that will cleanse away the heart and make even unwilling people to become devotees. And such devotees can actually become Krishna conscious and thereby make their life successful. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Pancharatra, Narayana, Chaturvyuha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3jkSsUpVw-4?t=123
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "According to Pancharatra, Narayana is the primal cause of all expansions of Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Narada Muni is directing everyone to chant the glories of the Supreme Lord, specifically in His form as Vasudeva, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, and Aniruddha. He then references the Pancharatra scripture, explaining that Narayana is the primal cause of all expansions of Godhead, including Krishna, Balarama, Rama, Parashurama, Varaha, Sankarsana, and Mahavishnu. He further elaborates on the four expansions: Vasudeva, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, and Aniruddha, and their positions.

one is able to remember the Lord's names, forms, qualities, pastimes, etc. So here, specifically, Narad Muni is directing, let us all chant the glories of the Supreme Lord, specifically in His form as Vasudeva, Sankarsana Pradyumna Aniruddha. So in the Purport Shri Lapa Prabhupada says, according to the Pancharatra, this is one of the scriptures, specifically connected with Bhakti or devotional service. So according to Pancharatra, Narayana is the primal cause of all expansions of Godhead. Now Krishna is there, the original personality, supreme personality of Godhead. He has got many expansions. Balarama is His expansion. Rama is His expansion. Parashurama is His expansion. Varaha is His expansion. Sankarsana is His expansion. Mahavishnu is His expansion, like that. So there are many, many expansions, actually unlimited expansions. But the Pancharatra describes Narayana the form of the Lord is the primeval cause of all expansions of Godhead. Now these are Vasudeva Sankarsana Pradyumna Aniruddha. The four expansions are specifically mentioned in the Pancharatra. Vasudeva Sankarsana Pradyumna Aniruddha. Now these four expansions are described as Vasudeva and Sankarsana are on the middle left and right. Pradyumna is on the right of Sankarsana and Aniruddha is on the left of Vasudeva. And thus the four deities are described. The four deities are situated.

### Chaturvyuha, Omkara, Vedic mantras
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3jkSsUpVw-4?t=271
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Omkara is actually another form of Krishna Omni, the sound representation of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining the Chaturvyuha, the fourfold expansions of the Supreme Lord. He then discusses the significance of 'Om' in Vedic mantras, stating that it is another form of Krishna, the sound representation of Krishna, and that Vedic mantras invoke the presence of the Lord through chanting.

They are known as the four HD camp of Lord Sri Krishna. The Sanskrit term is Chaturvyuha Rupa. Chaturvyuha. They are the four fold forms, expansions of the Supreme Lord. So this, whatever Narada Muni has told here, Om Namo Bhagavate Dubhyam. So this is a Vedic hymn or mantra beginning with Omkara Pranava. All Vedic mantras begin with Om. All Vedic mantras. So that Om is actually the Supreme Lord Krishna himself. That is explained in the Bhagavad Gita and all of the scriptures. Omkara is actually another form of Krishna Omni. The sound representation of Krishna. Thus the mantra is established by the transcendental chanting process namely Om, Namo, Dhimahi, etc. Any Vedic mantra begins with Om and it has got these words in the mantra in order to invoke the presence of the Lord through such chanting.

### fruitive work, transcendental realization, material benefits
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3jkSsUpVw-4?t=356
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Any transaction either in the field of fruity work or in emperic philosophy, which is not ultimately aimed at transcendental realization of the Supreme Lord is considered to be useless."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of the verse, stating that any activity, whether fruitive work or empiric philosophy, that is not aimed at transcendental realization of the Supreme Lord is useless. He emphasizes that the ultimate goal should always be transcendental realization of the Supreme Lord, as only that provides permanent benefit for the soul, contrasting it with temporary material gains that cannot protect one from the cataclysms of material nature.

The purport is, now what is the meaning of this whole thing that has been described in this particular verse. Any transaction either in the field of fruity work or in emperic philosophy, which is not ultimately aimed at transcendental realization of the Supreme Lord is considered to be useless. In the Vedas, there are so many recommendations in terms of doing fruity work. What is fruity work? Any work done which will give a result of some material benefit. And emperic philosophy is knowledge which will lead to realization of the Self. So whether in the field of karma or jnana, which is something which is not aimed at transcendental realization of the Supreme Lord, ultimately the goal should always be in every activity, even what is recommended in the scriptures or otherwise. The aim should be transcendental realization of the Supreme Lord. Only that is having a permanent benefit for the soul. Otherwise, any sort of material gain, it is simply a temporary benefit. It is not going to save us ultimately when we have to face the cataclysms of material nature. When we have to face death or when we have to face pralaya, when we have to face some natural calamities, or when we have to face material miseries which are forced upon us, then all the material benefits are useless. So anything that we do should be aimed at transcendental realization of the Supreme Lord. Because by realizing the Supreme Lord, immediately we are elevated beyond the three gunas to the spiritual platform where there are no miseries, there are no natural calamities, there is no illusion, there is no effect of the illusory energy. So that is very, very important. It is very important to be noted.

---
### devotional service, intimacy with Krishna, Krishna Prema
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3jkSsUpVw-4?t=559
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Each one of us as spirit-soul, we have an intimate, loving relationship with Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. It is already there. It is eternally there."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Naradji has explained the nature of unalloyed devotional service through his personal experience in developing intimacy with the Lord. He emphasizes that each spirit-soul has an eternal, intimate, loving relationship with Krishna, which can be developed through progressive devotional activities, culminating in Krishna Prema, the ultimate goal.

So Srila Prabhupada is further explaining here in the purport, Naradji has therefore explained the nature of unalloyed devotional service by his personal experience in the development of intimacy between the Lord and living entity. We should not forget, each one of us as spirit-soul, we have an intimate, loving relationship with Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. It is already there. It is eternally there. It is not some new relationship we have to discover or establish. No. It is eternally there. It always exists. And therefore this is so important to understand that the nature of pure devotional service is that one can develop this intimacy of one's relationship with Krishna by a gradual process of progressive devotional activities. This is what Narad Muni has been explaining in the several verses which he is instructing to Vyasa Deva. Such a progressive march of transcendental devotion for the Lord culminates in the attainment of loving service of the Lord which is called Prema. That is what Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is teaching us. Krishna Prema

---
### Krishna Prema, rasas, pure devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3jkSsUpVw-4?t=663
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ultimate goal of all processes is Krishna Prema."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna Prema, explaining that it is experienced in different transcendental variegatedness called rasas or tastes, making it a rich and elaborate subject. He distinguishes between pure and mixed devotional service, emphasizing the importance of aiming for pure devotional service.

as the ultimate goal of all processes. Ultimate goal of all processes is Krishna Prema. And this Krishna Prema is to be experienced in different transcendental variegatedness called rasas or tastes. It is not something which is stereotyped, hackneyed, boring, dull kind of experience. No. It is full of variegatedness. Rich variegated experience of different rasas. It is a very elaborate subject. A subject matter of transcendental taste, mellow taste of devotional service. Such devotional service is also executed in mixed forms namely mixed with fruity work or empirical philosophical speculation. So there is pure devotional service and there is mixed devotional service. What is the difference? So Srila Prabhupada says that we should aim for pure devotional service. And we should not be settled down for mixed devotional service.

---
### mixed devotional service, karma mishrita bhakti, jnana mishrita bhakti, eternal happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3jkSsUpVw-4?t=753
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "These will never satisfy the soul. These will never be permanent. They are all temporary results."
**Summary:** The speaker explains mixed devotional service, giving examples of performing devotional service with the aim of material gain or merging into the existence of the Supreme. He clarifies that these are karma mishrita bhakti and jnana mishrita bhakti, which will never satisfy the soul or be permanent, as spirit souls are always looking for eternal happiness, knowledge, and transcendental bliss.

For example, it has been explained already earlier in the second chapter of this first canto, but I will just briefly mention. Just like somebody performs devotional service with the aim of getting some material gain. That material gain could be something very, very pious. It may not be something sinful. But still such material gain being the aim of performing devotional service, as recommended in the Bhagavatam, that will make it mixed devotional service. Or somebody performs devotional service with the aim of merging into the existence of the Supreme. These are called karma mishrita bhakti and jnana mishrita bhakti. These will never satisfy the soul. These will never be permanent. They are all temporary results. They may give some temporary satisfaction, some temporary happiness, some temporary effects may be there. But they will not be eternally lasting. We should always remember that as spirit souls, we are always looking for eternal happiness, full of knowledge and transcendental bliss. That's what we are looking for. We are hankering for that. That is our constitutional position. That's our natural position. But anything short of that is never going to be satisfying ultimately.

---
### pure devotional service, satisfaction of the soul, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3jkSsUpVw-4?t=871
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "What can satisfy the soul completely? Only if devotional service is pure, unmixed with karma and jnana."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by quoting Suta Goswami and Rupa Goswami, emphasizing that only pure devotional service, unmixed with karma and jnana, can completely satisfy the soul. He reiterates that such pure devotional service is being explained and then ends the session.

So, Suta Goswami has already explained, savai pumsam paro dharmo yato bhaktir adhokshaje ahaituki apratihata yayatma supraseedati What can satisfy the soul completely? Only if devotional service is pure, unmixed with karma and jnana. Even Rupa Goswami's definition, Acharya's definition of pure devotional service is, jnana karmadi anavratam devotional service which is free from the mixture of jnana and karma. So, such pure devotional service is being explained here. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

We shall be discussing from verse number 39 of chapter 5 of 1st canto of Srim Bhagavatam in this session. Imam Svanigamam Brahman Avetya Mad Anushthitam Adanme Gyanam Aishwaryam Svasmin Bhavam Chakeshavah. The meaning of this verse is O Brahmana, thus by the Supreme Lord Krishna I was endowed first with the transcendental knowledge of the Lord as inculcated in the confidential parts of the Vedas then with the spiritual opulences and then with his intimate loving service. So this is Narad Muni speaking to Vyasadeva.

And after the Lord bestowed this transcendental knowledge of the Supreme Lord himself then he bestowed spiritual opulences on Narada. Then after that he bestowed Narada with the Lord's intimate loving service. So three things, confidential knowledge about the Supreme Lord revealed in the Vedas, then the spiritual opulences and intimate loving service. Three things the Lord bestowed upon Narada when Narada worshipped the Lord through his transcendental sound representation.

### transcendental sound, holy name, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NSljLqQXZc4?t=297
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada, explaining that communion with the Lord through transcendental sound is non-different from Krishna. He defines communion as a close relationship with exchanged thoughts and feelings, and explains that each spirit soul has a specific loving relationship with the Supreme Lord, covered by material elements, which can be revived through bhakti and the holy name.

This is explained by Srila Prabhupada in the purport. Prabhupada says communion with the Lord by transmission of the transcendental sound is non different from the whole spirit Lord Sri Krishna. What is the meaning of communion? Communion means a close relationship with someone in which thoughts and feelings are exchanged. Now this is the special thing about a personal loving relationship. You would have heard many times that actually in the scriptures it is revealed that each one of us, we are spirit soul, the Supreme Lord is the Supreme Spirit and each one of us has got a specific loving relationship with the Supreme Lord. Every one of us and that relationship is now covered by this material body, material mind, material intelligence, material ego. When the coverings are removed then gradually by the process of bhakti especially through approaching the Supreme Lord through his transcendental sound representation which is the holy name of the Supreme Lord.

What kind of relationship it is? It is a relationship of love. So because we in the material world have been away from the Lord for many many many births, we cannot count and go back in this regard. So without trying to trace the beginning of forgetfulness, Shastras say accept now your condition is you are forgetful of Krishna and through the process of bhakti revive this relationship and when it is revived then the Lord reveals himself. Svayam evas puratyada. In the Puranas it is said Atha Shri Krishna Nama adi na bhaved grahyam indriyaihi. Krishna's name, form, personality, personal qualities, personal activities are not to be understood through our blunt material senses.
