## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.6.27 | 22-11-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Narada Muni, previous life, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bjYKu8NSUOs?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 1.6.27
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So Narada Muni is going to conclude his description about his previous life."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the verse to be discussed, which concludes Narada Muni's description of his previous life and how he became free from material attachments.

In this session, we will be discussing the 27th verse of the 6th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, "And so, O Bramhna Vyasadeva, in due course of time, I, who was fully absorbed in thinking of Krishna and who therefore had no attachment to Krishna, am being completely free from all material taints, met with death, as lightning and illumination occurs simultaneously." So Narada Muni is going to conclude his description about his previous life.

---
### detachment, Krishna consciousness, material attachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bjYKu8NSUOs?t=62
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is important to note this that even if a devotee has a tinge of material attachment he has to take birth again after quitting this body in the material world."
**Summary:** This segment explains that a devotee free from material attachments, achieved through absorption in Krishna, does not have to take birth again in the material world.

This is the last verse about his previous life. What happened? As he was engaged in traveling throughout the earth, chanting and glorifying the Lord without any material formalities, he, in course of time, when he had become fully absorbed in thinking of Krishna and in such a state of consciousness, he did not think of Krishna. So he was not fully absorbed in thinking of Krishna. He had become completely free from all material attachments, no attachment to anything in this material world. It is important to note this that even if a devotee has a tinge of material attachment he has to take birth again after quitting this body in the material world, whereas that devotee who is completely free from all attachments, and how is that possible? Becoming fully absorbed in thinking of Krishna by constantly engaging in chanting and hearing and glorifying the name, fame, pastimes, activities, qualities of the Supreme Lord.

---
### purification, death, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bjYKu8NSUOs?t=162
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As one goes on chanting and hearing the heart becomes cleansed and no further contamination can enter because such a devotee is always engaged thinking of Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment describes how chanting and hearing about Krishna cleanses the heart, preventing further contamination, and how death for such a devotee is a change of body, like lightning and illumination occurring simultaneously.

Being completely freed from all material taints, completely free from all material taints, as one goes on chanting and hearing the heart becomes cleansed and no further contamination can enter because such a devotee is always engaged thinking of Krishna, always engaged in hearing about Krishna, always engaged in chanting the holy name, fame, qualities, past times of Krishna etc. So no further contamination and all past contamination is completely cleansed away. Then in course of time he met with death. What is that death? Quitting the present body. Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita, the soul doesn't die, the real person, the spiritual identity is not lost at the time of death. It is only the material body that one quits. So met with death as lightning and illumination occurs simultaneously. Now what is the significance of Narada Muni telling he met with death as lightning and illumination occurs simultaneously is explained by Prabhupada in the purport.

---
### detachment, material existence, spiritual reality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bjYKu8NSUOs?t=264
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As a very rich man has no hankerings for small petty things. So also a devotee of Lord Krishna who is guaranteed to pass on to the kingdom of God where life is eternal, fully cognizant and blissful."
**Summary:** This segment explains Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing that being absorbed in Krishna leads to detachment from material desires and guarantees passage to the eternal kingdom of God, contrasting material existence with spiritual reality.

So let us go through the purport. To be fully absorbed in the thought of Krishna means clearance of material dirts or hankerings. Such cleansing away of material dirt is not possible for somebody who is engaged in material activities. Material activity means actions and reactions under the influence of the three gunas. So material activity means one becomes further entangled more and more in the material existence. Whereas cleansing away the material dirt by being absorbed fully in thought of Krishna means no more material entanglement, no more material contamination. And Srila Prabhupada gives a nice example here. As a very rich man has no hankerings for small petty things. So also a devotee of Lord Krishna who is guaranteed to pass on to the kingdom of God where life is eternal, fully cognizant and blissful, Satchitananda, naturally has no hankerings for petty material things which are like dolls or shadows of the reality and are without permanent value. Srila Prabhupada is nicely summarizing. What is this material existence consisting of? Petty material things and these material things have no permanent value. Nothing material has permanent value. We discussed this in a previous session.

---
### material vs spiritual, spiritual body, reflection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bjYKu8NSUOs?t=394
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly anything material is nothing but a shadow or a reflection of the spiritual reality."
**Summary:** This segment reinforces the understanding that material things are shadows of spiritual reality, including the material body being a reflection of the spiritual form, and the material world being a reflection of the spiritual world.

So this being reinforced this understanding for our benefit because we have got a background of having developed value for so many different material objects or material things. But now in spiritual life we should understand none of those material things are of any permanent value. They are compared to material things, compared to shadows of reality. That means the real shadow means there is a real object of which the shadow is nothing but a reflection. So the real object is valuable. The shadow is not. Similarly anything material is nothing but a shadow or a reflection of the spiritual reality. So if we have a material body it is a reflection or a shadow of our spiritual form, spiritual body. That spiritual body is covered by material coverings. Similarly the entire material world itself, not only our own form but even the material world itself is nothing but a reflection or a shadow of the spiritual world, Krishna's personal abode. Parastasmatubhavonyo vyakto vyaktatsanatanah.

---
### kingdom of God, detachment, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bjYKu8NSUOs?t=510
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That is the sign of spiritually enriched persons. That's how they lose all attraction for everything material."
**Summary:** This segment describes the kingdom of God as eternal, fully cognizant, and blissful, free from miseries, and emphasizes that spiritually enriched persons lose attraction for material things, preparing them for a change of body.

That eternal abode of the Supreme Lord. It is called the kingdom of God where life is eternal, fully cognizant and blissful. There are no miseries. There is no ignorance. There is no temporary life. There is only eternal life. There is full cognizance, full knowledge. Full knowledge. There is no ignorance at all. And there is no misery. There is no suffering. There is no disease, old age. There is no death. Completely blissful. That place, the kingdom of God is of permanent value, is eternal. So that is where we belong. That should be our destination. So Srila Prabhupada says that is the sign of spiritually enriched persons. Spiritually enriched persons. That's how they lose all attraction for everything material. That's how they lose value for anything material. And in due course of time when a pure devotee is completely prepared. What is the complete preparation? Full absorption in thought of Krishna. Complete preparation of the self. Completely purified of all material taints and completely freed from all material hankerings. No more material contamination. No more material attachments. Completely freed.

---
### death, spiritual body, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bjYKu8NSUOs?t=625
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For a devotee, death is a sudden change of body."
**Summary:** This segment explains that for a devotee, death is a sudden change of body, like lightning and illumination, where the material body is shed and a spiritual body is developed simultaneously by the will of the Supreme Lord.

Then all of a sudden the change of body occurs which is commonly called the change of the body occurs which is commonly called death. For a devotee, death is a sudden change of body. What is this change of body? This change of body for a devotee is that it takes place exactly like lightning and illumination follow simultaneously. In this, when there are rains, you can see there is a flash of lightning. It is simultaneously occurring with the sound of the thunder. So like that a devotee simultaneously changes his material body and develops a spiritual body by the will of the Supreme. Both happen simultaneously. The material body, generally when we quit this material body, we enter another material body. For those who are not fully purified by devotional service, they will enter another material body. But those who are completely purified and are fully ready to go to the kingdom of God, to go back to Godhead, for them simultaneously the material body, the devotee quits the material body and simultaneously develops a fully spiritual body by the will of the Supreme. This happens by the will of the Lord.

---
### spiritualization, devotional service, pure devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bjYKu8NSUOs?t=743
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pure devotee's body, material body, so called material body, is not material at all. It is spiritualized because he completely engages his body, his mind, his words, everything in Krishna's pure devotional service."
**Summary:** This segment describes how a pure devotee's body becomes spiritualized through devotional service, similar to an iron rod becoming red hot in fire, and how upon quitting the body, the devotee goes back to Godhead in a fully spiritual body.

Actually, it is described here, even before death for a pure devotee, there is no material affection. Why? Because a pure devotee's body being completely engaged in pure devotional service becomes spiritualized. A pure devotee's body, material body, so called material body, is not material at all. It is spiritualized because he completely engages his body, his mind, his words, everything in Krishna's pure devotional service. Exactly like an iron rod when placed in fire becomes completely red hot and acts like fire. This is the best example. Easily we can understand. Iron rod placed in blazing fire becomes completely red hot, then it acts like fire. Similarly, a pure devotee's material body, which we see ordinarily with our eyes as a material body, actually is not anymore material because he is fully engaged in pure devotional service. It becomes completely spiritualized. And when he quits that body, then fully spiritual body, in that fully spiritual body, which he develops instantaneously, he goes back to Godhead. He does not take birth again in this world. So I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.6.28 | 23-11-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### spiritual body, karma, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ihCEgrPdLnI?t=172
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That is the wonderful achievement of a devotee who is perfected his devotional service."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the transcendental body awarded to Narada Muni and the cessation of karma for perfected devotees.

The body is described in this verse as befitting an associate of the Personality of Godhead. Everywhere in the scriptures, it is said that we are the same quality as Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Krishna has a completely transcendental form. Even we, in our original position, have a completely spiritual form. Narada Muni is ready for going back to Godhead, so he is awarded a transcendental body befitting an associate of the Supreme Personality of God. Then what happened to the other body? He says, "I quit the earlier body. I quit the body made of five material elements." Of course, everybody quits such a body, but then the advantage for Narada Muni is that all acquired fruitive results of work or karma stopped. No more karma, no more reactions. That is the wonderful achievement of a devotee who has perfected his devotional service.

---
### spiritual form, devotional service, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ihCEgrPdLnI?t=257
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By devotional service, the spiritual body develops exactly as we go on performing pure devotional service."
**Summary:** This segment explains that our spiritual form is currently undeveloped and how devotional service helps it develop, emphasizing the importance of the spiritual master and Krishna's mercy.

In the purport, Shrila Prabhupada says, informed by the Personality of Godhead, that he would be awarded a transcendental body befitting the Lord's association. Narada got his spiritual body as soon as he quit his material body. Immediately, actually our original form is not real itself but today's body as it is said also in the bible. As soon as he quit this material body immediately, actually our original form is a spiritual form. What is the meaning of being awarded? It means that our spiritual form is in an undeveloped state. That is our real identity. I am spirit soul. Spirit soul means spiritual form, but it is undeveloped in our present condition because we are covered by a material body due to our desire to enjoy materially. So that spiritual form is undeveloped. By spiritual practice, by devotional service, the spiritual body develops exactly as we go on performing pure devotional service. We go on purifying our material contamination. That spiritual body develops. We are awakened to spiritual reality as we go on doing pure devotional service, and in proportion to our awakening, we are engaged more and more and more in pure devotional service. This is only by the mercy of the spiritual master and Krishna. Guru Krishna, by their mercy only, it's possible.

---
### transcendental body, sense control, material attraction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ihCEgrPdLnI?t=393
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A transcendental body is free from material affinity, no material attraction at all."
**Summary:** This segment describes the freedom from fear and material attraction that comes with a transcendental body, contrasting it with the constant attraction to sense objects in the material world.

At the time of death for a devotee, there is no fear, there is no uncertainty, there is no anxiety, there are no reactions because the devotee is guaranteed to change his body from material body to spiritual form. Now, Prabhupada further explains this transit body is free from material affinity. It has got no material attraction, just like in this body you always feel some material attraction for material sense objects. The senses, body mean senses, so these material senses are always attracted to some material sense object. Eyes are looking for beautiful form, but it is very Klarboar instrument as well. This Transient ward secure character is beautiful form. Ears are searching for melodious music or some words of praise, honor. Nose is looking for nice fragrance or nice aromas like that, but a transcendental body is free from material affinity, no material attraction, no material attraction at all. So all the senses of the transcendental form are not disturbed, are never attracted to anything material, total freedom from all material attractions. That is the biggest problem in this material world for those who want to control their senses, that senses don't easily come under the control. To avoid sense gratification is very difficult because very naturally it seems like the senses are always attracted to the sense objects, but when we get a transcendental body, it is no longer so.

---
### transcendental qualities, eternity, material modes
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ihCEgrPdLnI?t=527
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By devotional service, we can rise above the gunas gradually as we go on doing devotional service."
**Summary:** This segment explains the three primary transcendental qualities: eternity, freedom from material modes, and freedom from reactions, contrasting them with the temporary and ever-changing nature of the material body influenced by the three gunas.

It is further said here, this transcendental body is invested with three primary transcendental qualities. There are three primary transcendental qualities. What are they? Eternity. That means this form is an eternal form, unlike this material form which is never eternal. It is therefore a fixed duration, that's all. And even during that limited duration of time, this body undergoes changes. They he knows me nathoday hey kahumaram Yoganam Jara it goes through stages of change through changes through changes. It goes through stages of change and youth and old age, and old age is a fundamental misery that we have to suffer invalidity due to old age. So a transcendental body is eternal means there is no material deterioration, there is no material destruction, and freedom from material modes. Not only is this body temporary what we have, but it is also influenced by the gunas, raja guna, sattva guna, tamo guna. The three influences will always be there. Sometimes sattva guna dominates, sometimes raja guna dominates, sometimes tamo guna dominates, accordingly our behavior also changes. So this is material existence. It's horrible. We don't have the freedom to act properly when we are under the influence of the three gunas, but by devotional service, we can rise above the gunas gradually as we go on doing devotional service, and devotional service leads to our freedom completely from the three gunas. Material mode, we come to the spiritual platform, being freed from all the three modes.

---
### fruitive activities, devotional service, transcendental qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ihCEgrPdLnI?t=660
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee's body becomes at once surcharged with transcendental qualities as soon as he is engaged in the devotional service of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment discusses freedom from reactions of fruitive activities in a transcendental body and emphasizes that a devotee's body immediately becomes surcharged with transcendental qualities upon engaging in devotional service.

Three modes means the influence of the three modes, and the third characteristic of the transcendental bodies, freedom from reactions of fruitive activities. Always this body is a store of previous fruitive activities, their reactions. These reactions keep on unfolding as Kala acts on this body. By the action of time, the reactions are unfolding, sometimes good, sometimes not so good. So both happiness and distress, Dvandva, Sukha Dukha, this we have to experience, but a transcendental body has no reactions at all, no reactions, such a wonderful state of existence. This is what Krishna is inviting us. Come back to me means come back to the spiritual life of eternity, bliss, and knowledge. The material body is always afflicted with a lack of these three qualities. Material body is not eternal. Material body is not free from material influence of the gunas. Material body is not free from reactions. Always these three things are there. A devotee's body becomes at once surcharged with transcendental qualities as soon as he is engaged in the devotional service of the Lord. This is a wonderful thing about devotional service, pure devotional service. As soon as we engage, we become surcharged with transcendental qualities immediately.

---
### devotional service, touchstone analogy, causeless mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ihCEgrPdLnI?t=775
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The effect on this material body is that the material body will not be an obstacle for our Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of a touchstone and iron to illustrate how devotional service immediately influences the material body, removing obstacles to Krishna consciousness, and highlights the causeless mercy of the Lord.

Immediately, you don't have to wait for some advancement in Krishna consciousness. You don't have to wait immediately. The body becomes surcharged with transcendental qualities. That's how we are able to do devotional service, and Prabhupada gives an example here. It acts like the magnetic influence of a touchstone upon iron. When there is iron, a magnetic stone or a magnet, it will immediately influence that. Magnetic influence on iron will always be there in the presence of a touchstone or a magnetic stone. Similarly, when we do devotional service, the effect on this material body is that the material body will not be an obstacle for our Krishna consciousness. That's why devotional service is described ahaituki apratihata. This is the nature of pure devotional service. Therefore, change of body means stoppage of the reaction of the three qualitative modes of material nature upon the pure devotee. No more influence, and there are many instances of this in the revealed scripture. Dhruva Maharaj and Prahlad Maharaj and many other devotees were able to see the Personality of God face to face apparently in the same body. This means how could they see with these eyes? We cannot see it spiritual form, transcendental form. How were they able to see? This means that the quality of a devotee's body changes from material to spiritual as soon as he engages in devotional service changes. So only the devotees free from influence of the reactions, influence of the three gunas because of the causeless mercy of the Supreme Lord upon the devotee. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Verse References:** BG 4.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Prabhupada's purport, explaining that the appearance of Narada and other pure devotees is a transcendental pastime, unlike the forced birth of conditioned souls due to karma.

Narada is known as the son of Brahma in this present life, and Lord Krishna is known as son of Vasudeva, the personality of Godhead. And his liberated devotees appear in the material world by the same process. That means a devotee taking birth in this material world, a pure devotee is taking birth in a material world is not like ordinary persons taking birth, just like the Supreme Lord's birth and activities are completely transcendental, Janma Karma Chame Divyam Evam Yo Vetthi Tatvatah. Same way, even pure devotees appearance and activities in this material world are completely transcendental. The process is the same as it is said in the Bhagavad Gita, Janma Karma Chame Divyam. That same thing Prabhupada is telling, the birth and activities of the Lord are all transcendental, completely spiritual. Therefore, according to authorized opinion, the birth of Narada as the son of Brahma is also a transcendental pastime. What does it mean? Narada is not forced to take birth like us; we are forced to take birth by laws of karma only because we have got residual reactions from the previous life or previous lives. Therefore, we take birth and we are forced to take birth by the laws of karma only because we have got these personal tendencies, but when a child is given food he is forced to take birth in a material body. Narada or the Supreme Lord, they also apparently take birth, but they appear in spiritual form; they don't accept a material body. So it is said the birth of Narada the son of Brahma in the present life is actually a transcendental pastime, just like the Lord appears for the sake of pastime. Narada absolutely doesn't need birth of a prábh 환 hanno and shouldn't but it is said between Narada also appears for the sake of transcendental pastimes.

**Summary:** The speaker explains the concept of simultaneous oneness and difference between the Lord and his devotees, emphasizing their shared spiritual nature and transcendence of material energy.

His, that means Narada's appearance and disappearance are practically on the same level as that of the Lord. There is no difference. The Lord and his devotees are therefore simultaneously one and different as spiritual entities. Spiritual entities, both are spiritual entities. But that doesn't mean that pure devotee is God. No. God is God, pure devotee is pure devotee. But they are in the same category. They are having similar qualities. They are completely spiritual beings. They belong to the same category of transcendence. They have nothing to do with any material energy. Energy, material body, material existence. Nothing. So even though they seem to take birth in this material world, they are completely aloof from everything material. How is that possible? Mam chayo avyabhicharena bhakti yogena sevate sagunan samatit ketan brahma bhuyaya kalpate. They are always on the transcendental platform, above the three gunas. Gunatita. Gunatita. So Lord is always gunatita, and his pure devotees who are engaged always in his pure devotional service are also gunatita. But they may appear like they are also conditioned in this material world. They may appear like that, but they are not. So this difference we always have to remember.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.6.31 | 26-11-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Narada Muni, devotional service, transcendental body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7wUSRHAOCp8?t=60
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Since then, by the grace of the Almighty Vishnu, I travel everywhere without restriction, both in the transcendental world and in the three divisions of the material world."
**Summary:** This segment introduces Narada Muni's description of his present life and his ability to travel freely due to his unbroken devotional service to Vishnu.

In this session, we will be discussing the 31st verse of the 6th chapter of the 1st canto of Sriman Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is: "Since then, by the grace of the Almighty Vishnu, I travel everywhere without restriction, both in the transcendental world and in the three divisions of the material world. This is because I am fixed in unbroken devotional service of the Lord." So, this verse pertains to Narad Muni describing to Vyasa Deva about his present life. As was explained in the previous verse, after the night of Brahma, the next day when the creation began, Narad Muni appeared as a son of Brahma, but he appeared in the same transcendental body. He changed from his material body to a transcendental body when he quit his body in his previous life. After the night of Brahma, the next day when the creation began, Narad Muni appeared as a son of Brahma, but he appeared in the same transcendental body. After that, once he is in a transcendental body, there is no more change of body. So, just along with Brahma, he entered into Garbhodakshayi Vishnu's transcendental form. And during the night of Brahma, he remained there and he came out appearing as the son of Brahma. But this appearance is complete, just like Krishna appears as the son of Vasudeva and Devaki. So, after that appearance, he is describing here, since then, by the grace of the Almighty Vishnu, Narad Muni is the pure devotee of the Supreme Lord. So, by the grace of the Supreme Lord, Almighty Vishnu, I travel, that means Narad Muni travels everywhere without restriction. Which all places does he travel? Both in the transcendental world and in the three divisions of the material world. Why? How is this possible? This is because I am fixed in unbroken devotional service to the Lord. How is it possible? Because Narad Muni is fixed up completely in unbroken devotional service of the Lord. That is described in the Sanskrit as unbroken vow, unbroken vow of devotional service.

---
### material world, spiritual world, devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7wUSRHAOCp8?t=257
**Verse References:** BG 9.4
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Sri Narada Muni could enter all these planets in both the material and spiritual spheres without restriction. This is the position of a pure devotee who is engaged in unbroken devotional service of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** This segment explains the divisions of the material and spiritual worlds, the nature of living entities in each, and how Narada Muni can travel freely due to his pure devotion.

In the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, as stated in the Bhagavad Gita, there are three divisions of the material spheres. This entire material universe, there are three divisions: Urdhva Loka, the upper planetary systems; Madhya Loka, the middle planetary systems. We are in the middle planetary system on the earth planet. And downward, below the earth, that is Adho Loka. Now, beyond the Urdhva Loka, beyond the upper planetary systems of which the Brahma Loka is the topmost in this universe, there are the material coverings of the universe. This whole universe is like a shell, Brahma Anda. It's called Brahma Anda, egg-shaped, Anda, therefore. And outside this shell, there are seven layers of coverings. And above this universe with its coverings, outside that, is the spiritual sky. And the spiritual sky is described as unlimited in expansion. Material sky is limited within the universal shell, whereas the spiritual sky is spread throughout, unlimited expansion, and contains unlimited self-illuminated Vaikuntha planets. Now, Vaikuntha planets are spiritual planets, unlike the material planets within the material universe. And these Vaikuntha planets are inhabited by God himself, along with his associates, who are all eternally liberated living entities. There are living entities in this material world. There are living entities in the spiritual world. What's the difference? Living entities in the material world are conditioned souls, bound up by their own reactions of their material actions, whereas in the spiritual world, everyone is a devotee, everyone is engaged in devotional service, and everyone is liberated eternally. Sri Narada Muni could enter all these planets in both the material and spiritual spheres without restriction. This is the position of a pure devotee who is engaged in unbroken devotional service of the Supreme Lord. He is free to travel everywhere. Why does he travel? Because he is engaged always in preaching Krishna consciousness, Krishna Bhakti. That is the reason he travels. Such a travelling devotee is called Parivrajaka Acharya. Acharya means the authority in devotional service, in spiritual line. And Parivrajaka means one who constantly travels, Parivrajaka Acharya. So, Narada Muni is free to personally travel in all the material planets as well as in the spiritual planets also, without any restriction. Srila Prabhupada says here, as much as the Almighty Lord is free to move personally in any part of His creation.

---

**Key Quote:** "Whereas spiritual activity, transcendental activity, devotional service is without material inebriety, without material defect, without any material contamination, just like when we chant Hare Krishna, that sound of the holy name."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the transcendental nature of Narada Muni's Veena and contrasts material activities with devotional service, emphasizing the purity of chanting Hare Krishna.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 32nd verse of the 6th chapter of the first canto of Shreemad Bhagavatam. Those who want to enjoy independent of Krishna, those who want to imitate Krishna, those who are envious of Krishna, in several ways it's described in different places, it's for the materialists. So it is described the Veena which was handed over by Krishna to Narada and carried always, carried by Narada, that is a transcendental instrument. Why is it a transcendental instrument? Because it is identical with Lord Sri Krishna. And Narada being in the same spiritual category, some vibrated by this instrument which Narada Muni carries, this Veena cannot be material because the Veena is itself transcendent, and therefore the glories and His pastimes. In this material world, whatever work we do ordinarily has got this material inebriety or material contamination or material defect, if I may use that word, it is a defect. So that material defect is the cause of bondage. It results in reactions, whereas spiritual activity, transcendental activity, devotional service is without material inebriety, without material defect, without any material contamination, just like when we chant Hare Krishna, that sound of the holy name.

---
### glorification of the Lord, spiritual platform, pure devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zNDKDvrKtYg?t=637
**Verse References:** BG 9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is possible to remain on the spiritual platform by constantly singing the glories of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment explains how self-realized individuals can remain on the spiritual platform by constantly singing the glories of the Lord, as described in the Bhagavad-gita, and how devotees worship Krishna with determination and humility.

Also properly use the sound meters namely saray gama etc in the service of the Lord by constantly singing the glories of the Lord as confirmed in the Bhagavad-gita. So anybody who is self-realized, if they want to remain on the spiritual platform, on the platform of spirit, spiritual platform, then Bhagavad-gita says it is possible to remain on the spiritual platform by constantly singing the glories of the Lord. In the Bhagavad-gita, Krishna explains in the ninth chapter how do devotees worship Krishna? They worship Krishna. They are always constantly singing the glories of the Lord, always without any break, without any interruption, 24 hours a day, and in that way they are engaged in such glorification of the Supreme Lord with determination, very firm determination, it's not shakeable. They always bow down with all humility, with devotion, they bow down to the Lord. They always bow down to the Lord and in this way they are always engaged in worshipping the Lord. This is a description of someone who is called a Mahatma. Mahatma means a pure devotee according to the Bhagavad-gita that is also described by a verse in the Gita: mahathmanastumam partha daivin prakritimahatmanastumam partha daivin prakritimahatmanastumahatmahatma mahathmanastumam partha daivin prakritimahat mahatma saan statu ka avachar.

**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Hearing, Krishna, Heart

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse to be discussed and explains that Krishna appears in the heart of the devotee who hears his glories.

In this session, we will be discussing the 33rd verse of the 6th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is: The Supreme Lord Shri Krishna, whose glories and activities are pleasing to hear, at once appears on the seat of my heart as if called for. As soon as I begin to hear, I will be able to hear. The Supreme Lord appears on the seat of his heart at once, as soon as he sings the glories, as if Narad Muni is calling out for the Lord. Like that, the Lord appears immediately. You called me? Yes, I am here.

---
### Absolute Truth, Relative Truth, Change
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lVBk_IkI5HY?t=184
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Absolute Truth is Truth which is Truth in all phases of time: past, present, and future."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between Absolute Truth and Relative Truth, explaining that Absolute Truth is unchanging, while Relative Truth is subject to change with time, using the example of a person residing in a house.

The very opening verse, it is said by Vyasadeva, the author of the Bhagavatam, that I meditate upon Shri Krishna because he is the Absolute Truth. Satyam is truth. Param there indicates Absolute Truth. So what is this Absolute Truth? Absolute Truth is Truth which is Truth in all phases of time: past, present, and future. The Truth is always the Truth. It doesn't change. Relative Truth is Truth which is not true in all phases of time. Every Truth in the material world of some material object or material activity is a Relative Truth. To give an example, if I say such and such a gentleman is residing in a particular apartment or house, now this statement is a Relative Truth. It is truth because what happens with time, in course of time, that gentleman who's staying in a particular house, when he passes away, when he changes body, when he dies, then that statement, such and such a gentleman residing in the house, is no longer true. Then we are to say he was residing, and when he died, he is no longer residing in the house, or even when he shifts from that residence due to whatever reason to another residence, then that statement, such a gentleman is residing in this house, is no longer true. So like this, every statement we make about a material phenomenon in this world is a relative truth. It is relative to time, whereas the absolute personality of Godhead Krishna is absolute truth. He is always the Lord. He doesn't change his body. He doesn't change his position, his form, his name, his qualities. His forms don't change. You look at our own form, material body, it changes. Anything material is constantly undergoing change. That's the nature of material existence, isn't it?

---
### Spiritual Existence, Rasa, Dynamic
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lVBk_IkI5HY?t=390
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual existence is full of Rasa, spirit is dynamic unlike matter, matter is static, spirit is dynamic."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts spiritual existence with material existence, stating that spiritual existence is unchanging, full of Rasa, and dynamic, while material existence is constantly changing and lacks substance.

Spiritual existence is not changing, unchanging reality. So unchanging reality should not be mistaken to be something which is uninteresting. In the material world, because inherently in anything material, there is no Rasa. Therefore, everything material is actually without any substance. It is without substance, is without life, really speaking, without any Vasu, whereas spiritual existence is full of Rasa. How is it interesting if it is unchanging? It is interesting because spiritual existence is full of Rasa. Even though unchanging, how is it interesting? It is spirit is dynamic, unlike matter. Matter is static, spirit is dynamic. So spirit is always alive, full of life. Spirit is actually full of life, and that life never expires. Just like we say, we understand from the Bhagavad Gita, that this real person inside this body, the spirit soul, does not die, is always living, but changes body. The body is something which is material, is subject to deterioration, and one day we have to quit this body when it becomes old and useless. So spirit is dynamic, spirit is full of life, spirit is actually full of Rasa. That is because the Supreme Lord, personality of Godhead Krishna, the absolute personality of Godhead, he is the ultimate reality, ultimate person, the ultimate source from him everything is emanating. So he is full of Rasa, he is full of life, he is full of love, he is full of activities.

---
### Devotional Service, Hearing, Chanting, Remembering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lVBk_IkI5HY?t=562
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as a pure devotee engages himself in the pure devotional service of hearing, chanting, and remembering the name, fame, and activities of the Lord, a pure devotee is engaged always in devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that a pure devotee engaged in devotional service, particularly hearing, chanting, and remembering the Lord's name, fame, and activities, experiences the Lord's presence.

All of them are actually absolute. All of them are absolute. They are non-different from each other. So as soon as a pure devotee engages himself in the pure devotional service of hearing, chanting, and remembering the name, fame, and activities of the Lord, a pure devotee is engaged always in devotional service. What does devotional service consist of? Nine different kinds of activities: shravanam kirtanam smaranam pada sevanam archanam vandanam dasyam sakhyam atmanivedanam, of which three are particularly mentioned here, the first three: hearing, chanting, and remembering, shravanam kirtanam smaranam. About what? Hearing about what, or chanting or remembering what? The name, fame, and activities of the Lord.

---
### Hearing, Spiritual Television, Transcendental Eyes
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lVBk_IkI5HY?t=622
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "At once the Supreme Lord becomes visible to the transcendental eyes of the pure devotee."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the process of hearing about Vishnu and how the Lord becomes visible to the devotee's transcendental eyes, using the analogy of a spiritual television.

That is the recommendation in the Bhagavata: shravanaam kirtanam Vishno smaranam pada sevanam about Vishnu, about the Supreme Lord. We have to hear about his names, his qualities, his pastimes, his forms, his paraphernalia, his Abode, his devotees, his relationships, his creation, his maintenance, destruction, everything about him. When we hear that, or we chant about it, or we remember them, at once the Supreme Lord becomes visible to the transcendental eyes of the pure devotee. This ordinary eyes we cannot see anything spiritual. How do the pure devotees see? They with transcendental eyes. And how do they see with transcendental eyes? That is described here by reflecting himself on the mirror of the heart of the spiritual by spiritual television. Prabhupada uses a term called spiritual television. Just like you have got experience of a material television, you have a television at home. Now the program that is broadcast by a station, you tune in or you connect to that station through your whatever the set of box or whatever, then the program being broadcast appears on your television screen and you are able to see that program. That program is going on somewhere, either it is recorded and the recording is broadcast, or live it is going on. Let's say a cricket match is going on in Australia and you are sitting in India, you can watch that cricket match live through this arrangement of television, is it not?

---
### Spiritual Television, Devotional Service, Presence of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lVBk_IkI5HY?t=751
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When a devotee sings the Lord's name or hears about the Lord's glories or remembers the Lord's glorification which he has heard before or recited before, immediately the Lord appears in his heart like a spiritual television is switched on."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the analogy of spiritual television, explaining that the Lord appears in the heart of a devotee engaged in devotional service, allowing them to experience His presence constantly.

Same way, there is something called a spiritual television. What is the spiritual television? That when a devotee sings the Lord's name or hears about the Lord's glories or remembers the Lord's glorification which he has heard before or recited before, immediately the Lord appears in his heart like a spiritual television is switched on. He reflects himself on the mirror of the heart by spiritual television, and this is possible for anybody who is engaged in pure devotional service. Seeing the Lord within the heart reflected by the spiritual television is possible for anyone who engages in pure devotional service of hearing or chanting or remembering. Therefore, a pure devotee who is related with the Lord in loving transcendental service can experience the presence of the Lord at every moment. What is the nature of pure devotees? They're always engaged in devotional service of the Supreme Lord. Therefore, they are always experiencing the presence of the Lord every moment because as soon as they sing or hear or do devotional service, the Lord is appearing on their heart.

---
### Narada Muni, Benefit of Others, Hearing

There are also other ways of receiving the glory of the Lord, just by hearing the glory of the Lord, simply by hearing the glories of the Lord from pure devotees, even those who are not spiritually advanced.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.6.34 | 29-11-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### anxiety, chanting, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/chsNODAkKKw?t=59
**Verse References:** SB 1.6.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anybody who is full of anxieties due to this material existence, they can very easily cross over this ocean of nescience by taking help of the constant chanting of the transcendental activities of Lord Krishna."
**Summary:** Narada Muni instructs Vyasa Deva that chanting the transcendental activities of Lord Krishna is the most suitable boat to cross the ocean of nescience.

In this session, we will be discussing the 34th verse of the 6th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, it is personally experienced by me that those who are always full of cares and anxieties due to desiring contact of the senses with their objects, can cross the ocean of nescience on a most suitable boat, the constant chanting of the transcendental activities of the Personality of Godhead. These are words spoken by Narad Muni. He is instructing Vyasa Deva that by his personal experience, he has found out that anybody who is full of anxieties due to this material existence, they can very easily cross over this ocean of nescience, ocean of ignorance, ocean of material existence, by taking help of the constant chanting of the transcendental activities of Lord Krishna.

---
### senses, materialism, dissatisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/chsNODAkKKw?t=159
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "None of these activities which are under the influence of the illusory energy can actually give us satisfaction."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that a living being cannot remain silent and is always engaged in some activity, but materialistic men think and discuss subjects that satisfy their senses, which ultimately leads to dissatisfaction.

So, Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, how does it work? The symptom of a living being is that he cannot remain silent even for some time. He can't find food. He can't even consume food. There is this past constantly silent. There is this present sensation of absence for just some night. Nothing will happen. Continuously nothing will happen. He is unable to became empty. Otherwise, the body is by itself dead matter. What appears like a living body is not a living body, it is a dead body animated by the living spirit soul, eternally living spirit soul. So Srila Prabhupada says a living being cannot remain silent at any time, so he must be doing something, or he must be thinking of something, or talking about something. So some activity is always there. If somebody is living, generally the materialistic men think and discuss about subjects which satisfy their senses. So normally people are in ignorance about who they are, and therefore they generally talk or discuss about subjects like what is meant for satisfying their senses, because such things are exercised under the influence of the external illusory energy. Such sensual activities do not actually give them any satisfaction, however it may be promising or pleasing for the senses. None of these activities which are under the influence of the illusory energy can actually give us satisfaction. None of them can give us satisfaction.

---
### maya, sense gratification, anxiety
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/chsNODAkKKw?t=312
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "People under the influence of the illusory energy, they are always accepting something which is not pleasurable as pleasurable."
**Summary:** Material activities cannot give satisfaction, but instead lead to anxiety, which is maya. Narada Muni says that satisfaction for frustrated beings engaged in sense gratification is to chant the activities of the Lord.

On the contrary, they become full with cares and anxieties. Then somebody tries to squeeze out some pleasure, or happiness, or some satisfaction out of material or sensual activities, then they are not able to get the satisfaction, at the same time they become filled with anxiety. This is called maya, or what is not. Maya means not this, that which cannot give them satisfaction is accepted as an object for satisfaction. People under the influence of the illusory energy, they are always accepting something which is not pleasurable as pleasurable. So Narada Muni, by his personal experience, he says that satisfaction for such frustrated beings engaged in sense gratification is to chant always the activities of Lord. What is sense gratification? The senses are seeking some objects. What is sense gratification? No. For the eyes, a beautiful form; for the ears, some melodious sound; for the nose, some fragrant smell; for the tongue, something very tasty to eat; for the sense of touch, something which is very soft, something which is very smooth, something which is very warm, etc. Like that, all the senses are ever seeking pleasure through this sense contact, but the result of the sense contact is not really pleasure. If at all there is some pleasure, some momentary pleasure may be there by such contact, but after that they are full of anxiety. Every action results in a reaction.

---
### entanglement, frustration, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/chsNODAkKKw?t=468
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The senses cannot actually give any satisfaction; on the other hand, they become entangled."
**Summary:** Indulging in satisfying the senses leads to anxiety and entanglement, which means complicated miseries. Chanting the activities of the Lord brings a different experience.

So they indulge in satisfying the senses, then they become very much filled with anxiety because the senses cannot actually give any satisfaction; on the other hand, they become entangled, and therefore they are always frustrated. Entanglement means there is only complicated miseries. Now what happens if somebody starts chanting the activities of the Lord? Then they experience something inside the heart.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.6.35 | 30-11-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### yoga, sense control, devotional service, soul satisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VYxhbcV1ixE?t=59
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Narada Muni says that devotional service to the Lord is more effective and practical than the system of artificially controlling the senses."
**Summary:** Narada Muni explains that while yoga can control the senses, it doesn't provide soul satisfaction, which comes from devotional service to the Personality of Godhead, making bhakti a more effective path.

In this session, we will be discussing the 35th verse of the 6th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. We are continuing the conversation between Narada and Vyasa Deva. Towards the end of the chapter, a few more verses and the chapter will conclude. The meaning of this verse is: It is true that by practicing restraint of the senses by the yoga system, one can get relief from the disturbances of desire and lust, but this is not sufficient to give satisfaction to the soul, for this satisfaction is derived from devotional service to the Personality of Godhead. So Srila Narada Muni is clearly explaining that the yoga system is helpful to actually control the senses, but it is not effective in giving satisfaction to the soul. Ultimately, we are always seeking satisfaction of the soul, though we do not know it, especially those in ignorance of the spiritual nature of the self. They are unaware that the satisfaction of the senses, mind, or intelligence is not satisfaction of the soul. I, the real person, am different from the body or the senses. I am different from the mind. I am different from my intelligence. I am spirit, so this spirit requires spiritual satisfaction, soul satisfaction. So that is explained very nicely by Srila Prabhupada in the purport. Yoga aims at controlling the senses. This is one of the main purposes of practicing yoga, to control the senses. Generally, our senses are totally uncontrolled, with the idea that whatever the senses demand, we are simply busy satisfying those demands, but that is not the way of spiritual life. So in yoga practice, there is, by practice of the mystic process of bodily exercise, in sitting, thinking, feeling, willing, concentrating, facing, and setting away, about that the scriptures are telling, the senses are considered like venomous serpents, and the yoga system is just to control them. Narada Muni is recommending another method for controlling the senses: the method of transcendental loving service of Mukunda, the Personality of Godhead, the system of Bhakti, the process of Bhakti. It is different than the Ashtanga Yoga process. By his experience, Narada Muni says that devotional service to the Lord is more effective and practical than the system of artificially controlling the senses.

---
### Ashtanga Yoga, bhakti, sense engagement, spiritual activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VYxhbcV1ixE?t=338
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhakti is positive engagement of course in a specific type of activity, spiritual activity."
**Summary:** Ashtanga Yoga is an artificial method of denying sense gratification, whereas devotional service (Bhakti) involves transcendentally engaging the senses in spiritual activities, offering a positive and fulfilling alternative.

The method of Ashtanga Yoga is an artificial method. That means you have to force yourself against your natural way of living, whereby you engage your senses in sense satisfaction, in some activity aimed at either satisfying the senses or the mind or the intelligence. So Ashtanga Yoga is denying the sense satisfaction, denying mind satisfaction, denying intellectual satisfaction, all this by forcibly restraining the senses, holding back the senses from their sense objects, holding back the mind, not indulging the mind or the senses or the intelligence. Whereas what happens in devotional service? In the service of the Lord Mukunda, Mukunda means another name for Krishna, the senses are transcendentally engaged. This is the big difference. It is not stopping the senses, it is engaging the senses, but in a different way. What is that different way? Spiritually engaging them, transcendentally engaging them. That means we do spiritual activities in Krishna Consciousness. That is the system of Krishna Consciousness, the system of Bhakti. We engage the senses in spiritual activities. Just like you may like to hear a very melodious Kirtan, Hare Krishna Kirtan, that is transcendentally engaging your ears, and your mind, and even your intelligence, or you may relish tasting nice Krishna Prasadam. That is engagement for the tongue, the mind, the intelligence, everything. So it is positive engagement. Bhakti is positive engagement of course in a specific type of activity, spiritual activity, and there are varieties, there is variety, ample variety of spiritual activities. In this way, if the senses are transcendentally engaged while serving Lord Mukunda, Lord Krishna, there is no chance of the senses being engaged in sense gratification, no chance. And the important point to notice, the senses want some engagement. They cannot be kept for long without any activity. That is artificial. To check them artificially is no check at all, because as soon as there is some opportunity for enjoyment, the serpent like senses will certainly take advantage of it.

---
### sense control, examples, devotional service, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VYxhbcV1ixE?t=556
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee who is engaged in devotional service cannot be allured by Maya because this is a superior engagement."
**Summary:** The lecture uses the examples of Vishwamitra Muni and Haridas Thakur to illustrate that artificial sense control is insufficient, while devotional service provides a superior engagement that protects one from Maya's allurements.

Prabhupada gives a nice example. There are many such instances in history, and they have been recorded in the Puranas, in the scriptures, just like the example is given of Vishwamitra Muni. He was a great king. He renounced his kingdom, and all the wealth and the comforts, royal comforts, and he went away to a forest, a secluded place, and he was performing tapasya, but while practicing tapasya, Vishwamitra Muni became a victim to the beauty of Menaka. Whenever any great personality is very determined to perform tapasya, Indra, the king of heaven, he becomes very worried that this person who is doing tapasya may overtake me and replace me as the king of heaven. So Indra sends Apsaras to disturb the tapasya, and that's what exactly happened when Vishwamitra Muni was sitting in the forest and performing meditation. Indra sent one Apsara by name Menaka, and Menaka came, and she allured Vishwamitra Muni, and Vishwamitra Muni became completely distracted from his tapasya. But the other example that is also given, a devotee of Lord Chaitanya, Thakur Haridas, he was allured at midnight by the well dressed Maya. Maya Devi herself came to allure Haridas Thakur when he was sitting and chanting Hare Krishna, still she could not induce him into her trap. She could not do that. In fact, Maya Devi surrendered to Haridas Thakur and begged for the holy name from him. So a devotee who is engaged in devotional service cannot be allured by Maya because this is a superior engagement. This is explained in the Bhagavad Gita, param drishtva nivartate. If you want to stop the senses from being engaged in mundane sense activities, then you should engage the senses in devotional service, and such engagement is superior.

---
### devotional service, yoga, jnana, karma, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VYxhbcV1ixE?t=736
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pure devotional service is actually the foremost procedure for self realization."
**Summary:** Without devotional service, yoga and philosophical speculation cannot be successful. Pure devotional service, without any mixture of karma, jnana, or tapasya, is the foremost procedure for self-realization.

The whole idea is that without devotional service of the Lord, neither the yoga system nor philosophical speculation can ever become successful. Always yoga means karma karma yoga, jnana jnana yoga, tapasya ashtanga yoga. They are different. Whenever the word yoga is suffixed to karma jnana or tapasya, then it means that you have to mix with devotional service. Now pure devotional service of the Lord is without any tinge of or without any mixture of fruity work, mystic yoga, or speculative philosophy, without any mixture of karma jnana or tapasya, that without any mixture of that, pure devotional service is actually the foremost procedure for self realization. This will be explained in detail in the later portions of the Bhagavatam. Such pure devotional services transcendental in nature, and the systems of yoga and jnana are subordinate to such a process. Yoga and jnana are dependent on mixture of bhakti, whereas pure bhakti is independent of yoga jnana karma, everything else. When the transcendental devotional service is mixed with a subordinate process, it is no longer transcendental, it is called mixed devotional service. So all this will be explained by Sri Vyasadeva in the text later on, but for now Narada Muni is telling bhakti can give complete satisfaction for the soul. Yoga can help in controlling the senses, but it cannot give satisfaction for the soul. Therefore he is recommending bhakti over the process of yoga or even the process of jnana or even karma. So bhakti, pure devotional service, is the topmost.

---
### devotion, yoga, conclusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VYxhbcV1ixE?t=891
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The topmost yogi is the devotee."
**Summary:** The conclusion is that pure devotional service is the topmost path, as confirmed by Lord Krishna, who states that the topmost yogi is the devotee.

This is the conclusion by Lord Sri Krishna in the 6th chapter: yogi naam api sarvesha madhgate nantaratmana shraddhavan bhajate yo ma same yukta tamo matah, yukta tama, the topmost yogi is the devotee. I will stop here. Thank you.

**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Srimad Bhagavatam, Narada Muni, Vyasa Deva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VCdbtugVKJI?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 1.6.36
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All this will be conducive for your personal satisfaction also."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that it is Narada Muni's response to Vyasa Deva's question about the cause of his dissatisfaction.

Om Agyanati Mirandasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chakshurunmilitam Jena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Namo Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nityananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shiva Sadhi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Narayanam Namaskritya Naramchaiva Narottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudireet. In this session, we'll be discussing from the 36th verse of the 6th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam: Sarvam Tadidam Aakyatam Yad Prishtoham Tvayanagha Janma Karma Rahasyamme Bhavata Shchatma Toshanam. The meaning of this verse is O Vyasa Deva, you are freed from all sins. Thus I have explained my birth and activities for self-realization as you asked. All this will be conducive for your personal satisfaction also. This is almost the concluding portion of this chapter.

---
### dissatisfaction, Vedic literature, Krishna glorification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VCdbtugVKJI?t=94
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You have not compiled one particular literature which exclusively glorifies Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhood."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Vyasa Deva's dissatisfaction after compiling Vedic literature and Narada Muni's arrival and instruction to compile Srimad Bhagavatam, which exclusively glorifies Krishna.

This was spoken by Narada in response to a question by Vyasa Deva. Vyasa Deva, after compiling the varieties of Vedic literature, he was somehow feeling dissatisfaction in his heart that he had not done enough for the welfare of the people. So, just at the time when he was contemplating what was the cause of his dissatisfaction, that's exactly when Narada Muni arrived in his ashram, in Vyasa's ashram. So, Vyasa received Narada very respectfully and inquired from him, what is the cause of my dissatisfaction? So, then Narada Muni pointed out, even though you have compiled so many varieties of Vedic literature, you have not compiled one particular literature which exclusively glorifies Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhood. And Vyasa Deva was instructed by Narada to compile that particular literature. So, Narada gave instructions to Vyasa Deva how to go about compiling the Srimad Bhagavatam, which is the exclusive literature glorifying Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Now not only did Vyasa get instructions about compiling Bhagavatam but he also inquired from Narada about his own self-realization and that is what Narada has explained. How did he become self-realized? How did he become the great devotee Narada? So the whole history has been described. I have explained my birth and activities for self-realization as you asked. So whatever Vyasa Dev asked, Narada has described. Now Vyasa Dev is being told by Narada, all this will be conducive for your personal satisfaction also. So Vyasa Dev was looking for a clue. What is the cause for his dissatisfaction? That's clearly answered by Narada.

---
### devotional service, bhakti, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VCdbtugVKJI?t=234
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That finally one is able to attain Krishna prema, the love of God."
**Summary:** The speaker shares Prabhupada's summary of Narada's instructions to Vyasa Deva, outlining the process of devotional activities from initial faith to Krishna prema.

So in the purpose of this video, I would like to share with you the quote. Prabhupada is summarizing Narada's instructions to Vyasa Deva. The process of devotional activities from the beginning to the stage of transcendence is all duly explained to satisfy the inquiries of Vyasa Deva. If you recollect, it was described the process of Bhakti begins with Shraddha, initial faith. And that results in anarthanevrtti, purification of the heart of unwanted desires. And then that leads to nishta, firm faith. And ruchi, taste for transcendental activities. And that leads to asakti, attachment to spiritual activities. And that will lead to bhava, ecstasy in performing devotional service. And that finally one is able to attain Krishna prema, the love of God. That is the topmost perfection of bhakti. So all these have been explained in brief, of course, by Narada to Vyasa.

---
### transcendental association, purification, Krishna katha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VCdbtugVKJI?t=339
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The boy became enlightened in their association by hearing them after he partook remnants of uh prasadam left behind by them so it had a very big transformation of his heart the small boy"
**Summary:** The speaker explains how Narada's devotional service began in his previous life as the son of a maidservant, through association with sages and partaking of their remnants.

So Prabhupada is further explained. Narada has explained how the seeds of devotional service were sown by the transcendental association. And how they gradually developed by hearing the sages. The bhaktivedantas who happened to be passing by that place where Narada in his previous life as the son of a maidservant was staying with his mother. And the sages happened to stop by in that spot. And Narada's mother was rendering some menial service to the sages. And Narada as a small boy joined his mother. And one day he took some remnants of some food left behind by the sages, the bhaktivedantas. And that resulted in his purification and he became attracted to the subject matter of Krishna katha which they were discussing. From those self-realized sages was detachment from worldliness so much so that even a small boy could receive the death news of his mother who was his only caretaker as the blessings of God. The boy became enlightened in their association by hearing them after he partook remnants of uh prasadam left behind by them so it had a very big transformation of his heart the small boy Narada in his previous life as a small boy and at once he took the opportunity to search out the Lord.

We have to re-establish our lost relationship with Krishna that is the whole practice of bhakti. That is what bhakti is meant for so this boy went for searching the Lord exactly as he had been instructed by those sages the bhaktivedantas a sincere urge for having an interview with the Lord was also granted to him although it is not possible for anyone to see the Lord with mundane eyes Krishna gave him brief darshan and afterwards Narada tried to have the darshan again and he could not but the Lord spoke to Narada to encourage him to continue his practice of meditation on the Supreme Lord in the heart as taught by the bhaktivedantas. So Narada Muni also explained to Vyasa Deva how by execution of pure transcendental service one can get rid of the fruitive action of accumulated work we have been doing so many material activities in contact with this material world contact with the three modes and that has resulted in so many reactions and those reactions result in a repetition of birth and death in this world we cannot get out of material existence but pure devotional service helps us to completely clear away all these reactions.

### Vedas, Srimad Bhagavatam, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VCdbtugVKJI?t=762
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Srimad Bhagavatam is the mature fruit of all Vedic tree of literature and nothing is directly explained in the original Vedas or the Upanishads but everything is directly explained in the Srimad Bhagavatam."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Prabhupada, explaining that the results of devotional life are hardly delineated in the Vedas, which focus on material gain, while Srimad Bhagavatam directly explains these truths and is most important in Kali Yuga.

Now Prabhupada Sri La Prabhupada says uh these uh results of devotional life are hardly delineated even the original texts of the vedas means it's very difficult to find this in the original vedas why why is it difficult to find Sri La Prabhupada explains in the vedas and upanishads there are only indirect hints to all this only indirect hints are there direct clear specific explanation is not there in the original vedas and the upanishads no why because the vedas are dealing with the actions and reactions to the three modes of material nature in this material world and because most people are so much interested in material gain through fruitive activity the vedas are suggesting how they can do pious activities and get their desires satisfied their material desires but we are not meant for material existence neither we can really achieve a soul's satisfaction but we have the right to achieve it without conflict. So therefore the conclusion is Srimad Bhagavatam is the mature fruit of all Vedic tree of literature and nothing is directly explained in the original Vedas or the Upanishads but everything is directly explained in the Srimad Bhagavatam. Therefore Bhagavatam is the most important Vedic literature for us especially in this age of Kali as explained by Narada to Vyasa and as recorded by Vyasa in great detail. So I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
