Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudireet. In this session we will be discussing the last two verses of the 6th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The last two verses are the 37th and the 38th. So let's begin with the 37th verse. Sūtavvāca evam sambhāśya bhagavān nārado vāsavīśutam āmantryavīnām ranayan yayau yadṛcchiko muniḥ. The meaning of this verse is Sūt Gosvāmī said. Remember, Sūt Gosvāmī is narrating all this to the sages of Naimisharanya based on their inquiry. So Sūt Goswāmī is telling the sages thus addressing Vyasa Deva, Srila Narada Muni took leave of him and vibrating on his Veena instrument, he left to wander at his free will.

---
### freedom, material bondage, transcendental service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GXDR8FdLemw?t=111
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every living being is anxious for full freedom because that is his transcendental nature."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Narada Muni's freedom, contrasting it with the limitations of material existence and emphasizing that true freedom is obtained through transcendental service to the Lord.

So Narada Muni is being glorified that he instructed Vyasa Deva exactly to the point and after finishing his instructions after completing his instructions he left to wander at his free will. He is a liberated soul and he simply is directed within his heart by the Supreme Lord directly. So we will see and here in the Mark Bhagavatam Narada Muni appearing in so many different situations in different places only for the sake of preaching about Bhakti, only for one purpose glorifying the Lord and thereby broadcasting these glories among different people, different persons. So in the purport Srila Prabhupada says something about Narada's freedom to travel at will wherever he wants. Every living being is anxious for full freedom because that is his transcendental nature. What is our nature as spirit soul? We have full freedom as spirit soul unlike this body. What is the nature of this body, material body? It is full of limitations, full of limitations. Every body has got a very specific characteristic just like there are creatures which can fly, creatures which can walk only, creatures which can only live in water, creatures which can only crawl on the ground, creatures which can only run on four legs, run or walk on four legs like that. Every body has got a limitation and every body is a material body subject to the influence of the three gunas, subject to threefold miseries, subject to fourfold fundamental problems and all these material bodies are temporary. So we should seriously consider what is being said here. This freedom is obtained only through the transcendental service of the Lord and the only way to obtain this freedom is only through the transcendental service of the Lord.

---
### illusion, conditioned soul, ignorance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GXDR8FdLemw?t=290
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "What people don't realize is that we are entitled to eternal life, we don't have to die, we don't have to take birth, we don't have to become old, we don't have to get diseased."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how people are illusioned into thinking they are free, while they are actually bound by the laws of nature, and emphasizes the importance of understanding our real spiritual nature.

This freedom from material bondage, freedom from the limitations of this body is obtained only one way. What is that? Through transcendental service of the Supreme Lord Krishna. Illusione by the external energy everyone thinks that is through themselves free. Common people, ordinary people generally think I'm free but actually he is bound up by the laws of nature. What people don't realize is that we are entitled to eternal life, we don't have to die, we don't have to take birth, we don't have to become old, we don't have to get diseased. Are people aware of this? Most people are ignorant about this. That is also the bad system of education that we have. People are not being taught the essential fundamentals of our real nature for real existence, spiritual existence.

---
### Bhagavad Gita, freedom, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GXDR8FdLemw?t=377
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A full-fledged free soul like Narada always engaged in the work of God, always engaged in chanting the Lord's glory is free to move not only on earth but also in any part of the universe as well as in any part of the spiritual sky."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights the Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam as sources of enlightenment, contrasting the conditioned soul with a free soul like Narada Muni, who is free to travel anywhere due to his devotion.

So Bhagavad-gita is the ultimate wisdom of the world. Bhagavatam is enlightening us. Srila Prabhupada is enlightening us that only under illusion we may think that I am free but actually we are not free. A conditioned soul, who is a conditioned soul? One who is subject to the influence of this material nature consisting of three gunas because of the material body, because of our desire to enjoy materially. So a conditioned soul cannot freely move from one place to another even on this earth and what to speak of one planet to another. On earth do we have freedom to just go wherever we want freely? No, we are bound by so many limitations. But a full-fledged free soul like Narada always engaged in the work of God, always engaged in chanting the Lord's glory is free to move not only on earth but also in any part of the universe as well as in any part of the spiritual sky. Just take note of the extent of freedom Narada Muni has. Yogis who have got mystic power can travel within this universe but they cannot penetrate the cover of the earth and they cannot penetrate the earth and they cannot penetrate the earth and they cannot penetrate the earth and they cannot of the universe by their mystic power, not possible. Devotees like Narada who are always engaged in glorification of the Supreme Lord they are free to travel anywhere including the spiritual world penetrating the seven layers of coverings of this universe. So that is the specific freedom that is there for pure devotees who are always engaged in broadcasting the glories of the supreme god. We can just imagine the extent and unlimitedness of his freedom, whose freedom? Narada Muni's freedom. To what extent is he really experiencing freedom which is as good as that of the supreme lord. The supreme lord is free to travel anywhere and everywhere in the whole creation in the material world or the spiritual world. Similarly Narada is also free to travel though Narada is not God. Nobody becomes equal to God but they are bestowed upon bestowed with the freedom to travel at will by the supreme lord. There is no reason or obligation for his traveling and no one can stop him from his free movement.

---
### devotional service, self-satisfaction, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GXDR8FdLemw?t=558
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pure devotee never wants anything, of course a pure devotee always prays to Krishna, may I always be engaged in your service."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Narada Muni is self-satisfied through devotional service and has no personal desires, contrasting this with the motivations of ordinary people. He also discusses the freedom of devotional service itself and the importance of surrendering to Krishna.

If we travel we generally will have a purpose, obligation, duty or some desire for enjoyment. We are always having a reason whereas Narada Bani is fully self-satisfied by devotional service to Krishna which he has for the entire creation of the supreme lord Krishna which he engages always and therefore he has no reason to travel that I want something, I want something. A pure devotee never wants anything, of course a pure devotee always prays to Krishna, may I always be engaged in your service. This is a pure devotee's prayer and the Lord is pleased with such prayers and he engages a pure devotee in devotional service always eternally. So Srila Prabhupada explains similarly the transcendental system of devotional service is also free. The transcendental system of devotional service is also free. It may or may not develop in a particular person even after he undergoes all the detailed formulas. There is no obligation on the part of the supreme lord to grant us this freedom by performing devotional service. It is not obligatory for the Lord. Of course the Lord is, Lord Krishna is always our topmost well-wisher but then we should surrender to him, execute devotional service without any reservation and then we can expect the mercy of the Lord by which alone we can get this freedom which a pure devotee enjoys by the mercy of the supreme lord. The association of the devotee is also free. One may be fortunate to have it or one may not have it even after thousands of endeavors. People have so many different kinds of associations in this world. Somebody's interest in sports then they are associating with other people and they are associating with the Lord because they are interested people in sports. Somebody's interested in some profession, some technical development and they associate with such technocrats. Somebody is interested in some mundane music, somebody else is interested in mundane literature. So many different interests are there and they are busy with that.

---
### compassion, acharyas, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GXDR8FdLemw?t=754
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Acharyas like Prabhupada come down and they want to liberally distribute devotional service to all those who are unfortunate, all those who are bereft of devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the difficulty of obtaining devotional service and the compassion of acharyas like Prabhupada, who distribute it liberally to the unfortunate. He concludes by summarizing the formula for attaining freedom through devotional service and the mercy of the Lord and spiritual master.

So, devotional service is not easily available in this material world. And yet, acharyas like Prabhupada come down and they want to liberally distribute devotional service to all those who are unfortunate, all those who are bereft of devotional service. Acharyas like Prabhupada, Prabhupada, his guru, his guru's guru, all the acharyas in the parampara, in the sampradaya, Vaishnava sampradaya, they are very, very, very compassionate upon the suffering souls of this world, just as Krishna is compassionate. Why does Krishna incarnate? For the sake of the conditioned souls, to rescue them. And he senses confidential devotees also to rescue the conditioned souls. Krishna is anxious for this. This is only anxiety. Prahlad Maharaj says, people are busy with illusory activities, simply for some illusory pleasure and they are simply wasting their time. Unfortunately, they are ignorant. But the ignorance, even if it is not difficult to remove, we may not become really delivered or free from material bondage, simply by some mechanical process. No. So, therefore, it is said here, devotional service, voluntarily taken up by somebody, and serve the spiritual master, and through the spiritual master, the Supreme Lord Krishna, without any reservation, and await the causeless mercy of the spiritual master and the Supreme Lord. This is the formula. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna. Thank you.

### illusion, suffering, material existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TSYAZClUueY?t=117
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "He is travelling everywhere to relieve the distress of those suffering souls who are conditioned souls in this world under the influence of illusion, mistakenly thinking that this world is all in all, mistakenly thinking that I am this body, mistakenly thinking illusory pleasure is real happiness."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Narada Muni's activities, emphasizing his role in relieving the distress of conditioned souls who are under the influence of illusion and material attachments.

What about? Wonderful personality Narad Muni is. So, he is being glorified by Suta Goswami that all glory and success to Srila Narad Muni Aho Devarashir Dhanayoyam. Why? He always is engaged in glorifying and glorifying the activities of Lord Krishna and by doing that, what happens? He himself takes pleasure and he also enlivens all the distressed souls of this universe. He is travelling everywhere to relieve the distress of those suffering souls who are conditioned souls in this world under the influence of illusion, mistakenly thinking that this world is all in all, mistakenly thinking that I am this body, mistakenly thinking illusory pleasure is real happiness.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.6.4-5 | 24-10-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### time, eternality, memory
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SrqsRyzPh8s?t=57
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O great sage, time annihilates everything in due course. So how is it that this subject matter, which happened prior to this day of Brahma, is still fresh in your memory, undisturbed by time?"
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces the question posed by Vyasa Deva to Narada Muni regarding how Narada remembers events from a previous kalpa despite the annihilating nature of time.

In this session, we will continue to discuss from the 6th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, verses 4 onwards. Today, we will discuss 4 to 9, that is about 6 verses. The meaning of this verse is, O great sage. This verse is spoken by Vyasa Deva as an inquiry to Narada Muni. Vyasa Deva is inquiring from Narada about the further events after his initiation in his previous life, Narada's previous life. So Vyasa Deva's inquiry now is, O great sage, time annihilates everything in due course. So how is it that this subject matter, which happened prior to this day of Brahma, is still fresh in your memory, undisturbed by time? We have to be reminded that Narada is speaking of his previous life in a previous kalpa. Kalpa means a day of Brahma. In one day of Brahma, there are 1000 cycles of the 4 yugas on this earth. So, something which happened billions of years back, and after that happened, everything was completely annihilated, still, how is it fresh in the mind of Narada? That is Vyasa Deva's question.

---
### spirit soul, consciousness, material body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SrqsRyzPh8s?t=180
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A spirit is not annihilated even after the annihilation of the material body. So also, spiritual consciousness is not annihilated."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains Srila Prabhupada's explanation that the spirit soul and spiritual consciousness are eternal and not destroyed like the material body.

So Srila Prabhupada explains, how is it fresh? Srila Prabhupada says, a spirit is not annihilated even after the annihilation of the material body. So also, spiritual consciousness is not annihilated. So, we are spirit soul. Spirit soul is eternal, never destroyed, no birth, no death, no deterioration. And the body is, of course, it is destroyed after death. So unlike the body which is destroyed, every time we acquire a body, it will be destroyed. So body is destroyed, yes, but not the spirit soul. Similarly, spiritual consciousness, the consciousness belongs to the spirit. Consciousness doesn't belong to the body or doesn't belong to anything material. Just like the body. Just like all the material objects around us, they are all dead matter. There is no consciousness in them. Similarly, the body itself independently does not have consciousness. Then, what is the consciousness we exhibit while in this body?

---
### spiritual consciousness, material consciousness, transformation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SrqsRyzPh8s?t=272
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Narada developed his spiritual consciousness even when he had his material body in the previous kalpa, in the previous day of Brahma. He developed the spiritual consciousness."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami elaborates on the difference between spiritual and material consciousness, explaining that material consciousness is temporary and changes with the body, while spiritual consciousness is eternal.

So that Srila Prabhupada explains, that Narada developed his spiritual consciousness even when he had his material body in the previous kalpa, in the previous day of Brahma. He developed the spiritual consciousness. Now, consciousness of the material body means spiritual consciousness expressed through the medium of a material body. Just like everybody exhibits consciousness, all living persons, even though they are not situated in spiritual consciousness, whatever consciousness they are exhibiting, they are exhibiting the consciousness of the spirit through the medium of the material body. Now, this consciousness exhibited through the material body, is inferior, it is destructible and it is perverted. It is not permanent. Just like even in this life, you can see a child exhibits consciousness of a small child behavior or the characteristics of a small child. The same child, when it grows up, when its consciousness changes from the consciousness of a child to the consciousness of a young adult, it is completely different. The consciousness is different. So, that consciousness of a child is gone with that child body. The child body is no longer there. So, that consciousness of a child, is not there exhibited anymore. It is gone with the body.

---
### spiritual consciousness, super consciousness, eternality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SrqsRyzPh8s?t=398
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The spiritual consciousness is not dependent on the condition or the state of the body. It is the awakening of the spirit."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami emphasizes that spiritual consciousness is independent of the material body, representing the awakening of the spirit and existing on a spiritual platform that is never destroyed.

Whereas, the spiritual consciousness is not dependent on the condition or the state of the body. It is the awakening of the spirit. It is the awakening of the spirit. So, that is the super consciousness of the supra mind, as Prabhupada says here. It is spiritual consciousness, which is above material consciousness. And it is of the super mind. The spiritual mind, not the material mind. And it is on the spiritual plane. It is on the spiritual platform. It is as good as the spirit soul and is never destroyed, never annihilated. So, this is the explanation Srila Prabhupada gives, how Narada did not forget what happened in a previous day of Brahma, when everything at the end of the day of Brahma was annihilated. So, everything is annihilated means except the spirit and the spiritual consciousness, everything else is annihilated.

---

So, the fifth verse, Narada Vacha, Narada Muni now responds to Vyasa Deva's questions. Bhikshu Bhir Vipravasite Vijnana Adheshtra Bhirmama Vartamano Vyasyaadhe Tata Etad Akarasham. Narada Muni is telling Vyasa Deva, Sri Narada said, the great sages who had imparted scientific knowledge, knowledge of transcendence to me departed for other places and I had to pass my life in this way. **Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains Srila Prabhupada's purport, highlighting the tangible change in Narada's life after receiving spiritual knowledge and emphasizing the visible and significant transformation that occurs after initiation by a bona fide spiritual master.

So, Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, in his previous life when Narada ji was impregnated with spiritual knowledge by the grace of the great sages, there was a tangible change in his life. Although he was only a boy of five years, there was a significant change in his life. Even though he was a small boy, that initiation or imparting the spiritual knowledge, awakening the spirit within, that is a significant change in the life of the initiated candidate. Small boy still, the change is very, very significant. And Srila Prabhupada says, that is an important symptom visible after initiation by the bona fide spiritual master. When the bona fide spiritual master initiates a person in spiritual knowledge, then there is a visible change, very, very visible and characteristic and significant change that is visible.

Actual association of devotees brings about a quick change in life for spiritual realization. The changes that happen in the practice of bhakti yoga, are very quick changes, so that one progresses very fast in spiritual realization. How fast? Whereas the other processes may take millions of lifetimes, bhakti, if properly administered, is possible, spiritual realization is possible in one moment. This is explained by Shukadeva Goswami in the beginning of the second canto. Parikshit Maharaj had only seven days for perfecting his spiritual realization.

### Parikshit Maharaj, Shukadeva Goswami, bhakti, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SrqsRyzPh8s?t=680
**Verse References:** SB 2.1
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The method of pure devotional service is very very powerful and even if somebody is able to execute that for a very short time, just even one moment, filled with Krishna Consciousness, is perfection of life achieved in a very short time."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami narrates the story of Parikshit Maharaj and Shukadeva Goswami, emphasizing that pure devotional service, even for a short time, can lead to perfection.

And he was inquiring, what should I do next seven days to achieve perfection? So these saintly persons were suggesting different methods, but all of them except Shukadeva Goswami, everyone else said, oh, these methods which we are suggesting will take time, seven days is very less time. So while this discussion was going on, Parikshit Maharaj was consulting the saintly persons. So at that time, Shukadeva Goswami walked into that assembly and everybody stood up, received him respectfully, and Parikshit Maharaj offered him a seat of honor and inquired from him, what should I do next seven days? I have only seven days time to perfect myself. So Shukadeva Goswami assured him that the method of pure devotional service is very very powerful and even if somebody is able to execute that for a very short time, just even one moment, filled with Krishna Consciousness, is perfection of life achieved in a very short time.

**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami recounts the story of Katvanga Rishi, who achieved perfection and went back to Godhead by meditating on the lotus feet of Krishna for just one muhurta.

So Shukadeva Goswami gave the example of one Katvanga Rishi. Katvanga Rishi. He said, there was one Katvanga Rishi who had only one muhurta time left and he simply fixed his mind upon the lotus feet of the deity form of Krishna in a temple of Krishna. And that one muhurta, by completely being absorbed in meditation on the deity of Krishna in the temple of Krishna, he quit his body and went back to Godhead. Because he was fully absorbed, his mind was fully absorbed in thought of the transcendental form of Krishna.

So now you sit and hear the Bhagavatam for seven days, fixing your mind on the descriptions of Krishna, which I am now going to narrate. And by doing so, you will achieve perfection very quickly. Even before seven days, you just are able to absorb your mind completely in hearing. Of course, Pariksit Maharaj was an exceptional devotee. He sat firmly and heard for seven days and nights continuously. And Shukadeva Goswami was the perfect spiritual master who spoke continuously, answering the various questions Pariksit raised in connection with the descriptions of Krishna, his incarnations, his devotees, his transcendental activities, his paraphernalia, his abode, everything centered around Krishna. And that is how Pariksit attained perfection in seven days. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna. Thank you very much.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.6.6-10 | 25-10-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### dependence, divine control, free will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Gt9GLkuxAWc?t=60
**Verse References:** SB 1.6.6, SB 1.6.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The world is under the full control of the Supreme Lord. Therefore, everyone is like a wooden doll in the hands of a puppet master."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Narada Muni's description of his early life and the apparent contradiction between divine control and individual free will.

This session will be discussing from the Srimad Bhagavatam, the first canto, sixth chapter. Sixth chapter is entitled Conversation between Narada and Vyasa Deva. This is the sixth verse I am going to read now. The meaning of this verse is, I was the only son of my mother who was not only a simple woman but a maid servant as well. Since I was her only offspring, she had no other alternative for protection. She bound me with the tie of affection. This is Narada Muni describing his previous life to Vyasa Deva. He is particularly answering Vyasa's question as to what did he do after initiation. So he was the only son of his mother and his mother was a maid servant doing some simple work and he was her only son. So she bound him up with her affectionate bondage. The next verse, the seventh verse. The meaning is she wanted to look after my maintenance properly but because she was not independent, she was not able to do anything for me. The world is under the full control of the Supreme Lord. Therefore, everyone is like a wooden doll in the hands of a puppet master. So Narada Muni is explaining that the mother was having a plan to look after this boy very nicely. But we should always remember, none of us in this world are independent. The world is under the full control, complete control of the Supreme Lord. It is described here. Therefore, everyone is like a wooden doll in the hands of a puppet master. It is described here. Therefore, everyone is like a wooden doll in the hands of a puppet master. It is described here. So does it mean that what can we do? Whatever happens is completely under the control of the Supreme Lord. So we are kind of totally helpless. This sort of understanding is not the correct understanding.

---
### free will, karma, responsibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Gt9GLkuxAWc?t=270
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord is in full control, yes, that's a fact, but he also has given each of the human beings, particularly, a little free will, little independence."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that while the Lord is in control, humans possess free will and are responsible for their actions and desires, which influence their experiences.

What is the correct understanding? The Lord is in full control, yes, that's a fact. But he also has given each of the human beings, particularly, a little free will, little independence. And we have to become responsible in properly utilizing the tiny amount of independence. Now this use of independence is dependent on our desires. In what way we want to cultivate our desires. So, depending on the desires and depending on our past karma, the Supreme Lord arranges everything for a person, for each one of us. So, ultimately, the Supreme Lord is in control. Our specific enjoyment and suffering is due to our own actions, our desires. It is not imposed by the Lord. Desires are not imposed. Activities are not imposed. So, therefore, we have to carefully understand that we are not independent, that whatever we want, we can do. No. There is always a control. The supreme control. Not just a control, but the supreme control of the Supreme Lord. That we are bound up based on our past actions, and based on our desires, to have a certain freedom within a boundary. We are not having any absolute freedom. Absolute freedom belongs only to the Supreme Lord and nobody else.

---
### dependence, divine will, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Gt9GLkuxAWc?t=444
**Verse References:** SB 1.6.8, SB 1.6.9
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Once upon a time, my poor mother, when going out one night to milk a cow, was bitten on the leg by a serpent, influenced by supreme time."
**Summary:** The speaker continues with Narada Muni's story, describing his mother's death and attributing it to the supreme will of the Lord.

So, I continue. The eighth verse. The meaning of this verse is, when I was a mere child of five years, I lived in a Brahmana school. I was dependent on my mother's affection, and had no experience of different lands. Very limited scope for the boy to do anything. He was just a five-year-old boy. And he was living in a Brahmana school. And he was dependent on his mother. The next verse, further Narad Muni explains, describes, Ninth verse, Ekadā nirgatāṁ yehād, Duhantīṁ nishigāṁ patī, Sarpo adasat padās prishtah, Kripanāṁ kāla choditah. The meaning is, once upon a time, my poor mother, when going out one night to milk a cow, was bitten on the leg by a serpent, influenced by supreme time. So, this is the supreme will of the Supreme Lord.

### mercy, detachment, illusory energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Gt9GLkuxAWc?t=533
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That is the way of dragging a sincere soul nearer to God."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the Lord's actions, even those that appear difficult, can be acts of mercy to draw devotees closer and break material attachments.

So, Śrīla Prabhupāda explains, that is the way of dragging a sincere soul nearer to God. The boy was bound up by his mother's affection. But, to release him from that bondage, the Lord has arranged something. What is that? The poor boy was being looked after only by his affectionate mother. And yet, the mother was taken away from the world by the supreme will, in order to put him completely at the mercy of the Lord. See, this is described here as the Lord's mercy. Upon this soul, Narada, in his previous life, actually, each one of us is completely dependent entirely on the Supreme Lord. It is not just the case with this boy, Narada. It is the case with everyone. But, we have created artificial sources of dependence, based on some bodily connections. But, all these are simply the effect of the illusory energy. The body itself is the arrangement of the illusory energy. So, what to speak of the bodily connections? They are all completely governed by kala karma guna. So, there is no certainty, how long a certain relationship will exist, and what will be the outcome of a certain relationship, at a certain time. Nobody can actually predict, or make a definite plan, though we try to make so many plans. But, all such plans are ultimately, nullified, by the action of the supreme time.

---
### surrender, mercy, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Gt9GLkuxAWc?t=689
**Verse References:** SB 1.6.10
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Who is there for me, other than the supreme lord, nobody else."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how Narada Muni accepted his mother's death as the Lord's mercy, leading him to complete dependence and devotion, and ultimately to his spiritual journey.

So, the boy was having only his mother. And, the mother was taken away, so that he could completely come to depend upon, the supreme lord. Who is the only available shelter, ultimately, and nobody else, is the shelter. The tenth verse, tadātadaham īśasya bhaktānāṁśaṁ abhīpsatah anugrahaṁ manyamāṇaḥ prātiṣṭham diśaṁ uttarāṁ The meaning is, I took this as the special mercy of the lord, who always desires benediction for his devotees. And so thinking, I started for the north. So, Śrīla Prabhupāda explains, confidential devotees of the lord, see in every step, a benedictory direction of the lord. What is considered to be an odd or difficult moment, in the mundane sense, accepted as a special mercy of the lord. This is the position of a devotee. This boy was initiated, instructed, and enlightened by the sages, even when he was a very small boy. But he could understand, Oh, now that my mother is taken away from me, who is there for me, other than the supreme lord, nobody else. And therefore, this boy thought that, it's the lord's mercy, that I have been placed in a situation, where I exclusively have to depend on him. And, he understood that, the lord has freed him from this bondage. And, as he was directed, or instructed by the sages, he actually, started towards the north. North means, going to the Himalayas, for, exclusively, meditating upon the supreme lord, as directed by, the saintly persons.

---
### detachment, spiritual perspective, material fever
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Gt9GLkuxAWc?t=857
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mundane prosperity is a kind of material fever."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by explaining that material prosperity can be a hindrance to spiritual progress, comparing it to a fever that needs to be reduced to achieve a healthy spiritual state.

So, it is explained here, mundane prosperity is a kind of material fever. And by the grace of the lord, the temperature of this material fever, is gradually diminished. So, what, we calculate according to our, material calculation, that is not, the proper understanding, from the spiritual perspective. Just like fever, is to be reduced. It's a symptom of an unhealthy body. Similarly, material prosperity, which may make us attached materially, or bound materially, is like a fever, unhealthy condition. So, when it comes down, then, that, we come out of the bondage, material bondage. And that's what happened to Narada Muni. Of course, people do not understand this. Ordinary people do not understand this. It's only spiritual, perspective, which helps us understand this situation. So, I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.7.1-2 | 05-12-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Srimad Bhagavatam, Narada Muni, Vyasa Deva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FHNY319MGjQ?t=41
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now, after Narada's departure, what did Vyasa Deva do?"
**Summary:** Shaunaka Rishi inquires about Vyasa Deva's activities after receiving instructions from Narada Muni, setting the stage for the narration of Srimad Bhagavatam and the story of Pariksit Maharaj.

OM NAMO BHAGVATE VASUDEVAYA NARAYANAM NAMASKRITYA NARAM CHAIVANAROTTAMAM DEVIM SARASVATIM VYASAM TATO JAYAM UDI RAYET. In this session, we will be discussing the 7th chapter of the first canto of Bhagavatam, entitled, The Son of Drona Punished, beginning with the first verse. SHAUNAKA VACHA NIRGATE NARADE SUTA BHHAGAVAN BHADARAYANAH SHRUTAVAM STAD ABHIPRETAM TATAKHIM AKAROD VIBHUHU. The meaning of this verse is, Rishi Shaunaka asked, O Suta! The great and transcendentally powerful Vyasa Deva heard everything from Shri Narada Muni. So after Narada's departure, what did Vyasa Deva do? If you remember, the conversation between the sages of Naimisharanya headed by Shaunaka Rishi and Suta Goswami is the beginning of the first chapter of the first canto of Bhagavatam, the opening of the Bhagavatam. So, seeing the eagerness of these sages to hear about Krishna, Suta Goswami decided to recite the Bhagavatam, which he had heard from Shukadeva Goswami. So before reciting the Bhagavatam, Shaunaka Rishi asked many other questions apart from the first six questions to Suta Goswami. So, in response to questions asked by Shaunaka Rishi, who is the representative of the sages of Naimisharanya, Suta Goswami is answering. Here, Shaunaka Rishi is asking to continue, what did Vyasa Deva do? After he heard everything about Narada Muni's previous birth, how Narada Muni actually got enlightenment from the Bhaktivedanta, how he practiced this Dharma practice, which is the realization of the teaching of the Sovak Sangha, he practiced going to a sacred place, the meditation on the Supreme Lord, how he had a brief glimpse of the darshan of the Lord, then the Lord spoke to him, and then he became perfect, and when he quit his body in his previous life, he changed from a material body to a completely spiritual body, and in that spiritual body now, in this day of Brahma, he is born as the son of Brahma, in his same transcendental form, and he travels everywhere simply singing the glories of the Supreme Lord, while vibrating on his transcendental instrument, the Veena, which is given to him by Krishna himself, and in this way, he is himself completely enlivened, and he enlivens others also. Now, after Narada's departure, what did Vyasa Deva do? That's the question now, Savanaka Rishi is raising. So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, in this chapter, the clue for describing Srimad Bhagavatam is picked up, as Maharaj Pariksit is miraculously saved in the womb of his mother.

### Pariksit Maharaj, Ashwatthama, Kurukshetra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FHNY319MGjQ?t=243
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now, Bhagavatam begins from the point where the end of the battle of Kurukshetra."
**Summary:** The Bhagavatam's narration begins with Pariksit Maharaj's miraculous saving in the womb, linking back to the end of the Kurukshetra battle and Ashwatthama's actions, which led to his punishment by Arjuna.

Now, Bhagavatam begins from the point where the end of the battle of Kurukshetra, Pariksit, see the central personalities for the recitation of the Bhagavatam, first time, is Pariksit Maharaj, who inquired from Shukadeva Goswami. Shukadeva Goswami learned the Bhagavatam from Vyasa Deva. Vyasa Deva compiled the Bhagavatam after hearing about Narada's instructions on Bhagavatam. So, that is the link. Now, the narration will begin by Suta Goswami about the Bhagavatam, how Pariksit was saved in the womb of his mother, and that is the description of the birth and activities of Pariksit Maharaj, which was inquired to Suta Goswami. Now, this reason for Pariksit being saved in the womb of his mother was because the cause was Ashwatthama, the son of Dronacharya, who killed the five sons of Draupadi while they were asleep, for which he was punished by Arjuna. That is the incident that is going to be narrated. In this chapter, in the seventh chapter, the son of Drona punished. By whom? By Arjuna. Because the son of Drona, Ashwatthama, he killed the five sleeping sons of Draupadi towards the end of the Battle of Kurukshetra.

Following Narada Muni's instructions to compile Bhagavatam, he realized the entire truth about Krishna, which is going to be described by Vyasa Deva while compiling the Bhagavatam, and Vyasa Deva is going to teach it to his own son, Shukadeva Goswami, who is already a liberated soul. So, we will hear now systematically about Vyasa Deva realizing the truth. The second verse. The meaning of this second verse of the same seventh chapter of the first canto of Bhagavatam. Shri Suta said. Suta Goswami is now responding to the question. On the western bank of the river Saraswati, which is intimately related with the Vedas, there is a cottage for meditation at Shamyaprasa, which enlivens the transcendental activities of the sages.

So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, For spiritual advancement of knowledge, a suitable place and atmosphere are definitely required. The place on the... The western bank of the Saraswati river is especially suitable for this purpose, and there is the ashram of Vyasa Deva at Shamyaprasa. So, the place where Vyasa Deva's ashram was located is a very, very holy place, a sacred place, most suitable for spiritual activities. The place on the western bank of the Saraswati river is especially suitable for this purpose, and there is the ashram of Vyasa Deva at Shamyaprasa. Hills of the Himalayas and even other places there devotees do visit that place it's called Vyasa Gufa the cave of Vyasa Deva that's the literal meaning in Hindi Vyasa Gufa so that place that is the place where Vyasa Deva's ashram is located and Vyasa Deva sat there and engaged in meditation and when he was and had reached Samadhi he was able to actually realize the entire truth about the absolute personality of Godhead Krishna that will be described now now Srila Vyasa Deva was a householder that's how he had a son Sukhdev Goswami yet his residential place is called an ashram so what is ashram and ashram is a place where spiritual culture is always foremost ashram is a residential place but it is a spiritually surcharged place because it is the most suitable place for such a spiritual place but it is a a place where you are spiritual shelter for any transcendentalist it is necessary to reside in a place where spiritual culture is foremost that means a transcendentalist does not live in any place simply for engaging in some sense enjoyment a transcendentalist voluntarily gives up sense enjoyment in order to cultivate spiritual life. So Vyasa Deva of course is perfect because he is the empowered incarnation of the Supreme Lord for compiling all the Vedic literature and he has appeared for this purpose specifically he says also an incarnation of the Lord Shakya Vesha Avatar so he has no need to practice but nonetheless to set an example he is situated in his ashram and he is performing meditation in order to fix his mind on the absolute personality of Godhead the absolute truth and realize the whole subject matter of the Bhagavad Gita before compiling.

### Varnashrama, Spiritual Culture, Stages of Life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FHNY319MGjQ?t=635
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The whole varnashrama system is so designed that each and every status of life is called an ashram."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the varnashrama system, highlighting the four varnas (brahmana, kshatriya, vaishya, shudra) and the four ashramas (brahmachari, grihastha, vanaprastha, sannyas), emphasizing that each stage is a spiritual shelter with spiritual culture as a prominent aspect.

Srila Prabhupada further explains it does not matter whether the place belongs to a householder or a mendicant mendicant means somebody who is a renunciant who is a tapasvi who is a renounced order of life the whole varnashrama system is so designed that each and every status of life is called an ashram varana ashrama four varanas brahmana, kshatriya, vaishya, shudra this defines the whole system of life this defines the occupational duty of four different classes of people in the civilized human society apart from these four varanas there are four ashramas what are the four ashramas? brahmachari ashram grihastha ashram vanaprastha ashram sannyas ashram these are four stages of gradual progress towards the ultimate goal of self realization while spiritual culture is very prominent in each of these stages of ashram life all are spiritual shelter brahmachari stays in the house of the spiritual master the guru guru kula and he is taught the vedic knowledge and then grihastha, householder householder, he lives with his wife and children, family life but he is engaged in spiritual activities even as a householder.

### Self-Realization, Renunciation, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FHNY319MGjQ?t=777
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Realization of Krishna is the ultimate self realization."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining the varnashrama system, describing the roles of vanaprastha and sannyasa ashrams, emphasizing that spiritual culture is the common factor for all stages of life, with the ultimate goal being the realization of Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

And of course after completing his duty as a householder he hands over charge of the house to his grown up son or some other guardian and he leaves home and goes away to the forest or a holy place to intensify his spiritual practice and become completely engaged in activities of self realization which is the ultimate goal of life to realize the self and finally sannyas ashram is when one is completely renounced and one simply engages exclusively in spiritual activities either one has already realized or one is just waiting to perfect his realization especially in the sannyas ashram so this means, Prabhupada says further spiritual culture is the common factor for all the brahmacharis, the grihasthas, the vanaprasthas and sannyasis all belong to the same mission of life namely realization of the supreme see important thing is self realization means I am also self, individual self jivatma but there is a supreme self the supreme paramatma krishna the supreme personality of god so realization of krishna is the ultimate self realization therefore none of them are less important as far as spiritual culture is concerned the difference is the formality on the strength of renunciation different rules and different formalities are there but ultimately all are meant for ultimate realization of the supreme personality of god krishna the sannyasis are held in high estimation on the strength of practical renunciation the sannyasis set the example by practicing the high standard of renunciation which is befitting them who are completely dedicated for spiritual practice or spiritual life. I will stop here thank you very much Hare Krishna.

Now each one of these terms has got multiple meanings. So combining the different meanings, there are so many meanings of the verse that can be derived.

And in his personal feature, he is always present in his transcendental abode of Goloka Vrindavana where he displays transcendental pastimes in all variegatedness. Now, Lord Vishnu's activities cannot be compared to anyone else's activities and therefore, the word Urukrama is applicable only to him. Only to Lord Vishnu and nobody else.

In that last line, one term Itthambhoota Guna has got different meanings. The word Itthambhoota is transcendentally exalted because it means full of transcendental bliss. Itthambhoota means full of transcendental bliss. Before this transcendental bliss, the bliss derived from merging into existence of the absolute Brahmananda, what is called as Brahmananda, becomes like a piece of straw in comparison. Which means, Brahmananda or the joy of merging into the existence of the Supreme is insignificant in front of the transcendental bliss derived by realization or connection with the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Vishnu.

**Summary:** Suta Goswami justifies why even liberated souls like Sukadev Goswami are attracted to Krishna due to His unlimited good qualities, as described by Lord Chaitanya to Srila Sanatana Goswami.

Though freed from all kinds of material bondage, desire to render unalloyed devotional service to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. This means the Lord possesses transcendental qualities and therefore can attract everyone, including liberated souls. If you remember, before this, there was a question raised by Saunakarishi to Suta Goswami, why did Sukadev Goswami, the great devotee of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, undertake the trouble of going through this vast literature of Bhagavatam from his father Vyasadeva, when he was already a liberated soul? So, here Suta Goswami is justifying that even liberated souls are attracted to Krishna because of his wonderful qualities. He is a reservoir of unlimited good qualities. Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur says, and who are the varieties of different transcendentalists who get attracted to Krishna? And even non-transcendentalists get attracted to Krishna.

Krishna's transcendental qualities of opulence, sweetness and mercy are perfect and full. As far as Krishna's affections are concerned, leaning toward his devotees is concerned he's so magnanimous that he can give himself to his devotees. Why is this said like this here? Because even if Krishna wants to offer anything to his devotees, the pure devotees do not accept anything in return for the service they render to Krishna. So Krishna says I have nothing in my possession which I can give you so I give myself to you. There is such a statement in these scriptures. Now Krishna has unlimited qualities. Ittambhuta gunoharihi, that is being described here. Krishna has unlimited qualities. The devotees are attracted by his uncommon beauty, by his mellows. Mellow means the rasa that is exchanged in transcendental loving service and the fragrance. Thus they are differently situated in different transcendental mellows. Devotees situated in different kinds of relationship with Krishna. Therefore Krishna is called all attractive. The name Krishna means one of the meanings Krishna means all attractive. He is completely attractive to everyone without exception. Now examples have been given by Lord Chaitanya the minds of the four boy sages, Kumaras, Sanaka, Sanatana, Sanandana and Sanat Kumara were attracted to the lotus feet of Krishna by the aroma of the Tursi that had been offered to the Lord. Lord Vishnu or Narayana always is worshipped by offering Tursi mixed with Chandan. We call it Charan Tursi. So that is offered to Lord Narayana or Krishna or Vishnu always by the devotees. So the fragrance of that Tursi mixed with Chandan attracted the attention of the people. These are the four liberated sages, Kumaras. So that is explained further. In the Bhagavatam, there is a statement. When the breeze carrying the aroma of Tursi leaves and saffron from the lotus feet of the lotus eyed personality of Godhead entered through the nostrils into the hearts of those sages, the Kumaras. They experienced a change in both body and mind. Even though they were attached to the inner world. Even though they were attached to the inner world. This was Murabbi's impassional Brahman understanding. There is a description in the 15th chapter of Srimad Bhagavatam.

### Shukadeva Goswami, Bhagavatam, Attraction to Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Ov_gpN6HEqc?t=656
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although I was fully situated in the transcendental position in impersonal Brahman realization, I was nonetheless attracted to the pastimes of Lord Krishna. Therefore, I studied Srimad Bhagavatam from my father."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how Shukadeva Goswami, initially situated in impersonal Brahman realization, was attracted to Krishna's pastimes upon hearing them from his father Vyasadeva, leading him to study Srimad Bhagavatam.

Swami's mind was carried away by hearing the pastimes of the Lord. So, it is said Shukadeva Goswami also was situated in impersonal understanding of the absolute truth, Brahman realization. But when he heard the pastimes of the Lord from his father Vyasadeva, when Vyasadeva compiled and was revising the Srimad Bhagavatam under the instruction of Narada, that is when Shukadeva Goswami got attracted to the personality of Godhead Krishna. So, Shukadeva Goswami's own words, it is described, Shukadeva Goswami is addressing Pariksit Maharaj, my dear king, although I was fully situated in the transcendental position in impersonal Brahman realization, I was nonetheless attracted to the pastimes of Lord Krishna. Therefore, I studied Srimad Bhagavatam from my father. Vyasadeva is a statement by Shukadeva Goswami himself. And Suta Goswami also confirms this by offering his respectful obeisances to Shukadeva Goswami. Suta Goswami says, I offer my respectful obeisances unto Srila Shukadeva Goswami, the son of Vyasadeva, and the destroyer of all sinful reactions. Being full in self-realization and bliss, he had no material desire. Still, he was attracted by the transcendental pastimes of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and out of compassion for the people in general. He described the transcendental historical literature called Srimad Bhagavatam. This is compared, this Bhagavatam is compared to the light of the absolute truth. Bhagavatam can enlighten us in the transcendental subject matter of the absolute truth. He described the personality of Godhead, Krishna, and his personal qualities, his personal activities, his personal form, his personal abode, his personal paraphernalia, his personal devotees.

In this session, we will be continuing the discussion about the Atmarama verse, the 10th verse of the 7th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Just to refresh your memory, the Atmarama verse, which is the famous one, we have been discussing in earlier sessions. This is the third session, and I am continuing the discussion on this verse. The meaning of this is, all different varieties of Atmaramas, Atmaramas are those who take pleasure in Atma or the spirit self, especially those established on the path of self-realization, though freed from all kinds of material bondage, desire to render unalloyed devotional service unto the personality of Godhead. This means that the Lord possesses transcendental qualities and therefore can attract everyone, including liberated souls. So, we already discussed how different personalities were attracted to Krishna, even though they were situated, some of them were situated in the impersonal liberation. For example, the four Kumaras and Shukadev Goswami. And I also mentioned in the previous session how the Gopis were attracted to Krishna. Now, I am going to talk about the Gopis. I am continuing the discussion about the other personalities attracted by Krishna.

### Krishna's qualities, attraction, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fG_0kfgjj48?t=153
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's qualities are so very attractive, that nobody else can possess qualities like Krishna to the extent that they may attract somebody more than Krishna. That is not possible."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on Krishna's all-attractive nature, quoting Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and highlighting how even the queens of Dwaraka and the goddess of fortune are drawn to Krishna's beauty and qualities, emphasizing that no one can possess qualities that attract more than Krishna.

This is described by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu to Sanatana Goswami. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, the queens in Dwaraka, headed by Rukmini, are also attracted to Krishna simply by hearing about his transcendental beauty and qualities. Simply by hearing about his transcendental beauty and qualities. This Atmarama verse is all about Krishna having unlimited transcendental qualities, attributes by which he attracts everyone. That's why he is called all-attractive. Krishna means all-attractive. He attracts everybody without exception. So, here is a statement in the Bhagavatam that Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is quoting. O most beautiful Krishna, I have heard about your transcendental qualities from others, and therefore all my bodily miseries are relieved. If someone sees your transcendental beauty, his eyes have attained everything profitable in life. O infallible one, I have become shameless after hearing of your qualities, and I have become attracted to you. So, Krishna's qualities are so very attractive, because somebody possesses some nice qualities that is attractive, then when we come across such a person, we become attracted to that person. That's natural. But here, Krishna's attraction is so very powerful, that nobody else can possess qualities like Krishna to the extent that they may attract somebody more than Krishna. That is not possible. Further, Lord Chaitanya says, Lord Krishna attracts even the mind of the goddess of fortune simply by vibrating his transcendental flute.

---
### Kalia, Krishna's mercy, lotus feet
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fG_0kfgjj48?t=303
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Indeed, we do not know how this serpent Kalia got such an opportunity, which even goddess of fortune, it is difficult to attain. How did Kalia get that?"
**Summary:** This segment narrates the story of Kalia being subdued by Krishna and receiving the dust of His lotus feet, highlighting the Nagapatnis' surprise at how Kalia, despite his sinful background, attained such a rare blessing, even more difficult to attain than for the goddess of fortune.

There is a statement in the Bhagavatam by the Nagapatnis. O Lord, we do not know how the serpent Kalia attained such an opportunity to be touched by the dust of your lotus feet. Kalia was chastised by Krishna. And because Kalia's wives, the Nagapatnis, were devotees of Krishna, Krishna spared the life of Kalia, because the Nagapatnis otherwise would have become widowed. So, Krishna, on the request of the Nagapatnis, spared his life. But still, how did he get the dust of the lotus feet of Krishna on his head? What did Krishna do to subdue Kalia? He actually pounded Kalia's hoods. So, in the process, Kalia got the blessings of the dust of the lotus feet of Krishna. So, the Nagapatnis are expressing surprise. He is a very, he has a sinful background. That's how he took the body of a serpent with so much of venom in his body. And unnecessarily such venomous serpents bite others and kill them. Innocent people, innocent creatures. So, it's not a very good birth because of past sinful activities. In spite of that, in spite of possessing such a body, how did Kalia be fortunate enough to get the dust of the lotus feet of Krishna? That is what the Nagapatnis are expressing in their prayers to Krishna. They are telling to get the dust of the lotus feet of Krishna. The goddess of fortune performed austerities for centuries, giving up all of their desires and taking austere vows. Means it's not easy for someone to get the dust of the lotus feet of Krishna on their head. Indeed, we do not know how this serpent Kalia got such an opportunity, which even goddess of fortune, it is difficult to attain. How did Kalia get that? That is the surprise that the Nagapatnis express in their prayers to Krishna.

---

Next, Lord Chaitanya explains, Krishna attracts not only the minds of the gopis and the goddesses of fortune, but the minds of all the young girls in the three worlds as well. How is that? That is another statement in the Bhagavatam. My dear Lord Krishna, where is that woman within the three worlds who would not be captivated by the rhythms of the sweet songs coming from your wonderful flute? Who would not fall down from the past though? Chastity in this way. Your beauty is the most sublime within the three worlds. Upon seeing your beauty, even cows, birds, animals and trees in the forest become stunned in jubilation. So, if such creatures like cows and birds can become captivated, what to speak of the girls? In the three worlds, the women in the three worlds. So, the women of Vrindavan who are on the level of superior guardians are attracted to Lord Krishna maternally. That means as mother, in a motherly relationship. The men of Vrindavan are attracted as servants, friends and fathers. Mind you, this is the description that everyone is attracted by Krishna. Everyone. Therefore, the term Krishna all attractive applies only to the Supreme Personality of God, Krishna and nobody else. Further, it is described here. The qualities of Krishna captivate and attract everything, living and non-living. Even birds, animals and trees are attracted to Krishna's qualities. This is already mentioned in the previous verse.

### Hari, inauspiciousness, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fG_0kfgjj48?t=590
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Hari means one who takes away, one who steals. So, what does Krishna take away that he is referred to as Hari? He takes away all inauspicious things from his devotees."
**Summary:** This segment explains the meaning of the name 'Hari' in the context of the Atmarama verse, highlighting that Hari takes away inauspiciousness from devotees and steals their minds with ecstatic love for God, emphasizing the transformative power of devotion.

Now, coming to the word Hari. Although the word Hari has many different meanings, two of them are foremost. In connection with the usage of the word Hari in this verse, ittham bhuta guno hari hi. The Supreme Personality of Godhead who is also called Hari is full of so many nice qualities by which she attracts everyone. This is a reference to the verse in which the word Hari is used. Now, which sense the word Hari is used? One meaning is that the Lord who is called as Hari takes away all inauspicious things from his devotee. Hari means one who takes away, one who steals. So, what does Krishna take away that he is referred to as Hari? He takes away all inauspicious things from his devotees. Isn't it so nice? Of course, this doesn't refer to devotees who are already free from all bad qualities or all inauspiciousness. It refers to devotees who are on the path, just entering the path of pure devotional service and very quickly they become purified by coming in touch with the Supreme Lord Krishna. The second meaning of the word Hari in this context of its usage in this Atmarama verse is that he attracts the mind by ecstatic love for God. He steals away the mind of those devotees who are looking after love of Krishna and by giving them the love, reciprocating them with them in love. He attracts their mind. So, now the name Hari the way it is used in Atmarama verse Chaitanya Mahaprabhu explains when the devotee some or other always remembers the Supreme Personality of Godhead anywhere and everywhere Devotee means he always thinks of Krishna. Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita Devotees who are practiced always think of Krishna. Such devotees what happens? Lord Hari takes away life's four miserable conditions. What are the four miserable conditions of life in this material world?

---
### sinful reactions, surrender, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fG_0kfgjj48?t=754
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna says I will protect you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear."
**Summary:** This segment explains how Krishna, as Hari, removes the four miserable conditions of life by taking away all sinful reactions, regardless of their severity, when a devotee surrenders to Him, referencing the Bhagavad Gita and Srila Prabhupada's explanation of the four types of sinful activities and the assurance of protection upon surrender.

So, Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport. The four miserable conditions are due to the four kinds of sinful activities known as 1. Pathaka 2. Urupathaka 3. Mahapathaka 4. Atipathaka. What do they mean? Pathaka means preliminary sin and Urupathaka means very great sin and Mahapathaka means greater sin and Atipathaka means the topmost sin. Among sinful activities there are gradations. So, based on the gradations there are four different gradations. Now doesn't matter whether somebody is committing sinful activities which are of lower grade or intermediate grade or very high grade It doesn't matter Krishna assures the devotee aham tvam sarva papebhya moksha itshyami mahashu jahan That means if somebody surrenders to Krishna even if before surrendering he was very very sinful Krishna says I will protect you from all sinful reactions Do not fear This is the verse in the Bhagavad Gita aham tvam sarva papebhya moksha itshyami mahashu jahan Now here Srila Prabhupada particularly explains the word sarva papebhya literally means all sinful reactions Now here particularly Prabhupada is telling the word sarva papebhya indicates four kinds of sinful activities Which are the four kinds? The four kinds of sinful activities which are classified as pataka, uru pataka, maha pataka and ati pataka It doesn't matter how sinful a devotee had been before coming to Krishna consciousness Krishna takes away all the sinful reactions before rather takes away the sinful reactions all kinds of sinful reactions when the devotee surrenders to Krishna The four basic sinful activities Prabhupada is reminding us are summarized in the Bhagavad Gita as illicit sex, intoxication gambling and meat eating So Krishna takes away all the sinful reactions So therefore he is called Hari I will stop here Thank you very much Hare Krishna

### Atmarama verse, liberation, Krishna's qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Eg1Ig6SdK_0?t=143
**Verse References:** SB 1.7.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This means that the Lord possesses transcendental qualities and therefore can attract everyone including liberated souls."
**Summary:** The speaker recites and translates the Atmarama verse, explaining how Krishna attracts even liberated souls through His transcendental qualities, citing examples like Shukadev Goswami, the four Kumaras, the Gopis, and even trees and animals.

So the verse is, Atmaramas chamunayo nirgrantha apyurukrame kurvanti ahaitu keem bhaktim ittham bhuta guno hari hi. The meaning of this verse as directly translated, all different varieties of Atmaramas, especially those established from the path of self-realization, though freed from all kinds of material bondage, desire to render self-realization, to the service, to the personality of Godhead. This means that the Lord possesses transcendental qualities and therefore can attract everyone including liberated souls. So in the last three sessions, we had heard the different meanings of the Atmarama verse pertaining to how Krishna attracts by his qualities, even the impersonalists like, those situated in impersonal liberation like Shukadev Goswami, like the four Kumaras, Krishna attracts the Gopis, Krishna attracts the Queens of Dwaraka, Krishna attracts all young girls all over the universe, Krishna attracts trees and birds, Krishna attracts all young girls all over the universe, and animals. So nobody is without attraction for Krishna. That's why Krishna all-attractive is the suitable, most appropriate name for the Supreme Lord, who is all-attractive to everyone without exception. So continuing the description, the description of the varieties of Atmaramas who become attracted to Krishna and begin to render pure devotional service to Krishna.

### devotional service, purification, obstacles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Eg1Ig6SdK_0?t=279
**Verse References:** SB 11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna says the nature of pure devotional service is such that, even if somebody who begins devotional service is having some contamination in the heart, all that is completely nullified by the fire of devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Krishna instructing Uddhava from the Bhagavatam, explaining that devotional service burns away sinful activities and removes impediments, leading to automatic cleansing of the heart and enlightenment.

So here the description is continuing, as all fuel is burnt to ashes by a full-fledged fire, all sinful activities are totally erased, when one engages in devotional service to Me. This is statement from the Bhagavatam 11th canto when Krishna is instructing Uddhava. Krishna says the nature of pure devotional service is such that, even if somebody who begins devotional service is having some contamination in the heart, all that is completely nullified by the fire of devotional service. In this way, when all sinful activities are vanquished by the grace of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, one gradually vanquishes all kinds of impediments on the path of devotional service, as well as the ignorance resulting from these impediments. In the path of Bhakti, these material contaminations are obstacles. Now as one goes on rendering devotional service to Krishna, the heart becomes cleansed automatically without separate endeavour, and impediments are all gone, the obstacles are removed. And when the obstacles are removed, even the ignorance is vanquished. One becomes enlightened from within automatically without separate endeavour. After this one totally manifests his original love of Godhead through devotional service in nine different ways, hearing, chanting, remembering, etc.

### attraction to Krishna, material desires, Purusharthas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Eg1Ig6SdK_0?t=396
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But Bhakti is greater than these four Purusharthas."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that when a devotee is freed from material contamination, Krishna attracts their body, mind, and senses to devotional service, leading them to lose interest in the four Purusharthas: Dharma, Artha, Karma, and Moksha, as Bhakti is the fifth and superior goal.

When the devotee is freed from all sinful material activities, Krishna attracts his body, mind and senses to perform devotional service to him. See, as long as there is material contamination, we have attraction for so many material things. But when the material contamination is gone, we get attracted to Krishna. And Krishna attracts our total being, our body, our mind, our words, everything completely. Krishna attracts. And thus it is described, Krishna is very, very merciful and his transcendental qualities are very attractive. He is full of so many transcendental qualities. Now further, Lord Chaitanya explains, when one's mind, senses and body are attracted to the transcendental qualities of the Supreme Lord Hari, one gives up the four principles of material success. What are the four principles of material success? Dharma, Artha, Karma, Moksha. Dharma means religious performance. Artha means economic development. Karma means sense gratification. And Moksha means liberation. So, generally people who are pious in this material world, they are interested in four goals of a civilized human being, mentioned in the Shastra. Dharma, Artha, Karma, Moksha. But Bhakti is greater than these four Purusharthas. Bhakti is called the Panchama Purushartha, the fifth goal of a human being, of a living being. So, when a devotee becomes attracted by Krishna and begins to render devotional service, automatically such a devotee loses interest in Dharma, Artha, Karma, Moksha. Those four Purusharthas don't interest the devotee anymore. Completely, the devotee rises above these four Purusharthas.

### absolute truth, relative truth, Brahma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Eg1Ig6SdK_0?t=559
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Absolute truth is truth in all phases of time, under all circumstances, in all conditions."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to explain the word Brahma, indicating the absolute truth, contrasting it with relative truth and defining it as truth in all phases of time, circumstances, and conditions, further elaborating on its meaning according to the Vishnu Purana.

Now, the next set of meanings that Lord Chaitanya explains. The word Brahma indicates the Samambonam, the absolute truth, which is greater than all other truths. In this material world, whatever we know as truth is called relative truth. And Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, is the absolute truth. What is absolute truth? Absolute truth is truth in all phases of time, under all circumstances, in all conditions. Whereas relative truth is not truth in all phases of time. It is the original identity, Brahma means the original identity, and there can be nothing equal to that absolute truth. So, there is a definition of this absolute truth as Brahma in the Vishnu Purana. It is stated there, I offer my respectful obeisances to the absolute truth, the Samambonam. He is the all-pervasive, all-increasing subject matter for the great yogis. He is changeless, and he is the soul of all, the soul of all souls, the super soul, the supreme soul. Actually, the proper meaning of the word Brahma is the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is one without a second, and without whom nothing else exists.

---
### Krishna as Absolute Truth, creation, eternality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Eg1Ig6SdK_0?t=669
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the Absolute Truth in all phases of time, in all conditions of existence."
**Summary:** The speaker substantiates that the absolute truth without a second is Lord Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, quoting Vedic literature and the Bhagavatam to emphasize Krishna's existence before, during, and after creation, making Him the unchanging reality.

This is substantiated in so many different parts of the Vedic literature. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is quoting one verse, That absolute truth without a second is Lord Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. He is the Supreme Truth in the past, present and future. That is the evidence of all revealed scriptures. In the Bhagavatam it is stated, Prior to the cosmic creation, only I exist and no phenomena exist, either gross or subtle or primordial. Why do we say Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead? Because Krishna existed as Krishna before the creation even began. Krishna remains through the creation as Krishna. And after the creation, everything is annihilated in this material world. Krishna remains as Krishna. Therefore, Krishna is the Absolute Truth in all phases of time, in all conditions of existence. After creation only, I exist in everything. And after annihilation only, I remain eternally. What about the created beings? Aren't they the same? Aren't they the same? Aren't they also existing after the creation, through the creation? They exist not in the different bodily forms, not in the different types of material bodies they accept. No. Those are all temporarily given to them according to their desires for enjoyment, according to their karma, according to their association with the gunas, so many things. They keep on changing, changing, changing. Through all these changes, the changeless reality is Krishna, the Supreme Lord. Only He remains unchanged.

---

The living entities are accepting different bodies. Material nature is constantly undergoing change. It is called Shara, constantly changing. Whereas Krishna is the unchanging, constant, eternal reality through all these changes in the material world. The word Atma, indicates the highest truth, Krishna. He is the all-pervasive witness of all and He is the Supreme form. Now the word Brahma indicates the supreme absolute truth and the highest understanding of that truth is Krishna. The word Atma also indicates the highest truth, Krishna, who is all-pervasive witness of everything and He is the Supreme form. And Hari, the word Hari, indicating the personality of Godhead, is the all-pervasive original source of everything. He is therefore the super-soul of everything. Now Chaitanya Mahaprabhu explains

### paths to Krishna, Brahman, Paramatman, Bhagavan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Eg1Ig6SdK_0?t=890
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that there are three paths to attain Krishna: philosophical speculation (jnana marga), mystic yoga (ashtanga yoga), and devotional service (bhakti), and that the absolute truth appears in three forms: Brahman, Paramatman, and Bhagavan, depending on the process of understanding.

there are three ways to attain the lotus feet of the absolute truth, Krishna. There is a process of philosophical speculation, the jnana marga. There is the practice of mystic yoga, that is the ashtanga yoga system. And there is the process of bhakti, the execution of devotional service. Each of these have different characteristics. Now Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says the absolute truth is the same, but according to the process by which one understands Him, He appears in three forms, as Brahman, Paramatman and Bhagavan, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. I will stop here. We will discuss more in the next session. Hare Krishna.
