### jnana, yoga, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bLRRbgVTBSg?t=149
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But through devotional service one realizes Krishna, the personality of Godhead Bhagavan."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the three processes of realizing the Absolute Truth: philosophical speculation (Jnana Marga), mystic yoga (Ashtanga Yoga), and devotional service (Bhakti). He emphasizes that only devotional service leads to the realization of Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Bhagavan, which includes the realizations of Brahman and Paramatma.

Krishna means the Absolute Truth is realized through three processes: the process of philosophical speculation, the process of performing Ashtanga, you call it a mystic yoga process, and the process of devotional service. So, Lord Chaitanya has learned this. So, Lord Chaitanya is now explaining, although the words Brahma and Aatma indicate Krishna, their direct meanings refer only to the impersonal Brahman and the super soul respectively. Brahma means impersonal Brahman. Atma means the super-soul in the heart of every living being. So why the distinction is being made now is, because if one follows the path of philosophical speculation, Gyanamarga, the Absolute Truth manifests Himself as impersonal Brahman. That means through the Jnana Marga one cannot realize Krishna simply by following the Jnana Marga. The topmost possible realization of the Absolute Truth through Jnana Marga is only the impersonal Brahman. If one follows the path of Mystic Yoga, Ashtanga Yoga system, he manifests himself as a Supersoul. So through Ashtanga Yoga only the Paramatma can be realized. Only the Paramatma. But through devotional service one realizes Krishna, the personality of Godhead Bhagavan. In the Bhagavatam it is explained there are three features: the Absolute Truth, the impersonal Brahman, the localized Paramatma or the Supersoul and ultimately the personality of Godhead Bhagavan. So among these three features, realization of Bhagavan, the personality of Godhead is the highest realization and that includes the other two realizations of Brahman and Paramatma.

---
### spontaneous devotion, regulative devotion, Vrindavan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bLRRbgVTBSg?t=285
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By spontaneous devotional service one attains the original personality of Godhead Krishna and by the Regulative process one attains the expansion of the Supreme personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** Lord Chaitanya explains the two kinds of devotional activity: spontaneous and regulative. Spontaneous devotional service in Vrindavan leads to Krishna, while regulative service leads to expansions of the Supreme Lord. Spontaneous devotion is characterized by constant thoughts of Krishna and absorption in Him.

So now Lord Chaitanya explains there are two kinds of devotional activity: spontaneous and regulative. By spontaneous devotional service one attains the original personality of Godhead Krishna and by the Regulative process one attains the expansion of the Supreme personality of Godhead. Krishna is present as Krishna and Krishna is also present in his various forms as Narayana, Vasudeva, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha, Narasimha, Vamana etc. etc. So many other forms. All are Krishna only, the same Supreme person. But there are still some differences. So Lord Chaitanya says by executing spontaneous devotional service in Vrindavan one attains the original Supreme personality of Godhead Krishna. The nature of devotional service in Vrindavan is that it is spontaneous. Spontaneous means they don't have to follow the rules and regulations. They have somehow attraction for Krishna to a degree that they only think of Krishna all the time. Their heart is completely filled with thoughts of Krishna. Their mind is absorbed in Krishna. They have become mad after Krishna. Almost like mad people. They are not mad. They are very intelligent. They are very conscientious. But they are so much absorbed in thought of Krishna. So that is spontaneous devotional service in Vrindavan.

### spontaneous devotion, Krishna's accessibility, Shukadeva Goswami
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bLRRbgVTBSg?t=395
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Supreme personality of Godhead Krishna, the son of Mother Yashoda is accessible to those devotees engaged in spontaneous loving service."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Shukadeva Goswami, emphasizing that Krishna is easily accessible to those engaged in spontaneous loving service, unlike mental speculators or those focused on austerities or bodily identification.

And Lord Chaitanya quotes a verse from the Bhagavatam. This verse is spoken by Shukadeva Goswami. Shukadeva Goswami tells Pariksit Maharaj after describing one past time of Krishna being bound to Krishna. Krishna being bound by the rope of Mother Yashoda. Krishna submits himself to his devotee Mother Yashoda. So Shukadeva Goswami says the Supreme personality of Godhead Krishna, the son of Mother Yashoda is accessible to those devotees engaged in spontaneous loving service, but he is not as easily accessible to mental speculators, to those striving for self-realization by severe austerities, or to those who consider the body the same as the self. So other processes other than spontaneous devotional service cannot help one realize Krishna the Supreme personality of Godhead.

### regulative devotion, Vaikuntha, spiritual qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bLRRbgVTBSg?t=463
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By executing regulative devotional service one becomes an associate of Narayana and attains the Vaikunthalokas, the spiritual planets and the spiritual sky."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts spontaneous devotion with regulative devotional service, explaining that the latter involves strictly following the rules and regulations of Bhakti, leading to association with Narayana and attainment of the Vaikunthalokas. He also mentions that those who discuss Krishna's activities exhibit spiritual qualities and are elevated to the spiritual world.

Now what about regulative devotional service? Regulative devotional service is following the rules and regulations of Bhakti very strictly. By executing regulative devotional service one becomes an associate of Narayana and attains the Vaikunthalokas, the spiritual planets and the spiritual sky. There are two kinds of spiritual planets. One is Goloka Vrindavana, Krishna's personal abode. And the other set of spiritual planets, are the Vaikunthalokas, where the Supreme Lord as Narayana is the predominator, is the Lord of Vaikuntha, Vaikunthapati. Now the explanation further about spontaneous devotional service. Those who discuss the activities of Lord Krishna are on the highest platform of devotional life and they evince the symptoms of tears in the eyes and bodily jubilation. Very naturally they experience bhava, spiritual emotions. Such persons discharge devotional service to Krishna without practicing the rules and regulations of the mystic yoga system. You see, they possess all spiritual qualities. This is spoken by Brahma. And they are elevated to the spiritual world beyond the material world.

### devotee categories, bhakti yoga, desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bLRRbgVTBSg?t=566
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotional service, however, is so strong and independent that it can give one all the desired results."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that devotees are divided into three categories: desireless (Akama), those desiring liberation (moksha kama), and those desiring material perfection (sarva kama). He emphasizes that even with ulterior motives, engaging in bhakti yoga can lead to desired results, as devotional service is powerful and independent.

Now, among devotees, Chaitanya explains, Lord Chaitanya explains, the devotees are divided into three categories: Akama, those who are desireless, means they don't have any material desires. Then there are some who are moksha kama, those who desire liberation. And sarva kama, those who have desire, material perfection. So it is said in the Bhagavatam, one who is actually intelligent, although he may be a devotee free from material desires, a karmi desiring all kinds of material facilities, or a jnani desiring liberation, should seriously engage in bhakti yoga for the satisfaction of supreme peace. That is what the Indian personality has called it. So the meaning of the word udaradhi is buddhiman, intelligent or considerate. Because of this, even for one's own sense gratification, one engages in the devotional service of Lord Krishna. Actually, devotional service is meant for giving us bhakti. Let us say somebody takes to devotional service but their goal is not bhakti. Their goal is some material gain or their goal is liberation. Then what happens? Something very, very interesting happens. The other processes of self-realization cannot yield results unless they are associated with devotional service. Devotional service, however, is so strong and independent that it can give one all the desired results.

Krishna's kindness attracts devotees, even those with material desires, leading them to forget their initial goals and become absorbed in Bhakti.

Dhruva Maharaj has become a Bhikkhus and has begun to worship Supreme Lord Krishna. That's what happened to Dhruva Maharaj. He began to worship Supreme Lord Vishnu and Vishnu gave him darshan. Now when Vishnu gave darshan, Dhruva thought, I am doing all these austerities to ask from the Lord for what, a kingdom in this material world, that he compared to a piece of broken glass. He is looking for a piece of broken glass, foolishly, by doing devotional service, worshipping Vishnu. But when Vishnu appeared and gave him darshan, then he thought after seeing Vishnu, when I have got diamonds, why will I be interested in a piece of broken glass? So, Dhruva Maharaj told Vishnu, when Vishnu said, yes, what is the boon you want from me? So, Dhruva Maharaj said, when I obtained you, my dear Lord, I am not interested in asking any other benediction from you, I don't want any other benediction. It is like a person who has got diamonds. Why will he be interested? Why will he be interested? Why will he be interested? Why will he be interested? Why will he be interested? Why will he be interested? Why is he interested? Why is he interested. Why is he interested? Why is he interested? Why is he interested? So, Krishna is so kind that when a devotee foolishly desires some material gain, Krishna gives himself to the devotee and he attracts the devotee so very much. The devotee completely forgets or considers the divine insignificant any other material gain that he might have been interested in before he began Bhakti. So this is Krishna's way of attracting a devotee who may not know the real goal of Bhakti but still nevertheless renders devotional service to Krishna even with a desire for some material gain. So that is Krishna's kindness. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

We continue the discussion. This is a discussion from the previous session on the same verse, Atmarama verse. I will directly go to the other meanings or explanations of the Atmarama verse. It was described in the previous session that four kinds of pious people begin to render devotional service to Krishna because they have a pious background. They are apt to be devoted to Krishna. They are apt to surrender to Krishna. So Lord Chaitanya explains, because they have a pious background, all four types of people are to be considered greatly fortunate. Such people gradually give up material desires and become pure devotees. How does it work? Lord Chaitanya says, one is elevated to the platform of devotional life by the mercy of the Vaishnava, the bona fide spiritual master, and by the special mercy of Krishna. On that platform, one wants to give up all material desires and one gives up the association of unwanted people also. Thus one is elevated to the platform of pure devotional service. Okay. Even though those who begin devotional service with a material motive are considered to be engaged in mixed devotional service, but gradually they give up all material desires, they give up unwanted association and they are elevated to the platform of pure devotional service. What happens to the platform of pure devotional service?

### Krishna's mercy, material desires, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oI5kN1Ecg50?t=174
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna forcibly gives one shelter at his lotus feet, where one will forget all other desires."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that when devotees with material desires worship Krishna, Krishna, out of his mercy, may not grant those desires if they are detrimental, but instead purifies the devotee and draws them closer to his lotus feet, fostering detachment from material pursuits.

When they come to the platform of pure devotional service, something very wonderful happens. When merciful Lord Krishna understands that a foolish devotee desires material prosperity, he gratefully gives him shelter off his lotus feet. In this way, the Lord covers the devotee's undesirable ambitions. See, another characteristic of Krishna. Whenever Krishna is in a position to fulfill one's desire, he undoubtedly does so, but he does not avoid anything which after being enjoyed will cause one to petition him again and again to fulfill further desires. Sometimes people ask, what is the difference between worshipping a devata for some material benefit and worshipping Krishna for the same material benefit? There is a world of difference. There is a big, big difference. That is what is being said here. If somebody worships a devata for some material benefit, if it is according to scriptural direction he is doing it, then the devata will award the particular benefit without any consideration whether it is good for the person whom he is awarding or not good. Devata will simply award it. That's all. According to, strictly the laws of material nature as stated in the scriptures. Now, when Krishna is worshipped by a devotee, by somebody who wants material benefit, then Krishna considers whether the benediction that he is asking from me, whether it is good for the devotee or not. Krishna has that consideration. If it is not good, Krishna will not award him that particular material benefit. Instead, Krishna purifies that devotee. So, it is said here, when one has other desires other than bhakti, but at the same time such a person engages in Krishna's service, Krishna forcibly gives one shelter at his lotus feet, where one will forget all other desires. See, this is Krishna's special mercy.

---
### Krishna's kindness, devatas, material entanglement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oI5kN1Ecg50?t=329
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna will forcibly give shelter to such a devotee at his lotus feet."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of asking for poison to illustrate Krishna's kindness in contrast to devatas, who grant material desires without considering their consequences, leading to further entanglement in material existence.

Krishna is so very kind. I am foolishly going and asking Krishna, give me some poison. Actually, what should I be asking from Krishna? Nectar. But I do not know. Poison is actually not good for me. I think poison is something very nice. So, I am asking Krishna, give me some poison. I think poison is something very nice. I am mistaken. Poison is like nectar. So, Krishna, will he simply give poison because you are asking for poison? No, never. What about devatas? You go and ask devata some poison. Will devata give? Yeah, he will give. He never refuses. When you are within the, you are following the scriptural injunctions and it is given the shastras, this devata can avoid this material benefit, some other devata can avoid some other material benefit. These material benefits are simply entangling the person more and more and more. The very cause of all our miseries is material existence, entanglement in material existence. And foolish people, what do they do? They think that if we worship some devata and gets more material benefit, oh, we can become free from all problems. What they don't realize is material benefits are of the nature that they further entangle you to make you suffer more and more and more and more material miseries. Neither the devatas will refuse you some material benefit that you may ask if you follow all the rules. Nor you yourself can realize that I am becoming more and more entangled. I am not getting free from this entanglement. I am not able to put an end to my miseries in this material world. Whereas, when somebody worships Krishna and goes and asks for some material benefit, Krishna will forcibly give shelter to such a devotee at his lotus feet. And that will make the devotee forget about all material nonsense, all material benefits. Forget about it. And he becomes...

What happens? Such a devotee actually becomes elevated to Krishna Consciousness and he will only want Bhakti and nothing else. The nectar, nectar of devotion. Bhakti, Rasa, Amrita. Now, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu begins to explain that those who are following the Gyanam Arga, if they come in contact with a pure devotee or some or the other take to devotional, pure devotional service, then they give up their desire for liberation, they give up their desire for any material gain, and they simply become interested in pure devotional service. So, Lord Chaitanya explains, there are two kinds of worshippers on the path of philosophical speculation. One is called a Brahma Upasaka, a worshipper of the impersonal Brahman. And the other is called Moksha Kamshi or one who desires liberation. Two kinds of people on the Gyanam Arga. Two kinds of worshippers.

### impersonal Brahman, devotional service, transcendental body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oI5kN1Ecg50?t=560
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such a person is awarded a transcendental body to engage in Lord Krishna's service directly."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the three types of Brahma Upasakas and explains that even those initially seeking Brahman realization through devotional service are ultimately drawn away from the impersonal Brahman and awarded a transcendental body to directly serve Krishna.

Now, among the worshippers of the impersonal Brahman, there are three types of people. The beginner, the one whose thoughts are absorbed in Brahman, and the one who has actually attained liberation by merging into impersonal Brahman. So, three kinds of people who are worshippers of impersonal Brahman. Now, without a tinge of devotional service, simply by following the path of philosophical speculation, one cannot attain the Brahman platform. One cannot be elevated to transcendence, not possible. So, when one actually begins devotional service, even for the sake of attaining some Brahman realization, still, one in devotional service is attracted away from the impersonal Brahman. Such a person is awarded a transcendental body to engage in Lord Krishna's service directly. See what happens with devotional service. When a devotee gets a spiritual body, he can remember the transcendental qualities of Krishna simply by being attracted to Krishna's transcendental qualities. Ittham bhuta guno hari. Krishna is full of transcendental qualities. Any one quality the devotee gets attracted, he becomes a pure devotee engaged directly in devotional service to Krishna. He starts off as an impersonalist worshipping the impersonal Brahman. But then to attain success in that worship, he has to mix some devotional service. So, when he begins to do some devotional service, then all these things happen. He is taken away from the goal of impersonal Brahman and is awarded a spiritual body. In spiritual body, he becomes aware of Krishna's transcendental qualities and gets attracted and becomes a pure devotee directly engaged in Krishna's service.

### liberated soul, Krishna's pastimes, association with devotees
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oI5kN1Ecg50?t=726
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's pastimes are supremely attractive to everyone."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes that even a liberated soul merged in Brahman is attracted to Krishna's pastimes and renders devotional service. He emphasizes the power of hearing about Krishna from pure devotees, leading to a revolution in the heart.

So, there is a quotation that supports this particular explanation by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. This quotation is that even a liberated soul merged in the impersonal Brahman is attracted to the pastimes of Krishna. He thus installs a Deity and renders the Lord devotional service. See, the situation of being merged in the impersonal Brahman is very very dull, very unattractive after the initial excitement that is there in the beginning. So, then such a person, it is said, if he is fortunate to hear about the pastimes of Krishna from a pure devotee, then he becomes attracted to the pastimes of Krishna. Krishna's pastimes are supremely attractive to everyone. But one has to hear that from the right source. Then the attraction is very very much powerful and one becomes a pure devotee. Then what about those engaged in mystic yoga practice? The example is given of the Navayogendras. From their very births, the nine great mystic yogis, Navayogendras, were impersonal philosophers of the absolute truth. They knew the absolute truth only as impersonal Brahman. They did not know about the personality of God. But because they heard about Lord Krishna's qualities from Lord Brahma, Lord Shiva and the great sage Narada, all the three are great devotees. Brahma is a great devotee, Shiva is a great devotee, Narada is a great devotee. So, these Navayogendras, they heard about Krishna's qualities from these three great devotees. They also became Krishna's devotees. See, association with devotees in which they hear about Krishna's transcendental qualities. Anything one hears about Krishna from the pure devotees is going to bring about a revolution in the heart. So, that is being explained here.

In the 11th canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, there is a full chapter, there is a full description of the devotional service of the nine yogendras, Navayogendras, who rendered devotional service because they were attracted by the Lord's transcendental qualities. These Navayogendras, when they heard about Krishna's transcendental qualities from Brahma, Shiva and Narada, even though they were situated in understanding of the Vedic knowledge, understanding of the impersonal Brahman from Vedas, they became attracted to Krishna and they wanted to enter Dwaraka. In this way, they finally ended up in the place known as Rangakshetra, Sri Rangam. They ended up that place. So, I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.7.10 Part 7 | 21-12-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Atmarama, Transcendentalists, Liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/efmL6P_NIH4?t=67
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This Atmarama word says that all different varieties of Atmarama, all different varieties of self-satisfied personalities, they, even though they are freed from material bondage, they engage in pure devotional service to Krishna because Krishna is full of transcendental qualities which are very, very attractive."
**Summary:** Lord Chaitanya explains the Atmarama verse, describing three categories of transcendentalists: those following Jnana Marga, those following mystic yoga, and devotees following bhakti yoga, further dividing the first category into those desiring liberation, those already liberated, and those who have realized Brahman.

We are continuing to describe the Atmarama words as explained by Lord Chaitanya to Srila Sanatan Goswami. This Atmarama word says that all different varieties of Atmarama, all different varieties of self-satisfied personalities, they, even though they are freed from material bondage, they engage in pure devotional service to Krishna because Krishna is full of transcendental qualities which are very, very attractive. Among the transcendentalists, there are three different categories of transcendentalists according to the process of transcendental realization they may accept. So this was mentioned in the previous session. Those who follow the Jnana Marga, the path of philosophical speculation, they are actually one category of transcendentalists, who desire liberation by merging into the impersonal Brahman. Then the second category of transcendentalists are those who follow the path of mystic yoga, Ashtanga yoga, meditation, and they desire to realize the absolute truth as Paramatma, because they meditate on the form of the Lord, Paramatma in the heart. And the third category of transcendentalists are the devotees, or those who follow bhakti yoga, devotional service, and they directly engage in devotional service to Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the Bhagavan feature of the absolute truth. So now Lord Chaitanya is describing the first category of transcendentalists, those who wish to merge into the impersonal, are also divided into three further subcategories: those desiring to be liberated, those who are already liberated, and those who have realized Brahman.

---
### Liberation, Devotional Service, Association of Devotees
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/efmL6P_NIH4?t=227
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because without a devotional service, one cannot get liberation ultimately."
**Summary:** Lord Chaitanya explains that even those desiring liberation render devotional service to Krishna because it is essential for liberation. He emphasizes the power of associating with devotees, which can awaken dormant devotional service and lead one to give up the desire for impersonal liberation.

Now Lord Chaitanya further says, there are many people within this material world who desire liberation, and for this purpose, they render devotional service to Krishna. Why is it that those who desire liberation render devotional service to Krishna? Because without a devotional service, one cannot get liberation ultimately. So in the Bhagavatam it is explained, those who want to be relieved from the reality, who want to be relieved from the clutches of the material energy, they give up worship of the various devatas, demigods. These demigods have fearful bodily features, ghorarupam, such peaceful devotees who are not envious of the demigods, worship the different forms of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Narayana. Again it is said, there may be some people who are attached to demigod worship, but if such people associate fortunately with the devotees of the Supreme Lord, then their dormant devotional service and appreciation of the Lord's qualities gradually awakens within their hearts. In this way, they also engage in Krishna's devotional service and give up their desire for liberation and also give up the desire to merge into the existence of the impersonal Brahman. So the association of devotees is a very, very powerful factor for people to understand the greatness of bhakti.

---
### Association of Devotees, Bhakti, Happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/efmL6P_NIH4?t=361
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although there are many faults in this material world, there is one good opportunity: the association with devotees. Such association brings about great happiness."
**Summary:** The lecture emphasizes the importance of associating with devotees, citing scriptures that praise its power to bring happiness and weaken the desire for impersonal liberation. It explains that the happiness derived from devotional service is far greater and more satisfying than the pursuit of liberation.

Now the association of devotees is particularly praised in many scriptures. One such scriptural quotation is cited by Lord Chaitanya. This scriptural quotation says, although there are many faults in this material world, there is one good opportunity: the association with devotees. Such association brings about great happiness. Due to this good quality, our strong desire to achieve liberation by merging into the Brahman effulgence has become weakened. What happens is somebody has got a desire to attain liberation by merging into the impersonal Brahman, but they associate with devotees and experience in devotional association a different quality of spiritual happiness due to devotional service. Then they give up this desire for liberation because that happiness of devotional service is so much greater and so much a satisfaction and so much nicer that automatically they lose interest in liberation.

---
### Narada, Saunaka Rishi, Krishna's Qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/efmL6P_NIH4?t=453
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Simply by meeting Krishna or receiving Krishna's special favour, one can give up the desire for liberation."
**Summary:** Lord Chaitanya gives examples of Saunaka Rishi giving up the desire for liberation after associating with Narada, and how simply meeting Krishna or receiving His favor can lead one to abandon the pursuit of liberation due to attraction to Krishna's transcendental qualities.

Now here is another example given by Lord Chaitanya. By associating with the great saint Narada, Saunaka and other great sages gave up the desire for liberation and engaged in Krishna's devotional service. This Saunaka Rishi was the leader of some great sages who assembled in a holy place called Naimisharanya in order to engage in a thousand year sacrifice. Now in the beginning, their aim was liberation, but after association with the great saint Narada, who was himself a great devotee, these Saunaka Rishis, they gave up the desire for liberation and became engaged in Krishna's devotional service. Another instance is being mentioned by Lord Chaitanya here. Simply by meeting Krishna or receiving Krishna's special favour, one can give up the desire for liberation. How does that happen? Being attracted by the transcendental qualities of Krishna, one can engage in Krishna's service.

---
### Krishna's Service, Transcendental Qualities, Attraction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/efmL6P_NIH4?t=540
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's service is something which is very, very pleasing to the heart. It is natural. It is due to the strong attraction by Krishna's transcendental qualities which are innumerable."
**Summary:** The lecture contrasts service in the material world with service to Krishna, emphasizing that Krishna's service is inherently pleasing and natural due to the attraction of His innumerable transcendental qualities, which appeal to different individuals.

Krishna's service should not be mistaken to be something like service rendered to another person in this material world. In material world, service means it is something not so very desirable, but is something which is unavoidable. Just like people render service to get some money, to get some material benefit, to get some favour, to learn something new. So, so many different reasons in this material world, people are engaged in service of another person. But serving Krishna is never to be mistaken like service rendered to another person in the material world. Krishna's service is something which is very, very pleasing to the heart. It is natural. It is due to the strong attraction by Krishna's transcendental qualities which are innumerable. And therefore, Krishna is able to attract different categories of persons who may have attraction for different transcendental qualities of Krishna.

---
### Dwaraka Dham, Spiritual Bliss, Impersonal Cultivation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/efmL6P_NIH4?t=645
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In this Dwaraka Dham, I am being attracted by the supreme personality of God, Krishna, who is personified spiritual bliss."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the experience of someone visiting Dwaraka Dham and being overwhelmed by Krishna's spiritual bliss, leading them to question the value of impersonal cultivation. Krishna is described as full of ananda, and seeing Him brings great happiness.

This is an instance of somebody who happened to visit Dwaraka Dham, where Krishna was ruling as the king of Dwaraka, Dwaraka Disha. So this personality is describing his experience. In this Dwaraka Dham, I am being attracted by the supreme personality of God, Krishna, who is personified spiritual bliss. See, Krishna is no ordinary person. Krishna is the Supreme Lord who is full of transcendental bliss, spiritual bliss, ānanda-maya. The Vedanta Sutra says, ānanda-mayo abhyāsāt. Krishna is full of ānanda. And simply by seeing Krishna, one can feel that great happiness which is characteristic of Krishna's personality. Simply by seeing him. It's so pleasing to the senses, to the mind, to the heart, seeing Krishna. Then this devotee, this personality who is feeling great happiness by simply seeing Krishna in Dwaraka Dham, he began to think, I have wasted so much time trying to become self-realized through impersonal cultivation. Before one comes to know what is the real nature of Krishna's personality, one may be engaged in Brahman realization, impersonal Brahman realization.

---
### Impersonal Brahman, Difficulties, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/efmL6P_NIH4?t=751
**Verse References:** BG 12.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "kleśo adhikatarasteśāṁ avyaktāsaktaśetasām. It is very, very tedious. It is filled with so many difficulties."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Bhagavad Gita to illustrate the tedious nature of impersonal Brahman realization, contrasting it with the happiness derived from seeing Krishna's form. He concludes by stating that various paths can lead individuals to Krishna's service and the perfection of devotion.

But that is so tedious. In the 12th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna explains, kleśo adhikatarasteśāṁ avyaktāsaktaśetasām. Avyaktā is the impersonal feature of the Absolute Truth, the impersonal Brahman. And those who are not having knowledge of Krishna, they engage in such a... meditation or worship or cultivation of knowledge of impersonal Brahman. And that is very, very tedious. kleśo adhikatarasteśāṁ avyaktāsaktaśetasām. It is very, very tedious. It is filled with so many difficulties. So therefore this personality in Dwaraka Dham, after just seeing Krishna's beautiful, pleasing, form, transcendental form, became so much filled with happiness, that thought, why was I wasting time trying to become self-realized through impersonal Brahman method? So like this, different varieties of personalities, even though they may be engaged in self-realization, through other methods than bhakti, they somehow or the other, by contact with a pure devotee, by contacting Krishna, by associating with devotees, by mercy of Krishna, so many different ways, they become attracted to Krishna and they naturally engage in Krishna's service, to reach the perfection of devotion. So I'll stop here.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. ### devotional service, liberation, fall down
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KlQcgvwrdF8?t=81
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though somebody may be liberated by some other process other than devotional service, they cannot sustain themselves in liberation without engaging in devotional service."
**Summary:** This segment explains that even those liberated through other processes eventually fall down if they don't engage in devotional service, as the soul's nature is to be active.

Here is that even though somebody may be liberated by some other process other than devotional service, they cannot sustain themselves in liberation without engaging in devotional service. The nature of the soul is to be always active. Soul cannot be without activity, though somebody may try to be inactive for some time, but very soon that person who is inactive is forced to begin activity. So, the explanation is given here by Lord Chaitanya. Those who are liberated by devotional service become more and more attracted by the transcendental qualities of Krishna and engage in his service. Bhakti means devotional service. Prabhupāda did not translate bhakti as devotion. He translates bhakti as devotional service indicating that bhakti means activity but not ordinary activity, not material activity. It is completely spiritual activity. What kind of spiritual activity it is? It is service to Kṛṣṇa. Serving Kṛṣṇa. And that is not same as material service rendered to some material person in this world, no. Now, Lord Caitanya is telling, those who are liberated by the speculative process, philosophical speculation, jñānāmārtha, eventually fall down, again, due to offensive activities like Sne properties. Now there is a statement in the Srimad Bhagavatam which says this, how those who are not engaged in devotional service of Krishna, even though they are liberated, they fall down. They don't sustain their existence on the liberated platform.

---
### devotional service, liberation, Garbhastuti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KlQcgvwrdF8?t=229
**Verse References:** SB 10.2
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who think they are liberated in this life but are without devotional service to you are of impure intelligence."
**Summary:** This segment quotes a prayer from the Srimad Bhagavatam (Garbhastuti) where the Devatas state that those who consider themselves liberated but lack devotional service are of impure intelligence and will fall down.

So what is that statement in the Bhagavatam? O Lotus-eyed one, this is a prayer offered by the Devatas to Krishna when he was in the womb of Mother Devaki and the Devatas were offering wonderful prayers to Krishna in the womb of Devaki, Garbhastuti, it's called, 10th canto, 2nd chapter. So there it is said by the Devatas, O Lotus-eyed one, they are addressing the Supreme Lord Krishna. Those who think they are liberated in this life but are without devotional service to you are of impure intelligence. Although they accept severe austerities and penances and rise to the spiritual position, to impersonal Brahman realization, they fall down again because they neglect to worship your lotus feet. There has to be some engagement after liberation. If there is no engagement after liberation, due to inactivity, one becomes very much disturbed in the mind. And eventually, such a person, without taking shelter of Krishna's lotus feet, cannot engage in the spiritual process. This is the statement in the Bhagavatam.

### devotional service, liberation, Brahman realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KlQcgvwrdF8?t=317
**Verse References:** BG 14.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One who is thus transcendentally situated at once realizes the Supreme Brahman and becomes fully joyful."
**Summary:** This segment references the Bhagavad Gita to explain the position of someone transcendentally situated on the Brahman platform, emphasizing joyfulness and equality towards all beings, ultimately leading to pure devotional service.

One cannot engage in spiritual activity. So what does the person do? The person will come down to material activity and that's called a fall down. Now, in the Bhagavad Gita, Lord Krishna explains what is the position of a person who is transcendentally situated on the Brahman platform. What is the actual position? Krishna says, One who is thus transcendentally situated at once realizes the Supreme Brahman and becomes fully joyful. Brahma bhuta prasamna atma. He never laments or desires to have anything. He is equally disposed to every living entity. In that state, he attains pure devotional service unto me.

### devotional service, Brahman realization, spiritual vision
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KlQcgvwrdF8?t=375
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When one actually comes to the transcendental platform one is able to realize the Supreme Brahman distinctly the Lord different from the individual soul the Supreme Personality of God."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the progressive transcendentalist who reaches the Brahman platform, experiences freedom from lamentation and hankering, sees equality in all beings, and ultimately realizes the Supreme Brahman as distinct from the individual soul, leading to devotional service.

This is a progressive transcendentalist who comes to the Brahman platform and then is able to experience freedom from lamentation and hankering and is able to see with spiritual vision equality of law and living beings even though they may be situated in different bodies. They are ultimately spiritual beings. Such a person, such a person becomes completely joyful and realizes the Supreme Brahman. Brahman realization may also mean that I am of the same quality as the Absolute Truth. I am also Brahman. Aham Brahmasmi. But when one is factually situated on the transcendental platform through gradual elevation through spiritual processes when one actually comes to the transcendental platform one is able to realize the Supreme Brahman distinctly the Lord different from the individual soul the Supreme Personality of God. And in that state one attains pure devotional service to the Supreme Lord. One becomes a devotee.

**Summary:** This segment explains that through devotional service, one attains their constitutional position as Krishna conscious beings, achieving a transcendental body even in this lifetime (Jeevan Mukta) and engaging in service at Krishna's lotus feet.

And what happens? One who has attained his constitutional position by strength of devotional service. What is his constitutional position? We are originally Krishna conscious beings. So that is called our constitutional position. And by strength of devotional service one is able to attain a transcendental body even in this lifetime. Jeevan Mukta. Liberated from material bondage and attains a transcendental form. What is a transcendental form? That is actually our original spiritual form which is actually covered presently by this material body and mind. Material mind. So by elevating to the spiritual platform through devotional service one attains a transcendental body. And being attracted by Lord Krishna's transcendental qualities one fully engages in service at his lotus feet. Krishna's lotus feet is the ultimate shelter for everyone. Krishna accommodates everybody. But to aim at the lotus feet of Krishna is possible only by engaging properly in devotional service to Krishna. Then further.

### maya, fear, material existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KlQcgvwrdF8?t=571
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Material existence means fear."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the position of those who oppose Krishna consciousness, explaining that material existence is characterized by fear due to the influence of maya, which is constantly changing and can bring sudden calamities.

Bhagavatam explains the position of a person in this material world who opposes Krishna consciousness due to this material influence one becomes conditioned and fearful due to the influence of maya. Material existence means influence of maya. The material energy. And that influence is such that one becomes fearful. Material existence means fear. Bhayam. It is a concomitant factor of material existence. That means in the material existence under the influence of maya one cannot avoid fear. People sometimes they say I am fearless. I am bold. But that is all under the illusion that I am safe or I am powerful. I am happy. I have got good protection against all dangers etc. But all these are a temporary position. It is a temporary position. Because maya is constantly changing. Adibhutam charo bhavah. And in this changing situation suddenly some material calamity natural calamity may afflict a person who is otherwise thinking I am very safe. Or suddenly some enemy may attack. Suddenly something may happen which can change our situation. A person has heart attack. And completely becomes fearful. Now what is to be done?

---
### devotional service, liberation, maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KlQcgvwrdF8?t=694
**Verse References:** BG 7.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Indeed there is no other way to get factual liberation from maya other than executing faithfully bhakti yoga devotional service to Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that the only way to achieve factual liberation from maya is through faithful devotional service (bhakti yoga) to Krishna, referencing the Bhagavad Gita to explain how the material energy is difficult to overcome without surrendering to Krishna.

So by executing devotional service faithfully one is liberated from maya. Indeed there is no other way to get factual liberation from maya other than executing faithfully bhakti yoga devotional service to Krishna. Krishna also says in the Bhagavad Gita in the seventh chapter fourteenth verse he says this divine energy of mind consisting of the three material modes of nature three guna sarto guna, rajo guna, tamo guna they are the components of the influence of the material energy called maya which influences us in such a way that we cannot overcome the material influence by our own strength by our own endeavour not possible. daivi he sha gunamayi mamamaya duratyaya. But Krishna also says those who have surrendered unto me mam eva ye prapadyante maya metam tarantite. They can easily cross beyond the influence of this material energy.

### devotional service, liberation, speculative knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KlQcgvwrdF8?t=793
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotional service unto the Supreme Lord is the only auspicious path."
**Summary:** This segment concludes that liberation is only possible through devotional service, stating that speculative knowledge without devotion leads to trouble and fruitless labor, like beating a husk devoid of grain.

So the conclusion of all these statements is that one cannot attain liberation without rendering devotional service. Liberation is possible only by devotional service. Technically somebody may think I am liberated by merging into impersonal Brahman or technically somebody may think I am liberated by meditating on Paramatma but factual liberation real liberation complete liberation perfect liberation is possible only by becoming fixed up in devotional service. So there is a statement like that in the Bhagavatam also which says devotional service unto the Supreme Lord is the only auspicious path. If one gives it up simply for speculative knowledge or the understanding that these living beings are spirit souls and the material world is false then such a person undergoes a great deal of trouble. He only gains troublesome and inauspicious actions. His actions are like beating a husk that is already devoid of grain. His labor becomes fruitless.

### devotional service, liberation, Brahman realization

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Real liberation is taking shelter of the lotus feet of Krishna and being fixed in devotional service to him."
**Summary:** This segment explains that those who think they are liberated by realizing they are Brahman and merging into the Absolute Truth are actually undergoing difficulty, and real liberation is found in taking shelter of Krishna's lotus feet and being fixed in devotional service.

This is a statement by Lord Brahma when he offers prayers to the Supreme Lord Krishna. Somebody may think this material world is false. We have nothing to do with this material world and engages in cultivation of knowledge through spirit. We engage in cultivation of knowledge through speculation. And through this method they may think I have become liberated because I have realized I am Brahman and I have merged myself into the existence of Brahman as the Absolute Truth. So such people they are actually simply undergoing so much of difficulty due to severe austerities. They do not get real liberation by such a process. Real liberation is described in that term taking shelter of the lotus feet of Krishna and being fixed in devotional service to him. That is real liberation. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.7.10 Part 9 | 24-12-2021
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### compassion, transformation, association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4yj7fRkzS9U?t=34
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When I see animals suffer due to pain, then I become very happy."
**Summary:** Narada Muni encounters a cruel hunter named Mrigari and begins to transform him by questioning his practice of half-killing animals and explaining the karmic consequences.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udhirayet. In this session also we will continue discussion of the Atmarama verse. Lord Chaitanya tells a very nice story of how a hunter, a cruel hunter, a very very uncivilized person, how he became a great devotee by the association of Srila Narada Muni, the greatest devotee of the Lord. Once upon a time, the great saint Narada, after visiting Lord Narayana in the Vaikuntha, went to Prayag. Prayag is that holy place, Allahabad, where there is a sangam, a confluence of three rivers, Ganga, Yamuna and Saraswati. And it's a very very holy place. And he saw a deer which was lying on the path through the forest, and this deer was pierced with an arrow. It had broken legs, and it was crying out of pain. That arrow had pierced its body, and it was crying out of pain. He went a little ahead and saw another boar which was pierced by a similar arrow. Its legs were also broken. It was also crying out in pain. And further he went, saw a rabbit also, same way it was suffering, with the arrow pierced, legs are broken, it is crying in pain. So Narada Muni, he was pained to see at heart that all these living beings are suffering so much. Then he saw a hunter hiding behind one tree. He was having so many arrows and he was ready to kill more animals. This hunter was having a black complexion, a dark complexion. He had reddish eyes and he appeared very very fierce. He was like the superintendent of death. Yamarath standing there with bow and arrows in his hands to kill so many more animals. Now Narada Muni, he left the path which was there in the forest and he went to the hunter who was hiding behind a tree to kill more animals. And as soon as Narada Muni approached the hunter, so many animals, they started running away. The hunter, he wanted to chastise Narada Muni with abusive language, that you spoiled my whole arrangement for killing animals. But when Narada Muni came closer to the hunter, due to Narada Muni's divine association or divine presence, that hunter restrained his tongue and did not abuse Narada Muni. He said, "Oh great saintly person, why are you leaving the general path and you are approaching me in this forest? Simply by seeing you, all the animals I was hunting have now run away. You have disturbed my occupation." So Narada Muni replied to this hunter, "I have come to you to clear one doubt that is there in my mind." So Narada Muni asked that hunter, "Those animals which are half killed, three animals I saw. I saw a deer, I saw a boar, I saw a rabbit. Were you the one who actually shot those arrows and half killed those animals?" The hunter replied, yes.

---
### karma, renunciation, guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4yj7fRkzS9U?t=494
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In your next life, they will kill you and give you so much suffering that is waiting for you."
**Summary:** Narada Muni explains the consequences of half-killing animals, leading the hunter to fear karmic reactions and seek liberation from sin. Narada then instructs him to break his bow, give up his possessions, and rely on him for sustenance.

Narada Muni inquired from this hunter, "Why are you not killing those animals completely? You are half killing them by piercing one arrow which does not fully kill them. Why are you doing like this?" Then the hunter replied to Narada Muni, "My name is Mrigari. Mrigari means I am the enemy of the animals. My father taught me how to kill in this way, half kill animals by piercing one arrow." And this hunter is telling Narada, "When I see animals suffer due to pain, then I become very happy." So Narada Muni told this hunter, "Can I beg from you one thing?" The hunter said, yes. "What do you want? You want the skin of some animals, tiger skin, deer skin, I can give you." Narada Muni said, "No, no, no. I do not want any skin from you. I want to request you that kindly don't half kill animals. You kill them completely, fully kill them. Don't half kill animals." So the hunter asked, "What's the difference between half killing and fully killing the animals?" So Narada Muni replied that when you half kill the animals, they suffer so much more pain than if you were to fully kill the animals. See, as a hunter, your business is to kill animals. Now that's an offense on your part, killing animals. But if you unnecessarily give them so much more pain by half killing them, you incur very great sin. So this is Narada Muni's divine influence on the hunter. So then the hunter began to inquire further. So in what way is this greater sin? So Narada Muni said, "These animals who are half killed by you, when they are getting unnecessary pain because you have not fully killed them, in your next life, they will kill you and give you so much suffering that is waiting for you." So by the association of Narada Muni, this hunter became convinced, yes, yes, what I am doing is very, very wrong. I have to suffer so much. I've killed so many animals and given them so much pain. So he became afraid. Then he said, "You see, I have been taught this killing business from my childhood. Now I have accumulated so much of sin. How am I going to become free from these sinful reactions?" See, this is the advantage of holy association, association with a pure devotee. One becomes purified and one becomes enlightened. And this hunter is inquiring, how can I become free from sinful reactions? So Narada Muni said, "If you are interested, I will find a way how you can become liberated. But before that, will you do what I say?" Hunter said, yes, yes. Narada Muni said, "Throw away this bow, break it and throw it away. Then I will tell you what next you should do." So the hunter said, "If I break my bow, how will I maintain myself? I have no other means of subsistence." So Narada Muni said, "Don't worry. I will supply you food every day. You don't worry about food, how you are going to get food. You throw that, break that bow and throw it away." So then Narada Muni's assurance, the hunter actually broke his bow and threw it away.

---
### devotion, transformation, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4yj7fRkzS9U?t=629
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "News spread in the surrounding villages that this hunter, cruel hunter has become a great Vaishnava."
**Summary:** Narada Muni instructs the hunter to distribute his wealth, build a cottage by the river, grow a tulsi plant, circumambulate it while offering water, and chant the Hare Krishna mantra. The hunter follows these instructions, and news of his transformation spreads.

Then Narada Muni said that you return home. You distribute whatever riches or wealth you have to brahmanas, to saintly persons. And then you and your wife should leave home taking only some minimum clothes to wear. That's all. Then you go to the river and there you construct a small cottage and grow one tulsi plant in the bank of the river. Then you should daily circumambulate tulsi by offering water and you should chant the Hare Krishna mantra continuously. I will send you sufficient food every day and you take as much as you want. There is no restriction I am imposing. Then after that, Narada Muni, he went to those three animals, pulled out the arrow that was struck in their body and then with his gentle touch, he actually nicely massaged the wounded portion and the animals immediately got up, felt relief and ran away from the place. Then the hunter was impressed. Oh, Narada Muni saved those three animals which were half dead, half killed. So he was understruck. This saintly person is an extraordinary saint. So he paid his respects to Narada Muni, bowed down and went home. Then after going home, Narada Muni of course left that place and went away. Then this hunter exactly followed the instructions of Narada Muni and news spread in the surrounding villages that this hunter, cruel hunter has become a great Vaishnava. He is sitting in front of a tulsi plant, he is chanting holy name and he is watering the tulsi and he has given up this business of killing animals.

---
### devotion, compassion, association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4yj7fRkzS9U?t=792
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "A man in devotional service is automatically decorated with all good qualities, including non-violence."
**Summary:** A year later, Narada Muni returns with Parvata Muni and witnesses the hunter's profound transformation, exemplified by his careful avoidance of harming even ants. Narada explains that devotional service automatically cultivates good qualities like non-violence.

So, so many people started coming to see him. They would bring some grains, some fruits, some edibles like that. Then this hunter, former hunter turned devotee, he was wonderstruck. Narada Muni is sending so much for me. And he would, with his wife, he would chant, he would accept little food and he would go on chanting, as instructed by Narada Muni. Then a year passed and Narada Muni was with another saint, Parvata Muni. So Narada, while travelling with Parvata Muni, he said, when he came in the vicinity of Prayag, he said, let us visit one former hunter's place and let me see what he is doing. I instructed him to stop killing animals and chant Hare Krishna. Let us see what he is doing. So as they started approaching the hut, the cottage of that former hunter turned devotee, they could see the hunter is approaching them. And as he was approaching them, he was carefully examining the ground. And what was he doing? There were so many ants which were running here and there on the path. So he took his cloth and gently, you know, moved the ants away from his path so that he will not stamp on the ants and kill them. So seeing this, Parvata Muni was astonished. Then Narada Muni said, "My dear hunter, such behavior is not at all astonishing. A man in devotional service is automatically decorated with all good qualities, including non-violence." So then, the hunter received these two saintly persons in a small cottage. He spread out a straw mat for them to sit. And with great devotion, he offered them some water. He washed their feet. And then he drank that water which had washed the feet of these two saintly persons, Narada Muni and Parvata Muni. Then he began to dance, chanting Hare Krishna. Narada Muni realized that this hunter has, former hunter has followed his instructions and become a first class devotee because of chanting Hare Krishna and worshiping Tulasi and giving up this business of killing animals. So this is an instance of an uncivilized hunter but associated with Narada becoming a great devotee. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### liberation, attraction to Krishna, Srimad Bhagavatam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WAK0W7hdh2k?t=56
**Verse References:** SB 1.7.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even liberated souls are attracted to Srimad Bhagavatam because it describes Krishna who is full of unlimitedly wonderful qualities."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam and explains that even liberated souls are attracted to Krishna's qualities as described in the Bhagavatam, referencing Srila Prabhupada's purport and the Brahma Vaivarta Purana.

In this session we will be discussing from the 11th verse of the 7th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Hare guna kshipta matir bhagavan badarayanihi adhyagat mahadakhyanam nityam vishnujana priyaha. The meaning of this verse is Shukadeva Goswami, son of Shri Vyasadeva, was not only transcendentally powerful but he was also very near to the devotees of the Lord. He underwent this study, Great narration Srimad Bhagavatam, Mahadakhyanam, Great narration of the Srimad Bhagavatam compiled by Vyasadeva. The question was raised, why did he take the trouble to study such literature when he was already liberated? The answer was a very significant verse, the 10th verse, the Atmarama verse, even liberated souls are attracted to Srimad Bhagavatam because it describes Krishna who is full of unlimitedly wonderful qualities. He attracts everyone, all kinds of persons, especially all kinds of transcendentalists. So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains that according to the Brahma Vaivartha Purana, another Purana, not this Bhagavata Purana, another Purana, Srila Sukhdev Goswami was a liberated soul. Even within the womb of his mother, such a birth is very, very rare.

---
### liberated souls, karma, Vyasadeva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WAK0W7hdh2k?t=173
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So there are higher purposes for which such liberated souls appear in the world."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the rare birth of liberated souls, explaining that they are not bound by karma and appear for higher purposes. He mentions Vyasadeva's knowledge of Shukadeva Goswami's future and his efforts to attach the child to the transcendental activities of the Lord.

Generally, we take birth because we are forced by the laws of nature to take birth in order to square up our karmic reactions from the previous life or previous lives. But a liberated soul does not have any reaction. So there are higher purposes for which such liberated souls appear in the world. Srila Vyasa Deva knew that the child after his birth would not stay at home. Vyasa Deva is a Trikalanya. He knows past, present and future. So he knew the future. He did not seek the present or future. The future of this child was that he would not stay at home as soon as he would be born he would just leave home and go away therefore vyasa deva impressed upon the child the synopsis of the bhagavatam so that the child could be made attached to the transcendental activities of the lord especially describing the personality of goddess activities qualities forms names paraphernalia aboard all personal features of the absolute truth after his birth that is chukadeva goswami.

### impersonal Brahman, personal feature of the Lord, devotee association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WAK0W7hdh2k?t=269
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But by the association of pure devotees like Vyasa Deva, even the liberated soul becomes attracted to the transcendental qualities of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Shukadeva Goswami, though a liberated soul, was further educated in the Bhagavatam. He contrasts the impersonal Brahman understanding with the personal feature of the Lord, emphasizing that association with pure devotees like Vyasadeva can attract even liberated souls to the Lord's transcendental qualities.

Who appeared as the son of yasa as a liberated soul the child was still more educating in the subject of the Bhagavatam by recitation of the actual poems, the actual verses of the Bhagavatam. The idea is that, why is Prabhupada telling this, quoting from the Brahma Vaivarta Purana? The idea is that generally the liberated souls are attached to the feature of impersonal Brahman with a monastic view of becoming one with the Supreme Whole. This is the impersonal Brahman understanding. The aim of such impersonalists is that they want liberation by merging their self into the Supreme Whole as impersonal Brahman. But by the association of pure devotees like Vyasa Deva, even the liberated soul becomes attracted to the transcendental qualities of the Lord. This is what was elaborately discussed in the previous verse, the 10th verse, the Atmarama verse. By the mercy of Shri Narada, Srila Vyasa Deva was able to narrate the great epic of Srimad Bhagavatam. And by the mercy of Vyasa Deva, Srila Shukadeva Goswami was able to narrate the epic of Srimad Bhagavatam. And by the mercy of Vyasa Deva, Srila Shukadeva Goswami was able to narrate the epic of Srimad Bhagavatam. The transcendental qualities of the Lord are so attractive that Srila Shukadeva Goswami became detached from being completely absorbed in impersonal Brahman and positively took up the personal activity of the Lord. This we have elaborately discussed in the previous verse.

### impersonal Brahman, material conception, transcendental qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WAK0W7hdh2k?t=394
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The transcendental qualities of the Lord are millions and billions of times more attractive than the features of the impersonal Brahman."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the transcendental qualities of the Lord with the impersonal Brahman and material conceptions. He describes the impersonal Brahman as a neutral stage above material miseries but not yet perfected.

The transcendental qualities of the Lord. The transcendental qualities of the Lord are millions and billions of times more attractive than the features of the impersonal Brahman. The impersonal Brahman understanding is in neutral stage. Lowest is the material conception, where one thinks in terms of material enjoyment, material pleasure, material pleasure, material gain, material paraphernalia, material existence, material pleasures, that is sense gratification, etc. etc. Material enjoyment in upper planets. But none of these are free from the material miseries, fundamental miseries, janma, mrityu, jara, vyadi, dukkha, dopa, etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. toshanudarshanam. So, above the material conception is the impersonal conception of the absolute truth. And that is the neutral stage where one is free from the material miseries, or material concept of life.

---
### impersonal Brahman, spiritual variety, personal feature of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WAK0W7hdh2k?t=481
**Verse References:** —

But one has not yet perfected. Perfected. Perfected. the realization of the Absolute Truth to the highest degree. So, if somebody mistakes the Absolute Truth to be only impersonal Brahman, then they cultivate that knowledge and they somehow try to seek satisfaction of realization of the impersonal feature. There is no variety, spiritual variety is lacking. There is no material inebriety or material defect in this realization of the Absolute Truth as impersonal Brahman. But there is no spiritual variety, spiritual variety. So, Srila Prabhupada says, practically he was thrown from the impersonal conception of the Absolute, thinking within himself that he had simply wasted so much time in devoting himself to the impersonal feature of the Supreme. After one comes to know of the personal feature, you will think, why was I wasting time with this impersonal feature? Why do they feel like that? Because he realized more transcendental bliss with the personal feature than the impersonal.

### Krishna's pastimes, transcendental nature, impersonalists vs. personalists
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WAK0W7hdh2k?t=818
**Verse References:** BG 4.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna as a supreme personality of Godhead even when he takes birth in this material world it is transcendental. Janma Karma Chame Divyam."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that impersonalists fail to understand the transcendental nature of Krishna's pastimes, considering them material descriptions. He emphasizes that Krishna's birth and activities are divine, and that Nanda Maharaj is a liberated soul with transcendental attraction for Krishna. He concludes by stating that impersonal and personal realizations don't go together, and Shukadeva Goswami naturally kept away from the impersonalists after realizing the personal feature.

Devotees discuss Oh Krishna he was crawling on his knees in the yard of Nanda Maharaj and Nanda Maharaj was absorbed in fondling this child. So they think what have we got to do with some child and some father fondling the child. They think this is all material description. What they don't realize is Krishna as a supreme personality of Godhead even when he takes birth in this material world it is transcendental. Janma Karma Chame Divyam. It is not like the birth of ordinary living entities, conditioned souls. It's not like that. Nanda Maharaj when he appears in this world to participate in Krishna's pastimes he is totally liberated soul. He's not a conditioned soul. He has no attraction for any material dealings. He has absolutely no attraction for material dealings. Neither Krishna is interested in any material dealings. So nanda Maharaj's attraction for Krishna is completely transcendental. That is what Srila Prabhupada is telling here. The impersonalists are unable to evaluate the devotees of the Lord. Thus from time immemorial, these two categories of transcendental pilgrims have sometimes been competitors. They like to keep themselves separate from each other because ultimately impersonal and personal realizations don't go together. So, Shukadeva Goswami, he became attracted to devotees after realizing the personal feature and naturally he would keep away from the impersonals. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Kurukshetra, violence, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C6fL6Ob2lX4?t=64
**Verse References:** SB 1.7.13, SB 1.7.14
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When the respective warriors of both camps, namely the Kauravas and the Pandavas, were killed on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, and the dead warriors obtained their deserved destinations, and when the son of Dhritarashtra fell down, left behind, and died in the battle, lamenting, his spine broken, being beaten by the club of Bhimasena, the son of Dronacharya Ashwathama, beheaded the five sleeping sons of Draupadi and delivered them as a prize to his master, foolishly thinking that he would be pleased."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verses from Srimad Bhagavatam that will be discussed, summarizing the events following the Battle of Kurukshetra, including the deaths of warriors and the heinous act of Ashwatthama.

In this session, we'll be discussing from the 13th and the 14th verses of the 7th chapter of the 1st canto of Sriman Bhagavatam. Yadam Hrithay Kauravasranjaya Nam Veereshu Atho Veeragatim Gateshu Prakodara Aavidha Gadabimarsha Bhaknor Dande Dhritarashtra Putre Bhartupriyam Draunil Itismapashyan Krishna Sutanaam Swapataam Shri Ramasih Upaharat Vipriyam Evatasya Jugup Siddham Karma Vigarha Yanti. Two verses are given together. The meaning of these two verses is also given together by Srila Prabhupada. When the respective warriors of both camps, namely the Kauravas and the Pandavas, were killed on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, and the dead warriors obtained their deserved destinations, and when the son of Dhritarashtra fell down, left behind, and died in the battle, lamenting, his spine broken, being beaten by the club of Bhimasena, the son of Dronacharya Ashwathama, beheaded the five sleeping sons of Draupadi and delivered them as a prize to his master, foolishly thinking that he would be pleased. Duryodhana, however, disapproved of the heinous act, and he was not pleased. He was not pleased. In the least.

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Both these battles are significant because in both the battles, the Supreme Lord is personally present, though in the battle of Kurukshetra, Krishna was not fighting, but his presence is significant."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Suta Goswami begins the narration of Srimad Bhagavatam with the renouncement of the Pandavas and the birth of King Parikshit, highlighting the significance of the Battle of Kurukshetra and comparing it to the Ramayana, emphasizing the presence and role of the Supreme Lord in both.

So Suta Goswami begins the narration of the Sriman Bhagavatam, beginning with the topics of the renouncement of the Pandavas and the birth activities and deliverance of King Pariksha. So first of all. He is beginning from the point where the end of the Battle of Kurukshetra, that famous battle. In our history of Greater India, there are two famous battles. One is the battle of the Ramayana between Lord Ramachandra and Ravana. Yes. Yes. And the other is the battle between the Pandavas and Kauravas as described in the Mahabharata. Both these battles are significant because in both the battles, the Supreme Lord is personally present, though in the battle of Kurukshetra, Krishna was not fighting, but his presence is significant. And in the battle of Ramayana, Lord Ramachandra is directly engaged in the battle, playing the role of a Kshatriya. Of course, Krishna was also playing the role of a Kshatriya on the battle when he was present on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. But because he wanted the battle to be a little bit more peaceful, he wanted the battle to go on in a fair manner, nobody could match the strength and the Kshatriya abilities of Krishna because Krishna is a supreme personality. So therefore, Krishna did not actually fight in that battle.
