**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### devotion, surrender, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1tzJ_ofI2yw?t=53
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For the sake of his devotees, he is prepared to do anything and everything to protect them."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna resides within everyone's heart as Paramatma and protects his devotees, emphasizing the importance of surrendering to Krishna alone.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 14th verse of the 8th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, the Lord of Supreme Mysticism, Yogeishvara Harihī, Shri Krishna, resides within everyone's heart as the Paramatma. As such, just to protect the progeny of the Kuru dynasty, he covered the embryo of Uttara by his personal energy. So what all is Shri Krishna doing? For the sake of his devotees, he is prepared to do anything and everything to protect them. So therefore, Shastras say, always depend on the Supreme Lord and nobody else. Even Krishna declares in the Bhagavad Gita, "You surrender to me alone, exclusively. Take my shelter exclusively." Because only Krishna is dependable for everyone, for everyone. And nobody is his enemy. Nobody is his friend. He is the well-wisher of everyone. Surdam Sarvabhutana.

---
### Paramatma, Krishna's energy, Kuru dynasty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1tzJ_ofI2yw?t=154
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This Paramatma feature is a very special feature of the Supreme Lord in the material world only."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the Paramatma feature of Krishna, explaining how He resides within everyone's heart and every atom, and how He protected Pariksit in Uttara's womb to save the Kuru dynasty.

In the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, "The Lord of Supreme Mysticism, Yogeshwaro Harihi, can simultaneously reside within everyone's heart or even within the atoms by his Paramatma feature, his plenary portion." This Paramatma feature is a very special feature of the Supreme Lord in the material world only. Paramatma feature is the expansion of the Lord who has entered into the heart of every living being within this material existence, and also entered into every material atom. So this Paramatma feature is a very, very, very special feature. Now it is mentioned here that Krishna is present as Paramatma in the heart of everyone. So therefore, it is said here, from within the body of Uttara, the mother of Pariksit, and Uttara had prayed to Krishna, "Please protect the child in my womb." So from within the body of Uttara, Lord Krishna covered the embryo to save Maharaj Pariksit and protect the progeny of Maharaj Kuru, of whom King Pandu was also a descendant. Kuru tantave, for the progeny of Maharaj Kuru. Pariksit is described as the progeny of Maharaj Kuru. Because this Pariksit was the only surviving descendant of the Pandavas and even the only surviving descendant of the Kuru dynasty.

---
### Kuru dynasty, Dhritarashtra's plan, Lineage
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1tzJ_ofI2yw?t=260
**Verse References:** BG 1.1
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Dhritarashtra making a distinction between his own sons and they are referred to as Kauravas descendants of Kuru dynasty but the Pandavas they are also descendants but they are not identified as descendants they are separated from the family lineage"
**Summary:** The speaker details the lineage of the Kuru dynasty, explaining how Pariksit was the last surviving descendant and how Dhritarashtra distinguished between his sons (Kauravas) and the Pandavas, even though both were descendants of Kuru.

Because all others were vanquished in the battle. All the hundred sons of Dhritarashtra were killed in the battle. So there was no, and their children and their, the, the grandsons of Dhritarashtra, the, all of them were killed. So therefore, to save the only descendant, remaining descendant of the Kuru dynasty, the Supreme Lord from within the body of Uttara, covered the embryo. Now, in doing so, he engaged his personal energy, his personal energy is his internal potency, non-different from him, and that internal potency is supremely powerful, as powerful as Krishna himself. So nobody can do any harm when the Supreme Lord, by his personal energy, covers or protects somebody. In his own sense the supreme authority doesn't matter. The Supreme Lord can do nothing. But He can change people in his own own way. For example, if you take the Kuru dynasty of Viparaj, for example, the way it is called in the holy Gita, if you look at the rosary, the rosary of Viparaj, the rosary of Kuru, the rosary of Viparaj, the rosary of the Supreme Lord, the rosary of Viparaj, everything is changed. That is also a special thing. But though, the Kurus or they should be called the present generation of Kurus of the Kuru dynasty but when there were some differences between the two families the sons of Dhritarashtra were known as Kurus where the sons of Pandu were known as Pandavas see this was particularly Dhritarashtra making a distinction it is explained in the first opening verse of the Bhagavad Gita itself in the first chapter first verse Dharmakshetre Kurukshetre Samaveta Yuyutsavaha Mamaka Pandavas Jaiva Dhritarashtra making a distinction between his own sons and they are referred to as Kauravas descendants of Kuru dynasty but the Pandavas they are also descendants but they are not identified as descendants they are separated from the family lineage

because he is all these sons and grandsons of the dhritarashtra were killed in the battle. The 15th verse: yadyapi astram brahma shiraha to amogham cha apratikriyam vaishnavam teja asadya samashamyan brahudvaha the meaning of this verse says oh shanaka although the Supreme Brahmastra weapon released by Ashurthama was irresistible and without check or counteraction it was neutralized and foiled when confronted by the strength of Vishnu Lord Krishna so So several times this has been explained that the Brahmastra weapon is irresistible. ### Brahmajyoti, Krishna's effulgence, Supreme Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1tzJ_ofI2yw?t=674
**Verse References:** BG 14.27

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

Now in the purport Srila Prabhupada says. In the Bhagavad Gita it is said that the Brahmajyoti or the glowing transcendental effulgence is resting on Lord Sri Krishna. This is explained in the 14th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita the 27th verse. Brahmano hi pratishta aham. Krishna says I am the basis of the impersonal Brahman. Brahmajyoti that is the effulgence emanating from the transcendental body of Krishna. And it covers the entire spiritual sky. It is the transcendental energy of the Supreme Lord. And that Brahmajyoti is the source of the entire material creation. Actually it is source from Krishna but through the Brahmajyoti. Through the Brahmajyoti. In other words the glowing effulgence known as Brahmatejas is nothing but the rays of the Lord. Just as the sun rays are rays of the sun disc. So this Brahmatejas which is described here.

### Krishna's independence, Absolute truth, Anu Atma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1tzJ_ofI2yw?t=759
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone else is dependent on Krishna. But Krishna is not dependent on anyone else. Krishna is always independent. Krishna is absolute."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes Krishna's independence and absolute nature, explaining that while everyone is dependent on Krishna, He is not dependent on anyone, and clarifies the distinction between the impersonal Brahman and the personal feature of the absolute truth, stating that the Anu Atma is a tiny portion of the Supreme Lord.

Yadyapi astram brahma shiraha tu amogham cha apratikriyam vaishnavam teja asadya. Vaishnavam means in relation to Vishnu. The. This Brahmastra actually materially irresistible. But it cannot surpass the supreme strength of Vishnu or Krishna. The weapon called Brahmastra released by Aswatthama was neutralized and foiled by Shri Krishna by his own energy. That is to say he did not wait for any others help because he is absolute. See this distinction we should always note between the Supreme Lord and anyone else. Everyone else is dependent on Krishna. But Krishna is not dependent on anyone else. Krishna is always independent. Krishna is absolute. This distinction eternally exists between the Supreme Lord Krishna and everyone else. In the entire creation. Even in the spiritual world also. Nobody is equal to Krishna. Nobody is greater than Krishna. Asamordhva. So this is an eternal fact. In fact those who do not know about the personal feature of the absolute truth. They wrongly think that the impersonal feature. The Brahmajyoti or the Brahman feature of the absolute truth. Is the same as the identity of the living being. The tiny living being. Anu Atma. That's what they think. Which is completely wrong. Anu Atma is a tiny particle, a portion of the Supreme Lord. And the Supreme Lord is always supremely powerful. Unlimitedly powerful. The source of everyone and everything. And his energy can never be equal with anything else. So that is being explained here. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Supreme Lord, Qualities of God, Queen Kunti's Prayers
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Hm1wVofDdeo?t=60
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nothing is impossible for the Supreme Lord, but all his actions are wonderful for us and thus he is always beyond the range of our conceivable limits."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the upcoming discussion of Queen Kunti's prayers and emphasizes the inconceivable nature and supreme power of the Lord, highlighting qualities like Ashtariya, Maya, and Achyuta.

In this session, we will be discussing the 16th verse of the 8th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, O Brahmanas, this is Suta Goswami addressing the sages of Naimisharanya. O Brahmanas! Do not think this to be especially wonderful in the activities of the mysterious and infallible personality of the Goddess. The Lord is described in so many ways according to His different characteristics. Qualities which are very unique to the Supreme Lord only. Here, Ashtariya, Maya, and Achyuta, two different qualities of the Supreme Lord are described. Because we think that he is a person like us or he is a person little more powerful than us. No, he is supremely powerful, he is unlimitedly powerful, he can never be compared with anybody else, he is incomparable and he is absolute. He always is in that position of having the supreme power, always, eternally it's a fact. So, therefore, this point, nothing is impossible for the Supreme Lord, but all his actions are wonderful for us and thus he is always beyond the range of our conceivable limits. This will be explained in a future verse, which is a prayer offer, a series of prayers offered by Queen Kunti. That's the main subject of this chapter, prayers by Queen Kunti, but we're going to start discussing that from the next session, the prayers of Queen Kunti and the series of prayers, wonderful prayers.

### Krishna's Supremacy, Perfection, Devotees
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Hm1wVofDdeo?t=363
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only Krishna is 100% perfect. No one is equal to or greater than Krishna. He is unrivaled."
**Summary:** This segment asserts Krishna's absolute perfection and unrivaled position, contrasting it with the partial perfections of other deities and living beings, as analyzed by the Goswamis of Vrindavan.

So, these prayers expressed by self-realized pure devotees clearly helps us understand what is the nature of the Supreme Lord. So, one of the things mentioned here is, the Supreme Lord's actions are always beyond the range of our conceivable limits. The Lord is the all-powerful, all-perfect personality of Godhead, all-powerful, all-perfect. There's no trace of any defect or any inebriety. The Lord is the Supreme Lord. The Lord is 100% perfect. Here, the Lord is referring to Krishna. Krishna is 100% perfect. Whereas others, namely Narayana, Brahma, Shiva, the demigods and all other living beings, possess only different percentages of such perfection. This is analyzed by the Goswamis of Vrindavan, the disciples of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. That only Krishna is 100% perfect. Others are all expansions of Krishna, or expansions of the expansions of Krishna, or very, very, very tiny parts of Krishna. And they possess always a percentage of the qualities of Krishna in perfection. No one is equal. No one is equal to or greater than Krishna. He is unrivaled. This is a fact.

---
### Kunti, Pure Devotion, Karma and Jnana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Hm1wVofDdeo?t=490
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pure devotion is completely independent of everything else."
**Summary:** This segment explains the context of Kunti's upcoming prayers, emphasizing her pure devotion (unalloyed bhakti) to Krishna, which is independent of karma and jnana, and highlighting the importance of bhakti for achieving perfection.

The 17th verse. The translation of the 17th verse. Thus said from the radiation. This is the translation of the Brahmastra. Kunti, the chaste devotee of the Lord. Pratha, Sati. Pratha means Kunti. Sati means the chaste devotee of the Lord. And her five sons and Draupadi. Atma jaihi, sah, Krishna ya. Krishna ya refers to Draupadi. Draupadi is also called Krishna. So, along with Kunti, along with her five sons and Draupadi, addressed Lord Krishna. As he started for home, Krishna finished his work of installing Yudhishthira Maharaj after the battle of Kurukshetra as the emperor of the world. Now he was going back to Dwaraka. So, while he was about to start for Dwaraka, Kunti approached Krishna and addressed him. And there will be a series of prayers Kunti will offer to Krishna. In the purport, it is explained by Srila Prabhupada, Kunti is described here in a sati or chaste due to her unalloyed devotion to Lord Sri Krishna. This term is used by Prabhupada many times in the Bhagavad Gita and the Bhagavad Gita. Unalloyed devotion. Unalloyed means it is sent person pure. There is no admixture of karma or jnana in such bhakti. Pure devotional service is described by Srila Prabhupada Swami as anyabila ashita shunyam jnana karmadi anavratam. jnana karmadi anavratam. It is unmixed with any jnana or karma. Because karma and jnana without bhakti is never successful. Any pious activity anybody may do, if it is not mixed with a little bhakti, it will always be incomplete, unsuccessful, imperfect. Same thing with transcendental knowledge. Unless that is mixed with a little bhakti, it is always imperfect, incomplete. So, both karma and jnana are dependent on bhakti for a person to achieve perfection. But bhakti, which is pure bhakti, is independent of karma and jnana. Bhakti is never dependent on karma or jnana. Pure devotion is completely independent of everything else. So, therefore, kunti is described as the most, the chaste lady, sati. And it is because she is the pure devotee of Krishna.

---
### Exclusive Dependence on Krishna, Protection, Devotee's Perspective
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Hm1wVofDdeo?t=696
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For a pure devotee of Krishna, Krishna is the only protector."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that pure devotees depend exclusively on Krishna for protection, rejecting reliance on other living beings or demigods, and highlighting the guarantee of protection that comes with complete dependence on Krishna.

Not only kunti, the five pandavas, Draupadi, Parikshit, all of them, Arjuna, everyone, they are all pure devotees of Krishna. 100% pure devotees. It is further said by Srila Prabhupada, her mind will now be expressed in the following prayers for Lord Krishna. Beginning from the 18th verse, prayers of Queen Kunti will be presented. A chaste devotee of the Lord does not look to others, namely any other living being or demigod, even for deliverance from danger. Typically, in the material world, we have got different sources for prayer, depending on the type of protection, depending on the type of danger, or depending on the type of problem we may face, or depending on the type of facilities we may have. We have so many sources of protection. We may have so many different dangers. The material world is always filled with dangers. Padam padam yat vipadam. But, for a pure devotee of Krishna, Krishna is the only protector. And, Krishna is the only protector means such a devotee never depends on another living being or another demigod. Never. Never, never, never, never. So, this is very, very, very significant. Nobody else in the material world, other than a pure devotee, depends exclusively on Krishna. So, this is what we should learn from the Bhagavatam and from the lives of great devotees. And, from Krishna's own instructions, we should learn this, that we have no guarantee of sent person protection except when we learn to completely depend on Krishna. So, if at all you want full protection, under all circumstances, you should learn to depend on Krishna exclusively. Now, now, Krishna is there and also so many other people are there to protect me. No.

---
### Pandava Family, Reciprocity, Krishna's Competence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Hm1wVofDdeo?t=840
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One should look for all help from Krishna alone, who is competent always to save his devotees."
**Summary:** This segment uses the Pandava family as an example of exclusive dependence on Krishna, explaining that Krishna reciprocates with protection and help based on the degree of one's dependence on Him, and concluding that one should seek help only from Krishna.

So, Srila Prabhupada says here, that was all along the characteristic of the whole family of the Pandavas. It's not just true of Kunti. It's true of all the family members of the Pandavas. Pandava family. They knew nothing except Krishna and therefore the Lord was also always ready to help them in all respects, in all circumstances. See, Krishna is always reciprocally giving protection or offering some help. If I depend completely on Krishna, then Krishna will protect me and help me always under all circumstances. But, if I am not so much completely dependent on Krishna, then, then Krishna will say, depending on who you want to take protection from, take protection from that source. And that is not guaranteed to protect us in all circumstances, other sources. So, therefore, one should not look for imperfect help from imperfect living beings or from demigods, but one should look for all help from Krishna alone, who is competent always to save his devotees. So, that is the conclusion. I will stop here.

### Krishna's Immanence, Surrender, All-pervasiveness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N3lWuBz_4_Y?t=640
**Verse References:** BG 15.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is both all-pervading and localized, but reserves the right of not being exposed to persons who are not surrendered souls."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's presence both within and outside everything, referencing the Bhagavad Gita to explain how Krishna resides in everyone's heart as the Supersoul, and concludes by discussing how only surrendered souls can truly recognize Krishna's supreme position.

Krishna is also addressed as Eeshwara. Eeshwaram Prakriteparam. Eeshwaram. Eeshwara here refers to controller. Now, the living beings also may have some position of control, just like Surya, Chandra, Indra, Varuna, Vayu. But they are not Supreme Eeshwara. Supreme Eeshwara is only one, that is Krishna. Eeshwaraha, Paramaha, Krishna, Sachidananda, Vikraha. In the Brahma Samhita it is said, Param Eeshwara. Supreme Eeshwara. And Krishna is also, Eeshwara means the controller. He is the ultimate controller. He is the controller of all controllers. There is nobody above him. There is nobody equal to him, Asamordhva. This is Krishna's position. Now, Krishna was present before Kunti Devi and all the others who had assembled to see him off. But Kunti knew that even though he is present in front of her, he is also seated in her heart. He is also seated in everybody's heart as a Supersoul. So, in the Bhagavad Gita this is confirmed. Kunti Devi is telling, you are present within everyone, and also you are present outside of everyone. So, how is it possible to understand this? That is explained by Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita, 15th chapter, 15th verse. Sarvasya Chaham Rudi Sanni Vishto. I am seated in everyone's heart as the Param Atma, Supersoul. And only due to Krishna do we get remembrance, knowledge, and gratefulness. Matthaha Smritir Gyanam Apohanamcha. And through all the Vedas, I am to be known. Vedaischa Sarvair Ahameva Vedyam. The Vedas, so many subject matters are discussed in the Vedic literature, but the aim is to know who is Krishna, who is the ultimate source of everything, who is the ultimate controller of everyone and everything. And that is declared as Krishna. And Krishna is the compiler of the Vedas, and he is the knower of the Vedas. So, this is the unique position of Krishna. Kuntidevi experienced that, even though Krishna was present outside, still, when the child in the womb of Uttara was in danger of being attacked by a Brahmastra released by Ashwathama, Krishna immediately entered the womb of Uttara to save the child from the attack of Ashwatthama's Brahmastra. So Kunti Devi actually herself was puzzled, is Krishna all-pervading or is localized? What do the meaning of all-pervading? He is present everywhere, but actually localized means he's in a specific place, particular place, just like he's standing in front of Kunti them in Hastinapura is present in front of Kunti. So easy localized or easy all-pervading? So the answer is Krishna is both. He is all-pervading is also localized, but in spite of being present every day he is standing before the Kunti Devi saying you would be some 20, 30 minutes everywhere in spite of being present within everything in spite of being present outside just like I can see you or you can see me like that everybody when Krishna incarnated could have seen Krishna but still he reserves the right of not being exposed to persons who are not surrendered souls. Those who are not surrendered when they see Krishna they see him as an another ordinary person they seem as another ordinary person they cannot recognize oh here is the supreme personality of God. So how is it that they're not able to recognize that we expand in the next words so I'll stop here thank you very much you.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.8.19 | 09-02-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna's Transcendence, Sense Perception, Spirituality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Grd67ulwNlQ?t=70
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's personality is totally spiritual, and therefore, he is beyond the range of limited sense perception."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Krishna is beyond the range of limited human sense perception because His form is completely spiritual.

The meaning of this verse is, being beyond the range of limited sense perception, you are the eternally irreproachable factor covered by the curtain of deluding energy. You are invisible to the foolish observer, exactly as an actor dressed as a player is not recognized. Some words which are maybe not easily understandable, let me try to explain them in a little more easily understandable way. Being beyond the range of limited sense perception means with our senses, we are seeing, hearing, smelling, touching, tasting, perceiving. We are getting knowledge from outside through these five senses. Krishna is beyond this range of our senses to perceive. Krishna's form is completely spiritual. So, these eyes cannot see spiritual form. These ears cannot hear spiritual sound. You see, that's why Krishna's personality is totally spiritual. And therefore, he is beyond the range of limited sense perception.

---
### Krishna's Qualities, Maya, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Grd67ulwNlQ?t=178
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is eternally faultless...and he is also covered by a curtain of deluding energy, maya javanika."
**Summary:** This segment describes Krishna as eternally irreproachable (faultless) and explains the concept of Maya, the deluding energy that covers Krishna, differentiating between Maha Maya (material energy) and Yoga Maya (spiritual energy).

Krishna is also described as the eternally irreproachable. In your case, Krishna is the irreproachable factor. Irreproachable means faultless. Krishna is eternally faultless. Whereas, one of the qualities of every living being is, the living being being very, very limited. Every living being being very limited is prone to commit mistakes. This is one of the characteristics of every living being. Even though, Krishna is a very limited being. He is a very limited being. Such great personalities like big big devatas, they also sometimes commit mistakes, so nobody is eternally faultless except Krishna. So, therefore Krishna is described here as eternally irreproachable factor, eternally faultless, and he is also covered by a curtain of deluding energy, maya javanika. Maya javanika means curtain. Maya means of the curtain made up of the deluding energy. Deluding energy means that energy which bewilders us. Krishna is covered by a curtain of deluding energy. Krishna has got two kinds of energies which bewilders us. One kind of energy is the material energy which bewilders us, the Maha Maya. And another kind of energy Krishna has got which is called Yoga Maya, the spiritual energy that is also bewildering for every living being. Both the spiritual energy which is personal energy of Krishna and Krishna's material energy both are bewildering for the living entities. Of course, those who are pure devotees, they are never bewildered by the material energy. And those who are non-devotees, they are subject to the bewilderment by even the material energy. Now, the bewilderment by the spiritual energy, Yogamaya, is meant for devotees to engage in pure devotional service of Krishna. And the bewilderment of non-devotees due to the material energy is facilitating the non-devotees to be able to think falsely that they are independent, they are the lord, or they are controllers, or to think that there is no God. Or to be ignorant about Krishna. All this is the effect of the bewilderment due to the material energy only upon non-devotees. So, here Kunti Devi is telling Krishna is always covered by the curtain of Yogamaya.

---
### Yogamaya, Krishna's Invisibility, Analogy of an Actor
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Grd67ulwNlQ?t=393
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Even when Krishna incarnates, foolish people cannot see him as the supreme lord."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Krishna is invisible to foolish observers due to Yogamaya, using the analogy of an actor in a drama who is not recognized in their role.

Now, how is it that Krishna is always covered by the curtain of Yogamaya? Invisible to the foolish observer. To those who are enlightened spiritually, Krishna is not invisible. They are able to see Krishna very clearly always because Krishna is always present, eternally present. But those who are foolish people who don't have spiritual knowledge, who don't have spiritual enlightenment, for them Krishna is always invisible. Now the example is given by Kunti Devi here, even when Krishna incarnates, foolish people cannot see him as the supreme lord. Now why is it like that? Kunti Devi says just like an actor who is dressed in a particular drama for playing a particular role in a drama, he is not recognized by the people. Who is the person who is playing this role? Of course this is understandable if you go back in time that formerly when there used to be a drama performance on the stage, a dramatic artist, a dramatic actor, he will have a makeup and he will speak, move, etc. exactly as what the role demands. Supposing an actor has to play the role of a king, he will dress up like a king, he will talk like a king, he will walk like a king, his voice will be very deep like that of a king, even though in ordinary life he will be just another ordinary person. So, he is using his acting skill to play a role of a king and when he actually appears on the stage, people who are watching that particular drama, they will not be able to say who is this person who is dressed like a king, even though that person may be known to the people who are watching the drama outside the stage. They may be known to him. The actor may be known to them. Okay, they may have actually seen a text. They may have recognized that somebody who's dressed like a king, but they cannot recognize who is this person who is dressed like us. Exactly like that, Krishna appears as the son of Vasudeva or his Rama, Narsimha, Varaha, people who don't have spiritual enlightenment, foolish observers, they cannot recognize here is God. They cannot have greatest. So in the purport Sri Lakravapalyee explains.

---
### Krishna's True Nature, Foolishness, Authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Grd67ulwNlQ?t=587
**Verse References:** BG 9.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment quotes the Bhagavad Gita to emphasize that less intelligent people mistake Krishna for an ordinary man and defines less intelligent persons as those who rebel against Krishna's authority.

Somebody stated in the Bhagavad Gita that less intelligent persons mistake Krishna to be an ordinary man. He says, avajananti mamurha manushin tanum ashritam. Bhagavad Gita 9th chapter, 11th verse. Krishna says that. So, the ordinary people, they think Krishna is another ordinary man. No, Krishna is not another ordinary person. He is the Supreme Lord. He is never without his power. He is never without his opulences. He is never minus the opulences. No. Kunti is now confirming this point that is described in the Bhagavad Gita. Less intelligent persons, who are the less intelligent persons? Those who rebel against the authority of Krishna. They are the less intelligent persons. They are called asuras. Asuras generally want to go against the authority established by the Supreme Lord in this world, in the material world.

### Krishna's Powers, Incarnations, Superhuman Activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Grd67ulwNlQ?t=660
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna exhibited extraordinary power even when he was lying on the lap of his mother Yashoda as a small child, infant, just born."
**Summary:** This segment describes Krishna's superhuman activities, such as killing Putana as an infant and lifting Govardhan Hill as a boy, to illustrate His extraordinary power.

When the Lord appears as Rama, Narasimha, Varaha, Vamana, any of the transcendental incarnations of the Lord, or even as Krishna, then he performs so many activities which are impossible to be performed by any person of this world, by any human being, or even any devata. They cannot perform. Now, in the tenth canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam, it is clearly explained that Krishna exhibited extraordinary power even when he was lying on the lap of his mother Yashoda as a small child, infant, not child, infant, just born. Say about ten days after Krishna took birth, he was attempted to be killed by one Putana. But when Putana came to breastfeed Krishna with her breast milk, she had severe deadly poison on her breast. And Krishna, he sucked out her life. When she tried to feed him poison through her breast milk, Krishna sucked out her life air. So, it is not possible for anybody of this world to suck out another person's adult's life air, prana, suck out the prana. And instantaneously, Putana died. Instantaneously. Now, when he was a small boy, seven years old, playfully he lifted the Govardhan hill on his little left finger. Now, as easily as a child picks up a mushroom, small mushroom, a child, sometimes children when they play in rainy season, there are mushrooms growing. They pick up that mushroom and hold it like an umbrella. Krishna apparently was holding the Govardhan hill on his little left finger as an umbrella for protecting the residents of Vrindavan when there was a torrential rain sent by Indra. But he did it as effortlessly as a child picks up a small mushroom and can effortlessly hold it. Exactly like that, Krishna picked up the huge Govardhan hill and held it up like an umbrella to protect the residents of Vrindavan.

---
### Krishna's Instructions, Bhagavad Gita, Authority of Acharyas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Grd67ulwNlQ?t=852
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna removed his illusion by speaking Bhagavad Gita and enlightening Arjuna and through Arjuna all of us."
**Summary:** This segment highlights Krishna's delivery of the Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna and emphasizes the acceptance of Krishna as the Supreme Lord by various authorities and acharyas.

So, these type of superhuman activities of Krishna are described in the tenth canto of the Bhagavatam. And even the activities of Lord Rama, Lord Varaha, Lord Narasimha, they are all described in the authentic scriptures. Now, Krishna has also delivered marvelous instructions. In the Bhagavad Gita, he spoke to Arjuna 45 minutes. When the battle was about to begin, Arjuna became bewildered. And Krishna removed his illusion by speaking Bhagavad Gita and enlightening Arjuna and through Arjuna all of us. So, that is only possible for Krishna. That cannot be done by anybody else. So, and Krishna is accepted as a very very great personality, the supreme personality by such authorities as Vyasa, Devala, Asita, Narada, Madhvacharya, Ramanujacharya, Shankaracharya, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Jeeva Goswami. All great personalities, they have accepted Krishna as the supreme lord. So, therefore, Kunti's asserting that Krishna is the supreme lord is perfectly accurate.

**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### devotional service, matter vs. spirit, Kunti's prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vQVNU9ZAuXs?t=35
**Verse References:** SB 1.8.20
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna descends to propagate the transcendental signs of devotional service."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces Queen Kunti's prayer and explains that even advanced transcendentalists need Krishna's grace to understand the difference between matter and spirit.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deviim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the 20th verse of the 8th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The topic of this 8th chapter is primarily the prayers by Queen Kunti. This is the third prayer by Queen Kunti, the third verse expressing her devotion to Krishna. Tathā paramahamsānām munīnām amalātmanām bhakti-yoga-vidhānārthaṁ kathaṁ paśye mahi-striyahā. The meaning of this verse is, "You yourself descend to propagate the transcendental signs of devotional service unto the hearts of the advanced transcendentalists and mental speculators who are purified by being able to discriminate between matter and spirit. How then can we women know you perfectly?" So, Kunti Devi is actually a pure devotee of the Supreme Lord. Therefore, she is able to recognize Krishna as not just her relative, but as the Supreme Personality of Godhead. In spite of Kunti Devi being able to recognize Krishna, she is helping us understand that those who are not devotees, they cannot recognize Krishna. They are not able to identify, here is the Supreme Absolute Truth, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. So, therefore, in this particular verse, Kunti Devi is saying, Krishna descends to propagate the transcendental signs of devotional service. Krishna descends to propagate the transcendental signs of devotional service. And such knowledge is even required to be given to the advanced transcendentalists, to very, very intelligent people who are purified by being able to discriminate between matter and spirit. See, there are two things in this world, matter and spirit. All living beings are spirit. All living beings are spirit. So, they belong to the solar system. This category of spiritual energy and everything else that we see around us, all material things, they are made of matter, material energy. So, there is a very, very, very, very big difference between matter and spirit.

---
### matter vs. spirit, devotional service, unknowable Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vQVNU9ZAuXs?t=217
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the difficulty in discriminating between matter and spirit, even for intelligent people, and emphasizes that devotional service is essential to understanding Krishna, which is explained by Prabhupada.

And it's not easy to discriminate like that. This is explained in the Bhagavad Gita, the difficulty for an ordinary person to discriminate between matter and spirit. Only the very, very intelligent people who are able to analyze every single thing, what is its fundamental nature, fundamental nature of a thing, whether it is matter or is it something different than matter, which is something which is not matter, spirit. So, if even the most intelligent people who can discriminate between matter and spirit, even they are unable to understand the personality of Godhead because they are not conversant with devotional service, the science of devotional service. So, such science of devotional service is propagated by Krishna himself when he descends in this world or by Krishna's pure devotees who also descend for the same purpose, just like Prabhupada has come to teach us the actual science of devotional service. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu came for the same purpose. Chaitanya's pure devotees come for the same purpose. So now Kunti Devi is considering the position of ordinary people, how can they understand or know Krishna perfectly. So now in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains even the greatest philosophical speculators cannot have access to the region of the Lord. Lord here means the supreme Personality of Godhead.

### Krishna's mercy, revelation, unknowable Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vQVNU9ZAuXs?t=341
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is knowable only by one who has got Krishna's mercy."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna is beyond the reach of philosophical speculation and can only be known through His mercy and revelation, a fact corroborated by Queen Kunti.

Why? Why is it so? Because it is said in the Upanishads that the supreme truth, the absolute personality of Godhead is beyond the range of the thinking power of the greatest philosopher, avaam manasa kochara. Even if the Upanishads describe who or what is the absolute truth in so many words, still even for the greatest philosopher, Krishna remains the supreme unknown. Krishna remains like that. So, therefore, Srila Prabhupada says Krishna is unknowable by great learning or by the greatest brain. Then Krishna is unknowable even by the greatest learned person. Then what is the way to know Krishna? That is explained by Srila Prabhupada as Krishna is knowable only by one who has got Krishna's mercy. That's the only way to know Krishna. Krishna has to reveal himself when he shows his mercy upon a particular person. Only then such person on whom Krishna's mercy is being showered, such a person can know Krishna by revelation. Others may go on thinking about him for years together, yet he is unknowable. This is a fact. So, this fact is corroborated by the Queen, Queen Kunti, who is playing the part of an innocent woman.

**Summary:** The speaker discusses the role of pure devotees and highlights women's inherent faith in the Lord's superiority, contrasting it with philosophical speculation.

Kunti Devi as a pure devotee has nothing to do with this material world. All pure devotees belong to the spiritual world. Then why are pure devotees here in this world? They are here to actually show us the way of devotional pure devotion is served by their own actions, by their own words. Kunti Devi as a pure devotee is now describing Krishna, Krishna's position. So, Srila Prabhupada says there are some categories of people who in this material world are not able to speculate and think very deeply, analyze things in an intelligent way, they don't have that capability. So, it is mentioned by Srila Prabhupada in the purport here, women in general are unable to speculate like philosophers, but they are blessed by the Lord because they believe at once in the superiority and all mightiness of the Lord. See, this is one advantage women have over men in general. Of course, this is speaking women as a class. So, they are not as intelligent as great philosophers in order to speculate who or what is the absolute truth, but they have a special quality. What is that? They believe at once in the superiority and all mightiness of the Supreme Lord.
