### devotional service, incarnation, purpose of Krishna's appearance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xrt90oUh-bA?t=61
**Verse References:** SB 1.8.35
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna appears for different purposes, and He simultaneously fulfills all the purposes when He appears in an incarnation."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam (1.8.35) and explains that Krishna appears for various purposes, including rejuvenating devotional service, and fulfills all purposes simultaneously in His incarnations.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 35th verse of the 8th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, continuing the prayers by Queen Kunti. Bhavesmin Klishyamananam Avidyakamakarmabhihi Shravanasmaranarhani Karishyan Nithikechana. The meaning of this verse is, and yet others say that you appear to rejuvenate the devotional service of hearing, remembering, worshipping, and so on, in order that the conditioned souls suffering from material pangs might take advantage and gain liberation. So Krishna appears for different purposes, and He simultaneously fulfills all the purposes when He appears in an incarnation. That's the greatness of His appearance.

---
### devotional service, liberation, conditioned souls
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xrt90oUh-bA?t=131
**Verse References:** SB 1.8.35
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such conditioned souls should take advantage of the process of devotional service and thereby very easily gain liberation from this material bondage."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Queen Kunti's understanding of Krishna's purposeful appearance to revive devotional service, enabling conditioned souls to attain liberation from material suffering through devotional practices.

So Queen Kunti, being a pure devotee, is very well aware that Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead and His appearance is very, very purposeful. So in this verse, she is saying that Krishna has also appeared for rejuvenating the devotional service of hearing, remembering, worshipping, etc. And why He has to revive that? So, the conditioned souls, conditioned souls means all those who are under the stringent laws of karma, they are suffering from material pangs simply because they are always engaged in some material activities and they get bound up and they suffer. So, such conditioned souls should take advantage of the process of devotional service and thereby very easily gain liberation from this material bondage. So, Krishna comes for this purpose also.

---
### dharma, re-establishment of religion, supreme authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xrt90oUh-bA?t=222
**Verse References:** BG 4.7-8
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The factual way of religion is to accept the Lord as a supreme authority, and thus render service unto him in spontaneous love."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada's explanation from the Bhagavad Gita, emphasizing that Krishna appears in every millennium to re-establish religious principles, and true religion involves accepting the Lord as the supreme authority and serving Him with love.

So, Sri Lepraopada explains in the purport, in the Srimad Bhagavad, sorry, in the Srimad Bhagavad Gita, the Lord asserts that he appears in every millennium just to reestablish the way of religion. Dharma samsthapana arthaya sambhavami yuge yuge. Every millennium, every yuga, he appears because the materialistic way of life is such that, after Krishna revives in one particular incarnation, gradually the religious principles deteriorate in human society. Then again Krishna comes in another incarnation, again he rejuvenates the religious principles. Again it starts deteriorating. So, periodically Krishna incarnates for this purpose. Dharma samsthapana arthaya. Now, Sri Lepraopada explains, the way of religion is made by the Supreme Lord. No one can manufacture a new path of religion as is the fashion for certain ambitious persons. In modern times, sometimes people say, 'Oh, I have got my own way of practicing Dharma.' But that is not real Dharma. That's not religion. That's not true religion. True religion is what is given by God, what is given by Krishna. The factual way of religion is to accept the Lord as a supreme authority, and thus render service unto him in spontaneous love. This is the perfect understanding of religion. That Krishna is the Supreme Lord, Supreme Personality of Godhead, and everyone should render loving devotional service to Krishna. That is the real religious principle.

---
### service, illusory energy, master and servant
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xrt90oUh-bA?t=358
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are made for doing service eternally. That's the only function: to render service."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that all living beings are constitutionally made to render service, and if they don't serve Krishna, they are forced to serve the illusory energy, Maya, highlighting the importance of serving the true master.

Now, Sri Lepraopada explains here, a living being, any living being, cannot help but render service because he is constitutionally made for that purpose. We are made for doing service eternally. That's the only function to render service. But, we are meant to serve Krishna. If we don't serve Krishna, then we are forced to serve illusory energy, Maya. Servant we are. We cannot avoid rendering service. So, it's explained here. The only function of the living being is to render service to the Lord, to Lord Krishna. The Lord is great and living beings are subordinate to him. Everyone is subordinate to Krishna, the Supreme Lord. So, everyone's duty is simply to serve Krishna. Unfortunately, the illusion living beings, out of misunderstanding only, become servants of the senses by material desire. We all have material desires, and these material desires dictate that we become servant of our senses. We think we are master in this material world, generally, under the influence of illusion. But, in reality, nobody is master. Krishna is the only master. Krishna is the only Lord. Krishna is the only controller.

---
### illusion, material desires, perverted sex life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xrt90oUh-bA?t=481
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Both failure and success are illusions, because we have nothing to do with this material world."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the illusory nature of material enjoyment and the futility of chasing material desires, highlighting how these desires lead to becoming servants of the senses and engaging in perverted activities, particularly in the context of sex life.

But, others have an illusory idea that I am controller, I am master. I am the enjoyer. And in such a false conception, they become servants of the senses. They simply chase their material desires, and that is never to be fulfilled. It is called durashaya. Durashaya, never to be satisfied, never. Though, sometimes people think, by my hard work, I have successfully worked for satisfying some material desire of mine, and I have become successful. But that success is another facet of the illusion only. Both failure and success are illusions, because we have nothing to do with this material world. So, we become, under illusion, servant of our own senses. Now, this desire, material desire, is called avidya, nescience. Now, only because of this nescience or avidya or material desires, the living beings make different plans for material enjoyment. Material enjoyment, there are so many plans made by different living beings. This is, the plan for material enjoyment is centered about a perverted sex life. Sex life, by itself, is not condemned in the scriptures. It's got a higher purpose, to produce Krishna conscious progeny, Krishna conscious children. But perverted sex life, simply meant for some so-called enjoyment, which actually is totally illusory.

---
### transmigration, birth and death, material entanglement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xrt90oUh-bA?t=621
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Death is not the end of our existence. Death means we migrate from this body to another body."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the concept of transmigration, emphasizing that death is not the end but a migration to another body, and life continues through the changes of the body, similar to how we change from childhood to youth.

Therefore, a person becomes entangled in the chain of birth and death by transmigrating into different bodies. You should understand this word, transmigration. Transmigration means, I am leaving one body and entering another body, while I am not aware that I have changed my body. I am existing through the changes, but I am not aware of that. That's why it's called transmigration. Migration, you understand. Just say, a person migrates from India to America. He leaves India and he goes to America. Say, migrated to America. Actually, a similar thing happens when we die. Death is not the end of our existence. Death means we migrate from this body to another body, new body. But because that change happens without our knowledge, without our understanding, it's called transmigration. Transmigration. Transmigration. And actually life is continuous through the changes of the body. Just like we change body from childhood to youth to old age.

---
### transmigration, illusion, threefold miseries
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xrt90oUh-bA?t=724
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unless one is beyond the boundary of this nescience or ignorance or misunderstanding, misconception, one cannot get free from the threefold miseries of material life."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining transmigration, highlighting the illusion of forgetting past lives and the incorrect understanding of coming into existence only at birth, and emphasizes that freedom from the threefold miseries of material life requires transcending ignorance and misunderstanding.

Do we think that we are different person from childhood to youth when the change happens? No. I am quite aware. I am the same person. I had the body of a child. Now I have the body of a young man when I become youthful, when I go to the youthful stage. So, I don't think that I am a different person. But what happens when we quit this body at the time of death and enter another body? Mostly, in most cases, a person forgets about the previous life. That is one thing. Second thing. In that next body, a person thinks, I have come into existence when I was born, which is a mistake, which is an incorrect understanding. So, in the chain of birth and death, by transmigrating to different bodies on different planets, this happens under the direction of the Supreme Lord. He is completely in control, the Supreme Lord. So, Srila Prabhupada explains, unless one is beyond the boundary of this nescience or ignorance or misunderstanding, misconception, one cannot get free from the threefold miseries of material life. One cannot get free. The threefold miseries will be there in material existence, even if we change bodies. Miseries are not going to vanish.

---
### devotional service, liberation, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xrt90oUh-bA?t=839
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Those who are accepting this path of devotional service, they are very, very fortunate souls."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the importance of devotional service as the path to liberation, highlighting Krishna's efforts to revive religious principles and the fortunate position of those who embrace bhakti, enabling them to solve the problem of repeated birth and death and return to Godhead.

So, therefore, the conclusion is that if we understand and take to devotional service, which Krishna actually every time appears in every yuga, and he revives the real religious principles in human society, Dharma, Samsthava and Artha, and we should take advantage of that. Krishna is doing it for our sake. It's not for his benefit. It's for our benefit. So, those who are accepting this path of devotional service, they are very, very fortunate souls, and they can solve this problem of repetition of birth and death by practicing bhakti and becoming liberated. Ultimate liberation is possible in the bhakti. Go back to Godhead, never to return again to this miserable material world. Thank you. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.8.36 | 05-03-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### birth and death, devotion, Krishna's lotus feet
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w2np8N3hAs4?t=54
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who continuously hear, chant, and repeat the transcendental activities of Krishna, or even if they don't do it, they simply take pleasure in somebody else who is doing that, such persons are able to have darshan of Krishna's lotus feet by which they are very easily able to conquer this material existence."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains Queen Kunti's prayer about how hearing and chanting Krishna's name can end the cycle of birth and death, emphasizing the importance of spiritual understanding over material pursuits.

In this session, we will be discussing the 36th verse of the 8th chapter of the 1st canto of Shreemad Bhagavatam, continuing the prayers by Queen Kunti. The meaning of this verse is: O Krishna, those who continuously hear, chant, and repeat your transcendental activities or take pleasure in others doing so, certainly see your lotus feet, which alone can stop the repetition of birth and death. So Kunti Devi is explaining how one can end this repetition of birth and death. Of course, most people do not know that the real fundamental problem of material existence is this repetition of birth and death. It's called samsara or bhava. So this accepting this material body, while we are actually not got anything to do with matter, we are pure spirit, and we have our spiritual body, we have our spiritual heart, we have our home, the abode of Krishna, Krishnaloka, but foolishly we give up that spiritual home, and we desire independent enjoyment, we desire to lord over material nature, and therefore we are forced to take this material body, which is called birth, and we cannot remain in this material body permanently, we have to quit this material body by force, that's called death, and after death, if we still have desire for lording over or we have desire for material enjoyment, independent enjoyment, then we have to continue to accept another body, and that's another thing, another birth. Jatasyahi dhruva mṛtyu dhruvam janma mṛtasyaca. Krishna explains this in the Bhagavad Gita: for one who has taken his birth in this material world, death is certain, and after death, if one is not liberated at the time of death, then one has to take birth again, cannot avoid. So Kunti Devi is now telling who can actually stop this repetition of birth and death: those who continuously hear, chant, and repeat the transcendental activities of Krishna, or even if they don't do it, they simply take pleasure in somebody else who is doing that, such persons are able to have darshan of Krishna's lotus feet by which they are very easily able to conquer this material existence, they are able to stop the repetition of birth and death.

---
### vision of God, devotional service, material qualification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w2np8N3hAs4?t=297
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Even though the Lord may be present before our eyes, it is not possible to see him unless we have the necessary vision."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains that seeing Krishna requires a change in vision through spontaneous love of God, not just material qualifications, and that even when Krishna incarnates, not everyone recognizes Him.

Sri La Pravapa explains that the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna cannot be seen by our present conditional vision, these eyes with which we normally see so many material things. It works under certain conditions, so this conditional vision is not suitable for seeing the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna. In order to see him, one has to change his present vision by developing a different condition of life, which is full of spontaneous love of God, not forced to do some devotional service, spontaneously one renders service to Krishna very enthusiastically, lovingly, very nicely. When Sri Krishna was present personally on the face of the globe, not everyone could see him as the Supreme Personality of Godhead. When Krishna incarnates, everybody can see him, but they don't recognize him, they don't understand here is the Supreme Personality of Godhead, they think he is another ordinary person. Materialists like Ravana, Hiranyakashipu, Kamsa, Jarasandha are highly qualified personalities by acquisition of material assets, but they were unable to appreciate the presence of the Lord. So somebody is materially qualified, that is not enough to recognize the Supreme Lord when he is incarnated or when he is personally present before our vision. Therefore, even though the Lord may be present before our eyes, it is not possible to see him unless we have the necessary vision. Sometimes people ask, can you show me God? Even if God were to appear in front of you, the question you should ask yourself before you ask the question, can you show me God, can I recognize God if God comes in front of me as one does God have and does he rule God and can be first in difficult or end able and have the strength to rank the planet later it would just rather be permanent above service does not see the Lord face to face.

---
### hearing, authorized sources, transcendental activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w2np8N3hAs4?t=486
**Verse References:** SB 10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Hearing from unauthorized persons will not result in any spiritual advancement."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami emphasizes the importance of hearing from authorized sources for spiritual advancement, warning against fashionable interpretations and lustful inclinations when hearing about Krishna's pastimes with the Gopis.

The reason is that the first item, shravana, is very important. If hearing is from the right sources, it acts very quickly. Generally, people hear from unauthorized persons. Such unauthorized persons may be very learned by academic qualifications, but because they do not follow the principles of devotional service, hearing from them becomes a sheer waste of time. Hearing from unauthorized persons will not result in any spiritual advancement. Sometimes the texts are interpreted fashionably to suit their own purpose. Therefore, first one should select the text that suits their own purpose. Therefore, first one should select the text that suits their own purpose. Therefore, first one should select a competent and bonafide speaker and then hear from him. When the hearing process is perfect and complete, the other processes become automatically perfect in their own way. After hearing comes chanting and then remembering, etc. There are different transcendental activities of the Lord and each and every one of them is competent to bestow the desired result provided the hearing process is perfect. In the Bhagavatam, the activities of the Lord begin from his dealings with the Pandavas. That's the first canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam. There are many other pastimes of the Lord in connection with his dealings with the asuras and others, and in the tenth canto, the sublime dealings with his conjugal associates, the Gopis, as well as his married wives at Dwaraka are mentioned. Since the Lord is absolute, there is no difference in the transcendental nature of each and every dealing of the Lord, whether the description in the first canto is about the Lord's dealings with the Pandavas or not the Lord's dealings with the Pandavas or in some other canto about the Lord's dealings with some other asuras or it is the dealing of the Lord with his most beloved devotees, the Gopis, or his beloved Queens at Dwaraka, all are transcendental, all are transcendental, but sometimes people in an unauthorized hearing process take more interest in hearing about his dealings with the Gopis. Such an inclination indicates the lusty feelings of the hearer, so a bona fide speaker of the dealings of the Lord never indulges in such hearings. One must hear about the Lord from the very beginning as in the Srimad Bhagavatam or any other scripture, and that will help the hearer attain perfection by progressive development.

---
### transcendental, mundane attachment, spiritual life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w2np8N3hAs4?t=689
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We must always remember that the Lord is always transcendental to any mundane attachment."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains that Krishna's dealings with the Pandavas are as important as his dealings with the Gopis, emphasizing Krishna's transcendence and the importance of hearing about him and his devotees for spiritual life.

One should not therefore consider that his dealings, that means Krishna's dealings with the Pandavas, are less important than his dealings with the Gopis. We must always remember that the Lord is always transcendental to any mundane attachment. Krishna doesn't have any attachment, any material attachment at all. In all the above mentioned dealings of the Lord, he is the hero in all circumstances and hearing about him or about his devotees or combatants is conducive to spiritual life. It is said that the Vedas and Puranas etc are all made to revive our lost relation with him, hearing of all these scriptures is absolutely essential. In the previous verses, Kunti Devi has explained that those who have come to this material world are working very hard like asses and have such a hard burden that they cannot bear it because the lusty desires have created heavy work that puts them always in trouble. Krishna comes to introduce the system by which one can get relief from this continuously troublesome life. Material life means troublesome heavy work, that's the essence of material line.

---
### religion, laws of God, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w2np8N3hAs4?t=797
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Religion consists of the laws of God."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami defines religion as following the laws of God, contrasting it with subjective beliefs, and emphasizes Krishna's role in correcting deviations and showing the path of devotional service for overcoming material existence.

Now as regards what is Pushpa Roshani Gratia? Religion consists of the laws of God. People who do not know this think that religion means faith, but although you may have faith in something, and I may have faith in something, and although I may believe you, and you may not, may or may not believe me, that is not religion. There is even a supposedly religious mission that says, you can manufacture your own way, whatever you think is right, that is right. This is their philosophy, wrong philosophy, bogus philosophy, but that is not science. Supposing I am a madman, is it all right? Is it all right to think, whatever I think is correct? Is it all right to think like that? How could this be? 2 plus 2 equals 4 is science. If I believe that 2 plus 2 equals 5 or 3, does it become true? No, never. So, there are laws of God, and when there is dharma, asya glani, deviation from these laws, we suffer. So, Krishna comes to correct these mistakes, and he shows us the way of devotional service. And if we follow that, very easily, we can cross over this most difficult material existence. I will stop here. Thank you. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### dependence on Krishna, protection, devotee's position
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I8T1xXo0pdk?t=65
**Verse References:** SB 1.8.37
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only Krishna can protect us."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of Kunti's prayer, emphasizing the devotee's complete dependence on Krishna for protection, contrasting it with the unsafe position of non-devotees who seek other shelters.

O my Lord, you have executed all duties yourself. Are you leaving us? Today though we are completely dependent on your mercy and have no one else to protect us. Now when all kings are at enmity with us. This verse spoken by Kunti Devi in a prayer to Krishna is clearly indicating the position of a pure devotee like Kunti Devi on the Supreme Lord Krishna. In a sense what is Kunti Devi telling? My dear Lord Krishna, we are dependent on you. We are totally dependent on your mercy. We have no one else for protecting us. Only you are our protector. This is the factual position of everyone, not only Kunti Devi. But pure devotees know this, recognize this and always take shelter of Krishna. The non-devotees, they try to take shelter of so many other so-called shelters. But they are not. But nobody else can protect us, the living beings, except Krishna. Only Krishna can protect us.

### material disease, independence, constitutional position
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I8T1xXo0pdk?t=155
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The material disease is due to thinking of becoming independent of everything."
**Summary:** The speaker defines the material disease as the desire to be independent of Krishna, contrasting it with our constitutional position of dependence, which, paradoxically, leads to true independence from other material influences.

Because non-devotee means somebody who is in a very unsafe position under the clutches of the illusory energy. It is not at all safe. It is not at all secure. It is not at all the best shelter. So Sri Lama Prabhupada explains in the purport to this verse. The Pandavas are most fortunate because with all good luck, they were entirely dependent on the mercy of the Lord. In the material world, to be dependent on the mercy of someone else is the utmost sign of misfortune. See, in the material world, people want to be independent. That's the material disease. What is the material disease? To be independent of Krishna. Because our constitutional position is dependence on Krishna. And by becoming dependent on Krishna, not by becoming, by remaining dependent on Krishna, we are completely independent of all other persons, all other so-called sources of protection. But in the case of our transcendental relation with the Lord, it is the most fortunate case where we can live completely dependent on Krishna. The material disease is due to thinking of becoming independent of everything.

Ravana lived millions and millions of years back in some Dretayuga former age this Ravana he wanted to prepare a direct staircase to the plants of heaven and transport people against the laws of God what are the laws of God that anybody wants to elevate themselves like we've energized performing pious activities and qualify themselves to go there impious persons cannot force themselves or somehow go there it's not possible so ramana thought why should people perform pious activities i will make a staircase to heaven from the earth and anybody wants to go even without performing any austerity they can go i will give them the access this is what ramana thought but that is never to happen. ### illusion, suffering, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I8T1xXo0pdk?t=573
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, we must try to be sanatha so that we can always be protected from the unfavorable condition of material existence."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the perfection of life is to depend on the Lord's will, contrasting it with the illusion of independence. He describes the cycle of misery and temporary relief in the material world, and the importance of being 'sanatha' (protected by the Lord) rather than 'anatha' (without a guardian).

We depend completely on the Supreme God. The perfection of life is therefore to become dependent on the will of the Lord instead of becoming falsely independent in the material world. Somebody may say I am an independent person in the material world but that person is speaking in ignorance under illusions somebody may think everything is fine with me now that is because after the previous misery that somebody has experienced then they get some relief from that misery they think everything is fine but very soon another misery is going to affect them miseries come one after another one after another one after another there is some relief period between two miseries and that period people wrongly think because wrongly think because wrongly think because wrongly think right now, I've got some relief, some misery, I was suffering, therefore I think everything is fine. Those who try to become falsely independent of the Lord are called anatha or without any guardian, anatha. Whereas those who are completely dependent on the will of the Lord are called sanatha or those having someone to protect them. Therefore, we must try to be sanatha so that we can always be protected from the unfavorable condition of material existence.

### maya, devotional service, Mahatma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I8T1xXo0pdk?t=696
**Verse References:** BG 7.19
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "After many, many births, one fortunate person becomes aware of the fact that Vasudeva or Krishna is all in all and that the best way of leading one's life is to surrender unto him completely."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the need for protection from Maya in the material world, achieved through dependence on Krishna via devotional service. He quotes Bhagavad Gita 7.19, explaining that after many births, a person realizes Krishna is everything and surrenders to Him, becoming a Mahatma, exemplified by the Pandavas.

As long as we are here, in the material world, we need protection against the onslaught of maya and how is that protection to be achieved? By becoming dependent on Krishna through devotional service. By the deluding power of the external material nature, we forget that material condition of life is the most undesirable perplexity. That's the power of maya. As I said, when there is some relief from some suffering, we think under the influence of illusion, I am very safe. I am protected or I am fine. I don't require any protection. That's what I think. The Bhagavad Gita, 7th chapter, 19th verse, therefore, directs us that after many, many births, one fortunate person becomes aware of the fact that Vasudeva or Krishna is all in all and that the best way of leading one's life is to surrender unto him completely. bahunam janmanamante jnanavan mam prapadyate jnanavan means one person. Got mature knowledge. Fully enlightened by properly maturing in transcendental knowledge. Such a person is actually a Mahatma. Mahatma means a pure devotee. All the members of the Pandava family were Mahatmas in household life. They were not renunciates. They were not living in a secluded place doing some meditation or tapasya. They were householders.

### scriptural lessons, dependence on Krishna, Kunti's prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I8T1xXo0pdk?t=828
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, devotees cannot live outside of the protection of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating that scriptures teach dependence on the Supreme Lord, illustrated by the Pandavas' reliance on Krishna. He uses the analogy of a fish out of water to describe a devotee separated from Krishna's protection, and refers back to Kunti's prayer asking Krishna to stay with them.

They had responsible duties as Kshatriyas. Maharaj Yudhishthira was the head of these Mahatmas, as Pandavas. And Queen Kunti Devi was the mother. The lessons of the Bhagavad Gita and all the Puranas, specifically the Bhagavata Puranas or Srimad Bhagavatam, are therefore inevitably connected with the history of the Pandava Mahatmas. The scriptures are teaching us that we should depend upon the Supreme Lord. That's what the scriptures are teaching us. And that is best illustrated by this description of the Pandavas' dependence on Krishna. For the Pandavas, separately, they were dependent on Krishna. For the Pandavas, separation from the Lord was just like separation of the fish from water. The fish cannot live outside of water. Similarly, devotees cannot live outside of the protection of Krishna. So Queen Kunti is pursuing the Lord. Please stay with us. Don't go home. Because we are totally dependent on you. So I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Thank you.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.8.38 | 07-03-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### prayer, dependence on Krishna, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/f2mPIU4z17Y?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The devotees are very well aware that their existence is totally dependent on Krishna, and only because of Krishna's mercy, they are able to perform their duties."
**Summary:** Queen Kunti's prayer emphasizes the devotee's complete dependence on Krishna for their existence and ability to perform duties, highlighting the futility of life without His grace.

Om Agyana Timiraandhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chakshurunmeelitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatyade Shatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shiva Sadhi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devim Saraswati Bhaktivinoda Satsang Nithinama. In this session, we will be discussing from the 38th verse of the 8th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, continuing the prayers by Queen Kunti. KVAYAM NAMA ROOPABHYAM YADUBI SAHAPANDAVAH BHAVATO ADHARSHANAM YARIHI HRISHIKANAM IVESHITUHU. The meaning of this verse is: As the name and fame of a particular body is finished with the disappearance of the living spirit, similarly, if you do not look upon us, all our fame and activities, along with the Pandavas and Yadus, will end at once. This is yet another prayer offered by Queen Kunti to Krishna, that the devotees are very well aware that their existence is totally dependent on Krishna, and only because of Krishna's mercy, they are able to perform their duties.

### dependence on Krishna, material existence, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/f2mPIU4z17Y?t=102
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without the guidance of Lord Krishna, all of them are non-entities."
**Summary:** The lecture emphasizes that devotees are dependent on Krishna's mercy to perform their duties, comparing the body without the spirit soul to the Pandavas and Yadus without Krishna's guidance, highlighting the importance of Krishna consciousness and avoiding complacency in material existence.

They are able to perform activities in a nice way. And the example is being given, when the spirit soul leaves the body, the body is a useless piece of dead matter. But as long as the spirit is there, the body is having so many nice life symptoms. Actually, the body is always matter, dead matter. But the presence of the spirit makes it appear to be having life. And the absence of the spirit means immediately the body starts decomposing. It cannot be sustained anymore. So in the purport, Prabhupada explains, the body is quite aware that the existence of the Pandavas is due to Shri Krishna only. This point has to be very, very nicely understood. And only then we become very serious in Krishna consciousness and not be complacent in our practice. In this material world, while we are still here, we should not be complacent. We should know that every moment I should be dependent on Krishna's mercy only. And for that, I have to be Krishna conscious always, not sometimes, always. The Pandavas are undoubtedly well established in name and fame and are guided by a great king, Yudhishthira, whose morality personified. He is called Dharma because he is morality personified. And the Yadus are undoubtedly great allies of the Pandavas. But without the guidance of Lord Krishna, all of them are non-entities.

Krishna's energy and Krishna himself are not different shakti shaktimata this is the philosophy philosophical understanding shakti means energy matayaha ashaktiman means krishna the possessor the owner the controller the enjoyer of all his energies all his energies so example is given by shlaparapa the sun for example is energetic and the sunshine is energy as long as the sunshine is present the sun is present and if the sun were not present the Sun's energy would also not be there just like at night the Sun is not in the sky so there's no sunshine sunshine can never exist without the Sun Sun can never be without the sunshine so this is the kind of interrelationship the energy and energetic must both exist although the Mayavadi philosophers do not accept the energetic but only the in-person energy we must accept both the energy and the energetic this is the special thing about Krishna conscious philosophy that Krishna is the energetic source of all energies and all energies belong to Krishna and Krishna can never be without his energy and energy cannot exist independently without Krishna so therefore the devotees always know energy is there means Krishna and Krishna is there means energies are automatically there always there so this understanding will help us actually become Krishna conscious I'll stop here thank you very much Hare Krishna.

### prosperity, industrialization, negative consequences
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KLL0oh07pmA?t=52
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even prosperity flourishes by natural gifts and not by gigantic industrial enterprises."
**Summary:** This segment introduces Queen Kunti's prayers about prosperity being due to Krishna's glance and contrasts it with the modern view of industrial development, highlighting the negative consequences of industrialization.

In this session, we will be discussing from the fortieth verse of the eighth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Continuing the prayers by Queen Kunti, Ime Janapada Sriddha Supak Kau Shadhi Veerudhaha Vanadrinad Yudanvanto Hedante Tava Bhikshitaihi. The meaning of this verse is all these cities and villages are flourishing in all respects because the herbs and grains are in abundance. The trees are full of fruits, the rivers are flowing, the hills are full of minerals, and the oceans full of wealth. And this is all due to your glancing over them. Kunti is offering prayers to Krishna because of Krishna's glance, there is prosperity abundance of everything. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, even prosperity flourishes by natural gifts and not by gigantic industrial enterprises. In the modern civilization, people think industries are generating many jobs, increasing the wealth of the nation, increasing the GDP and giving so many nice gadgets and facilities and machines for peoples. But actually, there is the negative side of this industrial development. So many problems due to these industries, which are not generally highlighted or discussed by the industrialists themselves, just like the waste generated, the pollution, the pollution, the pollution. So, there is a negative side of this industrial development.

---
### exploitation, natural gifts, dissatisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KLL0oh07pmA?t=172
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The more we go on increasing such troublesome industries to squeeze out the vital energy of the human being the more there will be unrest and dissatisfaction of the people in general."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the negative aspects of industrial development, focusing on the destruction of finer human sentiments, exploitation of the poor, and the dependence on artificial living, contrasting it with the natural gifts provided by Krishna.

But actually, there is the negative side of this industrial development. The dependence on artificial way of living, all this has actually led to destroying the finer sentiments of our human being. So, Srila Prabhupada explains some of these things in the purport. Industrial enterprises of products of a godless civilization and they caused the destruction of the noble aims of human life this is what Prabhupada is explaining they cause the destruction of the noble aims of human life the more we go on increasing such troublesome industries to squeeze out the vital energy of the human being the more there will be unrest and dissatisfaction of the people in general although if you only can live lavishly by exploitation not everybody actually is prosperous because of the industries it's only a few people who are becoming very very rich and they ensure that they have all the best facilities for their stay for their family members for their comfortable living etc but they end up exploiting the the poor people the natural gifts such as grains and vegetables, fruits, rivers, the hills of jewels and minerals, and the seas full of pearls are supplied by the order of the Supreme as He desires.

---
### material nature, exploitation, God consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KLL0oh07pmA?t=294
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The natural law is that the human being may take advantage of these godly gifts by nature and satisfactorily flourish on them without being captivated by the exploitative mentality of lording it over material nature."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the role of material nature and the Supreme Lord in providing natural gifts, emphasizing that humans should take advantage of these gifts without exploiting them, and that attempting to exploit material nature leads to entanglement and destruction of God consciousness.

Material nature produces them in abundance or restrict them at times according to the desire of the Supreme Lord. The natural law is that the human being may take advantage of these godly gifts by nature and satisfactorily flourish on them without being captivated by the exploitative mentality of lording it over material nature. The industrial enterprises are basically exploitation due to the tendency of lording, but accepting the gifts of nature. The gifts of nature given by Krishna, the Supreme Lord, who is actually the one who is directing material nature, so then we may accept them and live very nicely without destroying the noble aim of life that is God consciousness, God realization. The more we attempt to exploit material nature according to our whims of enjoyment, the more we shall become entrapped by the reaction of such exploitative attempts. Material nature is entangling if we try to exploit.

---
### exploitation, natural resources, industrial enterprises
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KLL0oh07pmA?t=386
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If the human civilization has sufficient grains, minerals, jewels, water, milk etc. Then why should we hanker after terrible industrial enterprises at the cost of the labor of some unfortunate men."

**Summary:** This segment describes how depending on God's creation leads to bliss and abundance, emphasizing the cooperation between all living entities with Krishna in the center, which is the essence of Vedic civilization and Krishna consciousness.

If we depend on God's creation there will be no scarcity but simply ananda or bliss. God's creation provides sufficient grains and grass while we eat. The grains and fruits the animals like the cows will eat the grass. The bulls will help produce grains and they will take only a little being satisfied with what we throw away. If we take fruit and throw away the skin the animal will be satisfied with the skin. If anybody has got a Goshala then they have experienced that we take the vegetable and peel we give it away to the animals. We take the fruit and the peel we give it to the animals and they are happy and they are satisfied and they are nourished by those peels. ### Krishna's power, material nature, creation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KLL0oh07pmA?t=773
**Verse References:** BG 9.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment further explains Krishna's power to create through His glance, stating that He injects living entities into material nature, and referencing the Brahma Samhita and Bhagavad Gita to support this concept.

And then becomes pregnant and gives birth to the living entities. Krishna injects the living entities into the womb of material nature. tasmin garbham dadamyaham, Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita. It is simply by Krishna's glance that plants, trees, animals and all of the living entities come forth. How is this possible? None of us can say simply by glancing over my wife. I can make her pregnant. Not possible for us. Although this is impossible for us, it is not impossible for Krishna. The Brahma Samhita says, angani yasya sakalendriya vritti manti. What does this mean? It means every part of Krishna's body has all the capabilities of all the other parts of his body. With our eyes, we can only see. But Krishna merely by seeing can make others pregnant. There is no need of sex. For simply by glancing, Krishna can create pregnancy. In Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says in the 9th chapter, 10th verse, maya dhyakshena prakriti hi suyatesu characharam. By my supervision, material nature gives birth to all moving and non-moving living beings. The word aksha means ice. So akshena indicates that all living entities take birth because of the Lord's glance.

### prayer, Krishna, Kunti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eyjg3Z-T0iE?t=56
**Verse References:** SB 1.8.43
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You possess all mystic powers and you are the preceptor of the entire universe. You are the Almighty God and I offer you my respectful obeisances."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami recites and translates Queen Kunti's prayer to Krishna, highlighting Krishna's various attributes and roles.

This is the last prayer by Queen Kunti in this chapter. Shri Krishna Krishna Sakha Vrishni Rishabhava Nidrup Rajanya Vamsha Dahanana Pavarga Virya Govinda Godvija Surarthi Haravatara Yogeshwara Kila Guru Bhagavan Namaste. Kunti Devi is addressing Krishna in different ways in this prayer. The meaning of this verse is: O Krishna, O friend of Arjuna, O chief among the descendants of Vrishni, You are the destroyer of those political parties which are disturbing elements on this earth. Your prowess never deteriorates, you are the proprietor of the transcendental abode and you descend to relieve the distress of the cows, the brahmanas and the devotees. You possess all mystic powers and you are the preceptor of the entire universe. You are the Almighty God and I offer you my respectful obeisances.

---
### Krishna, devotees, leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eyjg3Z-T0iE?t=138
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Actually the kings are meant to rule over the citizens exactly as the representative of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing Krishna's supreme position, his relationships with devotees, and the role of kings as representatives of Krishna.

So Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, a summary of the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna is made herein by Srimati Kunti Devi. Kunti Devi knows very well Krishna is not just a relative, not just an ordinary person, not just another powerful Kshatriya, but Krishna is the Supreme Lord. The Supreme Personality of God. And he has multiple relationships with his devotees. Accordingly, he is addressed here in different ways. So Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, the Almighty Lord has his eternal transcendental abode where he is engaged in keeping Surabhi cows in the Goloka Brindavana. Krishna is always engaged in pastimes with his devotees, especially the cowherd boys, the cows, the gopis, like that. Krishna is served by hundreds and thousands of goddesses of fortune in Goloka Brindavana. He descends on the material world to reclaim his devotees. He is the one who has the power to annihilate the disturbing elements in groups of political parties and kings who are supposed to be in charge of administration work. Actually the kings are meant to rule over the citizens exactly as the representative of Krishna. Because in reality it is Krishna who maintains everyone. Eko bahunam yo viddhati kama. But if the king does not conduct himself as the representative of Krishna, then he is not fit to be the ruler of the people in general. Then Krishna either incarnates to destroy such rulers, or he empowers his devotees to actually end the rule of such a king. So, this is a very important point.

Exactly according to his will the different energies will work and they will carry out Krishna's instructions or Krishna's desires even though Krishna creates maintains and annulates still he is always full with prowess and does not deteriorate in potency. In the material world we have experienced when we expand our energy unless we renew we feel exhausted whereas Krishna he employs his energies for so many different activities but his energy never reduces. He is always full of all energies. Further Srila Prabhupada says the cows the brahmanas and the devotees of the Lord are all objects of special attention. Cows brahmanas and devotees Krishna particularly is interested in the welfare of the cows and the brahmanas and devotees. Now all these cows brahmanas and Vaishnavas are object of special attention because they are very important factors for the general welfare of all living beings.

### kingship, Krishna's representation, impersonalism, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eyjg3Z-T0iE?t=691
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker discusses the role of a king as God's representative, the difference between Krishna and individual souls, and the importance of devotional service for understanding God, contrasting it with impersonal conceptions.

Now generally in the monarchy system the king is honored very very gorgeously. Why? King is meant to be the representative of God. So king should not be treated as an ordinary person particularly when the king is a devotee of Krishna. So in Sanskrit king is also called Naradeva which means God in human form. So his duty the king's duty is same as Krishna's duty that just like God maintains all living beings same way the king is a supreme citizen in this state and is responsible for the welfare of all the citizens of this state. So Prabhupada makes this point Krishna is not impersonal Krishna is the supreme person but each one of us we are limited, Krishna is unlimited this difference always exists between us and Krishna. Our individual persons is such that we have limited knowledge we are limited opulences but Krishna has unlimited forms or opulences or knowledge Krishna is unlimited. So we can never compare ourselves with Krishna but at the same time we can understand we are very very tiny and we do possess some of the qualities which Krishna has got. So therefore the understanding of God is possible only if we engage in pure devotional service. Now in the impersonal conception we are not able to understand exactly who or what is God. We are not able to understand exactly who or what is God. They simply deny different attributes of God without realizing that God cannot be understood simply by reading some books or simply by hearing some descriptions we have to hear from the right source, we have to read the right books coming written by a devotee then we may get some proper understanding of god so i'll stop here thank you very much harikushan

### Krishna's glories, devotion, unlimited nature of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qoSNt-0ILgM?t=121
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anything that is enchanting in the world is said to be a representation of the Lord. Ultimately, Krishna is always the source of everything."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing that Krishna is the source of everything enchanting, and that while conditioned souls are enchanted by Krishna's mystic powers, devotees are enchanted by his glories, and Krishna bestows blessings upon them.

So Srila Prabhupada says that in the purport, Anything that is enchanting in the world is said to be a representation of the Lord. Ultimately, Krishna is always the source of everything. In this world, in the spiritual world, everywhere. He is the ultimate source. Janmad yasiya taha. He is the ultimate source. Aham sarvasya prabhavo. So, anything enchanting is a representation of Krishna. Now, the conditioned souls, that means the living entities in this world, who are trapped in the illusory energy, they are enchanted by Krishna's mystic powers. But, the devotees of Krishna, the pure devotees, are also enchanted in a different way by the glories of Krishna. And, for the devotees, Krishna always bestows his blessings. So, Sri La Pravupada says, just like Krishna has many energies which work differently. So, Kunti Devi prayed to the Lord just to enunciate a fragment of his glories. The Lord's glories are unlimited, because he is an unlimited personality. He is the unlimited personality. Everybody else is limited. Only Krishna is unlimited.

### devotional prayers, Uttama Shloka, Krishna's satisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qoSNt-0ILgM?t=235
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Any amount of chosen words is not sufficient to enumerate the Lord's glory. Yet, the Supreme Lord is satisfied by the prayers offered by different pure devotees, because the prayers are offered by his beloved devotees, he accepts them."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how devotees glorify the Lord with chosen words, leading to the title Uttama Shloka, and explains that while no amount of words is sufficient, Krishna is pleased by sincere prayers, even from beginners, just as a father is pleased by a child's early attempts at language.

Now, devotees, when they glorify the Lord, they glorify him with chosen words, Kalapadaihi. And, therefore, the Lord is known as Uttama Shloka. The best of poetry is composed to describe the Supreme Lord. Uttama Shloka. Now, Sri La Pravupada says one important point here. That any amount of chosen words is not sufficient to enumerate the Lord's glory. Yet, the Supreme Lord is satisfied by the prayers offered by different pure devotees, because the prayers are offered by his beloved devotees, he accepts them. Prabhupada gives an example just like a father is satisfied even by the broken linguistic attempts of the growing child. This is a common experience for us that when a child speaks in the beginning in a broken language, it is very pleasing to the father. So similarly, even a beginner in Krishna consciousness offers sincere prayers to Krishna. Krishna is pleased with that. But Krishna's devotees who know Krishna, they always offer wonderful prayers which are composed by chosen words.

### Maya, mercy, pleasing Krishna

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains the two meanings of 'maya' - illusion and mercy - and how in this verse, it refers to the Lord's mercy upon Kunti Devi. He emphasizes that pleasing Krishna pleases the entire universe, as it is a part of Him.

Here in this verse, the word maya has been used. Maya has got two different meanings. One meaning of the word maya is delusion or illusion. And another meaning of the word maya is mercy. Now in this verse, the word maya has been used by Sita Goswami. In the sense of mercy, it is the Lord's mercy upon Kunti Devi, who offered wonderful prayers, that Kunti Devi was able to please the Lord by offering him wonderful prayers. So it's the mercy of the Lord that he accepted those prayers, and in return, he mildly smiled at Kunti Devi, indicating acceptance of the prayers, wonderfully offered by Kunti Devi. So Srila Prabhupada says, if Krishna is pleased and even smiles a little, then the whole universe will be pleased. See, Kunti Devi has pleased the Lord by prayers, and Krishna is pleased by Kunti Devi's prayers, then Krishna is pleased means the whole universe is pleased. Tasmanti trade jag If Krishna is pleased or satisfied, then the whole universe will be satisfied. Why? Because the whole universe is a part and parcel of Krishna only. But why not? Only then will we become liberation, reciprocatedband using many forms and microphones. Krishna will be satisfied if we do so.

### satisfying Krishna, centrality of Krishna, analogy of the tree
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qoSNt-0ILgM?t=472
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Just like you water a tree by pouring water at the root of the tree, similarly, by satisfying Krishna, you can satisfy all the others."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of the body and food in the stomach to illustrate that satisfying Krishna is essential for universal satisfaction. He further explains that Krishna is the central point and the root of the entire creation, and satisfying Him is like watering the root of a tree.

Just like it is given elsewhere in the Bhagavatam, prāṇopahārācchayathendriyāṇo, just like the parts of the body, they are working. Just like when there is food in the stomach, then the hunger is mitigated and one feels satisfaction. So, Srila Prabhupāda gives an example here. Even though we have various senses, which can be satisfied in different ways by sense gratification. If a person is hungry, then the sense gratification will not work till the hunger is mitigated. Similarly, till Krishna is pleased. You cannot make the whole world happy. Not possible. Now we should always remember Krishna is the central point of this entire creation. And Krishna is also like the root of this manifestation. Lord Krishna is the root of this manifestation. So, this So, just like you water a tree by pouring water at the root of the tree, similarly, by satisfying Krishna, you can satisfy all the others.

---
### Krishna as the source, material and spiritual creation, Maya as forgetfulness of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qoSNt-0ILgM?t=593
**Verse References:** BG 9.4
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna declares in the Bhagavad Gita, I am the source of everything. So, Krishna being the source, when we please Krishna or satisfy Krishna or make an offering to Krishna, then everyone becomes satisfied."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Krishna's declaration in the Bhagavad Gita that He is the source of everything, and explains that both the material and spiritual creations originate from Krishna. He uses the analogy of a cloud obscuring the sun to describe how maya, or forgetfulness of Krishna, covers our vision.

Krishna declares in the Bhagavad Gita, I am the source of everything. So, Krishna being the source, when we please Krishna or satisfy Krishna or make an offering to Krishna, then everyone becomes satisfied. Now, Srila Prabhupada further explains that the material creation of Krishna is the source of everything. The material creation and the spiritual creation are both creations of Krishna, but they are existing side by side. Even though they are existing side by side, the material creation is another part of the spiritual creation. Just like the cloud in the sky is a creation of the sunshine. Sunshine is not created by the cloud, but the cloud is created by the sunshine. So, when the cloud is created by the sunshine, it covers the very sunshine, which was the cause of its creation. But actually, can the cloud cover the sun or sunshine? Not possible. So, the cloud, when it covers, it covers our vision. It covers our vision. Similarly, maya means absence of Krishna consciousness. So, when you forget Krishna, that is maya. And that is a condition of all the living beings in the world. And that is a condition of all the living beings in the world. And that is a condition of all the living beings in the world. And that is a condition of all the living beings in this world who are taken birth by force of karma. They are forgetful of Krishna. Therefore, they are in maya.

---
### Krishna's omnipresence, overcoming Maya, offering prayers with feeling
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qoSNt-0ILgM?t=719
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Offering prayers to Supreme Lord means offering prayers with feelings."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna is all-pervading and resides in everyone's heart, but we are unable to see Him due to the illusion of maya. He emphasizes that coming to Krishna consciousness eliminates maya and that offering prayers to the Lord should be done with genuine feeling, following the example of devotees like Kunti.

In this material world, Krishna is all-pervading. He has entered into every atom. He has entered into everybody's heart. Why are we unable to see Krishna who sits in our heart? Because we have been covered by the illusion of maya. That is the only reason. So, when we come to Krishna consciousness, then there is no more maya. No more maya. Now, Srila Prabhupada says another important point. Offering prayers to Supreme Lord means offering prayers with feelings. We are persons. Krishna is the Supreme Person. So, there is an exchange between the devotee and the Lord. What is that exchange? That exchange is Krishna, Krishna, that we offer a praise to Krishna feelingly so Srila Prabhupada is telling us that Krishna is Uttama shloka glorified by the choicest poetry but all these poems or all these songs and all these creations are actually full of meaning full of meaning and very beautifully composed and many many prayers are there because Krishna has so many devotees and they offer wonderful prayers so we should learn these prayers and following the footsteps of great devotees like Kunti we should offer such prayers to Krishna that will be pleasing to Krishna I will stop here thank you very much

### devotion, divine interaction, love

**Verse References:** SB 1.8.45

**Summary:** This segment introduces the verse describing Krishna's departure from Hastinapura and Yudhishthira's loving request for him to stay, highlighting the power of loving devotion.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devi Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 45th verse of the 8th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Tam badham ityupa mantriya pravishya gajasah fayam striyascha svapuram yasyan premna ragnya nivaritaha. The meaning of this verse is, Thus accepting the prayers of Srimati Kunti Devi, the Lord subsequently informed other ladies of his departure by entering the palace of Hastinapura. But upon preparing to leave, he was stopped by King Yudhishthira, who implored him lovingly.

---
### devotion, divine grace, loving service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NQEqcHopZ24?t=100
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Almighty God is thus conquered only by loving service and nothing else."
**Summary:** This segment explains Srila Prabhupada's purport on the verse, emphasizing that Krishna is conquered by loving service and elaborating on the significance of Hastinapura as the capital of the world empire.

So this verse describes the Lord's departure from Hastinapura to Dwaraka after Yudhishthira had been installed as the emperor of the world after the battle of Kurukshetra. He accepted the prayers of Srimati Kunti Devi and he is now preparing to leave, but he was stopped by Yudhishthira and it is explained by Srila Prabhupada what was the kind of interaction between King Yudhishthira and Krishna. So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, No one could make Lord Krishna stay at Hastinapura when he decided to start for Dwaraka. But the simple request of King Yudhishthira that the Lord remain there for a few days more was immediately effective. This signifies that the power of King Yudhishthira was loving affection, which the Lord could not deny. The Almighty God is thus conquered only by loving service and nothing else. He is fully independent in all his dealings, but he voluntarily accepts obligations by the loving affection of his pure devotees. So Srila Prabhupada explains in another place the significance of the word Gajasa Hwayam that is used in this words. The capital of the world empire was called in those days Hastinapura or Gajasa Hwaya. Gaja means elephant. Hasti also means elephant. Formally the kings used to keep many many horses and elephants. And in those days there was only one king ruling the entire world. And the capital of that king who was ruling the whole world was Hastinapura. So there was only one flag and these things are mentioned in the Srimad Bhagavatam.

---
### Vedic culture, history, global unity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NQEqcHopZ24?t=263
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Formerly the whole planet was known as Bharatvarsha."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the Vedic culture that once encompassed the entire world, the historical context of Bharatvarsha, and the significance of Mahabharata as the history of greater Bharatvarsha.

There were not many states and the world was under one culture. Which culture? The Vedic culture. The advanced people in those times were Aryans. Now Srila Prabhupada explains Indo-European stock are also Aryans. They came from Central India and Central Asia. That is the history. Caspian Sea. Prabhupada gives an example. What today is called Caspian Sea was the place of Kashyapa Muni. From word Kashyapa comes the word Caspian. Formerly, another example Srila Prabhupada gives. Formerly the capital of Afghanistan was known as Gandhara Desha. Now it has become Kandahar by historic context. By historical references, Srila Prabhupada says, it will be ascertained that the whole planet was known as Bharatvarsha unlike now what is called as Bharat or Bharatvarsha is only the sub-continent of India. Formerly the whole planet was known as Bharatvarsha. And before it was known as Bharatvarsha, the whole planet, it was called Ilaavrata Varsha. But since the time of King Bharata, who was the forefather of the Pandavas, the planet was called Bharatvarsha and throughout the planet there was Vedic culture and Srila Prabhupada says the treasures are still available and the history of the whole world is called Mahabharata. Mahabharata means greater Bharata. It is like nowadays we have greater channels. Greater Chennai, greater Mumbai. So those days when Mahabharata was compiled, it means history of the greater Bharatvarsha, the entire planet.

The king of Hastinapura, once again Prabhupada is telling, was the emperor of the whole world, of this whole planet, earth planet. The king of Hastinapura, once again Prabhupada is telling, was the emperor of the whole world, of this whole planet, earth planet. They were- There were other states, but they were paying tax to the emperor. They were there were other states, but they were paying tax to the emperor. Now Krishna, after receiving the nice prayers by Kunti Devi, nicely composed and accepted the prayers by the word which is indicating that, Badham, Badham means, Yes. That's alright. Badham means, Yes. That's alright. He smiled, Krishna smiled. He smiled, Krishna smiled. Because whatever Kunti had said, that is true. There is no exaggeration. So Krishna smiled because whatever Kunti said, she was so pleased to enunciate the glories of Lord Krishna. Krishna, because she knows what is Krishna. In this way, Krishna was preparing to return to Dwaraka. Now Yudhishthira Maharaj requested Krishna, my dear Krishna, my dear brother, if you kindly stay for a few days more, be very nice. So Prabhupada explains, Krishna is fully independent. He could have refused. No, I cannot stay more. I have to go. But here it is said, premna. Premna means out of love. Yudhishthira implored Krishna, please stay back a few more days. So it is to be understood that Maharaj Yudhishthira cannot order the Supreme Personality of Godhead. But by love, everything is possible. Therefore, Srila Prabhupada says, if we increase our love for Krishna, we will be able to do everything.

### love, devotion, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NQEqcHopZ24?t=526
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We can purchase Krishna by love."
**Summary:** This segment explains that increasing love for Krishna allows devotees to 'purchase' Him, referencing Kunti Devi's prayer and Prabhupada's teachings, and illustrates this with the example of Arjuna ordering Krishna as his chariot driver.

If we increase our love for Krishna, we will be able to do everything. If we increase our love for Krishna, then Krishna becomes purchased by us. In an earlier verse, in the prayers by Kunti Devi, she said, Namo Akinchanavittaya. Akinchanavitta. He is the property of the pure devotees. Krishna is the property of the pure devotees. So that same point, Prabhupada is telling here, If we increase our love for Krishna, we will be able to do everything. If we increase our love for Krishna, we will be able to do everything. If we increase our love for Krishna, we will be able to do everything. Then Krishna becomes purchased by us. Krishna agrees. Whatever his loving devotees say, that is his kindness. Krishna is actually everyone's order giver. But he accepts the order of his devotee. Now another example Srila Prabhupada gives, when Krishna was driving Arjuna's chariot, It is described in the beginning of the Bhagavad Gita. Arjuna said, Senayorubhayor madhyeratham sthapayam echchuta. So what is the meaning of this, my dear Krishna? You are achchuta. Achchuta means one who never falls back. So, Arjuna is telling Krishna, You accepted the post as my charioteer, Parthasarathi. So, Krishna has to carry out the order of the king who is the master on the chariot. And chariot driver, after all, is supposed to take the order from the master and accordingly drive the chariot. But, Arjuna knew, Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead. Arjuna was very clear. Only, out of friendship, Krishna agreed to become Arjuna's chariot driver. So, when Arjuna requested Krishna, radham sthapayam echchuta, just place my chariot between the two armies. He was little hesitating that I am ordering Krishna who is the supreme personality of Godhead. But then, in spite of that, Arjuna is reminding Krishna also, although you are my master, for the time being, but because you have voluntarily accepted to become my chariot driver, therefore, I am asking you, it is not out of disrespect. So, Krishna also responded by telling, Yes, I am willing. This is the position. So, Prabhupada says, We can purchase Krishna by love.

### knowledge, devotion, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NQEqcHopZ24?t=694
**Verse References:** BG 7.25
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whatever is the subject matter, the goal is to understand Krishna, to know Krishna. That's the perfection of all knowledge."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Krishna is difficult to obtain through Vedic study alone, referencing Brahma Samhita and Bhagavad Gita, and illustrates how Chaitanya Mahaprabhu taught grammar with Krishna as the central focus.

And that is stated in the Brahma Samhita. Veda Shudurla. Dhurlabham adhurlabham atma bhaktau. Dhurlabha means very, very difficult to obtain. So, Brahma says, if you officially study the Vedas, to find out God, or to know about God, that is very, very difficult. So, for those who are trying to understand God by cultivating knowledge of the Vedas, or searching for God in the Vedas, it is very, very difficult for them to obtain Krishna. Krishna also says in the Bhagavad Gita, Naham Prakash Sarvasya Yogamaya Samavrtha. I do not expose myself to everyone, because I am covered by the Yogamaya curtain. So, also Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita, Vedash Sarvair Ahameva Vedyaha. That, all departments of knowledge are really meant for finding out Krishna. So, whichever branch of knowledge you take, Veda means all branches of knowledge, chemistry, physics, politics, sociology, medicine, whichever branch of knowledge that is there in the Vedas, the aim is to find out Krishna. And, this was, actually taught by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. When he was teaching grammar to his students, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, at a very young age, he became a scholar in Sanskrit grammar. So, he was teaching his students, he had opened a small school, and he was explaining what is called as Dhatu. Dhatu means verb in Sanskrit grammar. So, he was explaining simply Krishna. Krishna. Through all the methods of teaching different aspects of Sanskrit grammar, he would only teach about Krishna. That's all. The students somehow became a little dissatisfied. That means, Nimai Pandit, he was known as Nimai Pandit, he simply explains Krishna, Krishna, Krishna in everything he speaks or everything he teaches, he only explains Krishna. But, actually, that's the real point. Whatever is the subject matter, the goal is to understand Krishna, to know Krishna. That's the perfection of all knowledge.

### grief, justice, Kurukshetra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dT2QRHTaFRU?t=107
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Yudhishthira is thinking, for me to be enthroned as the emperor of the world, so much of massacre, it is not, he feels it is not justified."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Yudhishthira's grief over the massacre at Kurukshetra, explaining that he felt unworthy to be enthroned as emperor after so much bloodshed, even though he was the rightful claimant.

Yudhishthira was very much aggrieved for the huge massacre that had happened as a result of the battle of Kurukshetra. So Srila Prabhupada explains this elaborately in the purport: The pious king Yudhishthira was mortified because of the mass massacre of human beings in the battle of Kurukshetra, especially on his account. So it is explained why Yudhishthira was so aggrieved? Why Yudhishthira was so aggrieved? Why Yudhishthira was so aggrieved? In some sense, it would seem that the rules of the Yudhishthira contraption was actually a result of that conflict. But the understanding of which is that Yudhishthira was feeling much aggrieved regarding the things about whom he did not want to see, because in one sense there was no need of fighting. As the rightful claimant for the throne, the whole battle of Kurukshetra was fought and millions were massacred, were killed in the battle. So Yudhishthira is thinking, for me to be enthroned as the emperor of the world, so much of massacre, it is not, he feels it is not justified. But on the principle of justice, Yudhishthira was to replace Duryodhana. The whole clique of politics centered around this point. And all the kings and residents of the whole world became involved in this fight between the rival brothers.

### history, Mahabharata, Ramayana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dT2QRHTaFRU?t=209
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Practically, this was the greatest battle in the world within 5000 years."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the scale of the Kurukshetra war, its historical significance, and its place within the broader context of Vedic history, mentioning the Mahabharata and Ramayana as important historical texts.

Lord Krishna was also there on the side of King Yudhishthira. It is said in the Mahabharata, Adiparva, that 640 million men were killed in the 18 days of the battle of Kurukshetra, and some hundreds of thousands were missing. Practically, this was the greatest battle in the world within 5000 years. So, there is a detailed description in the Mahabharata about this entire episode of the battle, events leading to the battle. And it is an authentic historical record, this itihasa, itihasa means history. And two famous itihasas based on the history of Vedic culture, are the Ramayana and Mahabharata. So, as you are aware, Ramayana is the history of Lord Ramachandra, the incarnation of the Supreme Lord appearing on the earth and ruling the entire world from Ayodhya. And Mahabharata is the history of the Pandavas and Kauravas, and they are ruling the world.
