### devotee glorification, comparison to Indra, pure devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/g_bpM5E5wRU?t=445
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The fame of Maharaj Yudhishthira was not less than that of Indra."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Srila Prabhupada's explanation of the comparison between Yudhishthira and Indra, highlighting that despite Indra's greater opulence, Yudhishthira's fame was equal due to his pure devotion to Krishna. He emphasizes that a pure devotee naturally becomes famous through their actions, without striving for it.

Now Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport the comparison of Maharaj Yudhishthira to the king of heaven is significant. In what way is it significant? Srila Prabhupada explains the king of heaven is thousands and thousands of times greater than Maharaj Yudhishthira in opulence. Naturally the opulence of the inhabitants of the heavenly planets is much much much much more than those of the inhabitants of the earthly planets and correspondingly the king of heaven is many many many millions of times more opulent than the emperor of this earthly planet or this world. Yet it is described in the purport here the fame of Maharaj Yudhishthira was not less than that of Indra. How is that? So the reason is that Maharaj Yudhishthira was a pure devotee of the Lord. See Maharaj Yudhishthira's special position, what is that? He is a pure devotee of Krishna, all the five partners, they are pure devotees of Krishna and by Lord Krishna's grace only was King Yudhishthira on the level of the king of heaven. So the fame of Yudhishthira was because of his being a pure devotee of Krishna. A pure devotee is naturally very famous without any endeavor for attaining fame. By his actions a pure devotee automatically becomes naturally becomes very famous. So Srila Prabhupada explains that even though King Yudhishthira performed only three Ashwamedha Yajnas whereas the king of heaven performed hundreds, still Yudhishthira's fame was equal to that of Indra. So now Srila Prabhupada says that is a prerogative of the devotee of the Lord.

---
### Krishna's impartiality, devotee's connection, sun analogy, Krishna's mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/g_bpM5E5wRU?t=595
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord is equal to everyone, he freely gives away his mercy, he wants everyone to go back to him."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the potential misconception that Krishna is partial to devotees, clarifying that Krishna is equal to everyone, but devotees are more glorified due to their constant connection with the Supreme Lord. He uses the analogy of the sun's rays to illustrate how the Lord's mercy is equally distributed, but its reception depends on individual receptivity. He emphasizes Krishna's desire to deliver all conditioned souls.

Now here is a very important point that somebody may think that Krishna is partial to his devotees. So Srila Prabhupada says no, Krishna is not partial to his devotees. The Lord is equal to everyone, equal to everyone, but a devotee of the Lord is more glorified because he is always in touch with the all great, all great Supreme Lord. This is the position of the devotee, he never forgets Krishna Supreme Personality of Godhead, he is always in touch with the Supreme Personality of Godhead under all circumstances and all conditions at all times, always, always in touch. So therefore the pure devotees are naturally glorious. Now Srila Prabhupada gives an example here. The sun rays are equally distributed, but still there are some places which are always dark. This is you can understand this example it is not difficult that the sun is shining brightly in the sky and distributing the rays equally not only for this planet but for millions of other planets also the sun is distributing the light and heat, but there are some places where the sun light does not, does not reach at all. Those places will always be dark, always be dark. This is not due to the sun but to the receptive power, different places receive different quantity of sunlight. When the sun is shining in the sky that is dependent on the receptive power of the particular place that is different. Similarly now we understand this example we can compare this example those who are sent person devotees of the Lord get the full fledged mercy of the Lord which is always equally distributed everywhere. The Lord is equal to everyone he freely gives away his mercy, he wants everyone to go back to him. That's why he speaks Bhagavad Gita, that's why he personally comes so many times in different incarnations, personally he comes, he sends his representatives, he wants he wants to deliver all the fallen conditioned souls because everyone belongs to him.

---
### Krishna's ownership, rebellious nature, mercy reception, pure devotee benefit
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/g_bpM5E5wRU?t=783
**Verse References:** BG 4
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The pure devotee of the Lord, who are 10% pure devotees, they get the full fledged mercy of the Lord because they want it."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Krishna from the Bhagavad Gita, stating that every living being is His part and parcel, emphasizing our inherent spiritual nature and Krishna's desire for us to be with Him. He discusses our forgetfulness, rebelliousness, and envy as obstacles, urging listeners to rectify their misconceptions and discover the Lord's mercy. He concludes that pure devotees receive the full mercy of the Lord because they are open to it.

Ah, Krishna in the fourth chapter of the Bhagavad Gita says every living being is mine. Yajnatvana punarmoham evam yasyasi pandava sarvabhutani asheshani drakshasi atmani atomai he says every living entity is my part and parcel. Mamaivam shojeevaloke jeevabhutasanata vanatana eternally we are part and parcel of Krishna. So we have the same nature as Krishna, spiritual nature. So Krishna wants us with him, but we are forgetful, we are rebellious, we are envious, we want to be separate, we want to be independent. This is a fact. Many people may not agree, but with a cool and sober head if you think over you understand who you are essentially in a sense and then you consider this then you will realize and you will actually very easily be able to rectify your misconception and discover the mercy of the Lord which is equally available for everyone always. So Prabhupada says the pure devotee of the Lord, who are 10% pure devotees, they get the full fledged mercy of the Lord because they want it. They are open for receiving it and others don't want it, they don't take it. That's all it's available but they don't take it. I'll stop here, thank you.

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the seventh verse of the eighth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, describing Krishna's preparation for departure from Hastinapura after the Kurukshetra battle and his interaction with the Pandavas and Brahmanas.

In this session we will be discussing from the 7th verse of the 8th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, Aamantrya Pandu Putramshcha Shaineyodhava Samyutaha Dwaipayana Adibhir Vipraihi Poojitai Pratipoojitaha. The meaning of this verse is Lord Shri Krishna then prepared for his departure. He invited the sons of Pandu after having been worshipped by the brahmanas headed by Srila Vyasadeva. The Lord also reciprocated greetings.

---
### Krishna's supremacy, varnashrama, eternal position
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YfW_uuY71Z8?t=106
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's position as the Supreme Personality of Godhead is eternally a fact."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna's position as the Supreme Personality of Godhead is eternal and unchanging, even when he assumes different roles, contrasting this with the temporary and changeable positions in the material world.

We should understand the historical incidents in the proper context that Krishna came from Dwaraka in order to conduct the battle of Kurukshetra and after the battle of Kurukshetra Yudhishthira was enthroned as the Emperor and Krishna having completed his mission in visiting Hastinapura. He is now preparing for his departure to go to Kurukshetra. He is now preparing for his departure to go to Kurukshetra in order to return to Dwaraka. That is being described here that Krishna prepared for his departure and when he was leaving Hastinapura the Brahmanas headed by Srila Vyasadeva worshiped Krishna. So, that is explained by Srila Prabhupada in the purport. Apparently, Lord Sri Krishna was a Kshatriya and was not worshipable by the Brahmanas. The convention is Kshatriya should worship Brahmanas. That is the Varanashrama division of the society, members of the society. But the Brahmanas present there in Hastinapura, headed by Srila Vyasa Deva, all knew him, that is knew Krishna, to be the personality of Godhead and therefore they worshipped him. So, they were not wrong in worshipping a Kshatriya. The Brahmanas were not wrong because even though Krishna was playing the part of a Kshatriya, Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead under all circumstances, at all times, in all places. This is the difference between Krishna's position and our positions in the material world.

---
### Krishna's actions, absolute truth, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YfW_uuY71Z8?t=240
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "His position remains the same, absolute. Always the Supreme Personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Krishna's actions, even in subordinate roles, do not diminish his supreme position, and that those who know Krishna properly understand his eternal lordship and worship him accordingly.

In the material world, everybody's position is a temporary, changeable position. Krishna's position as the Supreme Personality of Godhead is eternally a fact. No matter what activities are performed by Krishna. Krishna may perform the role of the chariot driver of Arjuna, but that does not make Krishna come down to a lower position. No, he does not come down in his position. His position remains the same, absolute. Always the Supreme Personality of Godhead. That's how, even though he is seated on the chariot of Arjuna as the chariot driver, Sarathi, but still when Arjuna was perplexed, Krishna instructed Arjuna, and through Arjuna, the entire human society in the subject matter of Bhagavad Gita. So when he is speaking as Bhagavad Gita, he is speaking as the Supreme Lord. In fact, his actions, all his actions are performed in his original position only. He does not lose his position even though he may assume a subordinate position. He may assume an unequal position, but his position is unchanged. This is very important to understand for all of us. So therefore, the Brahmanas headed by Vyasadeva in Hastinapura, they knew Krishna to be the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Eternal Lord, at all times, circumstances, at all times. So therefore, they properly worshipped him. Those who know Krishna properly, they always understand he is Eternal Lord, and he is eternally the controller, eternally the master, and he is eternally worshipped by everyone. Hmm.

---
### social order, varnashrama dharma, example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YfW_uuY71Z8?t=397
**Verse References:** BG 4.13, BG 3.21
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord purposely observed all these social customs so that others would follow him in the future."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna reciprocated greetings to honor the social order and customary usages between the four orders of society, emphasizing the importance of following his example.

The Lord reciprocated the greetings just to honour the social order that a Kshatriya is obedient to the orders of the Brahmanas. Now, when the Brahmanas offer something to Krishna, Krishna reciprocates properly, receiving it, just to honour the social custom. Hmm. That a Kshatriya is obedient to the orders of the Brahmanas. That is social custom. Although Lord Sri Krishna was always offered the respects due the Supreme Lord from all responsible quarters, the Lord never deviated from the customary usages between the four orders of society. Hmm. In the fourth chapter of Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says, chaaturvarnyam maya-srishtam guna-karma-vibhaga-saha. He says, it is I who have created these four divisions of social orders in human society. chaaturvarnyam brahmana-kshatriya-vaisa-shudra. Society can be perfectly organised in that way. The intellectuals, the administrators, the business class, the productive class, that is the business class, and the labourers. And different people have different skills, different qualities. They are born under different influences of the gunas. Accordingly, they can be engaged properly in some livelihood, means of livelihood. But everybody is entitled eligible for ultimate liberation in the human form of life. And that's what the whole varnashrama dharma is about. How to worship Vishnu by performance of your duty in such a way that you will actually be ultimately liberated from material bondage. Hmm. So Krishna never deviates from the customary usages between the four orders of society. The Lord purposely observed all these social customs so that others would follow him in the future. See, to set an example, Krishna says in the third chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, yad yad acharati shrestha ha tattadeve tarojanah sayat pramanam kurute lokas tad anuvartate.

---
### Uttara's fear, Krishna's protection, departure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YfW_uuY71Z8?t=574
**Verse References:** SB 1.8.8
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as he seated himself on the chariot to start for Dwaraka, Krishna has seated himself. He has made preparations for his departure and he is seated on the chariot. He saw Uttara hurrying towards him in fear."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the eighth verse, describing Krishna's departure for Dwaraka and Uttara's fearful approach to him.

Then, in the next verse, the eighth verse, it is said here, gantum krta matir brahman tvarakam ratam asthitah upalebe abhi dhavantim uttaram bhaya vikvalam. The meaning of this verse is, as soon as he seated himself on the chariot to start for Dwaraka, Krishna has seated himself. He has made preparations for his departure and he is seated on the chariot. He saw Uttara hurrying towards him in fear. Uttara is running towards Krishna in fear. Uttara is the wife of Abhimanyu. The daughter-in-law of Arjuna and Subhadra, whose son is Abhimanyu. Abhimanyu was killed in the battle of Kurukshetra. So, Uttara had lost her husband in the battle. So, she was hurrying towards Krishna in fear.

### devotee's dependence, Krishna's protection, reciprocity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YfW_uuY71Z8?t=658
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord protects everyone, but one who depends completely upon him is especially looked after by the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the Pandavas were completely dependent on Krishna's protection as pure devotees, and that Krishna reciprocates with those who depend on him completely, like a father caring for a young child.

Now, Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, all the members of the family of the Pandavas were completely dependent on the protection of the Lord. Why? Because they are all pure devotees of the Lord. All the members of the family of the Pandavas. All of them are pure devotees of the Lord. And the symptom or characteristic of a pure devotee is complete dependence, dependence on the protection of the Lord. In fact, for anyone, there is no other protection outside the protection of Krishna. That's a fact. That's true for everybody, not just for the pure devotees. But, pure devotees know this fact and they always seek shelter of Krishna's lotus feet, Krishna's protection. Because the members of the family of the Pandavas are completely dependent on protection of the Supreme Lord, therefore, the Lord protected all of them in all circumstances. It is reciprocal. The Lord protects everyone, but one who depends completely upon him is especially looked after by the Lord. The father is more attentive to the little son who is exclusively dependent on the father. The example is being given by Srila Prabhupada. The father has many children. The grown up ones may not require as much care and attention and protection as much as the little one. So, the little son who is exclusively dependent on the father, the father is more attentive. Similarly, the pure devotees who are especially completely dependent upon him, the Lord is more attentive.

---
### Uttara's prayer, Krishna as savior, death
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YfW_uuY71Z8?t=781
**Verse References:** SB 1.8.9, BG 8.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O Lord of Lords, Lord of the Universe, You are the greatest of mystics. Please protect me for there is no one else who can save me from the clutches of death in this world of duality."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the ninth verse, where Uttara prays to Krishna for protection from death, recognizing him as the only savior in the material world.

And Uttara is one such pure devotee. And in the ninth verse, Uttara is telling the Supreme Lord, uttaro vacha pahi pahi mahayogin deva deva jagatpate nanyam tvad abhayam pashye yatra mrtyu parasparam. The meaning is that Uttara said, O Lord of Lords, Lord of the Universe, You are the greatest of mystics. Please protect me for there is no one else who can save me from the clutches of death in this world of duality. There is no one who can save me in this world of duality from the clutches of death. This world is called Mrtyuloka, a place where death is certain for everyone who takes birth. Krishna describes in the Bhagavad Gita, jatasyahi dhruva-mrtyur dhruvam janma-mrtyasya ca. For anyone who has taken birth in the material world, death is certain. That means this body, life in this body is going to come to an end. It is not going to be there forever. The soul is eternal, but the life in this body is not eternal. So that time comes when we have to quit the body, that is called death. So in this world of death, who can save anyone except Krishna? Nobody else can save. So therefore, Uttara is telling, please protect me for there is no one else who can save me from the clutches of death. So therefore, the response of the Lord also will be described and before that Uttara's prayer is not complete. Why is she asking for protection? That will be explained in the next verse. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces Srimad Bhagavatam 1.9.1, focusing on Bhishma Deva's passing away in Krishna's presence and Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing liberation through Krishna consciousness at the time of death and Bhishma's instructions to Yudhishthira.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudhirayet. In this session we will be discussing from the first verse of the Bhagavad Gita, the first verse of the 9th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This is a new chapter we are beginning. The chapter title is the passing away of Bhishma Deva in the presence of Lord Krishna. So the first verse: Suta Vacha Iti Bheeta Praja Drohat Sarva Dharma Vivitsaya Tato Vinashanam Praagat Yatra Deva Vrata Apatat. The meaning of this verse is Suta Goswami said, being afraid for having killed so many subjects on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Maharaj Yudhishthira went to the scene of the massacre. There Bhishma Deva was lying on a bed of arrows about to pass away. A new chapter. This will describe Bhishma's instructions to Yudhishthira to pass away from Kurukshetra to Yudhishthira and also Bhishma offering wonderful prayers before quitting his body. Bhishma offering wonderful prayers to Krishna, he was fortunate that Krishna was present in front of him when Bhishma was to quit his body, that's Krishna's blessing on Bhishma Deva who was, who is a great devotee of Krishna, one of the Mahajanas of the ideal devotees of Krishna. In the purport Srila Prabhupada says in this ninth chapter as it is billed by Lord Sri Krishna, Bhishma Deva will impart instructions to King Yudhishthira on the subject of occupational duties, Sarva Dharma Vivitsaya. Bhishma Deva will impart about occupational duties, Sarva Dharma Vivitsaya. Bhishma Deva will also offer his last prayer to the Lord on the verge of passing away from this mortal world and thus become liberated from the bondage of further material engagements.

### spiritual world, material existence, death
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dMhyC2PBG5A?t=179
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mortal world is the place of death; there is no such death in the spiritual world because in the spiritual world nobody has a material body, everybody has a spiritual form."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the contrast between material and spiritual existence, highlighting the absence of death and material bodies in the spiritual realm, and the cycle of birth and death in the material world.

Those who are Krishna conscious at the time of death, they don't have to take birth again in this material world, they straight go back to Godhead, no more birth in this material world, no more material existence. So Bhishma Deva was fortunate to be able to offer his last prayer to the Lord on the verge of passing away from this mortal world. Mortal world is the place of death, there is no such death in the spiritual world because in the spiritual world nobody has a material body, everybody has spiritual form. There is no death. Of course, for the spirit soul there is no death even in this world, but the spirit soul has to keep on taking different material bodies one after another and that is the sum and substance of material existence, samsara, repetition of birth and death. But in the spiritual world there is no birth, there is no death, there is no material body, there is no material existence.

Dharma means the word dharma also means the intrinsic quality of so in all these senses particularly in this context it is occupational duty. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Narayanam Namaskritya Naramchai Vanarottamam Devim Saraswati Vyasam Tatham Satoh Jaya Mudhi Raiyate. In this session, we will be discussing from the 10th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This very Lord was sitting before Bhishma Deva. And since Bhishma Deva knew of his glories, he worshipped him duly.

### Krishna, recognition, Mahabharata
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/siMvSgevGt0?t=118
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Even if Krishna comes in front of us, are we able to recognize he who is the supreme personality of God?"
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the difficulty in recognizing Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, even when He is present, using the example of Duryodhana's failure to recognize Krishna during the peace proposal in the Mahabharata.

If you remember, in an earlier verse, it was described when the Pandavas were coming to the battlefield where Bhishma was lying on a bed of arrows. Krishna accompanied the Pandavas. So, Bhishma was very, very, very fortunate and blessed to have Krishna in front of him when he was about to quit the body. Krishna is directly present in front of Bhishma. And here it is said, even if Krishna comes in front of us, are we able to recognize he who is the supreme personality of God? Not necessarily because Krishna looks like a human being. We may think when Krishna comes in front of us here is another human being and we may not be able to recognize him. It is said in the scriptures even when Krishna incarnated five thousand years back on the earth planet and moved around in human society many many many people thought he was an ordinary person. He was another human being. They could not recognize him because he looked like a human being and we may not be able to recognize him as the Supreme Personality of Godhead. A very good example is there in the Mahabharata when Krishna went with the peace proposal on behalf of Maharaja Yudhishthira before the Battle of Kurukshetra was about to be out of about to begin to avoid that battle world war. It was not just a battle, it was a world war. Yudhishthira wanted to negotiate for peace and Duryodhana refused so Yudhishthira thought let me send Krishna. Krishna went to Duryodhana but Duryodhana thought Krishna is another king of Dwarka another politician and he thought Krishna is very diplomatic in bringing a peace proposal from Yudhishthira so Duryodhana did not recognize Krishna as a Supreme Personality of Godhead. He did not recognize Krishna as a Supreme Personality of Godhead.

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes Bhishma Deva's ability to recognize Krishna due to being a Mahajana, one of the twelve great personalities who understand religious principles and the goal of life, and highlights Bhishma's instructions to Yudhishthira in the Mahabharata.

Got it and Krishna in order to convince Duryodhana that Krishna is not an ordinary person Krishna showed one of his universal forms to Duryodhana and Duryodhana thought Krishna is using some mystic power to bewilder Duryodhana to force him to accept the peace proposal. Duryodhana said I am not going to be carried away by your display of some mystic power. Bhishma Deva is one of the Mahajanas. The Mahajanas are twelve great personalities in this universe who know the religious principles perfectly and who know the goal of the practice of all religious principles is simply to understand Krishna the Supreme Personality of Godhead to attain the lotus feet of the shelter of the lotus feet of Krishna is the goal of following all religious principles. This is known only to the Mahajanas. Bhishma Deva is one of the Mahajanas. He knows who is Krishna. He knows who are the living entities. He knows what is this world, why this creation, what is the goal of life. All these things are known to Bhishma and Bhishma's instructions to Yudhishthira forms the bulk of the wisdom given in the Mahabharata. Over three Parvas, out of 18 Parvas, Bhishma's instructions to Yudhishthira are covered. Bhishma Parva, Anushasana Parva and Shanti Parva. Three Parvas. Parvas are big volumes of which the Mahabharata is constituted or divided. So 18 Parvas are there and out of those 18, three of them, the bulk of the Mahabharata is describing Yudhishthira's, sorry Bhishma's instructions to Yudhishthira on the subject matter of Dharma. So this is very very significant. That Bhishma Deva knew the glories of the Supreme Lord Krishna. Bhishma Deva could recognize here as the Supreme Personality of Godhead. He is not an ordinary person. He is not another King. He is not just a powerful King. He is the Supreme Lord of all Lords. He is above everyone including the topmost Devatas, Brahma, Shiva, everybody. So that Bhishma Deva knew.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The atma and paramatma are two not one though in the scriptures it is explained that the atma and paramatma are one in quality both are spiritual by nature qualitative equality."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between the individual soul (atma) and the Supreme Soul (paramatma), refuting the Mayavada philosophy that claims they are one and the same, and explaining that while they are qualitatively equal, they are quantitatively different.

Is also conscious of every body in fact that is the difference between ourselves and krishna atma and paramatma there is so much of so many different philosophies describing whether the atma and paramatma are two distinct different entities or they are one most people are misled by the mayavada philosophy which says atma and paramatma are one vaishnava philosophy clearly explains the truth that the atma and paramatma are two not one though in the scriptures it is explained that the atma and paramatma are one in quality both are spiritual by nature qualitative equality of.

Krishna conscious of Billions and trillions and innumerable bodies, everybody. So this is distinctly different in terms of quantity, omnipresent, omnipotent, omniscient. He is all-knowing. He is all-powerful. He is present everywhere, every nook and corner. So that Krishna is present in front of Bhishma Deva. So don't wrongly think Krishna is an ordinary person or just another powerful king. No. Krishna is the supreme person. No personality of God. So here the word that is used by Vyasa Deva to describe this, Asinam Jagadishwaram. Jagadishwaram. The lord of the universe. The lord of the universe is sitting in front of Bhishma Deva. So and it is said here, Krishna manifests his transcendental form by his internal potency, mayaya upatvigraham. Krishna's form is completely transcendental. You know, it looks like a material form of a human being. It is not a material form. Krishna's form is completely transcendental. And Bhishma knew about this. And therefore Bhishma duly worshipped him. Bhishma did not receive him as another king. No. Bhishma knew. Here is the supreme personality of God. He duly worshipped him.

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the scene, highlighting the Pandavas' gratitude towards Bhishma for raising them after Pandu's death, and connects this to the importance of the joint family system and the guidance of elders.

On the battlefield, after the battle is over, Bhishma is ready to quit his body. The Pandavas have come there, arrived there. The Pandavas were sitting near Bhishma Deva, overtaken with affection for their dying grandfather. The Pandavas could remember how Bhishma Deva actually took charge of their protection when Pandu died early, when the Pandavas were very young. So Bhishma took charge of the upbringing of the Pandavas. So they were naturally feeling so much affection for Bhishma Deva. They were very grateful. So Bhishma Deva met the Pandavas. And as far as Bhishma Deva is concerned, he was very happy to see the Pandavas getting back their kingdom, which was rightfully theirs. Only because of injustice by the Kauravas, the Pandavas were deprived so far of their kingdom. So Bhishma Deva was overwhelmed with love and affection and there were tears in his eyes. He was very happy that the Pandavas got back their kingdom after the battle of Kurukshetra. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, when Maharaj Pandu died, his sons were all small children and naturally they were brought up under the affection of elderly members of the royal family, specifically by Bhishma Deva. This is the very culture that the joint family system that prevails. Earlier, especially in the ancient times, everyone was properly taken care of by the elderly members of the family, especially the younger ones were taken care of by the elderly members of the family. So in a joint family system, the elders are there to guide the youngsters in every respect.

Minded cunning cheating mentality asuri swabhava this is Duryodhana and Duryodhana was supported even not only by Dhritarashtra even his younger brothers they were also supporting him so whole gang of cheaters and cunning persons cunning fellows so Bhishma was feeling very very sorry for the Pandavas that he could not take their side and when the battle was fought Bhishma had to fight on the side of the Kauravas and Bhishma was very very straightforward and upright and righteous person so he would not cheat the Kauravas when fighting on their side but in spite of that because of Krishna, the Pandavas were protected in spite of the Kauravas being very very strong, they could not win the battle. ### protection, righteousness, morality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KMfx3Zs7VN8?t=733
**Verse References:** BG 16.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All these types of terrible injustices, they actually were able to avoid being killed because of the protection by the Brahmanas, God and religion."
**Summary:** The Pandavas' survival despite numerous attempts on their lives is attributed to the protection of Brahmanas, God (Krishna), and religion, emphasizing that morality is inherent in God-conscious individuals.

So, here it is said, all these types of terrible injustices, they actually were able to avoid being killed because of the protection by the Brahmanas, God and religion. Brahmanas, because Brahmanas always bless those who are righteous, those who are following religious principles. Brahmanas will never extend blessings to sinful people, to unrighteous people like Duryodhana. They can never get the blessings of Brahmanas. Similarly, God, Krishna. Krishna is always on the side of Dharma. In the end of the Bhagavad Gita, it is said, Morality is always on the side of the righteous persons who are God-conscious people. Those who are godless, they can never be morally upright. Never, never. Sometimes they make a show of being morally upright. But that is just a show. It is cheating, actually. It is to... To fool innocent people that we are very morally upright. We are truthful. We are honest. We are hardworking. But actually, if somebody is godless, even if they make a show of being honest, it is no real value. It is no real value. So, like that, Bhagavad Gita teaches us that morality is always with the god-conscious people, not with godless people. Always with the divine-natured people, never with demoniac-natured persons. Never. They cannot be morally upright. Not possible. So the Pandavas were protected, as Bhishma Deva is telling, by Brahmanas, by God, Krishna, and by religion.

Om Agyana Timiraandasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chakshurun Militam Yena Tasvain Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatyade Shatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gada Dharashiva Sadhi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Narayanam Namaskritya Naramchaiva Narottamam Devi Saraswati Vyasam Tatto Jaya Mudirayet.

---
### suffering, Kunti, Bhishma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GUv3UZawHf0?t=62
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.13
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhishma Deva is recollecting the history of the Pandavas, particularly Kunti Devi; she suffered more than the others, the Pandavas themselves."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami begins discussing verse 13 of the 9th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining Bhishma's recollection of Kunti Devi's suffering after the death of Pandu.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 13th verse of the 9th chapter of the first cant of Sriman Bhagavatam. Samsthite Atirathe Pandav Pritha Balapraja Madhohu Yushmakrte Bahum Kleshan Praptatokavati Madhohu. The meaning of this verse is this is spoken by Bhishma. He is speaking to the Pandavas. As far as my daughter-in-law Kunti is concerned, upon the great general Pandu's death, she became a widow with many children, and therefore she suffered greatly. And when you were grown up, she suffered a great deal also because of your actions. So Bhishma Deva is recollecting the history of the Pandavas, particularly Kunti Devi; she suffered more than the others, the Pandavas themselves.

---
### Kunti, family, sacrifice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GUv3UZawHf0?t=140
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Kunti took responsibility for raising not only her three sons, but even her co-wives' two sons."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Kunti's early widowhood, her responsibility in raising her children and Madri's children, and her taking shelter in Hastinapura.

Srila Prabhupada explains in the third quote, the sufferings of Kunti Devi are doubly lamented. She suffered greatly because of early widowhood and to get her minor children brought up in the royal family. Kunti Devi was one of the wives of the great general Pandu, and Madri was the second wife. When Pandu died early, the children are all very small, three sons of Kunti Devi and two sons of Madri. Totally, Pandu had five sons. Now Madri decided to actually perform the Sati rite. That means she wanted to quit her body along with the funeral of General Pandu on his death. It is called Sahagamana in Sanskrit, and she entrusted her sons to the care of Kunti Devi. Kunti took responsibility for raising not only her three sons, but even her co-wives' two sons. So all the five Pandavas came under the care of Kunti Devi, and Kunti Devi herself, she is without any protection as far as her husband is concerned. So she had to take shelter of the parents of Hastinapura, which was having Dhritarashtra as the caretaker king, and of course Bhishma was there in the palace.

---
### family, politics, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GUv3UZawHf0?t=270
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Kunti had to suffer a great deal of trouble from this envious Duryodhana and his brothers."
**Summary:** The speaker details the relationships within the royal family, highlighting Bhishma's role as Pandu's uncle and Vidura's wisdom, and the threats faced by Kunti and the Pandavas from Duryodhana.

Bhishma was like the father-in-law of Kunti Devi, Pandu's father Vichitravirya, and Vichitravirya's brother was Bhishma. So Pandu's uncle, Bhishma was Pandu's uncle. So Bhishma had taken a vow to rule the kingdom, not to sit on the throne as the emperor of the world. He had forsaken his claim, and Vidura was not entitled. Vidura was one of the brothers of Dhritarashtra and Pandu, so Vidura was not entitled because he was the son of a maidservant, so Dasiputra. So he had no claim for the throne, but he was very, very wise, very intelligent, and Dhritarashtra relied on his guidance, his advice. More than guidance, it was advice. So both Vidura as the brother-in-law of Kunti Devi, and Bhishma as the father-in-law of Kunti Devi, they were actually sympathetic to the Pandavas, and therefore Kunti was under the care of the elders in the royal palace of Hastinapura. But there were attempts by Duryodhana, supported by Dhritarashtra, to kill the Pandavas before they could even grow up, become adults. So Kunti had to suffer a great deal of trouble from this envious Duryodhana and his brothers also.

### suffering, destiny, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GUv3UZawHf0?t=398
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the material world, everybody is under the control of the superior authority; nobody is independent, nobody is free."
**Summary:** The lecture shifts to a philosophical discussion about Kunti's continued suffering, attributing it to destiny and the influence of past actions, emphasizing that everyone in the material world is under the control of a higher authority.

And when Kunti's children were grown up, she continued to suffer because of her son's actions. Whatever, she is a widow, so once her sons were grown up, they had taken charge or responsibility of ruling, but they were denied the kingdom, which was rightfully theirs. They were denied by so many tricks. So because the Pandavas had to suffer due to cheating by all of us, Kunti along with her sons had to simply suffer. So her sufferings continued. That's what Prabhupada is telling her, her sufferings continued. This means that she was destined to suffer by providence. Providence is destiny according to one's own past actions and according to whatever is destined, whatever is given out by the higher authorities. In the material world, everybody is under the control of the superior authority.

### karma, time, materialism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GUv3UZawHf0?t=486
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone is bound up by Kala, Karma, and Gunas."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the lack of independence in the material world, highlighting the influence of Kala (time), Karma (actions), and Gunas (modes of nature), contrasting this with the materialistic view of modern civilization.

Nobody is independent, nobody is free. No created being is free, is independent. We cannot make this out by the action of Kala, the influence of the gunas, and the past reactions, reactions of the past activities we have performed, that we are carrying as the load of karma. So Kala, Karma, Guna, all three are very subtle. So as far as the modern civilization is concerned, they are independent about these controls. They are like animals in the language of Bhagavatam. They are like donkeys and cows, animals. But those who are guided by the scriptures, those who are guided by their devotees, those who are guided by learned authorities in the Vedic language, they know that everyone is bound up by Kala, Karma, and Gunas.

---
### suffering, tolerance, time
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GUv3UZawHf0?t=572
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One has to tolerate such suffering which is destined without being disturbed in the mind."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Kunti Devi was destined to suffer by providence, and one must tolerate suffering without mental disturbance, a key aspect of Vedic culture.

So therefore it is said here, Kunti Devi was destined to suffer by providence. This is called providence. It is beyond our control. So here Prabhupada says one has to tolerate such suffering which is destined without being disturbed in the mind. This is the training that is required for somebody who is born and brought up in the Vedic culture. Then the fourteenth verse, sarvam kala pritam manye bhavatam chayat apriyam sapalo yad vashe loko vayoriva ghanavadi. The meaning of this fourteen verses, in my opinion, this is all due to inevitable time, under whose control everyone in every planet is carried.

### time, control, material world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GUv3UZawHf0?t=662
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Kala is a representative of the supreme lord in this material world."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the control of time (kala) over the universe and planets, explaining that kala is a representative of the Supreme Lord in the material world, where creation, maintenance, and destruction perpetually occur.

As there is control by time all over the planets, you see total control by kala. Kala is a representative of the supreme lord in this material world. The action of kala is only in the material world. It is not there in the spiritual world. The spiritual world is eternal. The material world has got a limited duration of existence. So there is creation, maintenance, destruction. Then there is some time when there is nothing happening, inactivity. Again creation, maintenance, destruction, inactivity. Again creation, maintenance, destruction, inactivity. This is perpetually going on. Perpetually it is going on. So everyone who takes birth in this material world at the beginning of creation, they are completely controlled. So it is said here, all the big gigantic planets, including the sun, are being controlled by the force of air, as the clouds are carried by the force of air. The clouds are scattered in the skies, and they don't remain in one place. They are carried by the wind. Similarly, the big gigantic planets, they are floating, and they are not stationary. They are moving in their orbit all by the force of the wind. Similarly, the inevitable kala or time controls in the action of the air and other elements. Above the vayu or wind or the air, there is the control of kala. Kala is representative of the supreme. Everything therefore is controlled by the supreme kala, a forceful representative of the lord within the material world.

---
### devotion, suffering, time
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GUv3UZawHf0?t=790
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One should not be disturbed by the tricks of eternal time."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that even pious individuals face the effects of time and material nature, but they should remain faithful to the Lord, guided by religious principles, brahmanas, and vaishnavas, and not be disturbed by the tricks of time, preparing to return to Godhead.

So now the important thing what Prabhupada explains here, you should understand carefully. Thus Yudhisthira should not be sorry for the inconceivable action of time. Everyone has to bear the actions and reactions of time as long as one is within the conditions of the material world. Actions and reactions are coming continuously. We should learn to bear them. Yudhisthira should not think that he had committed sins in his previous birth and is suffering the consequence. Generally, a person suffers due to some past deeds, but somebody who is very, very pious, very strict in following all the principles like Yudhisthira, is not suffering because of his past sinful actions. It is said here even the most pious has to suffer the conditions of material nature, but a pious man is faithful to the lord, for he is guided by the bona fide brahmana and vaishnava following the religious principles. So pious people, they are guided by the brahma, the religious principles, then brahmanas and vaishnavas, and they are also surrendered to the supreme lord. So these three guiding principles should be the aim of life. One should not be disturbed by the tricks of eternal time. So one should not worry, even though I am following the religious principles, why am I suffering. So one should not grudge being controlled by time despite being a true follower of religious principles. This is the important part. So learning to do our duties, follow the religious principles, and prepare ourselves to go back to Godhead, always noting that we don't belong to this place. We are meant to go back to Godhead. We are meant to live with Krishna as his devotee in his personal abode, where there is none of the actions of kala karma. I will stop here. Thank you very much.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.9.15 | 12-04-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### time, inevitability, divine plan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/J04-jKPKnaU?t=55
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O how wonderful is the influence of inevitability over time, it is irreversible."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces verse 1.9.15 of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Bhishma's lament about the irreversible influence of time, even in the presence of powerful figures like the Pandavas and Krishna.

In this session, we will be discussing the 15th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Sriman Bhagavatam. This chapter describes the passing away of Bhishma Deva in the presence of Lord Krishna. The meaning of this 15th verse is, "O how wonderful is the influence of inevitability over time, it is irreversible, otherwise, how can there be reverses in the presence of King Yudhishtira, the son of the demigod-controlling origin, Bhima, the great fighter with the club, the great bowman Arjuna with his mighty weapon Gandiva, and above all the Lord, the direct belligerent of the Pandavas." So this is a verse spoken by Bhishma. He is describing the influence of time, kala, it is irreversible under all circumstances. Now he is explaining, look at the situation of the Pandavas, particularly he mentions three personalities among the Pandava brothers and he also mentions Krishna.

### duty, illusory energy, back to Godhead
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/J04-jKPKnaU?t=399
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Insignificant creatures, we have to do our duties depending upon the law, and our aim should always be to go back home, back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of performing one's duties according to dharma, aiming to return to Godhead, recognizing that both happiness and distress in the material world are products of illusory energy.

Insignificant creatures, we have to do our duties depending upon the law, and our aim should always be to go back home, back to Godhead, where we actually are meant to live eternal blissful life full of knowledge in the association of the Supreme Personality of God. Because here, both happiness and distress are effects of the illusory energy. Though we prefer happiness than distress, it is actually illusory, both happiness and distress are illusory, they are effects of illusory. So the transcendentalists advise us, follow the religious principles under proper guidance of a bona fide spiritual master in the association of great Vaishnavas, and come to the platform of self-realization, where you will be able to perceive the effect of the illusory energy as either illusory happiness or illusory distress. That is called the duality of this material world. Dvandva Mohena Bhadra Dvandva Moha. So that is the nature of this material existence, nobody can change that. So therefore we are advised, you do your duties, depending on Krishna, and always have the single-pointed aim of going back to Godhead.

---
### divine plan, bewilderment, Mahajanas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/J04-jKPKnaU?t=514
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O King, no one can know the plan of the Lord, Shri Krishna, even though great philosophers inquire exhaustively, they are bewildered."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces verse 1.9.16, explaining that even great philosophers cannot understand the Lord's plan, and Bhishma negates Yudhishthira's bewilderment by highlighting his position as one of the twelve Mahajanas.

To associate with Krishna in His personal abode, where He actually lives. In the 16th verse, it is said, Narhyasya Karhi Chitrajan Pumaan Veda Vinit Siddham Yad Vijignaasaya Yukta Mukhyanti Kavayokihi. The meaning of this 16th verse is, this is Bhishma addressing Yudhishthira, "O King, no one can know the plan of the Lord, Shri Krishna, even though great philosophers inquire exhaustively, they are bewildered." So it is a fact that by analyzing things in this material world, by becoming learned in the material world, by learning in the Vedic literature, one cannot ascertain what is exactly the plan of the Lord in different times, in different places, for different people. Even though great philosophers inquire exhaustively, they are bewildered. Kavayokihi Mukhyanti, they are bewildered. It explains this in the first part. The bewilderment of Maharaj Yudhishthira over his past sinful acts and the resultant sufferings is completely negated by the great authority of Bhishma. Yudhishthira was lamenting, "I am responsible for the massacre of millions of people in this bad and poor chamber. Just to enthrone me, so many people have to die." So, he was bewildered by seeing the number of deaths. And therefore, this bewilderment is completely negated by Bhishma. Now what is Bhishma's position? One of the twelve authorized Mahajanas, those who know religion perfectly. Among the created beings, only twelve personalities are described as Mahajanas. These are described as authorities in understanding the workings of nature.

---
### divine plan, surrender, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/J04-jKPKnaU?t=668
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Rather, we should abide by the orders of the Lord, the instructions given by the Lord, and pray to the Lord for His mercy to be able to maintain the single desire to go back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that even great deities like Brahma and Shiva cannot fully understand the Lord's plan, so humans should focus on following the Lord's instructions and praying for the mercy to return to Godhead.

Bhishma wanted to impress upon Maharaj Yudhishthira that since time immemorial, no one including such deities as Shiva and Brahma could ascertain the real plan of the world. Shiva and Brahma are the topmost devatas. Topmost devatas. Of course, above them is Vishnu or Krishna. But other than Vishnu or Krishna, who is the supreme one, everyone else is unable to properly understand what is the plan of the Lord. So what can we understand about it? We, human beings on the earth, what can we understand? When great devatas like Brahma and Shiva themselves cannot understand the plan of the Lord, human beings can never, never, never understand it. It is useless also to inquire about it, about the plan of the Lord. Rather, we should abide by the orders of the Lord, the instructions given by the Lord, and pray to the Lord for His mercy to be able to maintain the single desire to go back to Godhead in His association, in Krishna's association, in Krishna's personal abode, as a pure devotee of the Lord. This is the whole program. Other than this, everything else is simply the reactions, and reactions of the Kala, Karma, that's all, interactions.

---
### surrender, divine plan, devotee suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/J04-jKPKnaU?t=776
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Simply abide by the orders of Krishna without argument."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Prabhupada, stating that even great sages cannot ascertain the Lord's plan, so we should simply follow Krishna's orders without argument, understanding that the Pandavas' suffering was part of Krishna's plan to establish virtue.

So it is not very wise to make plans trying to understand the working of Kala. You can never understand. So Prabhupada says, "Even exhaustive philosophical inquiries of great sages cannot ascertain the plan of the Lord." Great sages also do not know exactly what is the plan. So we are instructed by the Acharyas, by our spiritual master, that simply abide by the orders of Krishna without argument. There are people who argue to get into debates, but they are never able to ascertain the truth or the reality of this material world. It is wisdom given to the greatest of philosophers. So it is now being concluded that the sufferings of the Pandavas were never due to their past deeds. Pandavas are pure devotees. They are participating in Krishna's Leela in the material world. The Lord had to execute the plan of establishing the kingdom of virtue, and therefore his own devotees suffered temporarily in order to establish the conquest of virtue. So this is Krishna's plan. Even his devotees have to temporarily suffer.

### divine will, virtue, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/J04-jKPKnaU?t=880
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because Lord Krishna wanted to show that the wise cannot conquer virtue. Virtue is always the supreme."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by explaining that Bhishma's satisfaction came from seeing the triumph of virtue and Yudhishthira on the throne, even though Bhishma fought against him, because it was the Lord's will to demonstrate that virtue is supreme and cannot be conquered by even the most powerful on the wrong side.

Because after this, Vigishtha is going to rule. Everything will be very nice. After the passing of Vishnu Deva. Bhishma Deva was certainly satisfied by seeing the triumph of virtue. And he was glad to see King Yudhishthira on the throne. Although he himself fought against Yudhishthira. Bhishma is also a pure devotee. He is doing his duty of fighting on the side of Kauravas because he will never take back Kauravas. But, Bhishma was certainly very happy because Yudhishthira was ascending the throne. Even a great fighter like Bhishma could not win the battle of Kurukshetra. Why? Because Lord Krishna wanted to show that the wise cannot conquer virtue. Virtue is always the supreme. Bhishma Deva was a great devotee of the Lord. But he chose to fight against the Pandavas by the will of the Lord. It is the will of the Lord that Bhishma Deva should fight against the Pandavas. Why? Because the Lord wanted to show to the whole world that if a powerful fighter like Bhishma cannot win on the wrong side. He has taken the side of Kauravas. He can never win even though he is the most powerful fighter. Because that is the will of the Lord. Okay. I will stop here. Thank you very much.

### divine plan, acceptance, administration
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OkGiwAEzJG0?t=55
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.17
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Accepting the inconceivable plan of the Lord, you must follow it."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 17th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining Bhishma's advice to Yudhishthira to accept the Lord's plan and take care of his subjects.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 17th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Tasmalidam Daivatantram Vyavasya Bharatarsyabha Tasyanu Vihito Anatha Natham Pahi Praja Prabho. The meaning of this verse is O best amongst the descendants of Bharata, I maintain therefore that all this is within the plan of the Lord. Accepting the inconceivable plan of the Lord, you must follow it. You are now the appointed administrative head, and my Lord, you should now take care of those subjects who are now rendered helpless. So Yudhishthira is being advised by Bhishma to simply accept the inconceivable plan of the Lord. This is the Lord's plan. Yudhishthira should rule the world. So Yudhishthira should not think that it was his selfish desire because of which the battle was fought. No, this is Krishna's plan. The battle should take place to actually bring together the miscreant demons who burden the earth and to actually finish the battle. So that plan of the Lord is inconceivable. The Pandavas are instructed to follow the plan of the Lord. Now Yudhishthira is the appointed administrative head. He should take care of the subjects, especially those who are rendered helpless after the battle.

---
### teaching, devotee, tribulations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OkGiwAEzJG0?t=169
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Lord teaches the world by teaching the devotee."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a mother-in-law teaching a daughter-in-law through her own daughter to explain how the Lord teaches the world through His devotees, using Arjuna and the Pandavas as examples.

So Srila Prabhupada says in the parthas, the popular saying is that a housewife teaches the daughter-in-law by teaching the daughter. There is a Vedic culture that a young girl who gets married, she is trained up. When she goes to her husband's house, she is trained up by the mother-in-law. But the mother-in-law cannot directly teach the daughter-in-law because the daughter-in-law is totally new to the house. So she teaches by teaching her own daughter with whom she is very, very familiar. And while she is teaching the daughter, the daughter-in-law has got ample opportunity to learn also. So if the daughter is making a mistake, she can learn. She can be easily corrected. So the daughter-in-law can learn by the teaching of the mother-in-law and the mistakes made by the sister-in-law. Both is possible. So like that, the example is given. Lord teaches the world by teaching the devotee. Devotee doesn't require the teaching. But the Lord teaches the world by teaching the devotee. Through Arjuna, Bhagavad Gita was spoken. Through the Pandavas, Krishna is teaching the whole world how even pious people may have to undergo some tribulations, which are the actions and reactions of the three modes.

---
### Krishna consciousness, inner guidance, devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OkGiwAEzJG0?t=280
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The devotee does not have to learn anything new from the Lord because the Lord teaches sincere devotee always from within."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that the purpose of creation is to practice Krishna Consciousness and return to Godhead, highlighting that the Lord guides sincere devotees from within their hearts.

So this word is simply meant for practicing Krishna Consciousness while you move out of life and go back to consciousness. There is no other purpose of this creation. The devotee does not have to learn anything new from the Lord because the Lord teaches sincere devotee always from within. The Lord is seated in everyone's hand. But for the devotee, the Lord is giving constantly the directions. TESHAM SATHATAYUKTANAM BHAJATAM PRITI KURVARKAM DADAMI BUDDHI YOGAMTAM YENAMAM UPAYANTITE. Krishna directs the devotee from within the heart how to ultimately attain the abode of the Lord. And if the Lord is teaching from within the heart of the devotee, what is the need for the devotee to learn externally from the Lord? What is the need? But the Lord is doing that. He is teaching from within the heart because the Lord actually wants to instruct the whole world through His instructions given to the devotees who go to the quiet.

### compassion, suffering, devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OkGiwAEzJG0?t=371
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whenever therefore a show is made to teach the devotee, as in the case of the teachings of Bhagavad Gita, it is for teaching the less intelligent men."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that teachings given to devotees, like the Bhagavad Gita, are meant for the less intelligent, as Krishna wants to reclaim all conditioned souls and alleviate their suffering.

Whenever therefore a show is made to teach the devotee, as in the case of the teachings of Bhagavad Gita, it is for teaching the less intelligent men. Krishna wants to reclaim all the conditioned souls who are His own part and parcel, who are suffering in this material world. Krishna doesn't like that. As a father doesn't like His children to suffer. Krishna is our Supreme Father. We are all His children in the material world. So Krishna doesn't want us to suffer. Therefore Krishna comes and He teaches the less intelligent people through different means. A devotee's duty, therefore, is to ungrudgingly accept tribulations from the Lord as a benediction. There is a verse in the Bhagavad Gita tattenu kampamsu saveksha māno bhunjāna evātma kṛtaṁ vipākam pradvāvapurvir vidanam namaste jīveta yogukti padesad āyapa. Lord Krishna actually is very kind to His devotees. This verse I just quoted tattenu kampamsu saveksha māno is a verse which is a prayer offered by Brahma to Krishna after the Brahma vimohana leela. What is Brahma telling? That when a devotee suffers, a pure devotee, when a pure devotee suffers miseries, those are the mercy of the Supreme Lord only.

---
### devotional service, suffering, administration
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OkGiwAEzJG0?t=478
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Suffering is also mercy."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of suffering as mercy, explaining that a devotee prays to suffer for past sinful actions and engages in devotional service without being disturbed by difficulties.

Suffering is also mercy. bhunjāna evātma kṛtaṁ vipākam. The devotee always prays to the Lord, My dear Lord, let me suffer for whatever past sinful actions I might have performed. This is pure devotional service. Let me suffer for whatever past sinful activities I have performed. Once a devotee prays like that, then he simply engages in his duties in Krishna consciousness without being disturbed by any difficulties, any material calamities or material trouble. Without being disturbed by that, he simply engages in devotional service to Krishna. Always tries to remain devotional. The Pandavas were advised by Bhishma Deva to accept the responsibility of administration without hesitation. Because Krishna wants pure devotees like the Pandavas to rule this world for the welfare of all the citizens.

---
### protection, tribulations, Gunas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OkGiwAEzJG0?t=564
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is only possible if we understand that both happiness and distress are interactions of the Gunas."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the welfare of citizens after the battle of Kurukshetra and how devotees accept tribulations as favors from the Lord, understanding that happiness and distress are interactions of the Gunas.

The poor subjects were without protection due to the battle of Kurukshetra. They would have lost their elders, they would have lost their the others who were fighting in the battle, who were protecting them. They would have lost all those persons. So therefore, Bhishma is advising the Yudhishthira, take care of these Prajams who are left without any superior help, with the help of some other well-to-do people. So therefore, Bhishma is advising the Yudhishthira. Srila Prabhupada, a pure devotee of the Lord, accepts tribulations as favors from the Lord. How is it possible that I am getting some suffering and I should think it is a mercy of the Lord? It is only possible if we understand that both happiness and distress are interactions of the Gunas. None of them are real. None of them are real. They are interactions of the Gunas. So while Gunan interaction is happening, we foolishly interpret something according to our conditioning as nice or bringing joy, based on how it gratifies our senses. What is the basis when I say something is auspicious or in the material world something is not auspicious? It is something which is favourable for devotional service and the other thing is what is unfavourable for devotional service.

---
### devotee, difficulties, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OkGiwAEzJG0?t=690
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All pure devotees accept any difficulties they face in this world as mercy of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that pure devotees accept difficulties as the Lord's mercy, understanding that there is no mundane difference between happiness and distress.

So this should be the understanding of the devotees that all pure devotees accept any difficulties they face in this world as mercy of the Supreme Lord. Since the Lord is absolute there is no mundane difference between happiness and distress or between tribulations and favors. There is no difference. Therefore, the influence of Kala that cannot be reversed, that cannot be avoided, as much as non-devotees undergo problems, devotees also have to face some problems. But these problems that devotees face are, if they are pure devotees, it is directly related to the Lord. It is directly given by Krishna it is given by Krishna for the sake of making his devotee fixed up in Krishna consciousness. When there are dangers, when there are difficulties in Krishna consciousness, a pure devotee by facing those dangers actually becomes strong spiritually within the heart. This one has to actually remember all this.

---
### devotional service, mind, scriptures
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OkGiwAEzJG0?t=784
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotional service is the only way to prepare oneself to go to this personal abode of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that being a devotee doesn't mean the absence of problems, emphasizing that devotional service is the only way to reach Krishna's abode and that it involves training the mind to realize the purity of scriptures.

That devotee does not mean no more problems. That is not the correct understanding of Kali Bhakti. Current understanding of Bhakti is that devotional service is the only way to prepare oneself to go to this personal abode of Krishna. There is no other way. There is no other possibility or possibility. So therefore, this this correct understanding, it is not a adjustment of the mind. It is a training for the mind to realize the purity of the original scriptures and and to abide by all the injunctions of the Shastras which are relevant to us without carrying any grudge within the heart. Without carrying any grudge. This is important. So how to remain cheerful even while going through some hell and that is through all the activities of Krishna Consciousness. Any activity in Krishna Consciousness will actually help us to overcome the difficulties or dangers we may face. I will stop here. Thank you very much.
