## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.9.18 | 15-04-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna's identity, Vrishni dynasty, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FGF3fqpSZcU?t=54
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.18
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only the pure devotees can recognize Krishna."
**Summary:** Bhishma Deva reveals Krishna's identity as the original personality of Godhead, despite appearing as a member of the Vrishni dynasty, and only pure devotees can recognize this truth.

In this session, we will be discussing the 18th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse: This Shri Krishna is no other than the inconceivable original personality of Godhead. He is the first Narayana, the supreme enjoyer, but he is moving amongst the descendants of King Vrishni just like one of us, and he is bewildering us with his self-created energy. This verse is spoken by Bhishma Deva. He is addressing the Pandavas and all the assembled sages. Bhishma Deva is revealing the identity of Shri Krishna. Shri Krishna appeared as one of the members of the Vrishni dynasty. Vasudeva, the father of Krishna, belonged to the Vrishni dynasty, descendant of King Vrishni. So Krishna appeared as the son of Vasudeva. In that dynasty, he is counted among the members of the Vrishni dynasty. So even though he is moving amongst the descendants of the Vrishni dynasty as one of them, he is actually the supreme personality of Godhead. He is not another living being. So who can recognize this? Only the pure devotees can recognize Krishna. Only the pure devotees can recognize Krishna. Others cannot. Therefore, Bhishma Deva is revealing Krishna is the original personality of Godhead. Srila Prabhupada explains this in the purport very nicely.

---
### Deductive knowledge, Parampara, Authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FGF3fqpSZcU?t=194
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Vedic knowledge is received perfectly by discipline succession from authorities."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains the Vedic system of acquiring knowledge through the deductive process, descending from Krishna through Brahma, Narada, and Vyasa, emphasizing its perfection and freedom from speculation, contrasting it with the ascending process of modern science.

Srila Prabhupada says, the Vedic system of acquiring knowledge is the deductive process. What is the meaning of deductive process? It descends or comes down from Krishna to Brahma to Narada to Vyasa. Successively, the knowledge is handed down without any change, as it is. Without any change, the knowledge is handed down. This is the deductive process. As opposed to this, there is the ascending process. Ascending process is the method of currently we know something by our own efforts. As opposed to this, there is the ascending process. By our own efforts, collective efforts, individual and collective efforts, we have done some amount of research in science. We know something. But further on they keep doing more research, and they add to the knowledge base. So this is the ascending process, going from the known to the unknown, little by little, but the process is fraught with so many problems. So those problems are not there in the Vedic system of deductive process, a descending process. The perfect complete knowledge is given by Krishna, the source of all knowledge, the all-knowing Supreme Personality. Krishna, he gives that knowledge to Brahma, the first created being in the whole universe, whole creation. And Brahma receives that knowledge and he hands down that knowledge to his sons and disciples. So it is perfect knowledge, it is complete knowledge, and it is properly transmitted and received without any change, without any speculation, without any errors, without any additions or subtractions, without any of those. So that is the Vedic system. So Srila Prabhupada says further, the Vedic knowledge is received perfectly by discipline succession from authorities. Discipline succession means guru to disciple, then the disciple to his disciple, he becomes the next guru and he hands over to his disciple. That's called the discipline succession. The Dharma Narada Vyasama.

---
### Authority, Dogmatism, Vedic Truths, Modern Science
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FGF3fqpSZcU?t=363
**Verse References:** BG 4.2
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The perfect system of learning is to receive it from authority."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the acceptance of scientific claims with the rejection of Vedic truths, highlighting the importance of accepting authority in both contexts, and addressing the misconception that Vedic knowledge is dogmatic.

Such knowledge is never dogmatic as ill-consumed by lesson intelligent persons. There are people who don't accept this knowledge which is coming down in parampara. They think it is dogmatic. The mother is the authority to verify the identity within the father and the few the identity of the father. She is the authority for such confidential knowledge. A child, how can a child know who is my father? The mother has to say such and such gentleman is your father. Other than that, there is no other way. Therefore, authority is not dogmatic. In the Bhagavad-gita, this truth is confirmed in the fourth chapter, in the second verse of the fourth chapter. And the perfect system of learning is to receive it from authority. If you had a proper authorized source, then what you receive from that source is perfect, perfect knowledge. The very same system is accepted universally as truth, but only the false arguer speaks against it. Now Prabhupada is pointing out a particular problem with the present generation of people who don't accept this method of receiving from authorities in discipline succession. For example, modern space crafts fly in the sky, and when scientists say that they travel to the other side of the moon, men believe these stories blindly because they have accepted the modern scientists as authorities. Isn't that what is happening? They say we have gone to the moon and we didn't find any life there and some rocks they have got it and that's the end of the story of moon exploration. Now the people simply believe the scientists because they accept the scientists as authorities. The authorities speak and the people in general believe them. But in the case of Vedic truths, they have been taught not to believe. When it comes to the Vedas, Vedas are perfect knowledge handed down discipline succession as it is coming from the ultimate source of everything, Krishna, but people have been taught not to believe. Even if they accept them, they give a different interpretation. There are some people who accept the Vedas, but they give their own interpretation. Each and every man wants a direct perception of Vedic knowledge, but foolishly they deny it. If something cannot be directly perceived using their five senses, this is the system of science, so called science, modern science, that what is acceptable is what is perceivable by the five senses.

---
### Five senses, Mind, Degeneration, Moral values
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FGF3fqpSZcU?t=570
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The misguided man can believe one authority, the scientist, but will reject the authority of the Vedas."
**Summary:** The speaker critiques the limitations of modern science's reliance on the five senses, using the example of the mind to illustrate the existence of things beyond sensory perception, and argues that rejecting Vedic authority leads to moral degeneration.

There are things which are not perceivable by the five senses, but doesn't mean that you don't accept them. For example, we have something called the mind. Now the mind is not perceived by the five senses. You cannot see the mind, you cannot touch the mind, you cannot smell the mind, you cannot taste the mind, you cannot feel the mind, you cannot hear the mind. Now does it mean that the mind doesn't exist? We all know I have got a mind, you have got a mind, everybody has got a mind and we say mind is very disturbed, our mind is not peaceful, our mind is peaceful. We accept for sure that there is each one each and every person has got a mind. Now if it is not perceived by the five senses, does it mean it doesn't exist? That's what they say. The scientists, modern day scientists say that this is their this is their way of ascertaining what is in existence, what is reality. This means that the misguided man can believe one authority, the scientist, but will reject the authority of the Vedas. The result is that people have degenerated without Vedic guidance. With a guidance of Vedic knowledge, people have become godless, they have become very much their moral values are degrading, they are losing good values just because they are rejecting the authority of the Vedas.

---
### Authority, Acharyas, Narayana, Transcendental Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FGF3fqpSZcU?t=688
**Verse References:** BG 10.14, SB 10.14.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is the way of receiving transcendental knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of accepting the authority of Bhishma Deva, Shankaracharya, and other Acharyas in understanding Krishna as the original personality of Godhead and explains the concept of Narayana in the spiritual world, concluding that transcendental knowledge is received through authorized sources.

Now Prabhupada says here is an authority speaking about Shri Krishna as the original personality of Godhead and the first Narayana. Bhishma Deva is an authority. Even such an impersonalist as Acharya Shankara has said in the beginning of his commentation on the Bhagavad Gita that Narayana, the personality of Godhead, is beyond the material creation. Narayana Paro unavyaktat. This is the Bhagavad Gita BASSHA by Shankaracharya. The universe is one of the material creations, but Narayana is transcendental to such material paraphernalia. This is what told by Shankaracharyam. Now about Bhishma Deva, Prabhupada says Bhishma Deva is one of the twelve Mahajanas who knows the principles of transcendental knowledge. These twelve Mahajanas are mentioned in the Srimad Bhagavatam. So Bhishma Deva is one of them. His confirmation of Lord Shri Krishna's being the original personality of Godhead is also corroborated by the impersonalist Shankara. All other Acharyas have also confirmed this statement and thus there is no chance of not accepting Lord Shri Krishna as the original personality of Godhead. So Bhishma Deva is one of the twelve Mahajanas. He is declaring Krishna to be the supreme personality of Godhead. Shankara Acharya is one of the Acharyas about who is explained the Vedic texts, and he accepts Krishna as the supreme personality of Godhead. The other Acharyas, Madhavacharya, Ramanujacharya, Lord Chaitanya, so many Acharyas, they have all accepted. They are confirming. So there is no question of not accepting Krishna as the supreme personality of Godhead. And even Brahma has confirmed this in the Bhagavatam, in the 10th canto 14th chapter 14th verse. So Krishna is the first Narayana. In the spiritual world, what is the meaning of first Narayana? Prabhupada explains this. In the spiritual world, there are unlimited number of Narayanas who are all the same personality of Godhead and are considered to be plenary expansions of the original personality of Godhead Krishna. Krishna expands as Balarama. Balarama expands as Sankarsana. Sankarsana expands as Narayana. So there are many Narayana forms, each Vaikuntha planet having one form of Narayana. There are unlimited Vaikuntha planets, unlimited forms of Narayana predominating each Vaikuntha planet. So this is the explanation given in the Bhagavatam, in the Vedas, in the Upanishads. So this is very very clear, perfect knowledge, but it has to be accepted based on the authority of Bhishma Deva, based on the authority of Shankaracharya, based on the authority of the other Acharyas, based on the authority of Brahma, Narada, Vyasa. So this is the way of receiving transcendental knowledge. That is the conclusion. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.9.19 | 16-04-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna's glories, devotional service, rasa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZShSbvHtcKM?t=68
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna can be known only through pure devotional service."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Krishna's glories are known confidentially through direct contact by pure devotees engaged in transcendental loving service, who experience different rasas in their relationship with Him.

The meaning of this verse is, O King, this is Bhishmadeva addressing King Yudhishthira. O King, Lord Shiva, Narada, the sage amongst the demigods, Devarshi, and Kapila, the incarnation of Godhead, all know very confidentially about his glories through direct contact. This verse is spoken by Bhishmadeva to King Yudhishthira and all the other assembled sages that Krishna is known very confidentially as the Supreme Personality of God because they have direct access to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, which common men do not have, because he is the transcendental personality. So, in our material condition, we are not able to see him, we are not able to approach him, we are not able to perceive him, we are not able to understand him, he is an inconceivable personality, he is a very, very great personality, he is a transcendental personality, etc. So, Prabhupada explains in the purport to this verse, pure devotees of the Lord are all Buddhas, or persons who know the glories of the Lord in different transcendental loving service. Bhagavad Gita has explained that Krishna can be known only through pure devotional service. So, those who are established in pure devotional service, they are called pure devotees of the Lord, and they engage always in transcendental loving service of the Supreme Lord, and in that way, they know the glories of the Supreme Lord Krishna. Of course, the glories of the Supreme Lord are unlimited, because he is an unlimited personality. He is the unlimited personality, but they know the glories according to the service they render and the revelation by the Supreme Lord. They know the glories. As the Lord has innumerable expansions of his plenary form, there are innumerable pure devotees of the Lord, because the Lord is not limited to one form. He has many, many, many, many forms. So, accordingly, he has many, many, many, many devotees of each of the form. Many devotees. Now, these devotees are engaged in pure devotional service in different humors, different rasas. Humor means rasa. Some are situated in sakya rasa, some are situated in vatsalya rasa. Just like Arjuna is situated in sakya rasa. He is a friend of Krishna. Yashoda and Nanda Maharaj are situated in vatsalya rasa. Radharani, Lalitha, Vishakha, they are situated in madhurya rasa. So, like this, there are different devotees situated in different rasas in their relationship and devotional service to Krishna.

---
### Mahajanas, devotees, Bhishmadeva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZShSbvHtcKM?t=237
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhishmadeva himself is one of the Mahajanas, he has mentioned only three important names of the twelve who know the glories of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment identifies the twelve great devotees, the Mahajanas, and notes that Bhishmadeva, himself one of them, only mentioned three names: Shiva, Narada, and Kapila.

Ordinarily, there are twelve great devotees of the Lord. They are called the Devotees of the Lord. They are called Mahajanas. Their names are mentioned here by Prabhupada: Brahma, Narada, Shiva, Kumara, Kapila, Manu, Prahlada, Bhishma, Janaka, Shukadeva, Goswami, Bali, Maharaj and Yamaraj. Twelve names. Four sets of three names were mentioned. Twelve names. Bhishmadeva, although one of them, Bhishmadeva himself is one of the Mahajanas. Although Bhishmadeva himself is one of the Mahajanas, he has mentioned only three important names of the twelve who know the glories of the Lord. Which three names has he mentioned? In this verse, the names are mentioned of Shiva, Narada and Kapila. So he has mentioned three names out of twelve names. He himself is one of them.

---
### devotional service, bhava, Krishna's nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZShSbvHtcKM?t=338
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "As we progress in devotional service, more and more revelation about Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, happens within our heart, within our consciousness."
**Summary:** This segment explains how devotional service leads to a better understanding of Krishna's transcendental nature and form, emphasizing that He is not a created being and never accepts a material body.

Srila Vishwanath Chakraborty Thakur is another Acharya. He is a previous Acharya. In the Gaudiya Vaishnava Sampradaya, he is one of the great Acharyas of the modern age. He explains that Anubhava or the glory of the Lord is first appreciated by the devotee in ecstasy, manifesting the symptoms of perspiring, trembling, weeping, bodily eruptions, etc., which are further enhanced by steady understanding of the glories of the Lord. So, this is a little technical thing. Let me explain this. As we progress in devotional service, more and more revelation about Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, happens within our heart, within our consciousness. Just like in the beginning, we just are told, chant Hare Krishna, and you hear about Krishna from the Bhagavad Gita, from the Bhagavad, through the medium of the pure devotees or their representatives, who repeat what the pure devotees are explaining. So, this practice of Sadhana Bhakti gradually leads to a better understanding of Krishna as the transcendental personality. Transcendental personality means, he is not like one of us. He is not like an ordinary, a created being. He is not a created being. He is the original Lord. He never accepts a material body. He never accepts a material body. He never has a material body. He doesn't require a material body. He has a transcendental form. He has the transcendental supreme form. So, he comes and incarnates in that same form. So, these things we will understand gradually as we practice devotional service, by chanting, hearing, reading, doing some practical service, worshipping the Deity, etc.

---
### bhava, anubhava, ecstasy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZShSbvHtcKM?t=472
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the stage of bhava, there is ecstasy, and that ecstasy means one is able to experience extreme joy, spiritual joy, through symptoms like perspiring, trembling, weeping, bodily eruptions, etc."
**Summary:** This segment describes the progression to bhava, a stage of understanding Krishna's absolute nature, and the associated ecstasy (anubhava) manifested through physical symptoms.

All these different ways of rendering service to Krishna, if we engage in those different activities, gradually our heart becomes cleansed of misgivings, of misconceptions, of ignorance, and we become enlightened, gradually. It takes time. So, as the enlightenment increases, as the knowledge about Krishna becomes more and more revealed in the heart, we come to a stage called bhava. Bhava is the stage where we are able to understand the absolute nature of Krishna. He is non-different from his name, from his quality, from his activity, from his paraphernalia, from his code, from his form, from his deity, from his descriptions in the Bhagavatam, he is non-different. All are identical, absolute, personality of Godhead. So, that stage is called bhava. In that bhava, there are different sub-divisions, one of which is explained here as anubhava. Anubhava is appreciated by the devotee in ecstasy. Ecstasy means bhava. So, in the stage of bhava, there is ecstasy, and that ecstasy means one is able to experience extreme joy, spiritual joy, through symptoms like perspiring, trembling, weeping, bodily eruptions, etc. There are eight such symptoms called ashtasatvika vikara. There is a change in the body. The body cannot contain the spiritual transformation. There is a transcendental joy called ecstasy. So, therefore, there are changes in the body. And these are all symptoms which are manifest in this body, which confirms that the devotee has advanced to the stage of being able to experience the ecstasy.

---
### bhava, devotional service, Krishna's glories
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZShSbvHtcKM?t=589
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna has no need to do anything, but is engaged in activities and relationship with his devotees out of love for them."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the enhancement of bhava through steady understanding of Krishna's glories, using examples like Yashoda binding Krishna and Krishna driving Arjuna's chariot to illustrate the exchange of love and rasa.

Because of coming to the stage of bhava, advancing to the stage of bhava. Now, these symptoms of bhava, like perspiring, trembling, weeping, etc., are further enhanced by steady understanding of the glories of the Lord. There is an initial understanding of the glories. We begin to understand the glories of the Lord, and then after that the devotional service continues and the understanding becomes steady. It is not an effect that is experienced for a little time and then the effect is gone. No, it becomes steady, it becomes continuous. Such different understandings of bhava are exchanged between Yashoda and the Lord as a small child, the son of mother Yashoda. The Lord binding Krishna, Yashoda binding Krishna with the rope, Damodar Leela and in the chariot driving by the Lord in the exchange of love with Arjuna. These are not ordinary activities. Yashoda binding Krishna with the rope, that is a very special pastime, where there is experience of ecstasy in such exchange of rasa between Krishna and Yashoda, or Krishna driving the chariot of Arjuna, Partha Sarathi on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. This is also an exchange of love between the Lord and his pure devotee Arjuna. Krishna has no need to do anything. Bhati is engaged in activities and relationship with his devotees out of love for them. And the devotees also, they are engaged in serving Krishna in their relationship with Krishna in different ways. So, in some of these exchanges of love, the Lord assumes an equal position or a subordinate position. In the case of Arjuna, normally Arjuna and Krishna as friends are on an equal platform.

---
### Krishna's glories, devotee's difficulties, transcendental bhava
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZShSbvHtcKM?t=756
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The glories of the Lord which are exhibited in this being subordinated before his devotees is another feature of the glories of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Krishna's willingness to assume a subordinate position out of love for his devotees, exemplified by becoming Arjuna's chariot driver, and explains that even the difficulties experienced by devotees are part of the transcendental exchange of bhava.

But, when Krishna agreed to become the chariot driver of Arjuna, then Krishna assumed the subordinate position. Arjuna is ordering Krishna. In the first chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, this statement is there by Arjuna to Krishna. Kindly take my chariot between the two armies who are assembled for fighting in the battlefield of Kurukshetra. Arjuna is ordering Krishna. Now, the special feature of such an exchange is explained here by Prabhupāda. The glories of the Lord which are exhibited in this being subordinated before his devotees is another feature of the glories of the Lord. You see, when the Lord agrees to become subordinate of Krishna, he agrees to become subordinated by the devotees because he is the Supreme Lord. He is always the Lord. He is always the Master in all circumstances, but he agrees to become subordinate in the exchange of love. So, it is another feature of the glories of the Lord. It is the glory of the Supreme Lord. Even though he is unconditionally always the Supreme Lord, he agrees to become subordinate to his devotees out of love for the devotees. So, Prabhupāda says, even the Pandavas experiencing some difficulties, that is another exchange of transcendental bhava between the Lord and the devotees, the Pandavas. So, the Lord says, I put my devotee into difficulty and thus the devotee becomes more purified in exchanging transcendental bhava with me. So, don't think the Pandavas experiencing difficulties is any kind of material problems which ordinary conditioned souls experience in their daily life. No, it is not that at all. The devotees are always free from material miseries even in the material world, but apparently they sometimes become distressed, but that is due to the Lord putting them in difficulty to exchange transcendental bhava. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Kṛṣṇa.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.9.20 | 17-04-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Bhagavatam, Bhishma, Krishna

**Verse References:** SB 1.9.20
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam that will be discussed, where Bhishma Deva addresses King Yudhishthira about Krishna.

In this session, we will be discussing the 20th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, O King, it's again Bhishma Deva addressing King Yudhishthira and the other assembled sages.

---
### Krishna's Energies, Paramatma, Guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RqD7dz64DxI?t=607
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore Krishna is already the chariot driver and counsel of every living being."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how Krishna is present everywhere through his energies and as the Paramatma, guiding everyone from within their hearts, using the analogy of the sun and its rays.

The sun is identified with every inch of the sun rays. The sun is somewhere in the spiritual sky, millions of miles away from the earth, but the sunshine is coming to the earth and providing heat and light and energy and sustaining life on this planet. So that sunshine is, in one sense, not different from the sun. It is the energy of the sun, and we can identify the sun as full of this energy, which we call sunshine, sun rays. Similarly, the Lord is distributed by his different energies. The Lord is present by virtue of his being present as energy, his own energy spread everywhere. He is Paramatma, the Supersoul, present in everything that is present in the universe and everyone has a supreme guidance. Not only Krishna's energies are spread everywhere in this material world as a material energy, he has also entered into everyone's heart, every living being's heart, as the Supersoul, as the Paramatma. That is another form of Krishna who is an expansion of Krishna, who has entered into everybody's heart, and he is giving guidance to every living being from within the heart. Therefore, Krishna is already the chariot driver and counsel of every living being. Sarathi. He is not just guiding Arjuna alone, he is guiding everyone as the Paramatma in their heart.

---
### Devotional Service, Krishna's Position, Arjuna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RqD7dz64DxI?t=700
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So it is only because of the devotional service that Arjuna wants to render to Krishna, Krishna agrees to become Arjuna's chariot driver."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's role as Arjuna's chariot driver, emphasizing that it was due to Arjuna's devotional service, as Krishna is already present in Arjuna's heart as the Paramatma.

So therefore, Prabhupada is explaining how when Krishna exhibits himself as chariot driver of Arjuna, there is no change in his exalted position. He is already seated in Arjuna's heart as the Paramatma. He is guiding Arjuna. So externally, when he apparently becomes the chariot driver of Arjuna and guides Arjuna while driving his chariot, there's no change in his position. So it is only because of the devotional service that Arjuna wants to render to Krishna, Krishna agrees to become Arjuna's chariot driver. Otherwise, to give guidance to Arjuna, Krishna is already in Arjuna's heart. What is the need for Krishna to come externally, appear as the chariot driver of Arjuna? There is no need.

---
### Teaching, Absolute Nature of Krishna, Loving Reciprocation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RqD7dz64DxI?t=774
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna being absolute, there is no difference between his superior position and inferior position in his relationship with his devotees, he remains always the supreme lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna's actions, whether seemingly superior or inferior, are driven by his loving reciprocation with his devotees, and that through Arjuna, Krishna instructs the whole world.

Then Prabhupada explains he does that for the sake of teaching the others, not Arjuna. For the sake of teaching others, he speaks the Bhagavad-gita to Arjuna. Even though he is a chariot driver of Arjuna, he is the Lord, and he speaks Bhagavad-gita, and through Arjuna, he is instructing and educating the whole world. So this is the absolute nature of Krishna. For Krishna, there is nothing superior and inferior action. When he is instructing Arjuna as the spiritual master of Arjuna, he is apparently in a superior position. Arjuna is submitting, surrendering, and Arjuna is inquiring from Krishna, kindly guide me, kindly instruct me, kindly enlighten me, and Krishna is instructing, enlightening Arjuna. So superior position. When Arjuna is ordering, take my chariot between the two armies, Krishna is in an inferior position apparently. But actually, it is explained Krishna being absolute, there is no difference between his superior position and inferior position in his relationship with his devotees, he remains always the supreme lord. He may assume any, he may do any activity inferior or superior according to our calculation, but as far as Krishna is concerned, it is simply his loving reciprocation with his devotees, whether it is Mother Yashoda, whether it is Radharani, whether it is Arjuna, whether it is any devotee, even Bhishma, we will hear how Bhishma is praying to Krishna, kindly be present before me at the time of my death, quitting the body. And Krishna fulfilled Bhishma's request very graciously. So that is the loving relationship between Krishna and his devotees.

---

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naramchaiva Narottamam Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udhirayet. Bhishma has already described Krishna, that Krishna is the absolute personality of Godhead. He is present in everyone's heart. He is equally kind to everyone. He is free from the false ego of differentiation. Therefore, whatever he does, he is equi-balanced. He is free from all material inebriety. Now further, he is describing Krishna's kindness upon himself, that when he is about to quit his body, Krishna has come in front of him. Now this is a very, very...

### devotion, protection, illusory energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H5XIV92y_1M?t=148
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is the position of every one of us, that we have no other protector other than Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of unflinching devotion (Ekanta Bhakta), emphasizing that Krishna is the ultimate protector, even though we may have well-wishers in the material world whose affection is ultimately based on the illusory energy.

Because Bhishma is describing his position as his unflinching servitor. Unflinching servitor, the word used is Ekanta Bhakteshu. Ekanta Bhakta. In the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, 'The Supreme Lord, the Absolute Personality of Godhead, Sri Krishna, although equal to everyone, is still more inclined to his unflinching devotee who is completely surrendered and knows no one else as his protector and master.' This is the position of every one of us, that we have no other protector other than Krishna. Why do I say that? Because, even though in the material world we have our well-wishers, our parents, or our near and dear family members, who actually, within the heart, they have a sense of affection for us, each one of us has got somebody who is very dear to us. Even then, all this affection is on the platform of the illusory energy. This truth is not known to most people in this world. I don't mean to say that people make a show of being affectionate while they don't have any affection in the heart. I don't mean that. What I mean is, each one of us, we are in ignorance about our real self. We are completely under the clutches of the three gunas, three modes of material nature. So, we are not able to express our affection. We are not able to express our affection. We are not able to express our affection. When our consciousness gets bewildered by the influence of the external energy.

---
### protection, transcendence, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H5XIV92y_1M?t=303
**Verse References:** BG 9.29
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "At the time of death who can really come to help you? That is only Krishna; nobody else within this entire world can actually do anything to save you or help you at the time of death."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to explain that because everyone in the material world is insecure, only Krishna can offer true protection. He quotes the Bhagavad Gita to support the idea that while Krishna is equal to all, He has a special consideration for unflinching devotees, emphasizing the reciprocal relationship where devotees depend solely on Krishna's protection, especially at the time of death.

So, even though I want to give protection to somebody whom I consider as my very, very, very dear relative or friend, I do not want to give protection to somebody whom I consider as my very, very, very dear relative or friend. Still, because I myself am a person who is a person, I am not able to express my affection, not in a secure position. How can I give protection to somebody else when my own position is very, very insecure? So therefore, who is the person who can really give everyone real protection? Who is above the three gunas, who's transcendental to the modes of material nature? So that is explained by Prabhupada that Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, absolute personality of Godhead, is equal to everyone. Samoham Sarva Bhut Eshu Namedh Veshwasti na Priyaha. In the Bhagavad Gita, this is explained by Krishna, but Yeh Bhajanthi Tumam Bhaktiyah, there is still a special concept of the Supreme Personality of Godhead that Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead in the Bhagavad Gita. This is explained by Krishna, but Yeh Bhajanthi Tumam Bhaktiyah, there is still a special consideration for those who are as unflinching devotees. Yeh Bhajanthi Tumam Bhaktiyah. So therefore here it says Tathapi Ekanta Bhakteshu Ekanta Bhakta Ananyabhakti Avyabhicharini Bhakti pure devotional service unflinching servitors because Bhishma is one of the unflinching devotees or unflinching servitors of Krishna. Krishna is more inclined to such devotees. Krishna is more inclined to such devotees and even that such devotees within the core of their heart they are completely dependent on Krishna's protection. It is reciprocal. It's not that Krishna is giving protection and the devotee is thinking somebody else is my protector. No, the devotee also knows other than Krishna and no one can give me real real protection especially at the time of death. At the time of death who can really come to help you? That is only Krishna. Nobody else within this entire world can actually do anything to to save you or help you at the time of death.

---
### faith, eternal life, material existence, dependence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H5XIV92y_1M?t=475
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Having unflinching faith in the Supreme Lord as one's protector, friend and master is a natural condition of eternal life."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada on having unflinching faith in Krishna as protector, friend, and master, which is the natural condition of eternal life. He contrasts this with the temporary nature of material existence under the influence of the three gunas, where security is illusory and dependence is relative.

So Srila Prabhupada further says, 'Having unflinching faith in the Supreme Lord as one's protector, friend and master is a natural condition of eternal life.' This is exactly what I mentioned, that it is natural for all of us to seek Krishna's protection, provided we are situated on the spiritual platform, the condition of eternal life. Right now, what is our condition? We are under the clutches of the illusory energy and therefore our life in this body is temporary. We are on the material platform of the three gunas in which there is creation, maintenance, and destruction. The characteristic of Raja Guna is creation. The characteristic of Sattva Guna is maintenance. The characteristic of Dhamma Guna is destruction. So this body is created and that is when we say birth takes place and then this body is maintained when we say there is sustenance of this body, the body is maintained and then at a certain time after a limited duration of time this body will be destroyed. This body cannot be maintained anymore. We say death. Death means the destruction of the body. So it is totally different on the spiritual platform. We as each one of us is spirit soul. It is totally different. A living entity is so made by the will of the Almighty that he is most happy when placing himself in a condition of absolute dependence. See the nature of spiritual life is such that there is absolute dependence on Krishna. And material life means there is relativity. Somebody I can depend more, somebody I cannot depend as much, somebody else may be somewhat dependable, all these relativities exist but there is no one I can absolutely depend on because nobody is fully secure themselves. Their own position is always shaky. In terms of their material body anyone who is in a position of security anyone who is in a position of security anyone who is in a position of security anyone who possesses a material body because of taking birth under the illusory energy their own position is insecure. So what security they can give to others when their own position is not secure.

---
### false ego, independence, envy, Krishna's protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H5XIV92y_1M?t=659
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The root cause of all troubles is there in false egotism."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the tendency to be independent of Krishna's protection is the cause of our fall down, stemming from misidentifying ourselves as independent and envious of Krishna's position as the supreme lord. This false ego covers our original consciousness of complete dependence on Krishna.

The opposite tendency is the cause of fall down. The tendency to be independent of Krishna's protection. The living entity has to be and has this tendency of falling down by dint of misidentifying himself as fully independent to lord it over material nature or material world. We have this tendency because of the enviousness towards Krishna or because of the desire to lord over like Krishna or because of the desire to lord over like Krishna is the supreme lord. He is the absolute lord of everyone and everything. So trying to imitate Krishna, trying to compete with Krishna, trying to forget Krishna, all these tendencies cause the fall down of the living entity from Krishna's protection, from Krishna's protection, from Krishna's protection. The root cause of all troubles is there in false egotism, false egotism. As soon as one desires to be independent of Krishna or one desires to lord over others or take Krishna's position or imitate Krishna immediately there is this false ego, immediately there is this false ego. This is the root cause of all problems. This is the root cause of all problems. That covers our original consciousness of complete dependence on Krishna, of complete dependence on Krishna. To become secure and safe under the protection of Krishna, under the absolute protection of Krishna means to draw towards the supreme lord Krishna in all circumstances. And that is what Bhishma Deva is teaching all of us.

---
### devotion, relationship with Krishna, soul connection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H5XIV92y_1M?t=796
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The appearance of Lord Krishna at the deathbed of Bhishma ji is due to his being an unflinching devotee of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Krishna's appearance at Bhishma's deathbed is due to Bhishma's unflinching devotion, highlighting the eternal relationship of the soul with the Supreme Soul, which transcends any material or bodily connections.

The appearance of Lord Krishna at the deathbed of Bhishma ji is due to his being an unflinching devotee of the Lord. So when somebody is a pure devotee under all circumstances, unfailing in all circumstances, then the Supreme Lord protects such a devotee always, especially when the devotee has to quit the body at the time of death. That is a critical moment in one's life. Arjuna had some bodily relation with Krishna because the Lord happened to be his maternal cousin. But he did not have any physical relation with Krishna. But Bhishma had no such bodily relation. When Krishna helps the Pandavas, somebody may think that, oh, because Krishna is related to the Pandavas through some bodily relationship. Actually, there is no bodily relationship between Krishna and anybody. Krishna does not have a material body. So where is the question of bodily relationship? But even if for the sake of his Leela, he appears as the son of Devaki or Vasudeva. Vasudeva and Kunti are having that blood relationship. Vasudeva is the brother of Kunti. So Kunti's sons, the Pandavas, and Vasudeva's son Krishna, through such relationships between Vasudeva and Kunti, they are related as maternal cousins. But what about Bhishma? Bhishma has no such bodily relationship. So therefore, Prabhupada says, in the case of Bhishma, the cause of attraction was due to the intimate relation of the soul. So how is it that Krishna appeared in front of Bhishma? And Bhishma is acknowledging that very kindly the Supreme Lord has appeared when I am about to quit the body. It is because of the relationship of the soul. And that relation is eternal. It is never based on any bodily connections. It is a relationship between the soul with the Supreme Soul.

---
### devotion, relationship with Krishna, names of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H5XIV92y_1M?t=952
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Though the Lord has no bodily relationship, but when he appears to have some relationship through his Deela of appearing as the son of Yashoda and Nanda, and he appears to have a loving relationship with Radharani, so then when he is addressed as Radharamana, Radhakanta, Yashodanandana, Nandanandana, he is very, very, very pleased."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by explaining that while Krishna has no material body, He is pleased when addressed by names associated with His pastimes, such as the son of Yashoda and Nanda, or the lover of Radharani. He reiterates that Krishna appeared before Bhishma because of Bhishma's eternal, unflinching devotion.

Yet, it is particularly explained by Prabhupada here, because the relation of the body is very pleasing and natural, the Lord is more pleased when he is addressed as the son of Maharajananda, the son of Yashoda, the lover of Radharani. Though the Lord has no bodily relationship, but when he appears to have some relationship through his Deela of appearing as the son of Yashoda and Nanda, and he appears to have a loving relationship with Radharani, so then when he is addressed as Radharamana, Radhakanta, Yashodanandana, Nandanandana, he is very, very, very pleased. So, we have to understand this point that Krishna has appeared because Bhishma is the unflinching servitor of the Lord, which is an eternal relationship with the Lord. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### devotion, liberation, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Rgze4lgXlDM?t=54
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The personality of Godhead who appears in the mind of the devotee by attentive devotion and meditation and by chanting of the holy name releases the devotee from the body of God."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse to be discussed, focusing on how attentive devotion, meditation, and chanting the holy name can liberate a devotee from the bondage of fruitive activities.

In this session we will be discussing from the 23rd verse of the 9th chapter. The meaning of this verse is: The personality of Godhead who appears in the mind of the devotee by attentive devotion and meditation and by chanting of the holy name releases the devotee from the body of God. The body of God is the bondage of fruitive activities at the time of quitting the material body. So Bhishma is the authority, Mahajana. He is explaining how when the Lord appears in the mind of the devotee by attentive devotion, meditation, and chanting of the holy name, that is explained here. VACHA YANNAM KIRTAYAN. So by chanting of the holy name, by attentive devotion and meditation, the Lord releases the devotee from the bondage of fruitive activities exactly at the time of quitting the body.

---
### yoga, samadhi, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Rgze4lgXlDM?t=152
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though a devotee is not, may not be engaged in Ashtanga Yoga practice, in Bhakti Yoga also the same perfection of Ashtanga Yoga called Samadhi or cent per cent absorption in the Supreme Lord is possible."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's purport on yoga, emphasizing that Bhakti Yoga can lead to Samadhi, a state of complete absorption in the Supreme Lord, even without practicing Ashtanga Yoga.

So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, yoga means concentration of the mind detached from all other subject matter. Simultaneously, concentration of the mind on the lotus feet of Krishna or on the holy name of Krishna or on the form of Krishna, this transcendental form of Krishna, while simultaneously being completely detached from all other subject matter. So such concentration, Srila Prabhupada explains, is Samadhi, the highest perfection of yoga or cent per cent engagement in the service of the Lord. Even though a devotee is not, may not be engaged in Ashtanga Yoga practice, in Bhakti Yoga also the same perfection of Ashtanga Yoga called Samadhi or cent per cent absorption in the Supreme Lord is possible. By cent per cent engagement in devotional service of the Lord, either through chanting or through hearing or through worshipping or through remembering the Lord's pastimes, the Lord's form, the Lord's name. So Srila Prabhupada further explains, one who concentrates his attention in that manner is called a yogi.

---
### devotional service, ninefold process, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Rgze4lgXlDM?t=287
**Verse References:** SB 7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotional service is rendered in nine different ways this is explained in the seventh cant of the Srimad Bhagavatam"
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the nine processes of devotional service as described in the Srimad Bhagavatam, including hearing, chanting, remembering, and offering everything to the Lord.

A devotee means bhakti yogi of the Lord engages himself 24 hours daily in the service of the Lord so that his whole attention is engrossed with the thoughts of the Lord in ninefold devotional service. Devotional service is rendered in nine different ways this is explained in the seventh cant of the Srimad Bhagavatam so Srila Prabhupada has given the English names: hearing, chanting, remembering, worshiping, praying, becoming a voluntary servant, carrying out orders, establishing a friendly relationship or offering all that one may possess in the service of the Lord.

### samadhi, yoga, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Rgze4lgXlDM?t=340
**Verse References:** BG 6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord calls such a rare devotee, the best amongst all the Yogis, Yoginam api sarvesham madhgate na antaratmana sraddhavan bhajate yomam same yukta tamo matah."
**Summary:** The speaker references the Bhagavad Gita to explain that the Lord considers a devotee absorbed in devotional service to be the best among all yogis, emphasizing the importance of faith and devotion.

Atmaniveenam last one by such practice of yoga or linking up in the service of the Lord one is recognized by the Lord himself as it is explained in the Bhagavad Gita concerning the highest perfectional stage of samadhi in the Bhagavad Gita 6th chapter Krishna explains this total advantage absorption of the mind in devotional service is actually Samadhi. The Lord calls such a rare devotee, the best amongst all the Yogis, Yoginam api sarvesham madhgate na antaratmana sraddhavan bhajate yomam same yukta tamo matah. Yukta, yukta tara, yukta tama. Positives, comparative and superlative. So, the best amongst all Yogis, yukta tamo matah, Yoginam api sarvesham, among all Yogis, the best amongst all Yogis is a a devotee who is totally absorbed in devotional service of the Supreme Lord 24 hours a day. Such a perfect Yogi is enabled by the divine grace of the Lord to concentrate his mind upon the Lord with a perfect sense of consciousness.

### consciousness, sense enjoyment, dependence on Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Rgze4lgXlDM?t=436
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Generally, those who are non-devotees, their background consciousness is that of sense enjoyment, or self-gratification, the sense of independent existence without any connection with Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker delves into the concept of consciousness, contrasting the consciousness of non-devotees, which is focused on sense enjoyment and independent existence, with the consciousness of devotees, which is characterized by dependence on Krishna.

What is the meaning of sense of consciousness? Even though physically we may be doing so many activities, even though mentally we may be engaging in different kinds of thoughts, but in the background, the consciousness, which is the energy of the soul, is very important consideration. What is the background consciousness? Generally, those who are non-devotees, their background consciousness is that of sense enjoyment, or self-gratification, the sense of independent existence without any connection with Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Such a sense of consciousness, independent existence, is actually the reason for the material bondage. And the opposite of this is the sense of belonging to Krishna eternally, absolute dependence on Krishna. And this sense of consciousness is called the perfect sense of consciousness, where one is able to concentrate the attention completely on Krishna.

---

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The devotee is at once transferred by the internal energy of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that by the Lord's grace, a devotee who chants the holy name at death is transferred to the eternal planets by the Lord's internal energy, distinguishing it from the material energy.

By the divine grace of Krishna, such concentration is not possible without the mercy of the Lord. And the Lord is very, very merciful and kind to his pure devotee, unflinching servitor. Thus, by chanting his holy name before quitting the body, the yogi is at once transferred by the internal energy of the Lord to one of the eternal planets, where there is no question of material life, and its concomitant factors. See what happens at the time of death, the devotee quits the body, chanting the holy name of the Supreme Lord, because he has practiced chanting constantly, always. Satatam kirtayam domaam. Kirtaniya Satatam. Satatam kirtayam domaam. The devotee is at once transferred by the internal energy of the Lord. This has been explained by Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita. This Daivim Prakriti is referring to the internal energy of the Lord, the personal energy which is specially meant for his devotees. For the Lord and his devotees, this personal energy which is the yoga maya, antaranga shakti, spiritual energy. As opposed to this, there is the material energy or maha maya or trigunatmaka maya, gunamayi maya.

---
### material existence, eternal planets, transcendental knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Rgze4lgXlDM?t=671
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the eternal planets, there is no death, there is no old age."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the obstacles of material existence with the eternal nature of the spiritual planets, where there is no birth, death, old age, or disease.

So this gunamayi maya offers an obstacle for those who want to get out of this material existence. It offers an obstacle, duratyaya. And the yoga maya or the atma maya, personal energy of the Lord, simply transfers the devotee immediately, at once to the eternal planets. And what is the nature of eternal planets? Parastasma tu bhavonyo vyakto vyakta sanatana. Sanatana. Those planets are sanatana, where there is no question of material life and its concomitant factors. Material life is characterized by repetition of birth and death. And between birth and death, there is janma mrittyu jara vyadhi dukha dosha anudarshanam. Again and again, repeatedly, we have to face these concomitant factors of disease, old age, death, rebirth, disease, old age, death and rebirth. We have to keep on facing these concomitant factors. In the eternal planets, there is no death, there is no old age. This knowledge is not known to many people.

---
### transcendental world, spiritual master, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Rgze4lgXlDM?t=771
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If you are not guided by a pure devotee, spiritual master, you will never understand all these things merely by your reading the Gita."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of being guided by a pure devotee and spiritual master to understand the Bhagavad Gita and the possibility of returning to Krishna's personal abode through devotional service and chanting at the time of death.

That there is another world, Parastasmatubhavonyo, Anyabhava, that is transcendental, Paraha. Parambhavam, or Parambrahma, Paramdhama. So this Paramdhama, so that transcendental world, that is unknown to people. They read Bhagavad Gita, but because they are not guided by the proper authority, therefore they do not know that Krishna doesn't belong to this world. They do not know Krishna is a transcendental supreme personality. He has his personal abode. He is inviting us, please come back to my personal abode. Don't remain in this material world which is subject to creation and destruction repeatedly. Bhutva Bhutva Praliyate. Everything is explained in the Bhagavad Gita very clearly. But if you are not... If you are not guided by a pure devotee, spiritual master, you will never understand all these things merely by your reading the Gita or following some translation which is done by a speculator, non-devotee. You will never understand all these facts. So here it is explained that simply by practicing devotional service and preparing oneself to be able to chant the holy name at the time of death, one can simply transfer oneself to the eternal planets where there is no repetition of birth and death. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyananjani Shalakaya Chakshurun Meelitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnu Padaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. ### devotion, Krishna's identity, Bhishma Deva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KpcKO1bYmOE?t=52
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.24
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhishma Deva knew well that Lord Krishna is the original Narayana, Govindam Adi Purusham Tamaham Bhajwami."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 24th verse of the 9th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Bhishma Deva's realization of Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the original Narayana.

In this session, we will be discussing the 24th verse of the 9th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, Sadeva Devo Bhagavan Pratikshatam, Kalevaram Yavad Idam Hino Myaham, Prasanna Haasaaruna Lochano Lassan, Mukham Bhujo Dhyana Patash Chatur Bhujah. The meaning of this verse is, May my Lord who is four-handed and whose beautifully decorated lotus face with eyes as red as the rising sun is smiling kindly await me at that moment when I quit this material body. Now, this is a realization of Bhishma Deva, who is an unflinching servitor of the Supreme Lord Krishna. What is the identity of Krishna? Though he appears as a son of Vasudeva and Devaki, though he appears to be like one of the descendants of the Vrishni dynasty, a Kshatriya, but he is not a Kshatriya, he is not a person of this world, he is the transcendental Supreme Personality. So, Shri La Pravapada says in the purport, Bhishma Deva knew well that Lord Krishna is the original Narayana, Govindam Adi Purusham Tamaham Bhajwami, this is what is said in the Brahma Samhita, by Lord Brahma, he declares Govinda or Krishna, Ishwaraha Paramahakrishnaha, he is the original Supreme Personality of God. His worshipable deity was four-handed Narayana, but he knew that four-handed Narayana is a plenary expansion of Lord Krishna, that Bhishma Deva knew. So he did not consider Krishna as an ordinary person.

---
### auspiciousness, devotee vs. non-devotee, rebirth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KpcKO1bYmOE?t=190
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For devotees, Krishna directly takes charge."
**Summary:** The lecture explains Bhishma Deva's ability to choose the moment of his death due to a blessing, contrasting it with the fate of non-devotees who are subject to the laws of nature and rebirth, while devotees are directly taken care of by Krishna.

Indirectly, he desired Lord Shri Krishna to manifest himself in his four-handed feature of Narayana. Bhishma Deva is preparing to quit the body because he had the blessing from his father that he could quit his body whenever he desired. In what sense, whenever he desired, what does it mean? It means that he can choose the most auspicious moment to quit the body. Again, this is explained in the Bhagavad Gita. That for somebody who is not a pure devotee of Krishna, they have to select an auspicious time to quit the body in order to escape rebirth. In order to escape rebirth. For a common person, generally, because the person has some material desires, he has the right to quit the body. So he has to quit the body. Such a person has to take birth again in this material world to fulfill those desires. But somebody who is free from all material desires, even such a person, if he is not a devotee, he has to quit the body at an auspicious time. That means the passage of the soul from this material world to the spiritual world is controlled by higher authorities, in the case of non-devotees. For devotees, Krishna directly takes charge. As was explained in the previous words, at once, by Krishna's arrangement, a pure devotee is transferred by the internal energy to one of the eternal planets, as soon as the devotee quits the body. As soon as. So that's not true for non-devotees. Non-devotees, their passage is controlled by higher authorities.

---
### humility, devotion, Narayana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KpcKO1bYmOE?t=329
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although it was certain that Bhishmadeva was approaching Vainkuttadharma just after leaving his material body, still as a humble Vaishnava he desired to see the beautiful face of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Bhishma Deva's desire to see Krishna's four-handed Narayana form at the time of death, emphasizing his humility as a Vaishnava, even though his destination to Vaikuntha is assured.

So here it is explained that Krishna, in his four-handed feature, as the Narayana, is the supreme transcendental Lord Narayana in the spiritual world, Bhishma is desiring that that Narayana manifest himself before Bhishma at the time of Bhishma quitting the body. Now why is Bhishma desiring this? That Srila Prabhupada says, a Vaishnava is always humble in his behavior. This is Bhishma's huge desire. Bhishma is always humble in his behavior. This is Bhishma's huge desire. Bhishma's humility as a Vaishnava. Why is it said that Bhishma is humble? Because it is explained by Prabhupada, although it was sent person certain that Bhishmadeva was approaching Vainkuttadharma just after leaving his material body, still as a humble Vaishnava he desired to see the beautiful face of the Lord, for after quitting the present body, he might not be in a position to see the Lord anymore. See, this is the humility of the Vaishali. Bhishma's mind is completely absorbed in the thought of Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. So he is guaranteed to actually go to the Vaikuntha planet after quitting his material body, because he is absorbed in that kind of thought. But then, he is doubly ensuring that he wants, at the last moment of quitting the body, to see the beautiful face of the Supreme Lord.

---
### meditation, devotion, Bhagavad Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KpcKO1bYmOE?t=453
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A Vaishnava is not puffed up, although the Lord guarantees his pure devotee entrance into his abode."
**Summary:** The speaker further explains Bhishma's prayer, quoting the verse describing the Lord's beautiful lotus face, and emphasizes the humility of a Vaishnava, who, despite the Lord's guarantee of entrance into His abode, remains focused on devotion and service.

That's why the description is there, that Kalevaram yavad idam hinomi when I quit this body, prasanna-hasa-aruna-lochi na ullasat mukham bujaha. The lotus face of the Lord may be visible to me at the time of quitting the body, because the Lord has come personally before Bhishma Deva. So, as a humble Vaishnava, he is thinking, I may not be able to see the Lord after I quit this body. Of course, that is assuming that he may not actually go back to the spiritual world. In that case, he still, till the last moment, he wants to be having darshan of the Lord, the last darshan he wants to have. A Vaishnava is not puffed up, although the Lord guarantees his pure devotee entrance into his abode. Krishna says, for his pure devotees, in the 8th chapter, Krishna explains this in the Bhagavad Gita, for my devotees, they can quit their body any time, because they are always absorbed in thinking of me, man-mana. In the 8th chapter, Krishna says, antakale chamam eva smaran muktvaka nevaram tasmat sarveshu kaleshu mam anusmara yudhyachu. So, all these verses explain that a devotee's position is, he is always thinking of Krishna, always chanting his name, always engaged in glorifying him, always engaged eternally in his service.

---
### devotional service, liberation, laws of nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KpcKO1bYmOE?t=562
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For ordinary people, who are not yogis having that perfection of being able to quit the body at the chosen auspicious moment, for those who are not such yogis, they are forced to leave the body at a certain time by the laws of nature, higher laws."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the constant engagement of a devotee in Krishna's service, guaranteeing their transfer to the Lord's personal abode, contrasting this with the forced departure of ordinary people from their bodies according to the laws of nature.

Like this, a devotee, such a devotee, who is constantly engaged, tesham satata yuktanam bhajatam priti purvakam. So, such a devotee, Krishna guarantees that such a devotee will straight be transferred to the eternal world of the personal abode of the Supreme Lord. So, Bhishma says, as long as I do not quit this body, that's what Bhishma Deva is telling, yavad idam hinomi aham kalevaram as long as I do not quit this body, this means, Prabhupada says, the great general Bhishma Deva would quit the body by his own will. He was not being forced by the laws of nature. For ordinary people, who are not yogis having that perfection of being able to quit the body at the chosen auspicious moment, for those who are not such yogis, they are forced to leave the body at a certain time by the laws of nature, higher laws. And all such people generally are attached to their body because they don't want to quit the body and they are forced and if they refuse to quit the body, the Yamadutas are there to force them to leave the body. They cannot remain against the laws.

---
### benediction, concentration, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KpcKO1bYmOE?t=672
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhishma very humbly, he is praying to the Lord. He desired that the Lord stay before him in his four-handed Narayana feature so that he might concentrate his attention upon this Narayana feature and thus be entranced in that meditation."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Bhishma's benediction from his father, granting him the power to choose his time of death, and his humble prayer to the Lord to appear before him in His Narayana form, enabling him to concentrate and purify his mind.

Whereas Bhishma, because he is blessed by his father, Maharaj Shantanu blessed Bhishma, who was his son, Shantanu's son Bhishma, was blessed by Shantanu, that you be blessed to quit your body at the auspicious time of your choice. So, Bhishma was very, very powerful. He could stay in his body as long as he desired. He got this benediction from his father. So therefore, Bhishma very humbly, he is praying to the Lord. He desired that the Lord stay before him in his four-handed Narayana feature so that he might concentrate his attention upon this Narayana feature and thus be entranced in that meditation. Already, Bhishma, being an unflinching servitor, is always engaged in devotional service of the Supreme Lord. But additionally, he wants that now that the Supreme Lord has appeared in front of me, let me concentrate my attention on his lotus face, beautiful lotus face, and be situated entranced in that way, by concentrating my attention on his lotus face. He is thinking that just in case my mind is not pure at the time of quitting this body, by such concentration of my attention on the lotus face of the Supreme Lord, Krishna, my mind will be sanctified by thinking of the Lord.

---
### devotee's attitude, dependence on Krishna, service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KpcKO1bYmOE?t=792
**Verse References:** BG 8.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pure devotee is never anxious to go back to the kingdom of God."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by describing the attitude of a pure devotee, who is not anxious about going back to the spiritual world but is completely dependent on the Lord's will and ready to serve Him anywhere, even in hellish planets, to fulfill His mission.

Then, in such a state, if he quits his body, he did not mind wherever he might go. A pure devotee actually is not very anxious that he has to quit this world and go to the spiritual world, the personal abode of the Lord. The devotee is not very anxious about that. Why? Srila Prabhupada says here, a pure devotee is never anxious to go back to the kingdom of God. Though, that is the destination of the pure devotee, and it is guaranteed by the Supreme Lord. In the Bhagavad Gita, punar janma naiti mam eti sorjuna. Krishna guarantees this for his devotees, pure devotees. That mam eti, they come back to me, my abode, madbhava magata. So, a pure devotee is completely dependent on the good will of the Lord. A devotee is never proud to think that I am a pure devotee. I am entitled to enter the personal abode of the Supreme Lord after quitting this body. He never thinks like that. So, a pure devotee always is dependent on the mercy of the Supreme Lord. And, even if the Lord wants him to go to hell, a devotee is ready. In the sense that, a devotee is ready to serve the Lord anywhere, even in the material world, even in hellish planets. Because the devotee's only business is to serve the mission of the Lord in this material world, especially the mission of the Lord to actually deliver the condition, and deliver the condition source. So, that, in that way, a pure devotee doesn't particularly desire to go to the spiritual planet. Anywhere the Lord wants him to serve, the devotee is ready. So, I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
