**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By pure meditation looking at Lord Sri Krishna, He at once was freed from all material inauspiciousness and was relieved of all bodily pains caused by the arrow wounds."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 31st verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Bhishma's pure meditation on Krishna and his liberation from material inauspiciousness and bodily pain.

Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. Devim Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 31st verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Vishuddhaya Dharanayahata Ashubhaha. Tad Eekshayai Vashu Gata Yudhashramaha. Nivritta Sarvendriya Vritti Vibhramaha. Tustava Janyam Visrujanjana Yudhasharanam. The meaning of this verse is: By pure meditation looking at Lord Sri Krishna, He at once was freed from all material inauspiciousness and was relieved of all bodily pains caused by the arrow wounds. Thus all the external activities of his senses at once stopped and he prayed transcendentally to the controller of all living beings while quitting his material body.

---
### yoga, bhakti, dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/L2bHf61IKDA?t=113
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhishma is simultaneously an expert ashtanga yogi and also perfect bhakti yogi; he is simultaneously both."
**Summary:** Bhishma is presented as an example of both an expert ashtanga yogi and a perfect bhakti yogi, teaching how to prepare for quitting the body and attain the highest perfection of dharma, which is to return to God.

So Bhishma is simultaneously an expert ashtanga yogi and also perfect bhakti yogi; he is simultaneously both. He is teaching by his example how an astanga yogi should prepare himself for quitting the body. He is also teaching the devotees how a devotee should prepare himself to quit his body. Bhishma is teaching both. He is, of course, the mahajana, the expert, the topmost authority among the topmost authorities, 12 authorities in practicing dharma and aiming to attain the highest perfection of practice of dharma, which is to go back to God at the end of this life in the human form of body.

---
### meditation, material body, inauspiciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/L2bHf61IKDA?t=174
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This material body is full of inauspiciousness in so many ways; what is this body made up of? Flesh and blood and bone and stool and mucus and urine and all kinds of obnoxious things."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of pure meditation and the inauspicious nature of the material body, emphasizing its composition of impure substances.

So here it is described: by pure meditation, this is the practice of the ashtanga yoga, pure meditation upon whom? Upon the supreme lord in the heart. Now Krishna says, 'I am seated in everybody's heart.' So it is Krishna only who's seated in everyone's heart. Therefore, Bhishma Deva, by pure meditation, looking at Lord Shri Krishna, of course, for Bhishma there is no need to focus his attention on the inside of the heart, no need, because Krishna was right in front of him. The same Krishna was seated on the heart was right in front of Bhishma Deva. So Bhishma Deva simply had to fix his mind upon the form of Lord Krishna standing in front of him, and by doing so, Bhishma was at once freed from all material inauspiciousness. This material body is full of inauspiciousness in so many ways; what is this body made up of? Flesh and blood and bone and stool and mucus and urine and all kinds of obnoxious things. So this body, which we adore so much just because of some facility for sense enjoyment, we give a lot of value to the body, and we are forgetting that this body, after all, is something material and not made up of very nice substances. It is now simply packaged with a covering called the skin. It's in another place in the Bhagavatam described, it's a leather bag, a skin bag made up of three elements called vata, pitta, and kapha. Vata is air, and pitta is bile secretion, and kapha is mucus, obnoxious things, mucus and stool and urine and bone and flesh and blood, all horrible things, and we adore this body so much in ignorance. This ignorance, actually, you should understand from the scriptures that this body is not at all auspicious. You should understand from the scriptures that this body is not at all auspicious.

---
### Krishna, illusion, eternal relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/L2bHf61IKDA?t=335
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Identification with the material body is due to forgetfulness of our eternal relationship with the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that by fixing attention on Krishna, one can be freed from material inauspiciousness and become situated in Krishna consciousness. He also discusses the illusory nature of the material body and the importance of recognizing our eternal relationship with Krishna.

It is full of inauspiciousness. So Bhishma Deva, by fixing his attention on Krishna, the transcendental form of Krishna in front of him, completely being absorbed in meditation on Krishna, he was freed at once from all material inauspiciousness. His conscience became completely spiritualized, Krishna consciousness. He became situated and complete Krishna consciousness and was relieved of all bodily pains caused by arrow wounds. All bodily pains completely gone because the consciousness is differently absorbed. Thus all of the external activities of his senses at one stop. So proper explains is what is this external activities of the senses. So proper says in the purport the material body is completely destroyed and the consciousness is completely destroyed. So this material energy is actually illusory energy, and this body is due to the illusory energy effect upon the soul, the spirit soul. So each one of us, we are not the body, we are spirit soul. We are completely spiritual by nature, but when there is the influence of the illusory energy, we become bewildered, we forget ourself, and we identify with the material body, which is the covering for our real self. Further, Sri Lakshmi says identification with the material body is due to forgetfulness of our eternal relationship with the Lord. All of us, each one of us, we have a eternal relationship with Krishna because we belong to Krishna. We have a relationship with Krishna in the spiritual world. We have a relationship with Krishna in the spiritual world, but we forget that when we are situated in the material body by the influence of the illusory energy, we forget Krishna, we forget our relationship with Krishna, and we identify with this body as myself. If you ask me, are you, I describe myself in terms of my body and mind, body and mind, which is not the real self, which is not me, which is my covering. 

---
### illusion, devotee, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/L2bHf61IKDA?t=485
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For a pure devotee of the Lord like Bhishma Deva, this illusion was at once removed as soon as the Lord arrived."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to explain the illusion of identifying with the material body and how a pure devotee like Bhishma Deva can have this illusion removed by the presence of Krishna.

There is a subtle covering called the mind and a gross covering called the material body, the external gross body. For a pure devotee of the Lord like Bhishma Deva, this illusion was at once removed as soon as the Lord arrived, as soon as Krishna came in front of Bhishma Deva. By just fixing his attention on Krishna, who is the most attractive personality, most attractive beautiful form in the whole creation, that supremely attractive Krishna came in front of Bhishma Deva. Bhishma Deva at once fixed his attention on Krishna and all his illusion was gone. Of course, as pure devotee was never an illusion, but even if there was any material pain or material inauspiciousness that is all completely.

---
### Krishna, illusion, Chaitanya Charitamrita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/L2bHf61IKDA?t=563
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Krishna is like the Sun, and the illusory external material energy is like darkness."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of the sun and darkness from Chaitanya Charitamrita to illustrate how the presence of Krishna removes material contamination and allows one to be transcendentally situated.

That is that disappeared when Krishna arrived in front of Bhishma Deva. So how does it work? Prabhupada explains by giving example from the Chaitanya Charita Amrita. Lord Krishna is like the Sun, Krishna's Surya Sama, and the illusory external material energy is like darkness, Maya Hoy Andhakar. Material energy, illusory energy is like darkness. Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, is like the Sun. In the presence of the Sun, there is no possibility that darkness can stand, just like in the morning when the Sun rises, there is no darkness that remains which was there at the night when the Sun was not in the sky. Similarly, just on the arrival of Lord Krishna, all material contamination is completely removed, and Bhismadeva was thus able to be transcendentally situated by stopping the activities of the impure senses in collaboration with matter.

---
### consciousness, senses, material body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/L2bHf61IKDA?t=626
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we are not Krishna conscious, then our consciousness is limited to our body and the mind."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how our consciousness is limited to the body and mind when we are not Krishna conscious, leading to impure activities through the material senses.

This requires little explanation. Now when we are not Krishna conscious, then our consciousness is limited to our body and the mind. In such a state of consciousness, we are acting, we are performing activities with our senses, with the body, in collaboration with matter. That means the original senses, which are our spiritual senses, they are the ones we are utilizing for performing material activities through the covering called the body, the material senses like the hands, the legs, the eyes, the ears. We are seeing through the eyes, we are hearing through the ears, we are utilizing our hands for different activities or the legs for walking or for any any other purpose, material purposes. So all this is impure because it is material, it's impure. The soul is originally pure, and also the senses, original senses are pure senses, spiritual senses. By material contamination, the senses assume the role of imperfection and impurity. So when we are covered by the material body, we identify with the body, and we wrongly think that my this material hand is my real hand or the material eyes, this whole arrangement called the material eye, I think that is my eyes. I am completely forgetful of my spiritual eye, which is different from this material life, my spiritual hand different from my material hand. All my spiritual senses are different from my material senses. The material senses are covering the spiritual senses.

---
### devotional service, spiritual senses, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/L2bHf61IKDA?t=779
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual life means awakening our spiritual senses by contact with Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how devotional service and yoga practice can revive our contact with Krishna, awaken our spiritual senses, and allow us to act spiritually.

So when we revive our contact with the supreme lord Krishna through devotional service or through yoga practice, the senses again become freed from material contamination. Then our original senses, spiritual senses are awakened, and we act with our spiritual senses. We act spiritually through with spiritual senses. We act, perform spiritual activities, the same activities, and through the material senses, we perform material activities, the same activities. So spiritual life means awakening our spiritual senses by contact with Krishna.

---
### chanting, Hare Krishna, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/L2bHf61IKDA?t=879
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This contact with Krishna is very easy through chanting and hearing the holy name of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the ease of contacting Krishna through chanting and hearing the holy name, explaining how it frees one from material misconception and allows for spiritual activities in spiritual consciousness.

This contact with Krishna is very easy through chanting and hearing the holy name of Krishna, chanting and hearing the holy name of Krishna Hare Krishna. When I say Hare Krishna, Krishna appears in the form of transcendental sound on my tongue, and Krishna appears in the form of sound, so I hear that sound and thereby I contact the transcendental personality Krishna in the form of this transcendental sound. So then immediately I am freed from this material a misconception, material covering. I'm free in the level of the consciousness, then I can perform spiritual activities in spiritual consciousness. So this is the whole process of bhakti yoga. This has to be practiced. Just like I say Hare Krishna, I am freed from the material misconception, but again after saying Hare Krishna, when I stop saying Hare Krishna, again I go back to my original consciousness, material consciousness. So by practice, we learn to come out of this material consciousness, and when we are completely out of the material consciousness by always being in Krishna consciousness, then no more this material covering will be effective, will be situated in our spiritual position in spiritual consciousness. We'll discuss more in the next verse. Thank you very much Hare Krishna.

Om Agyana Timiraandhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chakshurunmilitam Yena Tasmait Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swaminthinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatyade Shatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shiva Sali Gauravakta Vrinda. In this session, we will be discussing from the 33rd verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam.

---
### Krishna's form, devotion, Bhishma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Te07vgmzxvM?t=66
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.33
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhishma is very very fortunate that Krishna has actually blessed him by his presence, actual presence."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse as Bhishma's prayer to Krishna while quitting his body, describing Krishna's transcendental form and Bhishma's fortune to have Krishna's presence.

This verse is the second prayer offered by Bhishma to Krishna while he is quitting his body. TRIBHUVANA KAMANAM TAMALA VARNAM RAVIKARA GAURA VARAMBARAM DADHANE VAPURALAKA KULAVRTANAN AABJAM VIJAYA SAKHE RATI RASTUMENA VADHYA. The meaning of this verse is, Shri Krishna is the intimate friend of Arjuna. He has appeared on this earth in his transcendental body, which resembles the bluish color of the tamala tree. His body attracts everyone in the three planetary systems, upper, middle and lower. May his glittering yellow dress and his lotus face, covered with the paintings of sandalwood pulp, be the object of my attraction, and may I not desire fructive results. So here is a description of the beautiful transcendental form of Shri Krishna, who is standing before Bhishma while Bhishma is quitting his body. Bhishma is very very fortunate that Krishna has actually blessed him by his presence, actual presence.

---
### Krishna's energies, internal potency, conditioned souls
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Te07vgmzxvM?t=154
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna has got three kinds of energies... Internal potency is a spiritual energy and it is meant for Krishna's own purposes exclusively."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's purport, focusing on Krishna's three energies: internal, external, and marginal, and how Krishna appears on Earth through his internal potency.

In the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, When Shri Krishna by his own internal pleasure appears on earth, he does so by the agency of his internal potency. What is the meaning of this statement? Krishna has got three kinds of energies. Krishna is the supreme person, he has got energies which are serving him, which are working for him. So everything he does through the agency of his energy. He personally has nothing to do because his energies carry out whatever he wants to do according to his will. Simply he has to decide or will to do something and it is carried out perfectly. These three categories of energies are his internal potency, his external potency and his marginal potency. Internal potency is a spiritual energy and it is meant for Krishna's own purposes exclusively. The external potency is the material energy meant for the conditioned souls. And the marginal potency consists of the living beings, innumerable, countless living beings, some of whom are in the material world, the conditioned souls. Majority of the living beings belonging to the marginal potency are in the spiritual world as pure devotees of Krishna.

---
### illusory energy, material energy, pure devotees
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Te07vgmzxvM?t=285
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna never comes in contact with the illusory energy or the material energy."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining Krishna's internal potency and contrasts it with the external, material energy that influences conditioned souls, creating illusion and the desire for independence from Krishna. He emphasizes that Krishna and pure devotees are not affected by this illusory energy.

So, when it is said here by Srila Prabhupada that Krishna appears on this earth, by the agency of his internal potency, that is for his own purpose the internal potency is employed. Internal potency is completely spiritual as opposed to the external potency or the material energy which has an influence on all of us conditioned souls. It influences us by its illusory nature. The illusion that is created for enabling we conditioned souls to try to enjoy independent of Krishna, to try to think that I am independent, to try to think that I can be the lord of whoever I want to lord over. This is the necessity of the illusory energy acting on the conditioned souls. So, Krishna never comes in contact with the illusory energy or the material energy. Even when he comes to this material world, this material world is working by the action of the illusory energy or the material energy. But such illusory energy cannot act on Krishna, cannot or does not act on the pure devotees like Prabhupada, like Arjuna. The illusory energy does not act on them, the Pandavas. They are all pure devotees. And Krishna is the master, the controller of all energies including the material energy.

---
### Vishnu, Brahma, Shiva, transcendental form
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Te07vgmzxvM?t=413
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nowhere in the universe are there such beautiful bodily features as those of Lord Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how Krishna directs the material energy through Vishnu, Brahma, and Shiva, and emphasizes that Krishna appears in his transcendental form, which is unlike the material bodies of conditioned souls and is the most beautiful form in the universe.

But he directs the material energy through his directors, through his expansion as Vishnu and empowered living being as Brahma and through his incarnation as Lord Shiva. Brahma acts as the director of Rajoguna. Vishnu directs Satvaguna and Shiva directs the Tamoguna. Now, they three, Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva are acting as the directors exactly according to the will of Krishna. There are several verses in the scriptures to substantiate this explanation. Now, when Krishna comes to this material world, it is said here, he comes by his internal potency. So, exactly according to his will, his internal potency also will act for his own purposes. And then, he appears in his transcendental form. Unlike us, even though we have a spiritual form, our spiritual form is covered by a material body or a material form. Krishna never is covered by any material body or material form. He comes in his transcendental form. That form looks like a human form, but it is his original form. Now, the attractive features of his transcendental body are desired in all the three worlds, namely the upper, middle and lower planetary systems. It's so beautiful, Krishna's form is so beautiful that everyone who can get an opportunity to see that beautiful form, they are attracted by all the attractive features. There are many, many attractive features of Krishna's beautiful form, transcendental form.

---
### Krishna's beauty, Arjuna, Vijaya Sakhe, devotee relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Te07vgmzxvM?t=575
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is always pleased when he is addressed conjointly with his devotees who are related with him in different transcendental humors."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights the unparalleled beauty of Krishna's form and explains why Bhishma addresses Krishna as 'Vijaya Sakhe' (friend of Arjuna), emphasizing Krishna's pleasure in being associated with his devotees in various transcendental relationships (Rasas).

Now, another important point Prabhupada says in this purport is that nowhere in the universe are there such beautiful bodily features as those of Lord Krishna. Now, if there is some particular feature that is very attractive for everyone, nobody else in the entire universe possesses such beautiful features. It's not possible. Therefore, the conclusion is that Krishna's transcendental body has nothing to do with any material creation, with any materially created form. It is completely different. Arjuna is described here as the conqueror and Krishna is described here as his intimate friend. Now, Bhishma Deva is remembering the particular dress of Lord Krishna which Krishna put on as the driver of Arjuna's chariot. Krishna was acting as the Sarathi or the driver of Arjuna's chariot. He was having a particular dress. That dress Bhishma is remembering. Now, while fighting was going on between Arjuna and Bhishma, Bhishma's attraction was drawn by the glittering dress of Krishna. And indirectly, Bhishma is admiring his so-called enemy Arjuna for possessing Lord Krishna as his own friend. Arjuna was always a conqueror because the Lord was his friend. And Bhishma Deva takes this opportunity to address Lord Krishna as Vijaya Sakhe meaning friend of Arjuna. Why is he addressing Krishna as friend of Arjuna? Because Krishna is always pleased when he is addressed conjointly with his devotees who are related with him in different transcendental humors. Humors means Rasa.

---
### rasas, devotional relationships, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Te07vgmzxvM?t=719
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotees are simply by remembering Krishna's beautiful form... they are able to completely situate themselves on the transcendental platform and they are never touched by the material energy."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the five spiritual Rasas (Shanta, Dasya, Sakhya, Vatsalya, Madhurya) and how devotees relish their specific relationship with Krishna. He explains how remembering Krishna's form, name, or activities can elevate devotees to the transcendental platform, untouched by material energy.

There are five spiritual Rasas which the devotees relish in their relationship with Krishna. Shanta Rasa, Dasi Rasa, Sakya Rasa, Vatsali Rasa, Madhuri Rasa. Depending on the type of relationship, they relish different Rasas. Arjuna is situated in Sakya Rasa. Therefore, Vijaya Sakhe Vijaya Sakha Sakha is addressing form of address. So Vijaya is another name of Arjuna. So Arjuna's friend that is Krishna. While Krishna was the charioteer of Arjuna sun rays glittered on the dress of the Lord and the beautiful hue created by the reflection of such rays was never forgotten by Bhishma Deva. Devotees are simply by remembering Krishna's beautiful form, Krishna's beautiful smile, Krishna's beautiful address or Krishna's beautiful features or Krishna's name, Krishna's form, Krishna's activity, Krishna's quality, anything they are able to completely situate themselves on the transcendental platform and they are never touched by the material energy. So Bhishma Deva remembering Krishna's beautiful form as the chariot driver of Arjuna he is able to immediately situate himself on the platform of Krishna consciousness. Now, Bhishma is himself a great fighter and as a great fighter he was relishing the relation of Krishna in the chivalrous humor. It's called virya rasa. There are five primary rasas and seven secondary rasas. One of the seven secondary rasas devotees have with Krishna is called virya rasa, chivalrous humor. Bhishma is situated in a relationship with Krishna as his devotee in the virya rasa or chivalrous humor. So, transcendental relation with the lord in any one of the different rasas or humors is relished by the respective devotees in the highest ecstasy.

---
### personal relationship, rasa, ecstasy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Te07vgmzxvM?t=881
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A particular devotee's own relationship with Krishna is relished by the devotee to the highest degree."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing that each devotee relishes their unique relationship (Rasa) with Krishna to the highest degree, whether it's Bhishma in virya rasa, Radharani in madhurya rasa, or Arjuna in sakhya rasa.

This is very important to understand that a particular devotee's own relationship with Krishna is relished by the devotee to the highest degree. If Bhishma is situated in virya rasa relationship with Krishna, Bhishma relishes virya rasa to the highest degree. Radharani is situated in madhuriya rasa relationship with Krishna, she relishes that to the highest degree. Arjuna is situated in friendly relationship with Krishna, he relishes that friendly relationship to the highest degree. So, any of the rasas are relished to the highest degree by the respective devotees. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Shreemad Bhagavatam, Bhishma, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KJ6AOliQ5J4?t=46
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "On the battlefield where Shri Krishna attended Arjuna out of friendship, the flowing hair of Lord Krishna turned ashen due to the dust raised by the hooves of the horses."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 34th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Shreemad Bhagavatam, describing Bhishma Deva's contemplation of Krishna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naramchaiva Narottamam. Devim Saraswateem Vyasam Tatham Jaya Mudhi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the 34th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Shreemad Bhagavatam. Yudhi Turaga Rajo Vidhumra Vishvak Kachalulita Shramavarya Lankratasye Mama Nishita Sharair Vibhidhyamana Tvachi Vilasat Kavachestu Krishna Aatma. The meaning of this verse is: On the battlefield where Shri Krishna attended Arjuna out of friendship, the flowing hair of Lord Krishna turned ashen due to the dust raised by the hooves of the horses. And because of his labour, beads of sweat wetted his face. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. All these decorations intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. ### Virya Rasa, Transcendental Body, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KJ6AOliQ5J4?t=130
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, Bhishma is attacking Arjuna as the enemy but Krishna is trying to protect Arjuna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Bhishma's transcendental relationship (Virya Rasa) with Krishna, where Krishna enjoys fighting with Bhishma while protecting Arjuna. He emphasizes the spiritual nature of Krishna's body, which cannot be wounded, and the importance of hearing such descriptions from pure devotees.

Bhishma Deva, he is fixing his mind on Shri Krishna by remembering the different transcendental forms of the Lord on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. Bhishma, as has been explained earlier, is situated in a transcendental relationship called Virya Rasa with Lord Shri Krishna, so Krishna enjoys that fighting with Bhishma and in the battlefield of Kurukshetra Krishna is not fighting, Arjuna is fighting. So, Bhishma is attacking Arjuna as the enemy but Krishna is trying to protect Arjuna. So, Bhishma is releasing arrows to kill Arjuna but Krishna is intercepting those arrows and those arrows are striking Krishna's transcendental body. Now, the transcendental body, as will be explained by Srila Prabhupada, cannot be wounded, cannot be destroyed, cannot be cut. It is completely spiritual. So, that is the difficult part to understand for a common man. Therefore, we have to hear such descriptions from the lips of a pure devotee who will explain perfectly how to understand these dealings between the lord and his devotees.

---
### Eternity, Bliss, Knowledge, Rasa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KJ6AOliQ5J4?t=242
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone has a specific relationship with Krishna, every living being."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's absolute form of eternity, bliss, and knowledge (Sat, Chit, Ananda) and the five primary relationships (Shanta, Dasya, Sakya, Vatsalya, and Madhurya) one can have with the Lord. He emphasizes that everyone has a specific relationship with Krishna and the perfection of life is to reinstate oneself in that loving relationship.

So, Srila Prabhupada explains elaborately in this purport. The lord is the absolute form of eternity, bliss and knowledge. Eternity, his body, his form, his existence is eternal. Sat, chit, knowledge, full of knowledge, ananda, full of bliss. As such, transcendental loving service to the lord in one of the five principles. Namely, shanta, dasya, sakya, vatsalya and madhurya. That is neutrality, servitorship, fraternity, filial affection and conjugal love is graciously accepted by the lord when offered to the lord in genuine love and affection. Everyone has a specific relationship with Krishna, every living being. So, the perfection of our life is to reinstate ourselves in that relationship with Krishna loving relationship with Krishna in one of the five primary relationships. So Sri Bhishma Deva is a great devotee of the lord in the relation of servitorship. There are secondary rasas and primary rasas primary rasas are based on the primary relationship and while exchanging this rasa with the supreme lord there are secondary rasas also in the course of exchange of the primary rasa that is what gives rise to variety of the rasas exchanged in the activities of that relationship.

### Dasya, Worship, Transcendental
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KJ6AOliQ5J4?t=362
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is receiving it exactly like another devotee offers soft roses while worshipping the lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Bhishma's act of shooting arrows at Krishna is akin to worship, as Krishna receives it with the same affection as if Bhishma were offering soft roses. He also notes that Bhishma may appear to be repenting his actions, but Krishna's body is not pained due to its transcendental nature.

So Bhishma is situated primarily in the relationship of dasya as a servant as a servitor thus his throwing of sharp arrows at the transcendental body of the lord is as good as the worship of another devotee who throws soft roses upon him. So how is Krishna receiving these arrows shot by Bhishma? Krishna is receiving it exactly like another devotee offers soft roses while worshipping the lord. For Krishna both are same there is no difference why because Bhishma is a devotee. It appears that Bhishma Deva is repenting the actions he committed against the person of the lord the way Bhishma is describing here in his prayers it appears like he is repenting why did I shoot arrows at the supreme lord or why did my arrows which are meant for striking Arjuna go and hit Krishna my worshipable lord but factually the lord's body was not at all pained due to his actions.

### Spiritual Identity, Material Body, Transcendental Form
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KJ6AOliQ5J4?t=452
**Verse References:** BG 2.11-30
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This material body is not our identity."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the material body with the spiritual form, explaining that the material body is subject to wounds and change, while the spiritual form is eternal and unaffected by material elements. He emphasizes that our true identity is as eternal devotees of Krishna in a spiritual form, not defined by our current material coverings.

This is a transcendental existence. This we understand from the Bhagavad Gita. Spirit or spiritual form, transcendental form is of a different quality than material form. We know this material body is subject to being wounded when somebody shoots an arrow at my body then my body can become wounded but Krishna's form or body is not material his body is not matter both he himself and his body are complete spiritual identity spiritual it is spiritual and Krishna has his identity as the supreme lord. This material body is not our identity. I appear like a human being because I have got a human like material body but actually I am not this body neither my identity is a human being this is in this life my mind and body system works like a human beings mind and body in another life if I have the body of a Devata then I will act and behave like a Devata if some other living being has got the body of a dog then the living being in that dog's body will behave and act like a dog but we are neither dog nor we are human nor we are Devata we are eternally devotee of Krishna in the spiritual form so that form spiritual form is not having an identity based on this material coverings this is not our identity.

### Spiritual Form, Indestructible, Uncontaminated
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KJ6AOliQ5J4?t=580
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker continues to describe the spiritual form as indestructible and uncontaminated by material elements, referencing the Bhagavad Gita and Skanda Purana. He explains that even with a material covering, the spiritual form remains unaffected and eternal.

So Krishna's identity is he is the supreme lord and he is the supreme lord in a spiritual form as Krishna. Spirit is never pierced, burnt, dried, moistened etc. Spirit cannot be destroyed by anything by any of the elements by fire by water by air by wind by some weapon material weapon made of one of the elements like metal or any of those elements it cannot be destroyed. This is vividly explained in the Bhagavad Gita chapter verses 11 to 30 clearly it is explained. So also it is stated in the Skanda Purana this confirmed in the Skanda Purana also spiritual form cannot be destroyed by any weapon it is said there that spirit is always uncontaminated and indestructible spirit is never contaminated by matter even in our case even though there is a material covering for our original spiritual form still that spiritual form or spirit cannot be contaminated by matter this remains like a superficial covering only and it cannot be destroyed spiritual form cannot be destroyed it is eternal form it cannot be distressed nor it can be dried up it cannot be distressed the spiritual form there is no question of distress.

**Verse References:** —

He emphasizes that the Lord is never killed because the spirit is never annihilated, and this concept can be bewildering to non-devotees.

When lord Vishnu in his incarnation appears before us he seems to be like one of the conditioned souls materially encaged encaged means material body has trapped us in this body or we have become imprisoned in this body but that imprisonment is at the level of consciousness because the spirit soul is not a physical entity so imprisonment here means I am conditioned now Vishnu appears to be like one of the conditioned souls just to bewilder the asuras or the non-believers who are always alert to kill the lord even from the very beginning of his appearance the asuras or the demons they are always envious of Krishna and because they are envious of Krishna when Krishna appears they want to kill him Kamsa wanted to kill Krishna and Ravana wanted to kill Rama you have heard this from the scriptures because foolishly they were unaware of the fact that the lord is never killed for the spirit is never annihilated lord is completely spiritual so how can he be killed by anybody by any weapon not possible therefore Bhishma's piercing of the body of lord Krishna is a sort of bewildering problem for the non-devotee atheist.

The non-devotees hear this they will ridicule you consider Krishna as god but how is Bhishma able to pierce his body by shooting sharp arrows so non-devotees cannot understand but those who are devotees or liberated souls are not bewildered by this description they are not bewildered by this description nothing happens to Krishna as far as his transcendental form is concerned it cannot be affected by any of the weapons. Bhishma Deva appreciated the all-merciful attitude of the lord because he did not leave Arjuna alone although he was disturbed by the sharpened arrows of Bhishma Deva nor was he reluctant to come before Bhishma's deathbed even though he was ill-treated by Bhishma on the battlefield see Krishna is so kind to his devotees he understands the devotees mood of serving and therefore he did not mind the sharpened arrows of Bhishma when Bhishma was fighting on the battlefield nor did he become reluctant to come in front of Bhishma when Bhishma was about to quit his body so therefore Bhishma's relationship with the lord is one of pure devotion and that is to be understood in this exchange between Bhishma and the supreme lord. ## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.9.35 | 03-05-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna's mercy, devotion, Bhishma Deva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yQj7KGowIic?t=58
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.35
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Let my mind be fixed upon that Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces verse 35 from the ninth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Bhishma Deva's glorification of Krishna and Krishna's merciful glance.

In this session, we will be discussing the thirty-fifth verse of the ninth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, continuing the prayers by Bhishma Deva to Lord Krishna. The meaning of this verse is: In obedience to the command of his friend, Lord Sri Krishna entered the arena of the battlefield of Kurukshetra between the soldiers of Arjuna and Duryodhana. And while there, he shortened the life-spans of the opposite party by his merciful glance. This was done simply by his looking at the enemy. Let my mind be fixed upon that Krishna. This is the glorification of Krishna by Bhishma Deva, the great devotee of Krishna.

---
### devotion, Krishna's supremacy, Arjuna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yQj7KGowIic?t=116
**Verse References:** BG 1.21-25
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is actually nobody's order carrier. Everyone is his servant and everyone carries out Krishna's order, whether willingly or unwillingly."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna, although acting as Arjuna's chariot driver, is not a servant but the Supreme Lord, whose orders everyone follows, willingly or unwillingly, and highlights the sweet relationship between Krishna and Arjuna.

It is clearly explained here that Krishna, simply by his merciful glance, can shorten the duration of the life of the enemy to make Arjuna, his devotee, victorious. Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport to this verse, in the Bhagavad Gita, 1.21-25, Arjuna ordered the infallible Lord Sri Krishna to place his chariot between the phalanxes of the soldiers. He asked Krishna to stay there until he had finished observing the enemies he had to face in the battle. When the Lord was so asked, he at once did so, just like an order carrier. Krishna is actually nobody's order carrier. Everyone is his servant and everyone carries out Krishna's order, whether willingly or unwillingly. Devotees carry out Krishna's orders willingly, and non-devotees are forced to carry out Krishna's orders unwillingly. Because non-devotees are in the material world in forgetfulness of Krishna, but they are forced by Krishna. They are forced by Krishna's material energy to act according to the conditioning. So, nobody is independent among the living entities. Nobody is independent. So, here it is described, the sweet relationship between Krishna and Arjuna, such that when Arjuna ordered Krishna, 'Take my chariot between the two armies,' Krishna did so, just like an order carrier, though he is not an order carrier.

---
### devotion, Krishna's love, reciprocity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yQj7KGowIic?t=245
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Out of his causeless mercy and affection for his pure devotees, sometimes Krishna carries out the order of the devotee like an awaiting servant. Krishna takes pleasure in doing this."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's causeless mercy and affection for his devotees, explaining that Krishna willingly fulfills their desires out of love, similar to a father indulging a child, emphasizing the sweet relationship between Krishna and his devotees.

And the Lord pointed out all the important men on the opposite side saying, 'Here is Bhishma, here is Drona, and so on.' The Lord, being the supreme living being, is never the order supplier or carrier of anyone. Krishna is never the order supplier or order carrier of anyone, wherever that person may be in the material estimation. There are some people who are very, very powerful, very great. Some people who are not so great. Some people are very insignificant. But Krishna has nothing to do with any of these material positions of different living beings. Out of his causeless mercy and affection for his pure devotees, sometimes Krishna carries out the order of the devotee like an awaiting servant. Krishna takes pleasure in doing this. That my devotee is very dear to me. So if my devotee wants something, I will gladly reciprocate with the devotee, exactly as the devotee wants me to reciprocate. By executing the order of a devotee, the Lord becomes pleased, as a father is pleased to carry out the order of his devotees. Similarly, the Lord is pleased to carry out the order of a small child. You have experienced that a small child may sometimes ask the father like a master commands his servant. 'Give me that toy.' So the father, out of love for the child, will actually carry out the order, not because the child has got any authority or any power. No. Similarly, nobody has authority or power to give orders to Krishna. On the other hand, Krishna is always the order giver, and everyone is his servant without exception. But Krishna takes pleasure in executing the order of a devotee because of the devotee, the sweet relationship between Krishna and the devotee.

---
### love, devotion, Krishna's names
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yQj7KGowIic?t=394
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is very very pleased if you address him in relationship with his devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Krishna's willingness to carry out the orders of his devotees stems from pure transcendental love, highlighting Bhishma Deva's awareness of this fact and Krishna's pleasure in being addressed in relation to his devotees.

Now, this sort of carrying out orders by the Lord of his devotee is possible only out of pure transcendental love between the Lord and his devotees. And Bhishma Deva was aware of this fact. He therefore addresses the Lord as the friend of Arjuna, Partha Sakha, Vijaya Sakha. As Prabhupada has explained earlier, Krishna is very very pleased if you address him in relationship with his devotees. Keshodanandana, Radhakantam, Gopijanavallabha. Always Krishna in relationship with his devotees. Krishna is never without his devotees, and devotees are never without Krishna. So always both devotees and Krishna like to hear an address which is describing the relationship of the Lord and his devotees. Therefore, Bhishma is addressing Krishna as Partha Sakha, Vijaya Sakha. Further, Srila Prabhupada says, the Lord diminished the duration of life of the opposite party by his merciful glance.

---
### karma, liberation, Krishna's mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yQj7KGowIic?t=477
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is said that all the fighters who assembled on the battlefield of Kurukshetra attained salvation by personally seeing the Lord at the time of death."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that while lifespan is fixed based on karma, Krishna can shorten or extend it, and that the enemies of Arjuna attained salvation by seeing Krishna at the time of death on the battlefield of Kurukshetra.

Our lifespan, at the time of birth, is fixed, of a certain duration is fixed based on our past karma. But that duration, even though it is fixed, it can be shortened or increased by Krishna. If somebody becomes very very sinful, their duration of life is shortened. If somebody is able to please a pure devotee, their duration of life may be extended. So, the duration of life, though fixed, is subject to being reduced or increased by Krishna or by a pure devotee. So in this case, it is described that the duration of life of the enemy of Arjuna was shortened by Krishna. It is said that all the fighters who assembled on the battlefield of Kurukshetra attained salvation by personally seeing the Lord at the time of death. Krishna was present on the battlefield of Kurukshetra to deliver all those who were eligible because simply by seeing Krishna at the time of death, one is able to get liberation immediately. So that is the mercy of Krishna upon the living entities.

---
### Krishna's impartiality, liberation, death
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yQj7KGowIic?t=572
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whether the enemy is dying in the battlefield or the enemy will have to die later on somewhere else, death is certain for every living being who has taken birth in this world."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that Krishna's shortening the lifespan of Arjuna's enemies was not partiality but an act of mercy, as it allowed them to attain liberation by seeing Krishna at the time of death, which is superior to an ordinary death.

Therefore, his diminishing the duration of life of Arjuna's enemy does not mean that he was partial to the cause of Arjuna. Somebody may think that Krishna is shortening the duration of life of the enemy, so he is giving benefit to Arjuna in fighting this battle which is not fair. But actually Krishna is not doing this simply for benefiting Arjuna alone. He is also benefiting the enemy. Whether the enemy is dying in the battlefield or the enemy will have to die later on somewhere else, death is certain for every living being who has taken birth in this world. So death cannot be avoided. But if the death is caused by a pure devotee of Krishna, then the person who dies actually attains liberation immediately because of the presence of Krishna, the glance of Krishna. So Krishna benefited the enemy also, not only Arjuna. Factually, he was merciful to the opposite party because they would not have attained salvation by dying at home in the ordinary course of life. This we have to remember.

---
### Krishna's goodness, impartiality, spiritual benefit
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yQj7KGowIic?t=687
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's protection and Krishna's killing both are good for the person who receives the protection from Krishna or is killed by Krishna. For both of them, it is ultimately beneficial spiritually."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates that Krishna is all-good, impartial, and that all his actions, including diminishing lifespan, are ultimately beneficial for everyone involved, leading to spiritual benefit.

Somebody may not die on the battlefield, but they have to go back home and they have to die afterwards. Such death is not at all going to give them liberation, ordinary death. Here was a chance to see the Lord at the time of death, and thus attain salvation from material life. See, Krishna's mercy upon the enemies. So therefore, Krishna is always all good. He has no enmity. He has no feeling of partiality. He does not favor one person and deny another person. Never. Krishna never does that, because every living being is his part and parcel. mamayivamso jivaloke jivabhuta-sanatana. Eternally we are parts and parcel. So how can Krishna be partial to anyone? It's not possible. Therefore, the Lord is all good, and whatever he does is for everyone's good. Krishna's actions are on the absolute platform. So Krishna's protection and Krishna's killing both are good for the person who receives the protection from Krishna or is killed by Krishna. For both of them, it is ultimately beneficial spiritually.

---
### liberation, Krishna consciousness, transcendental activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yQj7KGowIic?t=799
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To put an end to this miserable existence is possible by coming in contact with Krishna or his pure devotee."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing that material calculations are insignificant compared to the spiritual benefit of encountering Krishna, highlighting that understanding Krishna's transcendental activities leads to liberation.

Don't make material calculations. That is not going to help the person by some material calculations if they think they are escaping dying on the battlefield. They are forgetting that they have to die sometime later. And at that time, they may not have the benefit of Krishna consciousness. Because we have to take birth again in this material world and suffer the consequences. So to put an end to this miserable existence is possible by coming in contact with Krishna or his pure devotee. Apparently, it was victory for Arjuna, but factually, it was for the good of Arjuna's enemies. Whatever Krishna did was good for Arjuna's enemies also. That way, the Lord is all good. Such are the transcendental activities of the Lord. And whoever understands this also gets salvation after putting his material body. janma karma chame divyam evam yo veti tatvaram yo veti tatvatah. The activities of the Lord are all transcendental. And if we understand the transcendental nature of Krishna's activities, we also become liberated. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.9.36 | 04-05-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### duty, leadership, Vedic culture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AVB6fWBe8bc?t=80
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unlike the modern-day defense ministers or presidents, head of state, who never go to a battlefield to fight or defend the country."
**Summary:** Bhishmadeva's prayer refers to Arjuna's bewilderment at the start of the Kurukshetra war, when Krishna eradicated his ignorance by speaking the Bhagavad Gita, and Srila Prabhupada explains the difference between Vedic Kshatriyas and modern leaders.

The meaning of this verse is, when Arjuna was seemingly polluted by ignorance upon observing the soldiers and commanders before him on the battlefield, the Lord eradicated his ignorance by delivering transcendental knowledge. May his lotus feet always remain the object of my attraction. So this prayer by Bhishmadeva refers to Arjuna's bewilderment upon seeing the enemy at the beginning of the battle of Kurukshetra, when Krishna eradicated Arjuna's ignorance by speaking Bhagavad Gita. So directly it is a reference to Krishna instructing Arjuna in the subject matter of Bhagavad Gita. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, the kings and the commanders were to stand in the front of the fighting soldiers. They are the system of actual fighting. The kings and commanders were not so-called presidents or ministers of defense as they are today. Prabhupada is comparing the Vedic times when the Kshatriyas with the proper spirit, they would execute their duties boldly, courageously, chivalrously. Unlike the modern-day defense ministers or presidents, head of state, who never go to a battlefield to fight or defend the country. They simply sit in closed offices, secure offices and issue commands. They would not stay home while the poor soldiers or mercenaries were fighting face to face. This is not the case with the Kshatriyas like Arjuna. Bhishma, all of them fought in the front line. The soldiers were behind. The generals and the commanders were in the front.

---
### leadership, duty, social order
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AVB6fWBe8bc?t=209
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nowadays, when we elect a monarch, we elect a monarch. We don't check the qualification of the person who is elected or the person who is able to face the enemy in a battle."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the qualities of Vedic monarchs with modern elected leaders, emphasizing the importance of courage and qualification, and explains that citizens were protected during battles and paid taxes to the ruler, regardless of who it was.

This may be the regulation of modern democracy. But when actual monarchy was prevailing, the monarchs were not cowards elected without consideration of qualifications. Nowadays, when we elect a monarch, we elect a monarch. We don't check the qualification of the person who is elected or the person who is able to face the enemy in a battle. We don't check that. Whereas in the former times, a Kshatriya was not a Kshatriya for namesake. He was actually bold, he was brave, he was ready to lay down his life for protecting the citizens in a battle. As it was evident from the battlefield of Kurukshetra, all the executive heads of both parties like Drona, Bhishma, Arjuna and Duryodhana were not sleeping. All of them were actual participants in the fighting, which was selected to be executed at a place of war away from the civil residential quarters. Civilians were not affected by the battle of Kurukshetra because the battle was fought between Kshatriyas only. This means that the innocent citizens were immune from all effects of fighting between the rival royal parties. Citizens were kept safe irrespective of who would rule and if they had to challenge one king had to challenge another king, they would fight on the battlefield, not fight in the middle of some other place. No, they would not do that. The citizens had no power, no business in seeing what was going to happen during such fighting. It is not that they are watching some match, wrestling match. No. It's not like that. The citizens were protected whoever was the king. Prabhupada explains they were to pay the citizens were to pay one fourth of their income to the ruler whether he be Arjuna or Duryodhana. Who was the ruler? The scriptures state the citizens should give one fourth of his income to the ruler.

---
### compassion, illusion, Krishna's plan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AVB6fWBe8bc?t=378
**Verse References:** BG 4
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Actually as a devotee Arjuna's intelligence cannot be polluted. But it was apparently polluted because Krishna wanted to speak Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna. And through Arjuna Krishna wants to instruct the Bhagavad Gita to all other living beings in this world."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Arjuna's compassion and initial reluctance to fight, explaining that his apparent bewilderment was part of Krishna's plan to deliver the Bhagavad Gita for the benefit of all conditioned souls.

All the commanders of the parties on the battlefield of Kurukshetra were standing face to face and Arjuna saw them with great compassion and lamented that he was to kill his kinsmen on the battlefield for the sake of the empire. This was Arjuna's beginning reaction when he saw the enemy party. He saw all his own friends and relatives and he felt compassion for them. Why should I kill them? For me to win this battle and rule over the kingdom of the whole world? No, I don't want. Arjuna felt like that. Arjuna was not at all afraid of the giant military phalanx presented by Duryodhana. But as a merciful devotee of the Lord renunciation of worldly things was natural for him. There is a quality of a devotee. Prabhupada explains in the first chapter of the Bhagavad Gita in one of the purports. Arjuna's kind and soft-hearted nature is the reason why Arjuna was feeling compassion for his enemies. He was not afraid of his enemies. Never. And thus Arjuna decided not to fight for worldly possessions. But this was due to a poor fund of knowledge and therefore it is said here that his intelligence became polluted. Kumatim aharad atmavigyaya, kumatim, his intelligence became polluted. Kumatim. His intelligence was polluted by such thinking. Actually as a devotee Arjuna's intelligence cannot be polluted. But it was apparently polluted because Krishna wanted to speak Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna. And through Arjuna Krishna wants to instruct the Bhagavad Gita to all other living beings in this world. It is Krishna's plan to deliver all the conditioned souls in so many different ways.

---
### Krishna's plan, devotee, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AVB6fWBe8bc?t=533
**Verse References:** BG 4
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Otherwise his devotee can never come under the influence of illusion. Apparently Arjuna's intelligence became polluted because otherwise there would have been no chance to deliver the teachings of the Gita for the good of all polluted conditioned souls."
**Summary:** Arjuna is described as a constant companion of the Lord, and the speaker explains Krishna's answer to Arjuna's question about how he could have spoken the Bhagavad Gita millions of years ago, emphasizing that Krishna's plan involved putting Arjuna in a position of illusion to deliver the Gita for the benefit of all.

Now Arjuna is described as a constant companion of the Lord as is clear on the fourth chapter of the Bhagavad Gita. In the fourth chapter Arjuna asks Krishna that you are telling me you spoke this science of yoga to some god millions of years back. Now how can I understand you spoke this millions of years back when I am seeing that you were born at about the same time I was born. So how could he have spoken this knowledge before millions of years to the sun god. So Krishna answers both you and I keep taking birth again and again. You can't remember your previous births I can remember all my previous appearances. So Krishna's position is that he never is any time forgetful of Krishna. Arjuna's position is like that, pure devotee's position is like that. But still to show mercy upon the conditioned souls Krishna put Arjuna into a position of becoming illusioned and spoke the Bhagavad Gita to become make him free from illusion. So it is Krishna's plan. Otherwise his devotee can never come under the influence of illusion. Apparently Arjuna's intelligence became polluted because otherwise there would have been no chance to deliver the teachings of the Gita for the good of all polluted conditioned souls. For our benefit Krishna spoke Bhagavad Gita. So it is for our sake that Krishna spoke the Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna.

---
### false ego, spiritual knowledge, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AVB6fWBe8bc?t=689
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The Bhagavad Gita was delivered to the conditioned souls of the world to deliver them from the wrong conception of identifying the body with the soul. This is the fundamental problem all of us have."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna spoke the Bhagavad Gita for the good of all polluted conditioned souls, emphasizing the importance of understanding that we are spirit souls and re-establishing our relationship with Krishna through devotional service and transcendental knowledge.

Now Prabhupada explains Krishna is speaking the Bhagavad Gita for the good of all polluted conditioned souls who are engaged in material bondage by the conception of the false material body. We are having this body we wrongly think I am this body each one of us and we are misled by the body to think in terms of material existence. We are simply bound up the more we engage in different activities in this world, material activities, the more we become entangled in this material world. So therefore the Bhagavad Gita is spoken by Krishna to help us understand primarily that we are spirit soul and Krishna suggests a method by which we can come out of this material existence itself by devotional service, by understanding Krishna, by knowing Krishna, by understanding Krishna, by understanding Krishna. So therefore it is explained the Bhagavad Gita was delivered to the conditioned souls of the world to deliver them from the wrong conception of identifying the body with the soul. This is the fundamental problem all of us have. Each one of us we are spirit soul but we identify with this body and that is the greatest mistake. In ignorance we only think in terms of body that is all. Whereas Krishna speaks Bhagavad Gita to help us get out of this misconception and we should also re-establish our eternal relationship with the Supreme Lord Krishna and with the Supreme Lord Krishna. Nearly getting out of material existence is not enough. We have to also re-establish our lost relationship with Krishna. So therefore Atma Vidya or transcendental knowledge of himself was primarily spoken by the Lord for the benefit of all concerned in all parts of the universe. Krishna is speaking Atma Vidya or Brahma Vidya, transcendental knowledge, spiritual knowledge for the benefit of everyone throughout the universe. There is no restriction or limitation for anyone to take up the instructions of Krishna given in the Bhagavad Gita and make their life successful. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.9.37 | 06-05-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Transcendental nature of Krishna's actions, Elevating Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/idXfNsA7ED0?t=57
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.37
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every action of Krishna is completely transcendental, and remembering such events is automatically elevating the relationship with Krishna and elevating one's consciousness to the spiritual platform."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the verse to be discussed and emphasizes the transcendental nature of Krishna's activities and their power to elevate consciousness.

In this session, we will be discussing the 37th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, continuing the prayers by Bhishma Deva. The meaning of this verse is: "Fulfilling my desire and sacrificing his own promise, he got down from the chariot, took up its wheel, and ran towards me hurriedly, just as a lion goes to kill an elephant. He even dropped his outer garment on the way." Bhishma Deva is recollecting the different events that happened in the past in relationship with Krishna. Every action of Krishna is completely transcendental, and remembering such events is automatically elevating the relationship with Krishna and elevating one's consciousness to the spiritual platform. Every single activity the Lord performs, he does so on the spiritual plane. None of the activities of Krishna are material, even though Krishna is in the material world. He does not come in contact with the material energy at all. He is completely aloof from it.

---
### Dharma Yudha, Kshatriya Principles, Bhishma's Vow
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/idXfNsA7ED0?t=173
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A kshatriya doesn't mind losing a battle provided he fights to his best capacity and sacrifices his life."
**Summary:** This segment explains Srila Prabhupada's purport regarding the battle of Kurukshetra, the nature of a Kshatriya, and Duryodhana's criticism of Bhishma, leading to Bhishma's vow to kill the Pandavas.

In the purport, Srila Prabhupada says the battle of Kurukshetra was fought on military principles, but at the same time in a sporting spirit, like a friend's fight with another friend. They are simultaneously fighting, following the military principles, and at the same time, in a friendly spirit. That's the nature of a kshatriya. A kshatriya doesn't mind losing a battle provided he fights to his best capacity and gives up his life in the course of fighting, sacrifices his life. Such a kshatriya actually is able to elevate himself to the heavenly planets, minimum, by fighting in the proper kshatriya spirit in a Dharma Yudha. So here it is explained by Srila Prabhupada in the purport: Duryodhana criticized Bhishma Deva, allegedly, that he was reluctant to kill Arjuna because of paternal affection. Bhishma's position is very unique. He is related to both the Pandavas and Kauravas. Both the Pandavas and Kauravas are the grandsons of his brother, Bhishma's brother. So Bhishma is equally related to the Pandavas and Kauravas. He was not acting partially, but Duryodhana criticized him, saying that Bhishma is not killing Arjuna because he has some affection for the Pandavas, especially Arjuna. So it is explained by Prabhupada here: a kshatriya cannot tolerate insults on the principle of fighting because of this.

---
### Bhishma's Vow, Krishna's Plan, Arjuna's Request
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/idXfNsA7ED0?t=307
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhishma Deva therefore promised that the next day he would kill all the five Pandavas with special weapons made for the purpose."
**Summary:** This segment details Bhishma's vow to kill the Pandavas, Duryodhana's possession of the special arrows, and Arjuna's request to Duryodhana to obtain those arrows, orchestrated by Krishna.

Bhishma Deva therefore promised that the next day he would kill all the five Pandavas with special weapons made for the purpose. Bhishma Deva, when he heard this criticism of Duryodhana, he became angry, and he took a vow that he is going to kill all the five Pandavas the next day of the battle. Duryodhana was satisfied, and he kept the arrows with him to be delivered the next day during the fight. Bhishma prepared five special arrows, and Duryodhana thought that Bhishma has prepared these arrows, but if anything happens from now onwards till the next day morning when the battle starts, anything can happen. So Duryodhana said, "Please give me those five arrows, and I'll keep them with me safely, and I'll give them to you tomorrow morning just before the battle begins." Bhishma said, "Okay." By tricks, Arjuna took the arrows from Duryodhana, and Bhishma Deva could understand that that was the trick of Lord Krishna, so he took a vow that the next day Krishna would have to take up weapons himself, otherwise his friend Arjuna would die. Krishna knew that Bhishma has prepared five special weapons, and it is safely kept with Duryodhana, so Krishna sent Arjuna, "Go and ask Duryodhana for those five arrows." So Arjuna went to Duryodhana's camp, and Duryodhana welcomed him and said yes.

---
### Krishna's Promise, Bhishma's Determination, Krishna's Anger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/idXfNsA7ED0?t=460
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unless Krishna will break his promise and take weapons and fight himself to save his friend."
**Summary:** This segment describes how Arjuna obtained the arrows, Bhishma's continued determination to fight, the intensity of the battle, and Krishna's decision to break his promise to protect Arjuna.

Arjuna said, "I want the five arrows which Bhishma has prepared and kept it safe. You have kept it safely with you." Duryodhana said, "Okay, I will give you those arrows." He gave away those arrows. And Bhishma came to know that Duryodhana was supposed to safe keep those weapons. In spite of that, by Krishna's trick, the arrows were taken away by Arjuna. So he said, "Never mind. Even if those arrows are not there, I am going to fight very, very vigorously. And Arjuna's life is really in danger the next day. Unless Krishna will break his promise and take weapons and fight himself to save his friend." So the next day's fighting, Bhishma Deva fought so violently that both Arjuna and Krishna were in trouble. It was such a fierce battle. Arjuna was almost defeated. The situation was so tense that he was about to be killed by Bhishma Deva the very next moment. At that very moment when Bhishma was about to kill Arjuna, Krishna wanted to please his devotee Bhishma by keeping Bhishma's promise, which is more important than his own promise. Bhishma's promise was that to save Arjuna, Krishna will have to take his weapon. Otherwise, if Bhishma was to take it or not, Krishna did not have to take it. Krishna promised to not use his own weapon, but left Bhishma with enemies who are his obligations. When Bhishma left, Krishna saw Krishna was not in the right position in the old age and forced him to give up his 2020 success. He promised before the beginning of Brukshetra's ancient battle, Chris's duty to Kurukshetra, that one day he would not use his wealth on the intimidated battles, and support anorm the Kurukshetra group. When Krishna was killed, Kurukshetra's aid was punched into hold by Arjuna without depending upon his strategy towards safety. But to protect Arjuna, Bhishma's promise was refused. He confessed to Arjuna's promise to Kurukshetra, for which, I never know why he betrayed him, and hurriedly rushed Bhishma Deva in an angry mood as a lion goes to kill an elephant.

---
### Krishna's Devotion, Surrender, Kshatriya Principles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/idXfNsA7ED0?t=594
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For a devotee, if the Lord is going to kill me, that is the greatest blessing."
**Summary:** This segment explains the analogy of the lion and elephant, Krishna's anger, Bhishma's surrender, and the Kshatriya principle of not killing a surrendered opponent.

When there is a fight between a lion and an elephant, both are very powerful, but they are powerful in their own way. So generally lions don't bother elephants, elephants don't bother lions, because both are very powerful. But sometimes when there is a fight between a lion and an elephant, then both will fight very vigorously, and the outcome of this fight is uncertain. Sometimes the elephant is victorious, sometimes the lion is victorious. So in this case, Krishna rushed at Bhishma just like a lion goes to kill an elephant. That's the description here. Now while Krishna was going in that angry mood, charging at Bhishma Deva, he dropped his covering cloth on the way, and out of great anger he did not know that he had dropped it. Bhishma Deva at once gave up his weapons and stood to be killed by Krishna, his beloved Lord. For a devotee, if the Lord is going to kill me, that is the greatest blessing, because the devotees know Lord's killing and Lord's protection both are absolute. There is no difference. So Bhishma did not mind Krishna killing him. He said that is the best way to ensure that I will be completely purified and I will get ultimate salvation and liberation. So the fighting of the day ended at that very moment, and Arjuna was saved, because the Kshatriya principle is, if somebody surrenders, then the battle has to be stopped. It cannot continue. One Kshatriya will not kill another Kshatriya if that other Kshatriya surrenders. He will not kill. That's the military principles by which the battle of Kurukshetra was arranged to be fought.

---
### Krishna's Plan, Devotee's Desire, Sporting Spirit
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/idXfNsA7ED0?t=730
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhishma will not care for anything else other than fulfilling his devotee's desire."
**Summary:** This segment summarizes Krishna's plan to fulfill Bhishma's desire, emphasizing Krishna's love for his devotees and the sporting spirit in which the battle was fought, dispelling notions of political diplomacy or enmity.

So there was no possibility of Arjuna's death because the Lord himself was on the chariot. But because Bhishma Deva wanted to see Krishna take up some weapon to save his friend Arjuna, so the Lord created the situation making Arjuna's death imminent. Krishna created the situation. It should be for Bhishma Deva to show that his promise was fulfilled. Bhishma's promise was fulfilled, and that Krishna had taken up the wheel as Bhishma had predicted. So this is a confidential explanation of the Lord's action on the battlefield, breaking his own promise and taking up the wheel of a chariot in order to save Arjuna while Arjuna's life was in danger when Bhishma was fighting very violently. So it is explained here by Bhishma that the Lord fulfilled my desire because I am his devotee. He broke his own promise. Bhishma will not care for anything else other than fulfilling his devotee's desire. That is Krishna's love for his devotees, his affection for his devotees. So that is being described here by Srila Prabhupada in great detail, the confidential explanation for this whole incident, which ordinarily people will not understand. They will think something is going on between Arjuna, Krishna, Bhishma. Something is going on. No, it is not any kind of political diplomacy or not any kind of enmity. It is actually in a sporting spirit the whole battle was fought. So I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---
