## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.9.44 | 14-05-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Bhishma Deva, Liberation, Analogy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sGLdAzVMr5U?t=35
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.44
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Knowing that Bhishma Deva had merged into the unlimited eternity of the Supreme Absolute, all present there became silent like birds at the end of the day."
**Summary:** This segment describes Bhishma Deva quitting his body and merging into the Supreme Absolute, and the reaction of the saintly persons present, drawing an analogy to birds returning to their nests at sunset.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Jai Vanarottamam Deviim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 44th verse of the 9th chapter of the first chant of Srimad Bhagavatam. The description is continuing what happened after Bhishma Deva quit his body. Sampadyamanam Agyaya Bhishmam Brahmani Nishkale Sarve Babhu Te Tushneem Vayam Siva Bhishma Dinakya Ye. The meaning of this verse is knowing that Bhishma Deva had merged into the unlimited eternity of the Supreme Absolute, all present there became silent like birds at the end of the day. Now here is an analogy given that when birds are living in one tree, there are a lot of birds. In the morning they go out searching for food, etc. They go chirping and they come back in the evening around the time of sunset. Again they come back chirping and once they settle down in their particular nest, they become silent. Something like this happened after Bhishma quit his body. Of course he was lying there on the bed of arrows. But he chose the most auspicious moment as one who had the benediction to choose an auspicious time to quit the body. But most importantly, he was fully absorbed in thought of Krishna who was personally present before Bhishma Deva. So everyone present there, so many great saintly persons had assembled to witness Bhishma quitting his body. They knew Bhishma was a great personality. He is one of the Mahajanas, one of the ideal devotees in the whole universe. So when he was quitting his body, Bhishma was quitting his body. So all the great saintly persons from all over the universe had come. Great sages had come there, assembled there. All of them, they became silent, knowing that Bhishma Deva had attained his perfection of going back home, back to Godhead. That is described here as, he had merged into the unlimited eternity of the Supreme Absolute.

---
### Liberation, Relationship with Krishna, Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sGLdAzVMr5U?t=229
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The living beings are all component parts and parcels of the Absolute Personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** This segment explains Srila Prabhupada's explanation of merging into the unlimited eternity of the Supreme Absolute, emphasizing the eternal relationship between the living being and Krishna as servitor and served.

So Srila Prabhupada explains what does it mean to merge into the unlimited eternity of the Supreme Absolute. So Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, to enter into or become merged into the unlimited eternity of the Supreme Absolute means to enter the original home of the living being. This is described by Prabhupāda many times in other places as, let us go back home, back to Godhead. That's where all of us belong. Everyone, every living entity belongs to the home, our home, that is Kṛṣṇa's personal abode, the unlimited eternity of the Supreme Absolute. So Śrīla Prabhupāda says further, the living beings are all component parts and parcels of the Absolute Personality of Godhead. We are all very, very, very tiny spirit souls, part of Kṛṣṇa. Parcel means inseparable part, cannot be separated. Our relationship with Kṛṣṇa is eternal. We are connected with Kṛṣṇa eternally. Only, point is that temporarily we may forget when in the material world we forget Kṛṣṇa under the influence of the material energy, we forget Kṛṣṇa. But the relationship is not broken. It is permanently there. It's a spiritual relationship, so it can never be broken. Spirit means eternal. Spiritual relationship is eternal. Therefore, they are eternally related with Him as the servitor and the served. Every spirit soul, every living being, each one of us, we are related with Kṛṣṇa as His servitors. We are meant to serve Him. Don't mistake this to be something like material service. It is actually spiritual service, loving, devotional service. That's the correct explanation. The Lord is served by all His parts and parcels as the complete machine is served by its parts and parcels. This is an example being given by Prabhupāda to help us understand how we are connected with the Lord.

---
### Analogy, Material World, Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sGLdAzVMr5U?t=392
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, we as spirit soul, tiny, tiny, tiny spirit soul, we are totally useless when we are separate from Kṛṣṇa."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of a machine and its parts, this segment explains that a spirit soul separated from Krishna is useless, and that material activities are useless because they are not in service of the Lord. It also describes the material world as a prison.

Any part of the machine removed from the whole is no longer important. You can understand this point. Let's say there is a small screw which is holding some particular item in the entire machine, huge machine, several parts. Now that screw, if it falls off, what is the value of this screw? But as long as it is connected with the machine, it is very, very important. It is doing a very specific function and it is very important. So, any part of the machine removed from the whole is no longer important. So, we as spirit soul, tiny, tiny, tiny spirit soul, we are totally useless when we are separate from Kṛṣṇa. Of course, we cannot be separated, inseparable. But if we want to separate or we want to try to separate, then our life is useless. Similarly, any part and parcel of the Absolute detached from the service of the Lord is useless. Now, material activity means so many activities we perform which is not service of the Lord. That is useless. All material activities are useless because they are not of the nature of service of the Supreme Lord Krishna. The living beings who are in the material world are all disintegrated parts and parcel of the Supreme Whole and they are no longer as important as the original parts and parcels. Originally, we are all part and parcel of Kṛṣṇa. So, as original parts, we are very, very important. Now, apart from the living beings in this material world Prabhupāda is telling, there are, however, more integrated living beings who are eternally liberated in the spiritual world. They are eternally liberated. The material energy of the Lord called Durga Shakti or the superintendent of the prison house takes charge of the disintegrated parts and parcels and thus they undergo a conditioned life under the laws of material nature. What is our life in this material world, material existence as we say? It is a life in a prison. Prison because we are originally liberated souls but now we are conditioned by the laws of material nature.

---
### Spiritual Urge, Brahma Jijnasa, Liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sGLdAzVMr5U?t=573
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Human form of life is meant for this."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the limitations of material existence and the development of the spiritual urge (Brahma Jijnasa) to return to Godhead, emphasizing that the human form of life is meant for this inquiry.

The laws of material nature are forcing us to live in this body and this body means full of limitations. When the living being becomes conscious of this fact, just like all of us are now understanding this point as we study here Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, we become conscious of this fact, then the living being tries to go back home, back to Godhead and thus the spiritual urge of the living being begins. When we develop a desire, let me go back home, back to Godhead, where I belong, where life is eternal, full of bliss, full of knowledge, in association with the most loving, supreme personality of Godhead, Krishna, very, very attractive proposition, then that's called the spiritual urge of the living being begins. This spiritual urge is called Brahman. Brahma Jijnasa or inquiry about Brahman. Brahman means Krishna. So inquiry about Krishna. It's called Brahma Jijnasa. Inquire about Krishna. Human form of life is meant for this. Principally this Brahma Jijnasa is successful by knowledge, renunciation and devotional service to the Lord. Jnana, Vairagya, Bhakti. Three things are required to ensure success of Brahma Jijnasa, the spiritual urge.

---
### Jnana, Vairagya, Bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sGLdAzVMr5U?t=670
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Our original position is in the spiritual world in relationship with Krishna as his servant, as his servitor, as his devotee, serving in love."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the three requirements for success in Brahma Jijnasa: Jnana (knowledge), Vairagya (renunciation), and Bhakti (devotional service), and describes the three categories of transcendentalists: Jnanis, Yogis, and Bhaktas.

Jnana or knowledge means knowledge of everything of Brahman, the Supreme. We should get proper understanding of who is Krishna or what is Krishna, from the proper source, from the proper authorities. Renunciation means detachment of material affection. In this material world we develop so many different types of material attachments to so many things, to so many material situations, material desires, material objects, material bodies, material relationships. We get attached. So that material affection we have to give up. That is renunciation. And devotional service is the revival by practice of the original position of the living being. Our original position is in the spiritual world in relationship with Krishna as his servant, as his servitor, as his devotee, serving in love. The successful living beings who are eligible to enter into the realm of the Absolute are called the Jnanis, the Yogis and the Bhaktas. Three categories of transcendentalists. Jnanis, Yogis and Bhaktas. Now of these three categories of transcendentalists, the Jnanis and Yogis enter into the impersonal race of the Supreme. In the spiritual world, Krishna is personally present in his personal abode, the spiritual planets, one of the spiritual planets. And outside the spiritual planets is the spiritual sky consisting of the impersonal Brahmajyoti. So the Jnanis and Yogis enter the impersonal Brahmajyoti, not the spiritual planets, not the personal abode of Krishna. But the Bhaktas enter into the spiritual planets known as the Vaikunthas. The spiritual planets, Bhaktas enter. This is basically the third category of transcendentalists. Jnanis, Yogis, Bhaktas. Only the Bhaktas enter into Vaikuntha.

---
### Vaikuntha, Bhakti, Liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sGLdAzVMr5U?t=824
**Verse References:** BG 8.16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who don't render loving service, they have no place in the Vaikuntha."
**Summary:** This segment describes life in the Vaikuntha planets, the relationships devotees have with the Lord, and the fate of Jnanis and Yogis who do not take to devotional service, emphasizing that only Bhaktas can enter Vaikuntha and never return to the material world.

In these spiritual planets, the Supreme Lord as Narayana predominates. And the healthy, unconditioned living beings live there by rendering loving service to the Lord in the capacity of servant, friend, parents and fiancée. Four kinds of relationship. Dasya, Sakya, Vatsalya, Madhurya. Different types of relationship different devotees have with the Supreme Lord in the spiritual world. They render service. There the unconditioned living beings enjoy life in full freedom with the Lord. Whereas the impersonalist Jnanis and Yogis enter into the impersonal glowing effulgence of the Vaikuntha planets. So they can't enter because they have to graduate to come to the point of Bhakti, rendering loving service. Those who don't render loving service, they have no place in the Vaikuntha. So these Jnanis and Yogis, if they take to devotional service, then their spiritual life is successful. If they don't take to devotional service after attaining the Brahmajyoti, then they fall down into the material world. This is explained in the Bhagavatam. They come back to the material world. Whereas devotees, they never come back, having gone once there in the Bhagavad Gita. yad gatva na nivartante taddhama paramam mama. So that is the position of the devotees. So I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Bhismadeva, respect, planetary systems
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7N63l-laWHc?t=46
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.45
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhishma Deva was respected both by the human beings and by the demigods."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam about Bhismadeva and explains how he was respected by both humans and demigods across different planetary systems (Bhuloka, Bhuvarloka, and Svarloka).

In this session, we will be discussing from the 45th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. Tatra Dundubhayo Neduhu Deva Manava Vaditaha Shashamsuhu Sadavo Radyam Khadpetu Pushpa Vrishdayaha. Thereafter, both men and demigods sounded drums in honor, and the honest royal order commenced demonstrations of honor and respect, and from the sky fell showers of flowers. This indicates that Bhismadeva is a very, very great personality, not only recognized as a great personality on this earth planet, but also in the upper planets even by the devatas. That is the greatness of Bhismadeva. So in the purport, Sri Laprabhupada explains the significance of this way of showing respect to Bhishma Deva. Bhishma Deva was respected both by the human beings and by the demigods. The human beings live on earth and similar other planets in the Bhur and Bhuvara group of planets. Bhuloka, Bhuvarloka. Bhuloka is the earthly planetary system. There are planets which are on this level of the Bhuloka. Loka means a group of planets belonging to a similar category. Bhuvarloka is above Bhuloka, but that is also a planetary system where the humans live, human beings live. But the demigods live in the Svarloka group. Svarloka, heavenly planets. And all of them knew Bhishma Deva as a great warrior and devotee of the Lord.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.9.46 | 17-05-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### devotion, politics, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Hw1wtbon5j0?t=55
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So although Yudhishthira knew that my uncle is sending me to this house to set it on fire, still he was very obedient."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the context of verse SB 1.9.46, describing Yudhishthira's grief after Bhishma Deva's passing and then elaborates on the political intrigues and dangers faced by the Pandavas due to Dhritarashtra's desire for his own sons to rule.

In this session we will be discussing from the 46th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, O descendant of Bhrigu, after performing funeral rituals for the dead body of Bhishma Deva, Maharaj Yudhishthira was momentarily overtaken with grief. So, Suta Goswami speaking this verse, he is addressing Shavunaka Rishi, the sages headed by Shavunaka Rishi. Shavunaka Rishi is addressed as Bhargava, descendant of Bhrigu Rishi or Bhrigu Muni. He is coming in the dynasty of Bhrigu Rishi, Bhrigu Vamsha. So, the description, the description is being given here that Yudhishthira, after Bhishma Deva passed away, he quit his body at the most auspicious moment, and more importantly, he quit his body in the presence of Lord Sri Krishna, and he quit his body while his mind was completely absorbed in thought, in remembrance of Krishna. So, perfect way of quitting the body, ideal for all the devotees, for everyone. So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, Bhishma Deva was not only a great family head of Maharaj Yudhishthira, but also he was a great philosopher and friend to him, his brothers and his mother, the entire Pandavas. Kunti is the name of a family, the five Pandavas brothers, and their mother Kunti, Kunti Devi. Since Maharaj Pandu, the father of the five brothers headed by Maharaj Yudhishthira, had died, Bhishma Deva was the most affectionate grandfather of the Pandavas, and caretaker of the widow daughter-in-law Kunti Devi. Bhishma Deva's granddaughter was the daughter of Lord Pundit, Krishna's daughter, Bhishmadeva's brother was Vichitravirya. Vichitravirya's son was Pandu. So, Bhishmadeva's daughter-in-law or Vichitravirya's daughter-in-law was Kuntidevi. In that way, it is being described here, Bhishmadeva's relationship with Kuntidevi. Although Maharaj Dhritarashtra, the elder uncle of Maharaj Yudhishthira, was there to look after them, his affection, that is Dhritarashtra's affection, was more on the side of his hundred sons, headed by Duryodhana. Dhritarashtra was very much unhappy about the fact that Dhritarashtra himself could not ascend the throne of the emperor of the entire world, the throne of Hastinapura, because Dhritarashtra was blind by birth. He was disqualified. And Pandu had taken charge of ruling the entire world as the emperor, and Pandu died. And Yudhishthira should have ascended the throne, but if Dhritarashtra is not dead, would that mean he shall not be a successor? Since Yudhishthira was not an adult, he was not quite eighteen years old. Therefore, after Pandu died, as long as Yudhishthira would yielded to the throne, a k added to it, a giver-king was needed. And that caretaker king Bhishma refused because Bhishma had taken a vow not to sit on the throne. He had given a promise to his father. Therefore Dhritarashtra was the caretaker king and Bhishma and Vidura were advising. They were advisors to Dhritarashtra and the entire royal household. But Dhritarashtra was not fair in his dealings, especially with the Pandavas. So Srila Prabhupada explained, ultimately a colossal clique was fabricated to deprive the five fatherless brothers of the rightful claim of the kingdom of Hastinapura. There was a great intrigue common in imperial palaces and the five brothers were exiled to wilderness. This is explained by Prabhupada in another place. What was this? This was a colossal clique that because these Pandavas were great devotees of Krishna and Dhritarashtra and Duryodhana were very materialistic. Naturally, Pandavas were put into many dangerous conditions. That is the policy of Dhritarashtra. How to kill the sons of Pandu? He wanted to kill the five sons of Pandu and usurp the kingdom for his own sons. He could not ascend the throne. So he felt that at least my sons should become the emperors. So he wanted to kill the five sons of Pandu just so that his son can become the emperor. So this was the policy from the very beginning. But the Pandavas, they were very obedient to Dhritarashtra because Dhritarashtra was their guardian, their uncle, elderly uncle, who had taken charge of protecting the five Pandavas and their mother Kunti in the absence of Pandu. So what did Dhritarashtra do? He constructed a house of shellac. Shellac means as soon as you touch one matchstick, immediately the whole palace will be on fire. So he asked his nephews and sisters-in-law Kunti that for some time you go. I constructed a very nice palace for you. You go and live there. Why you have to share this palace? So Vidura, he was very alert. He knew that Dhritarashtra and Duryodhana were plotting to kill the Pandavas. So Vidura informed Yudhishthira very secretly that Dhritarashtra wants to burn you to ashes when you go to that house. Be careful. And Duryodhana could understand that Vidura had already informed. Therefore Duryodhana was very anxious. He was very angry against Vidura. And these are all, this is the politics that was going on in the palace of Hastinapura during the time of the Pandavas and Kauravas. So although Yudhishthira knew that my uncle is sending me to this house to set it on fire, still he was very obedient. So he agreed to go. He didn't want to disobey the superior because Yudhishthira was always morally upright. So they dug a subway into that house and they escaped. They dug a subway within that house, a tunnel, and then they escaped. So this is the way they escaped that danger to their life.

---
### devotion, protection, cheating
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Hw1wtbon5j0?t=611
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But Draupadi took shelter of Krishna and she was protected."
**Summary:** The speaker continues narrating the hardships faced by the Pandavas, including attempts on Bhima's life, encounters with demons, the disrobing of Draupadi, and the rigged gambling match that led to their exile.

The five Pandavas along with Kunti. Then another time Bhima was fed a poisoned cake. And again it's described in detail in the Mahabharata. Bhima was also saved. Then they had to meet one man-eater, rakshasa demon, Hidimba. So Bhima fought with him and killed him. And then in the assembly of ruffians, asatsabhaya, they attempted to disrobe Draupadi. But Draupadi took shelter of Krishna and she was protected. Then that is also described in detail in the Mahabharata. And then in a gambling match, which was completely rigged, the Pandavas were totally cheated. And when they lost the match, the terms were that whoever loses has to go and stay in the forest in exile for 13 years, out of which the last year, 13th year, they have to live incognito. They have to hide themselves. So in this way, the Pandavas, they were cheated and their life was in danger. Even when they were living in exile, Duryodhana attempted to attack the Pandavas and kill them. But the Pandavas fought back. The Pandavas fought back. And therefore the description is that they could not ultimately kill the Pandavas, though they tried. And in the battle of Kurukshetra, because Krishna was on the side of the Pandavas, the Pandavas could not be defeated by the Kauravas, even though the Kauravas were very powerful. Because Krishna was on the side of the Pandavas.

### devotion, divine grace, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Hw1wtbon5j0?t=784
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is always on the side of his devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing Krishna's support for the Pandavas due to their devotion, highlighting Bhishma's knowledge of their eventual victory, and explaining Yudhishthira's grief at Bhishma's passing despite knowing his spiritual destination.

So it is described here, even though Bhishma was fighting on the side of the Kauravas, because he was obliged to the caretaker, Dhritarashtra, but still he was a sincerely sympathetic, well-wisher, grandfather, friend and philosopher to Maharaja Yudhishtira. Even up to the last moment of his life, he died very happily seeing Maharaja Yudhishtira ascend to the throne. Otherwise, he would have died long, long back. Instead of suffering agony over the undue suffering of the Pandavas, he would have quit his body long back. But he was waiting for the opportune moment because he knew that the sons of Pandavas would come out victorious. He knew this in the battle of Kurukshetra. And he knew that because Krishna was on the side of the Pandavas. Now why did Krishna take the side of the Pandavas? Krishna is always on the side of his devotees. And who can be a devotee of Krishna? Anyone, everyone, everyone can become a devotee of Krishna. But these demoniac minded people, they refuse to become devotees of Krishna. And as a result of that, they keep on suffering. And Bhishma knew Yudhishtira cannot be ultimately vanquished because he is a pure devotee of Krishna. Therefore, Yudhishtira also, he had respect for Bhishma Deva. And he felt great separation when Bhishma Deva quit his body. And therefore, momentarily he was feeling grief on account of the death of Bhishma. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.9.47 | 18-05-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### knowledge, realization, application
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2NJ6K-6aPtQ?t=48
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Application of the knowledge, that is called realization."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the difference between possessing knowledge and realizing it through practical application, highlighting the difficulty of applying knowledge and achieving realization.

In this session, we will be discussing the 47th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is: All the great sages then glorified Lord Sri Krishna, who was present there by confidential Vedic hymns. Then all of them returned to the Lord and returned to their respective hermitages, bearing always Lord Krishna within their hearts. The description was being given earlier in this chapter that at the time of Bhishma preparing to quit his body, great sages from all over the universe, from all the upper planetary systems, assembled at the site where Bhishma had made preparations by lying on a bed of arrows. They all heard Bhishma instruct Yudhishthira in the presence of Krishna. Therefore, after Bhishma's passing away, which they witnessed, how perfectly Bhishma quit his body, that was a lesson for everyone, even all the great sages, because even though the great sages know philosophically in terms of knowledge, they have knowledge of how to quit the body, but in reality, when one has to put that knowledge into practice, that is a different thing altogether. Application of the knowledge, that is called realization. That realization is not very easy.

---
### mercy, submission, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2NJ6K-6aPtQ?t=163
**Verse References:** BG 8.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is the perfection of life, that one is able to constantly think of Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of mercy from liberated souls and the Supreme Lord for applying knowledge, achieved through submission, service, and humility, ultimately leading to Krishna consciousness and a prepared state for death.

Of course, the knowledge also is quite difficult to understand, but even if the knowledge is quite difficult to understand, even if somebody somehow acquires that knowledge and gets an understanding, to apply that knowledge, that is only possible by blessings, mercy of great liberated personalities, of great souls, great devotees, great tapasvis, of the Supreme Lord Himself. The mercy is required, and that mercy one has to actually get by submission, by service, by humility. So here the description is given that all the great sages glorified Lord Sri Krishna, who was present there by confidential Vedic hymns. Then all of them returned to their respective hermitages, bearing always Lord Krishna within their hearts. This is the description of this verse. So Srila Prabhupada explains, the devotees of the Lord are always in the heart of the Lord, and the Lord is always in the hearts of the devotees. It is described in this verse that the sages who had assembled, they went back to their home, always remembering, always bearing Lord Krishna within their hearts. This is the perfection of life, that one is able to constantly think of Krishna, or one is able to constantly remember Krishna, one is able to constantly chant the holy name of the Supreme Lord Krishna. That way they can remain Krishna conscious. Whenever death comes, they are ready to quit the body. That is the practice actually, which Krishna Himself recommends in the Bhagavad Gita.

---
### devotee, reciprocity, love
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2NJ6K-6aPtQ?t=288
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "My devotees do not know anything else but me, and I do not know anyone else but them."
**Summary:** This segment explains the reciprocal relationship between the Lord and his pure devotees, where the Lord resides in the devotee's heart and vice versa, emphasizing the Lord's constant attention to the welfare of his devotees despite his self-sufficiency.

So in the Bhagavatam, this is described: sadhavo hrudayam mahyam sadhunam hrudayam tu aham, maranya tena jananti naaham tebhyo managapi. This means the pure devotee is always within the core of my heart. This is a statement spoken by Lord Vishnu to Durvasamuni. So Vishnu is telling, Supreme Lord Vishnu is telling, the pure devotee is always within the core of my heart, and I am always in the heart of my pure devotee. See, it is reciprocal. Vishnu or Krishna carries always the memory of the pure devotee within his heart, and a pure devotee always carries Vishnu or Krishna within his heart, always, always. So in this way, Vishnu is telling, my devotees do not know anything else but me, and I do not know anyone else but them. See, this is the sweet relationship between the Lord and his devotees. Prabhupada said this is a sweet relationship due to unalloyed love and devotion for the Lord. The devotees always see the Lord within themselves, and although the Lord has nothing to do and nothing to aspire for, he is perfect and complete in himself, still he is always busy in attending to the welfare of his devotees. So for the ordinary living people, for the ordinary living beings, the laws of nature are there for all actions and reactions.

---
### protection, devotional service, grace
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2NJ6K-6aPtQ?t=401
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That is a special protection personally given by Krishna to his devotees who are on the path of pure devotional service."
**Summary:** This segment describes the special protection Krishna offers to devotees on the path of pure devotional service, guiding them back on track if they stray, a protection not extended to those practicing other yogic paths.

But the Lord is always anxious to put his devotees on the right path. If somebody is taken to pure devotional service, then in the stage of practice of pure devotional service, sometimes devotees may slip away from the actual path. Then Krishna is always watching, guarding, protecting, so he will put back the devotee who is on the pure devotional path back on track. That is a special protection personally given by Krishna to his devotees who are on the path of pure devotional service. This particular protection is only for those who are on the path of pure devotional service. It is not there for those practicing the process of Jnana, process of Ashtanga Yoga, process of Karma Yoga. For them, this specific protection is not there, personally by the Lord. That protection is not there.

---
### devotee, pastimes, perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2NJ6K-6aPtQ?t=488
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A sage reaches perfection when he becomes a devotee of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how the Lord voluntarily places himself under the care of his devotees through his pastimes, emphasizing that true perfection for a sage is achieved through devotion to the Lord, implying the incompleteness of impersonalistic approaches.

So Prabhupada says, the devotees, meaning the pure devotees or those on the path of pure devotional service, therefore are under the direct care of the Lord, and the Lord also voluntarily puts himself under the care of his devotees only. This is the pastime of the Lord. This is the Leela, Krishna Leela, Vishnu Leela. What is that? The Lord appears in this world as a son of his pure devotee, or he appears as an associate of another pure devotee. So that way also, the Lord, even though he is completely independent, still he puts himself under the care of his devotees. So all the sages headed by Vyasadeva were devotees of the Lord. This is very important. A sage reaches perfection when he becomes a devotee of the Lord. So by this statement, we understand those who are impersonalist sages, they are not exactly perfect in their spiritual life. They have not yet reached perfection of the spiritual life.

---
### Vedas, Krishna, glorification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2NJ6K-6aPtQ?t=613
**Verse References:** BG 15.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All the Vedic literature is meant for understanding me."
**Summary:** This segment explains that all Vedic hymns are chanted to please Lord Krishna, with all Vedic literature ultimately aimed at understanding and glorifying Him, as confirmed in the Bhagavad Gita and Chaitanya Charitamrita.

Therefore, they chanted the Vedic hymns after the funeral ceremony just to please the Lord, who is present there personally. Now, why did they chant the Vedic hymns? So Prabhupada explains, all the Vedic hymns are chanted to please Lord Krishna. All the Vedas, this is confirmed in the Bhagavad Gita, in the 15th chapter, 15th verse. Krishna says, what is the meaning of this? All the Vedic literature is meant for understanding me. Who is telling this? Krishna. So all the Vedic literature is meant for understanding Krishna, knowing Krishna. And Prabhupada also says, all the Vedas, Upanishads, Vedanta, are seeking Krishna only, and all Vedas and all hymns, Vedic hymns are for glorifying Krishna only. In the Chaitanya Charita Amrita, this is explained: VEDE RAMAYANE CHAIVA, PURANE BHARATE TATHA, ADHAV ANTE CHA MADHYA CHA, HARI SARVATRA GIYATE. The meaning of this verse is in the Vedic literature, including the Ramayana, Puranas, and Mahabharata, from the very beginning, ADHAV, to the end, ANTE CHA, as well as in the middle, MADHYA CHA, only HARI, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, is explained. HARI SARVATRA GIYATE, only He is glorified, only He is explained.

---
### rituals, Vedas, departure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2NJ6K-6aPtQ?t=720
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhishma quitted his body in perfect Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** This segment describes how the sages performed activities suitable for the occasion of Bhishma's departure, participating in funeral rituals and glorifying Krishna by reciting Vedic hymns, drawing from different Vedas, Puranas, and Itihasas, all aimed at glorifying Krishna.

Therefore, the sages performed the exact activities suitable for the purpose of that occasion. Bhishma quitted his body in perfect Krishna consciousness. The sages who were assembled there, they participated in that funeral rituals, funeral ceremony of Bhishma Deva, great personality, and after that, before leaving for their own hermitages, they glorified Krishna. And how did they glorify Krishna? By reciting the Vedic hymns. Different sages are experts in different portions of the Vedas, and that's how Vyasa Deva, he divided the one Veda into four, and he entrusted each division of the Veda to different sages who are all his disciples and followers. So whoever was expert in Rig Veda, they chanted the Vedic Rig Veda hymns. Somebody else was expert in Yajur Veda, they chanted the Yajur Veda hymns. Like this, these sages chanted the Vedic hymns to glorify Krishna, who was personally present when Bhishma Deva was about to quit his body. That was Krishna's mercy on Bhishma Deva. So the sages, they were all chanting from their Vedic, the Vedic hymns from the different Vedas, and the Puranas and Itihasas. There are 18 Mahapuranas, 18 Upapuranas, and there are also the Itihasas, especially the Ramayana and Mahabharata. All these are meant for glorification of Krishna. They are called the 5th Veda, Panchama Veda Ucyate, Itihasa Purananam, Panchama Veda Ucyate, the statement like this in the Chandogya Upanishad, as well as in the Bhagavatam, is described, the 5th Veda. So even the experts in the Puranas and Itihasas, they also chanted verses from the Puranas and Itihasas, glorifying Krishna. So that is the way they happily departed for their respective hermitages after glorifying Krishna and witnessing the greatest personality of their time, Bhishma Deva, quit his body in perfection, thinking of Krishna, in Krishna Consciousness. I will stop here.

### Bhagavatam, Kurukshetra, Krishna's Supremacy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I_kwD4O4Mzs?t=50
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.48
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "whosoever side Krishna is, that side ultimately is going to win."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam describing Yudhishthira's visit to Dhritarashtra and Gandhari after the Kurukshetra war, emphasizing Krishna's presence guarantees victory and Dhritarashtra's materialistic miscalculations.

In this session, we will be discussing the 48th verse of the 9th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. We will come on almost to the end of this chapter, describing the passing away of Bhishma Deva in the presence of Lord Krishna, tato yudhishthira gatva sahakrishno gajafayam pitaram santvayam asa gandhareemcha tapasvinim. The meaning of this verse is thereafter Maharaj Yudhishthira at once went to his capital Hastinapura accompanied by Lord Shri Krishna, and there he consoled his uncle and aunt Gandhari, uncle Dhritarashtra and aunt Gandhari who was an ascetic, Gandhareemcha tapasvinim, tapasvi she was an ascetic. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, Dhritarashtra and Gandhari, the father and the mother of Duryodhana and his brothers, were the elder uncle and aunt of Maharaj Yudhishthira. After the battle of Kurukshetra, the celebrated couple, having lost all their sons and grandsons, were under the care of Maharaj Yudhishthira. What happened in the battle of Kurukshetra? All the 100 sons of Dhritarashtra and Gandhari, they were killed in the battle, and even the grandsons. Everyone was killed from Dhritarashtra's side, the Kaurava side. Dhritarashtra was very, very confident that his sons would win the battle because they had the best support, but Dhritarashtra, being materialistic, he made a miscalculation, he did not stop the war. So, consider Krishna, even though he was not fighting, actually, he had not taken up any weapons in the battle, he was on the side of the Pandavas. Yes, Jayastu Pandu Putranam, Esham Pakshe Janardana, whosoever side Krishna is, that side ultimately is going to win.

---
### Krishna, Devotion, Victory
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I_kwD4O4Mzs?t=185
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Wherever there is Krishna and wherever there is Arjuna, his pure devotee, there, there is certainly victory."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Sanjaya's words from the Bhagavad Gita, emphasizing that victory is assured wherever Krishna and his devotee Arjuna are present, highlighting the futility of material calculations.

Even Sanjaya, at the end of the Bhagavad Gita, while narrating what is happening on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, before the battle is about to begin, Sanjaya says, at the end of the Bhagavad Gita, 45 minutes, that's all, Krishna spoke Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna. So, Sanjaya concludes, Yatra Yogeshwara Krishna, Yatra Partho Dhanurdhara, Tatra Shri Vijayo, Bhutir Dhruva, Neetir Matir Mama. Wherever there is Krishna and wherever there is Arjuna, his pure devotee, there, there is certainly victory. So, even before the battle began, Sanjaya predicted and announced to Dhritarashtra that don't expect victory for the Kauravas because Krishna and Arjuna, Krishna and his pure devotee, of course, all the Pandavas are pure devotees, but still, wherever there is Krishna and Arjuna, there, there is victory. Pandavas. Pandavas are guaranteed to win. So, all of the material calculations, ultimately, are a failure.

### Free Will, Krishna's Plan, Humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I_kwD4O4Mzs?t=278
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that everything happens according to Krishna's plan, even while respecting free will, and emphasizes the insignificance of living entities compared to the Supreme Lord, cautioning against the foolishness of trying to compete with God.

Everything actually happens even in the material world, according to the plan of the Supreme Lord, according to the will of Govinda. That's how everything actually is happening, is going on. Though Krishna never takes away the free will of the living entities, he never takes away the free will, but the living entities are very, very, very, very, very tiny. They are insignificant parts of the Supreme Lord, who is unlimitedly powerful. And we have some power, some strength, some knowledge, some ability. We are very, very, very, very, very, very tiny, insignificant, atomic, we are atomic, and Krishna is the complete whole, the total manifestation of the material, spiritual, everything. So, there is no comparison. It is foolish to think that... To think that, I can become God, I can compete with God, Krishna. That is utterly foolish, utterly foolish.

now regarding Gandhari there's a particular mention here Gandhari was a powerful and powerful woman who was a powerful woman who was a powerful woman who was a powerful woman who was a powerful woman who was an ascetic although she was living the life of a faithful wife and a kind mother Gandhari she was not like Dhritarashtra materialistic she was very very very advanced spiritually the pravapada says she was on the level of Bhishma as regards Brahmapurias spiritual attainment because she was a very faithful wife of her husband Dhritarashtra it is said that Gandhari also voluntarily closed her eyes because of the blindness of her husband when she came to know that I am going to get married to a blind King before her marriage she voluntarily agreed to remain blind whole life voluntarily her eyesight was perfect but she voluntarily agreed to close her eyes whole life because of the blindness of her husband a wife's duty is to follow the husband cent percent and Gandhari was so true to her husband that she followed him even his perpetual blindness in the entire life she followed him you know therefore interaction she was a great ascetic tapas win increased Tapaswini described here particularly well described here besides this austere T here undertaken the shock she suffered because the wholesale killing of her 100 sons and grandsons also a searchance a whole jam same aggressive really to stop iPads beatings to your mother so she couldn't certainly too much for a woman. ### Yudhishthira, Dharma, Krishna's Approval
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/u1zrcRLfM8M?t=63
**Verse References:** SB 1.9.49
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Yudhishthira assumed responsibility of ruling the entire world exactly in accordance with the codes and principles given in the shastras approved by Dhritarashtra, his uncle and by Lord Sri Krishna, the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of the verse, focusing on Yudhishthira's assumption of royal power according to dharma, approved by elders and Krishna.

Pitrachanumato Raja Vasudevanumoditaha Chakararajyam Dharmena Pitrpaitamaham Vibhuhu. The meaning of this verse is, after this, the great religious king Maharaj Yudhishthira executed the royal power in the kingdom strictly according to the codes and royal principles approved by his uncle and confirmed by Lord Sri Krishna. So, this is the ending of this chapter. What did Yudhishthira do after Bhishma Deva passed away? He performed a funeral ceremony and the sages glorified Krishna and went back to their respective hermitages. Then Krishna and Yudhishthira immediately returned to the palace of Hastinapura to pacify Dhritarashtra and Gandhari. And after that, Yudhishthira assumed responsibility of ruling the entire world exactly in accordance with the codes and principles given in the shastras approved by Dhritarashtra, his uncle and by Lord Sri Krishna, the Supreme Lord.

---

He has got responsibility towards the citizens who are under his care. Citizens are under his care. So, Prabhupada explains the Vedic conception of what is the duty of a king. The king is to see the welfare of the citizens from all angles of socio, political, economic, and spiritual upliftment. ### Rajadharma, Liberation, Responsibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/u1zrcRLfM8M?t=207
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The responsibility is there for the king towards his citizens to ensure the upliftment of the citizens, ultimately the citizens have to become attraction liberated by practicing spiritual life becoming responsible to understand the great opportunity as a human being."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Rajadharma, the duties of a king, emphasizing that a king should not merely be a tax collector but should guide citizens towards liberation from material suffering.

Rajadharma which describes the duties of the Raja or the king, what are the duties of a king, so Prabhupada says Rajadharma is a great science elsewhere he describes this unlike modern diplomacy for political supremacy being a king or an emperor is actually based on following the science of Rajadharma as given in the Dharma Shastras. The kings were trained systematically to become munificent and not merely be tax collectors, not that I am the king and I enjoy power, wealth, and a nice army and then simply Lord over everyone else and try to you know suppress any rebellion, quell any uprising, so this is not the way to rule the kingdom. The responsibility is there for the king towards his citizens to ensure the upliftment of the citizens, ultimately the citizens have to become attraction liberated by practicing spiritual life becoming responsible to understand the great opportunity as a human being. This is the responsibility. The kings were trained to perform different sacrifices only for the prosperity of the subjects, only for the prosperity of the citizens. The king was to lead the prajas to the attainment of salvation, that was the great duty of the king. So, Prabhupada says the father, the spiritual master, and the king are all natural leaders of their respective dependents and they are not to become irresponsible in the matter of leading their subjects to the path of ultimate liberation from birth, death, disease, and old age. This is the responsibility whether somebody is a father or somebody is a spiritual master or somebody is a king. Father has got responsibility towards his children and the spiritual master has got responsibility towards his disciples and the king has got responsibility towards the citizens you whom is ruling so they have to liberate ultimately their dependents from birth, death, diseases, and old age.

They don't do the real duties of an administrator or a king or a government leader or government head. They don't perform their duties now.

### Vedic Civilization, Dharma Shastras, Guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/u1zrcRLfM8M?t=520
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The qualified brahmanas are meant to give direction to the kings for proper administration in terms of the scriptures like Manu Samhita and the dharma shastras of Parashara."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the role of qualified brahmanas in guiding kings according to Dharma Shastras like Manu Samhita and Parashara Smriti, emphasizing the importance of pious and religious leadership.

In the Vedic times or the Vedic culture, Vedic civilization, the qualified brahmanas are meant to give direction to the kings for proper administration in terms of the scriptures like Manu Samhita and the dharma shastras of Parashara, Parashara Smriti. They describe exactly how the king should rule and the king is given instructions and directions according to the codes given in the Manu Samhita and other dharma shastras. So the brahmanas are well-versed in the dharma shastra, they are well-versed in these scriptures and they give correct advice, correct guidance to the kings. If the king is pious, religious, chivalrous and munificent the citizens generally will follow him, it's natural. Such a king is never a lazy sensuous person living at the cost of the subjects but he is always alert to kill three or four people at a time, a person is always alert to kill thieves and decoys in the kingdom to protect the weaker sections of the society. The kings, the pious kings in the former days were not merciful to decoys and thieves in the name of nonsensical ahimsa.

---
### Punishment, Justice, Social Order
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/u1zrcRLfM8M?t=604
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The king would punish those who would steal or take somebody else's wealth in such a way that if somebody was caught stealing, their fingers would be cut off."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of punishing thieves and decoys, contrasting it with modern-day non-violence policies, and gives an example of strict justice in Kashmir where thieves had their fingers cut off.

A king should never adopt foolishly the policy of non-violence. It's like modern-day politicians they say non-violence, no, they should remain silent, never be non-violent when it comes to punishing thieves and decoys. They should never show non-violence principles. Now, the thieves and decoys were punished in an exemplary way so that in future no one would dare commit such nuisance in an organized form. That was the way. Prabhupada says even about 150 years ago in Kashmir, the ruling king was so very strict in punishing the thieves that if somebody dropped, let's say, a gold coin on the way, that gold coin would lie on the street till the actual owner who has lost it comes and picks it up. Nobody else would dare touch that because it's not theirs. The king would punish those who would steal or take somebody else's wealth in such a way that if somebody was caught stealing, their fingers would be cut off. So, this is the way to rule that there will be no nuisance of these thieves, rogues, plunderers, and anti-socials.

---
### King's Duty, Liberation, Bhagavad Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/u1zrcRLfM8M?t=697
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The king must know that the human form of life is meant for liberating the trapped soul from the bondage of material conditions."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates the king's duty to help citizens attain liberation, highlighting Yudhishthira's adherence to these principles and Krishna's role in providing guidance through the Bhagavad Gita.

Nobody dares to do any crime. That is the way to rule. So, Prabhupada says in the purport, the king must know that the human form of life is meant for liberating the trapped soul from the bondage of material conditions. Therefore, the king's duty is to see that the citizens are properly looked after to attain the highest stage of perfection. The king has got a goal, he's got a target, how the citizens can get out of this material bondage. King Yudhishthira followed these principles strictly as will be explained in the next chapter. Not only did he follow the principles but he got approval also from his old uncle who was experienced in political affairs and it was also confirmed by Lord Shri Krishna, the speaker of the philosophy of Bhagavad Gita. See, Krishna spoke Bhagavad Gita first to Sun God because they are to rule in accordance with the teachings of Bhagavad Gita. That's why Krishna spoke to Sun God. Later he spoke to Arjuna, was also a Kshatriya, rulers. Maharaja Yudhishthira is the ideal monarch and monarchy under a trained king like Maharaja Yudhishthira is by far the most superior form of government.

---
### Monarchy, Democracy, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/u1zrcRLfM8M?t=803
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A trained, shatria who can rule according to the codes of Dharmashastra is far far superior than an elected government by the people who do not have the qualification to select a proper government leader."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts monarchy with modern democracy, arguing that a trained Kshatriya king following Dharma Shastras is superior to elected governments due to the unqualified nature of voters in Kali Yuga.

Superior to modern republics or governments of the people by the people. Nowadays people are very proud that we have got what is called as democracy. But in democracy, the defect is that the mass of people who elect their leaders or their rulers, they are especially in the Jivkali, they are basically low born, they are ill trained, they are unfortunate and badly associated. This is described in the Bhagavatam. In Kali Yuga people are disqualified in so many ways. So, they themselves do not know the highest perfectional aim of life therefore votes cast by them actually have no value. And persons elected by such illiterate people, irresponsible votes cannot be people elected by such irresponsible votes cannot be responsible representatives like Maharaja Yudhishthira. So, the conclusion is a trained, shatria who can rule according to the codes of Dharmashastra is far far superior than an elected government by the people who do not have the qualification to select a proper government leader. So, I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the fifth verse of the ninth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, describing the assembly of great souls who came to see Bhishma on his deathbed at Kurukshetra.

In this session we will be discussing from the fifth verse of the ninth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavata. Tatra Brahmarsaya Sarve Devar Shayascha Sattama Rajar Shayascha Tatrasan Drashtum Bharata Pungavam. The meaning of this verse is just to see the chief of the descendants of King Bharata. All the great souls in the universe, namely the rishis amongst the demigods, brahmanas and kings all situated in the quality of goodness were assembled there. So the background is that the Pandavas headed by Yudhishthira along with Krishna have gone to the scene of the battle of Kurukshetra and there they have gone to see Bhishma who is lying on a bed of arrows and not only the Pandavas but even all the great souls in the universe. So there was interplanetary travel in those days and great souls like rishis, all varieties of rishis Devarishi, rishi among the demigods, Brahmarshi rishi among the brahmanas, Rajarshi Rishi among the kings, all of them who are situated in the quality of goodness, Sattva guna, they came to see Bhishma because Bhishma was such a great personality. So in the Purportsila Prabhupada he came to see Bhishma because Bhishma was such a great personality.

Yogis can travel through the Ganga river. Let us say they are in Haridwar and they want to come to Prayag. So, they take a dip in the Ganga in Haridwar and they come out of the Ganga in Prayag. So, this is a Siddhi, a perfection. And those who are very strict Ashtanga Yogis, when they perfect their Ashtanga Yoga meditation, they are able to they are able to actually utilize the Siddhis for their travel. So, one of the Siddhis is they travel through the Ganga river. They travel through the Ganga river. And so, they travel through a ray of sun. But because of his being very much devoted to following religious principles, he was able to actually come out successful, even though he was facing a lot of difficulties. The material world is declared to be a place filled with so many difficulties. That's the nature of this material world. So, by spending time hearing from saintly persons about great personalities of the past, how they led their life in an ideal way, following the religious principles, taking association from saintly persons, and not losing their focus on the ultimate goal in spite of difficult circumstances. Parvato Narado Dhaumyo Bhagavan Badarayanaha Brihadashvo Bharadvajaha Sashishyo Renukasutaha Vasishta Indra Pramadaha Trito Gritsamado Asitaha Kakshivan Gautamo Atrishcha Kaushiko Atha Sudarshanaha. ### Narada Muni, disciplic succession, Madhva Sampradaya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ORIHp5uJvWI?t=136
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And thus, the Madhva Sampradaya, in which the Gaudiya Sampradaya is also included, has spread all over the universe."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the biography of Narada Muni, highlighting his previous life, his role as a Deva Rishi, his initiation by Brahma, and his initiation of prominent devotees like Prahlad Maharaj, Dhruva Maharaj, and Vyasa Deva, emphasizing his importance in the Madhva Sampradaya.

Narada Muni. Narada Muni is inevitably associated with the narrations of the Puranas. He is described in the Bhagavatam. In his previous life, he was the son of a maidservant. But by good association with pure devotees, he became enlightened in devotional service. And in the next life, he became a perfect man, comparable with himself only. In the Mahabharata, his name is mentioned in many places. He is the principal Deva Rishi, or the chief sage, amongst the demigods. He is the son and disciple of Brahma ji. And from him, the disciplic succession in the line of Brahma has been spread. He initiated Prahlad Maharaj, Dhruva Maharaj, and many celebrated devotees of the Lord. He initiated even Vyasa Deva, the author of Vedic literatures. He initiated Vyasa Deva, the author of Vedic literatures. And from Vyasa Deva, Madhvacharya was initiated. And thus, the Madhva Sampradaya, in which the Gaudiya Sampradaya is also included, has spread all over the universe.

---
### parampara, preaching, Narada Muni
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ORIHp5uJvWI?t=221
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, Srila Prabhupada explains that this parampara understanding is required in all places in the material creation."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of parampara (disciplic succession) even in higher planets, the preaching activities of pure devotees throughout the material world, and the lineage of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu within the Madhva Sampradaya, highlighting Narada's instructions to various kings and devotees.

So, Srila Prabhupada explains that this parampara understanding is required in all places in the material creation. Even in the upper planets, though they are much more intelligent, very sober, in the quality of goodness still, they also require to hear from the authoritative sources and actually gather proper knowledge. The preaching is going on not only on this earth planet, but in other planets also. So, pure devotees travel in the material world, not only in this world of ours, this earth planet, but even to higher planets. And for them, there is no restriction because they are liberated space persons. Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu belonged to this Madhva Sampradaya. Therefore, Brahmaji, Narada, Vyasa, down to Madhva, Chaitanya and the Goswamis, the six Goswamis of Vrindavan, all belong to the same line of disciplic succession. Naradji has instructed many kings from time immemorial. In the Bhagavatam, we can see that he instructed Prahlad Maharaj while he was in the womb of his mother. He instructed Vasudeva, Lord Krishna, as well as Maharaj Yudhishthira. So, this is a brief summary of Narada's life. Next is Dhaumya.

Sages are always guiding all categories of people particularly the Pandavas when they were in exile they required a lot of guidance so they were accompanied by great sages and one of them is this Dhaumya Rishi who actually used to clear their confusion he instructed them how to live incognito for one year and his instructions were strictly followed by the Pandavas during that time the Pandavas were sent away to exile in the forest after the Kauravas challenged them to a gambling match and cheated the Pandavas in such a match and...

### Dhaumya Rishi, Pandavas, Mahabharata
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ORIHp5uJvWI?t=433
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker continues the discussion on Dhaumya Rishi, explaining how he helped the Pandavas live incognito during their exile, his presence at the funeral ceremony after the battle of Kurukshetra, and his religious instructions to Maharaj Yudhishtira, emphasizing his role as a guide for householders on the path of religion.

And therefore the Pandavas had to be defeated and they went away to the forest 13 years. Now 12 years they lived normally, but 13th year they were supposed to hide and nobody should discover them that here are the Pandavas. So the this this Pandavas when they completed 12 years of exile in the forest then they were supposed to live one year incognito without being recognized by anybody so they took help of their Rishi was accompanying them Dhaumya Rishi. Dhaumya Rishi gave Pandavas instructions how to live one year incognito and they were successful in living so. Dhaumya Rishi's name is also mentioned when the general funeral ceremony was performed after the battle of Kurukshetra. So, you can imagine after the battle of Kurukshetra there were so many millions of people who are dead. So, there was a funeral ceremony performed for all the departed souls. In the Anushasana Parva of Mahabharata, Dhaumya Rishi gave religious instructions very elaborately to Maharaj Yudhishtira. The main speaker in the Mahabharata giving instructions to Yudhishtira after the battle of Kurukshetra is Bhishma. But others also used to speak. So, Dhaumya Rishi gave instructions to Yudhishtira. Dhaumya Rishi was actually the right type of priest of a householder for he could guide the Pandavas on the right path of religion.

Satyavati Sutta, Parashariya, Parasharatmaja, Badarayana, Vyasadeva, Vedavyasa, etc. He was the son of Mahamuni Parasharatha. Parashara in the womb of Satyavati prior to her betrothal with Maharaj Shantanu, the father of the great general Bhishma. He is a powerful incarnation of Narayana. Vyasadeva or Vedavyasa is a powerful incarnation of Narayana. And he broadcasts the Vedic wisdom to the world. So only because of Vyasadeva compiling the Vedas, transmitting the knowledge of the Vedas, that we are able to understand the Vedic knowledge. As such, Vyasadeva is offered respects before one chants the Vedic literature, especially the Puranas.

### Vyasadeva, Mahabharata, penance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ORIHp5uJvWI?t=784
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Amongst sages, he is the most respected author of by dint of severe penances."
**Summary:** The speaker details Vyasadeva's authorship of the Brahma Sutras, Mahabharata, and Srimad Bhagavatam, emphasizing his performance of severe penances and the collaborative effort with Ganesha in compiling the Mahabharata.

The Brahma Sutras or the Vedanta Sutras or Badarayana Sutras were compiled by Vyasadeva. Amongst sages, he is the most respected author of by dint of severe penances. Vyasadeva performed a lot of penances. Penance means voluntarily accepting some inconvenience for the body for the sake of spiritual progress. When Vyasadeva wanted to record the great epic Mahabharata for the welfare of all people in the age of Kali, he was feeling the necessity of a powerful writer who could take up his dictation. By the order of Brahmaji, Sri Ganeshji took up the charge of noting down the dictation on the condition that Vyasadeva would not stop dictation for a moment. The Mahabharata was thus compiled by the joint endeavor of Vyasadeva and Ganesha.

By the order of his mother, Satyavati, who was later married to Maharaj Shantanu, and by the request of Bhishma Deva, Vyasadeva begot three brilliant sons whose names are Dhrishtarashtra, Pandu and Vidura. The Mahabharata was compiled by Vyasadeva after the Battle of Kurukshetra and after the death of all the heroes of Mahabharata. **Summary:** The speaker discusses how the Supreme Lord incarnates to revive dharma, which is lost over time due to the deteriorating influence of time in the material world.

By great sages, by narrating historical incidents in connection with the Supreme Lord, His incarnations, and His pure devotees. Now, whenever the Supreme Lord incarnates, He revives dharma, or the real religious principles, which are generally lost over a period of time. In this material world, time has got a deteriorating influence on everything. So, let us say the Supreme Lord taught religious principles in His incarnation as Ramachandra.

### spiritual knowledge, inquiry, guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZZmJKwfy_6w?t=511
**Verse References:** BG 4.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "By making relevant inquiries, one should actually learn the eternal religious principles from the spiritual master."
**Summary:** This segment outlines various subjects Shukadeva Goswami learned from his father, emphasizing the importance of inquiry and learning from a spiritual master, referencing Krishna's words in the Bhagavad Gita about approaching a guru.

The ways and means of attaining spiritual realization, the four ashramas, namely the student life, the householder's life, the retired life, and the renounced life, brahmacharya, yajna, vanaprastha, sannyasa, the sublime position of the Supreme Personality of God, the process of seeing the Supreme Lord eye to eye, the bona fide candidate for receiving knowledge, the consideration of the five elements, the unique position of intelligence, the consciousness of the material nature and the living entity, the symptoms of the self-realized soul, the working principles of the material body, the symptoms of the influential modes of nature, the tree of perpetual desire and psychic activities. Prabhupada is giving various subjects, important subjects, that Shukadeva Goswami learnt from his great father by inquiring from him. In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says, 'tan viddhi pranipatena pariprasnena sevayat pariprasnena.' By making relevant inquiries, one should actually learn the eternal religious principles from the spiritual master.

---
### Shukadeva Goswami, creation, Prajapatis
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZZmJKwfy_6w?t=625
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So it's only by being empowered specifically to generate a specific species that the Prajapatis can give birth, can generate different species of living beings."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Shukadeva Goswami's travels and then discusses Kashyapa Muni, one of the Prajapatis, explaining how the Prajapatis are empowered to create different species at the beginning of creation.

Shukadeva Goswami went to the sun planet with the permission of his father and Narayanji. Descriptions of his travel and space are given in the Shanti Parva of the Mahabharata. Mahabharata is generally understood as containing the narration of the Pandavas and Kauravas, but it contains much much more than that. It is the history of Creator India, Mahabharata. At last, Shukadeva Goswami attained the transcendental realm. He is known by different names, like Araneya, Aranesuta, Vyasaki, and Asatmaja. These are some of the other names of Shukadeva Goswami. Next, Srila Prabhupada describes Kashyapa Muni, one of the Prajapati, the son of Marichi, and one of the sons-in-law of Prajapati Daksha. Kashyapa Muni married many daughters of Daksha. Kashyapa Muni is the father of the gigantic bird Garuda, who was given elephants and tortoise as eatables. Garuda is the son of Kashyapa Muni. Now, how is it possible that a bird can be the son of a Rishi? Kashyapa Muni. This is the power of the Prajapatis at the time of the beginning of the creation, at the time of the beginning of the creation of this Universe. Brahma takes birth from Vishnu, Garbhodakshayi Vishnu, and Brahma is empowered to create the Prajapatis. So when the Prajapatis have to further increase the population in different species, they are empowered to generate the different, the different species, by different powers which are given to them. So it's only by being empowered specifically to generate a specific species that the Prajapatis can give birth, can generate different species of living beings.

---
### Kashyapa Muni, Arjuna, Nara-Narayana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZZmJKwfy_6w?t=800
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Human life is meant for practicing austerities for spiritual perfection."
**Summary:** The speaker continues discussing Kashyapa Muni, his wives and children, and then transitions to Arjuna's extraordinary birth as the twin brother of Nara-Narayana Rishi, emphasizing their example of performing austerities for spiritual perfection.

Kashyapa Muni married thirteen daughters of Prajapati Daksha, and their names are given in the Bhagavad Gita: Aditi, Diti, Dhanu, Kashtha, Arishta, Surasa, Ila, Muni, Prothashwa, Tamra, Surabhi, Sarama and Kini. He begot many children, both demigods and demons by those wives. Generally the sons of Aditi are called Adityas and the sons of Diti are called Daityas. Daityas are generally demons and Adityas are demigods. From his first wife Aditi all the twelve Adityas were born and one of the Adityas is Vamana, the incarnation of God. This great sage Kashyapa was also presented at the time of Arjuna's birth. Now Arjuna's birth is extraordinary, it's not ordinary because Arjuna is the constant companion of Krishna. So when Krishna appeared as the sage Narayana, Arjuna appeared as his twin brother Nara, Nararishi. So Nara Narayana Rishi are twins and they are performing great austerities in the Himalayas even now. They are exemplary in their performing austerities. Human life is meant for practicing austerities for spiritual perfection. So they are teaching by their example, Nara Narayana Rishi. So like this, Arjuna's birth is not ordinary. He is a great, he is a pure devotee of Krishna and he participates in Krishna's pastimes on this earth planet, in this material world.

### detachment, Kashyapa Muni, Angirasa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZZmJKwfy_6w?t=931
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker describes Kashyapa Muni receiving the world in charity from Parashurama and then discusses Angirasa (Vrihaspati), the priest of the demigods, and his interactions with Indra, highlighting the power of illusory energy.

Kashyapa Muni received a presentation of the whole world from Parashurama and later on he asked Parashurama to go out of the world. The sinking persons are completely detached. They are not attached to anything. So they teach by their exemplary behaviour how they are completely detached. Parashurama is the one who gave the whole world in charity to Kashyapa Muni and Kashyapa Muni is telling now you can leave this place and go away. Kashyapa Muni has got another name, Arishta Nevi. He lives on the northern side of the universe. See these sages, they have very very long life span. They live in different parts of the universe at different times. Next, the description of Angirasa. Angirasa is the son of Maharaj Angira and is known as Vrihaspati, the priest of the demigods. Shukracharya was the spiritual master of the demons and Vrihaspati challenged him. His son Kacha and he, and he delivered the fire weapons first to Bharadwaj Muni. He could travel in space and therefore he could present himself even in the planets of Brahma Loka and Indra Loka. Once this Vrihaspati cursed Indra to become a hog and Indra was unwilling to return to heaven after his period of curse was over. Such is the powerful power of the attraction of the illusory energy. He was the religious preceptor of natives of different planets. So that's the description of Vrihaspati or Angirasa. I'll stop here.

**Summary:** The lecture begins with traditional Vaishnava prayers and mantras, invoking blessings and paying respects to spiritual teachers and deities.

Om Agyanati Mirandasya Gyananjali Shalakaya Chakshuram Meelitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Namaho Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhaktivedanta Swaminviti Namine. **Verse References:** SB 1.9.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the ninth verse of the ninth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Bhishma Deva's qualities and his understanding of religious principles according to time and place.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 9th verse of the 9th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Tansame Tana Mahabhagan Upalabhya Vasudha Maha Poojaya Mahasadharma Gno Desha Kala Vibhagavit. The meaning of this verse is Bhishma Deva was the best amongst the eight Vasus, received and welcomed all the great and powerful Rishis who were assembled there and knew perfectly all the religious principles according to time and place. Desha Kala Vibhagavit.

So the background for this verse is Bhishma is above the Buddhist body, and the Pandavas have come to meet Bhishma Deva to receive instructions from him, especially Yudhishthira, and Krishna has also accompanied the Pandavas, and all the great Rishis from all over the world, all over the universe, they have arrived there. Why? Because a great soul is going to quit his body. Bhishma Deva is one of the twelve Mahajanas, so this quitting the body in the right consciousness is the perfection of one's life.

### Bhagavad Gita, Krishna consciousness, death
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vOjC2S9dsxc?t=155
**Verse References:** BG 8.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whoever quits his body remembering me alone attains to my nature."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Bhagavad Gita, emphasizing that remembering Krishna at the time of death leads to attaining His nature, and connects this to Bhishma Deva's imminent departure.

This is what Lord Krishna describes in the Bhagavad Gita, Antakaale Chamam eva Smaran Muktva Kalevaram Yaprayati Samadbhavam Yati Nargajya Samushayaham. Krishna says whoever quits his body remembering me alone attains to my nature, and we will see in this chapter of Bhagavatam Bhishma Deva is going to quit his body thinking of Krishna, glorifying Krishna, and it is Bhishma's good fortune that Krishna is right in front of him. But still Bhishma Deva received all the great sages who had come at that time, so he received and welcomed all the great and powerful Rishis because he knew perfectly the religious principles according to time and place.

### religious principles, time and place, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vOjC2S9dsxc?t=232
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Expert religionists know perfectly well how to adjust religious principles in terms of time and place."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada on adjusting religious principles according to time and place, citing examples of acharyas like Ramanujacharya, Madhavacharya, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and Srila Prabhupada himself.

So Srila Prabhupada explains in the third part, expert religionist know perfectly well how to adjust religious principles in terms of time and place. According to time and place some adjustments have to be made. The religious principles are given very elaborately in the dharma shastras, but some adjustment has to be made according to time and place, and Bhishma Deva knew all the principles and therefore he is able to properly receive the great and powerful rishis. Srila Prabhupada says all the great acharyas or religious preachers or reformers of the world executed their mission by adjustment of religious principles in terms of time and place, in terms of time and place, in terms of time and place, just like Srila Prabhupada himself. He went to the western countries and introduced Krishna consciousness among those who are not very familiar with the very culture, though he did not change any essential fundamental principle, but he adapted according to the time and place circumstances. So the acharyas know how to make adjustment of religious principles in terms of time and place. Even in the past Ramanujacharya, Madhavacharya, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, all of them made adjustment of religious principles according to time and place. There are different climates and situations in different parts of the world, and if one has to discharge his duties to preach the message of the Lord, he must be expert in adjusting things in terms of time and place.
